### tweety — 2013-04-17T19:40:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84350>

Such good comments in this thread. HoneyChild you are so right about progesterone and thyroid- without tests, I intuitively feel my P is way too high, and wouldn't you know that I shifted from 180mg armour down to 60mg (where I am perfectly happy!) in a ridiculously short amount of time.

I haven't done labs yet, and won't for a while so I am totally following intuition. Interesting thought about when the need to change or make impulsive changes comes. For years I would go through a love/hate cycle with my husband. For two weeks out of the month I was convinced we should divorce. Then, something would happen (didn't realize at the time it was hormones shifting) and I was totally in love with him again. I said it before, my poor husband. This was every month for the majority of our marriage. Adding P almost eliminated it completely. But then P went too high, and my emotions have gotten messy again. At least now I can rationalize through this insanity. I still feel it, but I can see rationale waiting on the other side.

I just know my body is so unhappy. It's acting out in all kinds of ways, I just can't speak its language yet. I guess that's all of us here.

### BJK77 — 2013-04-18T17:15:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84396>

I'm beginning to wonder if a lot of this doesn't have to do with the seasonal change. I am absolutely craving the sun! Unfortunately the 10-day forecast shows very little chance of me getting sun anytime soon  I'm planning to hit the tanning bed this afternoon though to see if that helps.

Would the seasonal change cause me to have an increased need for Pg? It seems to me it should lower my need for T3, but I can't figure out why extra light would cause my body to need more Pg??

This overheated feeling has been driving me crazy, so I decided to try cutting back on Pg last night to 250 mg before bed instead of 300 mg . . . my sleep was total crap! And then I woke up and couldn't have a BM - definitely signs of low Pg. This also tends to happen at the beginning of my cycle. This concerns me that it's happening mid-cycle now because just a few weeks ago I was fine on 200 mg Pg (at least in the BM department). Why the sudden need for so much Pg . . . I've definitely massively decreased my EMF exposure and increased my spring water consumption, so that shouldn't be the cause.

I do feel my thyroid is humming along pretty well right now. I can't remember the last time I had cold hands or feet which was my tell-tale sign of hypoT before. The really strange thing now though is that I'm gaining weight. When I was hypoT everyone always acted so surprised because I was really thin; now that my thyroid is actually working I'm putting on the pounds! Is it the Pg? I have no clue!

My gut is beginning to tell me it's driven from E2, but my last labs showed low E2 (with a decent Pg:E2 ratio of 231). I'm trying to CT as much as possible to detox any extra E2. I just ordered some DIM too . . . anyone have experience with that? Also trying to up my intake of cruciferous veggies since they should help clear E2, but I don't want to overdo it and have those affect my thyroid either.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-18T17:34:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84403>

Seasonal changes can have big impact on hormones in my experience.

it could be, that your body is actually leveraging what Pg your giving it, along with the water to address dehydration at the cellular level and as you increase your hydration, you would have a need for greater Pg - to sustain it. Think of pregnancy. high Pg, larger blood volume = more water.

so as your water/hydration increases, so does your need for more Pg.

as for T3 -what you think you need, vs what you actually need are two different things... You might need more T3 b/c your body's actually trying to make Pg itself... in addition to what you're supplementing... you might just be laying some good ground work right now.

One things for sure - with the recent spring forward my cycle is completely discombobulated. and until your post, I didn't even think that it could have been the impact of changing seasons, in addition to EMF and the need for more Pg...

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-19T00:27:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84449>

Dzugan gives the following ranges for Women:

Total Estrogen 250-437 pg/ml
Progesterone: 6-28ng/ml
Testosterone: 60-76ng/dl
DHEA-S 250-380 ug/dl
Pregnenolone: 200-230ng/dl
cortisol (am) 15-22.4 ug/dl

these are his optimal ranges - I think Jack has a different view on ranges as well. THe question is HOW DO YOU FEEL - given where you're at in the RANGE. Good? Bad? So-So? super crapy?

This is why journaling, and detailed information is so key! each of us has our own bodies, and they each respond differently to seasonal changes, light cycles, foods, BHRT and supplements... it all comes back to how you feel

### tweety — 2013-04-19T00:29:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84450>

Stress plays a role in lowering hormones, food intolerances, underlying conditions and unchecked health problems, and I would think maternal health at the time of our conception and pregnancy... I am about to be 35, and this started at puberty, but really the peak was a few years ago probably. I am thinking our mothers' generation had less nutrition than theirs, and that alone set us up for a different profile than they even had. Likewise, our children will be in a different boat than us. Now add to that our increased environmental stressors (emf, toxins, etc) and our life quality stressors (work, school, make more, do more, be more, etc)- well we are fighting a losing battle. Unless we start attacking everything we can.

One of my biggest emf offenses would be carrying my cell phone in my pocket. While walking the dog, cleaning the house, or just when out running errands... it was always in my pocket. It was wreaking havoc on my hormones the whole time just by directly radiating my ovaries. I try very hard to keep it away from me, but at this point no distance is safe enough.

> these are his optimal ranges - I think Jack has a different view on ranges as well. THe question is HOW DO YOU FEEL - given where you're at in the RANGE. Good? Bad? So-So? super crapy?
>
> This is why journaling, and detailed information is so key! each of us has our own bodies, and they each respond differently to seasonal changes, light cycles, foods, BHRT and supplements... it all comes back to how you feel

Very true. I am not getting labs, but my thyroid med is perfect for me right now. Its not where I thought it should be, but I feel happy! I may be overweight and unhappy with my body comp at the moment, but emotionally and mentally I feel wonderful! I am in such a good mood and have such great energy, so I know its right. I actually went off of it and by day 3 I was feeling low and sleepy. I decided to get back to it asap! So while I will get labs at some point, I am ignoring numbers at the moment and going by how I feel.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-04-19T15:36:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84476>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=84449)
>
> Dzugan gives the following ranges for Women:
>
> Total Estrogen 250-437 pg/ml
> Progesterone: 6-28ng/ml
> Testosterone: 60-76ng/dl
> DHEA-S 250-380 ug/dl
> Pregnenolone: 200-230ng/dl
> cortisol (am) 15-22.4 ug/dl
>
> these are his optimal ranges - I think Jack has a different view on ranges as well. THe question is HOW DO YOU FEEL - given where you're at in the RANGE. Good? Bad? So-So? super crapy?
>

Thanks for this! Hmmm curious then about why it took so long for them to give me tri-est? My estrogen was around 120. Granted my p is 10.8 but it seems like I had to ask for it a few times. I basically just asked and said we should try it

### diane — 2013-04-20T19:49:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84549>

Awesome thread! Have been away so just catching up, but there's some great stuff in here. And it really helps to see that there is way more going on than just fixing one thing. I'm also trying to figure out the thyroid/progesterone balance - but now going to try pregnenolone as well.

One thing I realized while I was awa is that a lot of the really tired and crappy days have vanished. I know that because when I was travelling I felt them - but for me, sleep was the key. For the first time ever I wasn't the one going out late at night (who is this person) and sleep definitely helped me feel better than I would have. Needed coffee to keep me going - that was the other clue that things were off the rails. But it can be good when you slide back - because then you realize how far you've come! I think it will all get sorted out quickly now that I'm home.

### Inger — 2013-04-20T21:16:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84556>

Great stuff Diane.

I cannot stay up late at all anymore.. it feels so bad so I never do.. Not even new years eve was worth that struggle, I went to bed at 10 PM. No regrets.  Bed is calling me loud when comes evening and it feels like luxury.

It really is amazing to not be tired in the day, but at night instead.

its all about hydration... go back and re-read the dehydration thread - then go back and read the quantum water and quantum PCOS

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-04-26T00:19:16+0300

Here's another P question...bc I'm on day 21 of m cycle and supposedly this is when P is higher than E...I feel horrid. Tired, bloated, swollen, bigger busts and chest. I jsut want to go home and go to sleep. Will adding E to at this point HELP? or is that diverting the purpose...bc should I theoretically be feeling GREAT with this much P? Is that the point? Obv I need to focus on hydration (still working on that)

Also - it has me thinking. I've been so scared and also annoyed/impatient to delay pregnancy due to my health issues. We really want to start bc Im 31 and not getting any younger...however, we wory about the babys health obviously and mine. However...

If HCG makes me feel better - and it does - if it were possible to be on it nonstop I would! -is it possible that getting preggers (which naturally produces HCG of course) and also during that time doing everything "right": lower carb, seasonlly eating, reducing EMF wherever possible, grounding, amber glasses, hydration...eating nutritionally dense foods, is it possible for pregnancy to actually help a body HEAL? is that crazy? I know many women usually come out of pregnancy with more hormonal problems than they began but so many of them are usually on an ice-cream and potato-chip diet at the time or at the very best the CW diet of "healthy"...

I'd be interested in anyone who has used DrKruse's info while pregnant (the ones appropriate for pregnant ladies of course!)...anyone out there? It would be so intersting to track Kruser babies

>

>

I know many women usually come out of pregnancy with more hormonal problems than they began but so many of them are usually on an ice-cream and potato-chip diet at the time or at the very best the CW diet of "healthy"...
>
> I'd be interested in anyone who has used DrKruse's info while pregnant (the ones appropriate for pregnant ladies of course!)...anyone out there? It would be so intersting to track Kruser babies

looking at your symptoms - and going back to Dr Reiss's book [https://www.uzzireissmd.com/book\_naturalhormone.html](https://www.uzzireissmd.com/book_naturalhormone.html) your symptoms are classic ESTROGEN DOMINANCE - you don't have enough Pg at this point in your cycle - give Dzugan an update ASAP! don't supplement w/E! Tell him exactly what your feeling and when it started. you need more Pg - a lot more is what I'm guessing.

I can't speak as to whether things would get better or worse with pregnancy - I can' only tell you that my pregnancies weren't the most pleasant experiences for me - I was miserable -with the exception that I didn't have migraines. I would say that you need to have an OB on board w/what you're doing. you also need to be aware that an upside down Pg:E2 ratio is one of the reasons women have pre-mature babies. I spent last friday afternoon w/a dear friend who's on medical bed rest in the hosptial b/c she was having pre-term labor at 26wks -right now the goals is to get her and the baby to 28wks.

> [nuttmegs17 said:](/goto/post?id=84830)
>
> Gretchen - in your Quanton Guest Post, what was it that suddenly got you into that pathway, grounding? and that finally changed your body comp and got you in smaller clothes? Bc youve been doing everything so consistently...it seems you just added in Grounding, addressed hydration right? How much do you ground a day? Perhaps this will straighten out my P

I would say ground helped, but it wasn't the MASTER KEY for me. the MASTER KEY was my thyroid. I have come to believe - that when you put everything thing together - you still need a MASTER KEY to turn the lock. I think that is the thyroid. why? LDL + vitamin A + T3 = pregnenolnone, from pregneonlone you make progesterone. Add in Cold and you enter the PPP. Ground is just one more way to bring in the electrons, which help "Fill up" your tank.

my most recent labs while not great confirmed for me my Thyroid is what's limiting my success. I'm finally on a dose of Armor that's working for me. While my T4 is still kind of low, my TSH is .45 but my T3 is out of the range - but I FEEL GREAT. I'm not doing anything different food wise. but weight is dropping off me. people I haven't seen in while keep commenting about how I'm loosing weight and what I'm doing.. my last set of test confirmed for my PCP that my thyroid has been untreated for a while...

Grounding, CT, and eating right is part of the solution...but for me it was fixing the thyroid.

Hi Nuttmegs, I hope to speak from experience in that don't bring a baby into the world if you are not optimal as you can possible be. I am a product of a non optimal mom and grandma. Low P all the way and I can see how it has affected me and my children. By the time I was 15, there was something obviously wrong and of course no way to know what to do. My son was born with life threatening allergies to peanuts, wheat and dairy. The doctor said that 30 years ago, he would not have lived but since there was a very special formula that cost $24.00 per can, he could survive. I would love to rewind and go back and fix me before I had any kids. Keep plugging away and you will find optimal. Sometimes it is so close, but yet so far away. I too think thyroid is a huge key for me.

it appears as classic E Dom but when I was using just p I gained weight and swelled up so much in the boobs it was painful. And as much as I want to push the P my body rebels. They had me on lower and lower dosed bc I got incredibly dry eyes and felt catatonic. I guess I hope that by supp E maybe it will allow me to handle the P a bit better and perhaps even down regulate the E my body is making. I am so confused though. One other side effect I'm experiencing right now is dry eyes and sleepiness. And right now I'm not on anything yet I feel like I did t when I had too much P...

I put in another update to Dugan giving them the symptons I gave u. I look forward to their feedback! I'm taking your advice at updating them more regularly

I am so happy that you are getting the thyroid under control. That is HUGE. HUGE!

At my last appt the NP drove me nuts bc she kept saying that all my probs (high LDL among other things) were thyroid and adrenal related but basically was of no help. Like you my T4 is a bit lower and now my T3 is slightly elevated but I show no signs of hyper. Again so confused she wanted me to back off the t3 but I see no need. I certainly have the LDL plus vit A( i take fermented cod liver oil) and I think the pregnelone (dzugan upped me to 300) so I pray it all clicks soon

I haven't been CTing the traditional way. I've just been biking outside in the cold a lot. The other day it was 45 degrees and pouring on my way home. I was freezing and soaked but i told myself i was CTing! in fact my legs especially were that mice pink color for hours afterwards. I will be increasing the cold showers again though

Would you u mind sharing what a typical day of meals is for you? What about when u go out to eat?

Updated to include thyroid numbers)
My TSH is low though .004 (.450 - 4.500)
Ft3 4.9 (upper range is 4.4)
Ft4 .89 ( .82-1.77)
Rt3 10.2 (9.2 - 24.1)

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-04-26T04:39:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84853>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=84849)
>
> I am sorry you feel that but please don't give up (it's just a feeling and it will pass )....you have found this great place and have gotten rock star advice. Just this knowledge puts you lightyears ahead of many folks....so just keep up all you are doing.....you will get there  I have no doubt....

Thank you . I just get frustrated. I have been following dr k since way back in the day over at the MDA thread. Sometimes I feel like ill never be the healthy person I used to be but then I try to remember I have made progress.

I stumbled on some paperwork the other day. It was information on taking a leave of absence from work. I almost forgot about that but thankfully I am no longer at that point! So I need to remind myself of those things. I'm no longer calling in sick. I'm grateful for that!

I've seen people like Gretchen (who has the biggest brain I've ever seen!) and mama grok make so much progress but I know they've had their frustrations too. Even glamarama has spun off into a glamorous blogger doing great things! Everyone is so inspiring! People like Colleen kindly share their experiences. We r a lucky group

Sometimes though I wonder if I make it harder on myself. I think I'm overly critical which can be good to a point. I think of my friends who aren't running labs all the time: they might get PMS or acid reflux but they shrug it off and if anyone were to ask they'd say "I'm in great health!" It's like I know too much. Sometimes knowledge is power but then in my dark moments I think "ignorance is bliss". I pray I'll be better in the long run though and most certainly my future children will be! I've gotta be careful not to stress myself out Its a fine line

Interesting stuff Gretchen! I always learn so much from reading your posts on this forum. For people like myself who need things simplified, you really do a good job of making everything easier to understand. Thank you so much for that.

I am a 30 year old male, and most posts on here are related to women and their hormone levels at various periods during their cycle. But for me, what are your thoughts on taking T3 only when one has a T3:RT3 ratio that is bad and below 20? My Free T3 is really low and out of range, and TSH is 4.0 and out of range on last tests almost a year ago. T4 was low barely in range as well. My dhea-s was right in the middle but my testosterone was very low as well. So from trying to put the pieces together from the hormone 101 blog and all the other blogs and forums relevant to this topic, my very low Free T3 is causing low hormones across the board more than likely, correct?

Also, since I am getting up to urinate at least 3x a night, this would also indicate a low Progesterone more than likely, correct? I have read about preg steal syndrome, and have no doubt this is what happened with me. I went through a 2 year period of fasting for 2 days, serious overtraining and over exercising despite having about 7% bodyfat, eating disorders, taking 1200 mgs of caffeine, no sleep at all, being extremely stressed all day every day due to high stakes gambling as a living, and sitting with a computer on my lap for 8+ hours everyday. I am trying to piece this together, and I would appreciate it if you think I am on the right track.

Such a great thread!

I am trying to piece this together, and I would appreciate it if you think I am on the right track.
>
> Such a great thread!

With those thyroid levels yes you most certainly need hormone. That TSH is very high. I personally would feel catatonic with that level. Back before having hypo my normal TSH range was just below 1. ### JanSz — 2013-04-29T01:29:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-85067>

> [nuttmegs17 said:](/goto/post?id=84448)
>
> What should estrogen be? I always see things like "you want progesterone at least 11 or higher...Testosterone should be in the 60s, etc" but besides saying you want P 300 times higher then E, I never really see any other rules of thumb.

Try thick blue line at estradiol chart:
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)

Thick blue line on progesterone chart is for orientation only.
Use that as a minimum that you would fall into when you can't help it.

Dr Kruse recommends P/E2~500
use calculator (link included)
or
use table I included there.
use your E2 then on right row read corresponding value for progesterone (when P/E2=500)
example
you have:
E2=40pg/ml------>progesterone=23ng/mL ---->P/E=498
E2=60pg/ml------>progesterone=34.6ng/mL--->P/E2=500

note that chart on progesterone posted by Wiki goes only to 25ng/ml, that is lots of progesterone that dr Kruse is asking for, nobody have that much.

(So, practically, nobody should have a reason to talk about having to much progesterone).
..............................

At first I push from both ends

pregnenolone
and
DHEA

using pills
that works for me, (I am man), I do this already for over 1.5years

man & woman are equal in natural healthy progesterone levels in first half of cycle
woman needs more progesterone in second half

I suggest that women work first on stabilization of first half of cycle by using pregnenolone and DHEA only
for second half some women may need (additionally) direct progesterone

\------------------

I have tried variety of pregnenolones, pills, trasdermal, sublingual, variety of different bases and applicators... etc
The only one that works for me is

pregnolone Micronized Lipid Matrix 150mg tabs from Nutricology

also

DHEA Micronized Lipid Matrix 50mg tabs from Allergy Research

I use 2 pills/day of each of the above

\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About Cycloset.
It differs from other (bromocriptine mesylates) available that it is absorbs rapidly (therefore the nausea and some gastric upsets when first using it.

As any (bromocriptine mesylates) it lowers Prolactin.

Dr Kruse mentions 6 tabs dose of 0.8mg Cysloset in the morning.
I am using Cycloset but only two tabs and already my Prolactin is slightly below bottom range

prolactin=3.4 with 2 tabs 0.8mg Cycloset

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?2927-Cycloset-and-FBG-update-oh-my!](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?2927-Cycloset-and-FBG-update-oh-my!)

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=44090)
>
> This is the OSA battling the cycloset......I'd tell you to give it some time to work itself through. If sleep is still an issue You may want to increase DHEA to help foster sleep. The cycloset can cause an upset stomach because of how it dramatically alters the gut flora because of the acute changes in cortisol and dopamine in the brain and the gut.
>
> Remember the **cycloset is made for increasing AM cortisol......Works awesome and is a great way to stop taking hydrocortisone in LR states or in Adrenal fatigue.** Lots of educational consultants are hearing about how I might consider to use it now.
>
> **Cortisol is a stress hormone that is linked with dopamine production in the brain. Levels of cortisol in the bloodstream are highest during rising in the AM, which in turn leads to higher dopamine levels during daytime. Dopamine, a neurotransmitter, is directly responsible for a number of important functions in the brain, including attention and learning when we rise.**
>
> Recent studies have suggested that the circadian neuroendocrine rhythm mediated by dopamine and serotonin plays a large role in insulin sensitivity. In people without diabetes, the postmeal state causes a decline in endogenous glucose production due to suppression of glucagon and inhibition of lipolysis. In individuals with T2D, a drop in dopamine levels is thought to lead to an inadequate hypothalamus response, resulting in elevated levels of blood glucose, free fatty acids (FFA), and triglycerides (TG), which contribute to insulin resistance, visceral adiposity, and beta-cell dysfunction. **Clinical studies suggest that bromocriptine-QR administered in the morning (within 2 hours of waking) mimics the normal peak of dopamine in the central nervous system, consequently thought to reset this circadian rhythm. The main target organ of bromocriptine is the brain.**
>
> However, subsequent effects have been observed in adipose tissue (due to decreased levels of glucose, FFAs, and TGs) and the liver (reduced hepatic gluconeogenesis due to reduced cortisol levels in response to dopamine). Higher brain dopamine levels will reduce the cortisol spikes seen in Leptin resistant states like T2D or IR.
>
> 1\. Meier A, Cincotta A. Circadian rhythms regulate the expression of the thrifty genotype/phenotype. Diabetes Rev. 1996;4:464-487.

> [JanSz\]OSA what is it? \[/QUOTE\] \[QUOTE=Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=44198)

> [bennyha said:](/goto/post?id=84998)
>
> Interesting stuff Gretchen! I always learn so much from reading your posts on this forum. For people like myself who need things simplified, you really do a good job of making everything easier to understand. Thank you so much for that.
>
> I am a 30 year old male, and most posts on here are related to women and their hormone levels at various periods during their cycle. But for me, what are your thoughts on taking T3 only when one has a T3:RT3 ratio that is bad and below 20? My Free T3 is really low and out of range, and TSH is 4.0 and out of range on last tests almost a year ago. T4 was low barely in range as well. My dhea-s was right in the middle but my testosterone was very low as well. So from trying to put the pieces together from the hormone 101 blog and all the other blogs and forums relevant to this topic, my very low Free T3 is causing low hormones across the board more than likely, correct?
>
> Also, since I am getting up to urinate at least 3x a night, this would also indicate a low Progesterone more than likely, correct? I have read about preg steal syndrome, and have no doubt this is what happened with me. I went through a 2 year period of fasting for 2 days, serious overtraining and over exercising despite having about 7% bodyfat, eating disorders, taking 1200 mgs of caffeine, no sleep at all, being extremely stressed all day every day due to high stakes gambling as a living, and sitting with a computer on my lap for 8+ hours everyday. I am trying to piece this together, and I would appreciate it if you think I am on the right track.
>
> Such a great thread!

Sorry for the late response Bennyha, been overwhelmed at work! My thoughts on addressing RT3 is that 1st you need to identify why you're having the conversion problem in the first place.

initially I started w/T3 bt it didn't make me feel any better. Instead my BHRT doc and PCP decided to switch me to Armour. slowly and steadily we fixed the RT3 problem which for me was the result of a thyroid set on idle for years. after 6mths of Armour we cleared the RT3 issue, but once that cleared we discovered that I wasn't producing enough T4... and my pitutary kept pumping out TSH - eventually pushing my TSH level almost to 8. Once I got on the right dosage of armour split across the day -things began to turn around quick for me.

add in the grounding, magnetico sleep pad and CT and suddenly sleep became awesome, breakfast became almost non-existent - there are some days I just can't eat breakfast - I'm too full... so I have my bulletproof coffee and off I go. I've lost weight and now fit in to my pre-pregnancy clothes that I wore 6years ago and they're starting to get loose! I've also moved into a period in which I have more energy, more brain function. My biggest win though is I've been migraine free for 2 whole cycles.

Given your history - you need to start focusing on eat Epi-Paleo using the Leptin reset, focus on circadian cycles and limit your EMFs and CT. If you can run some labs and start working to find a doctor to help you figure out whats going on with your Thryoid. With out labs - we can't pinpoint where your major issues are but at least you can focus on the basics.

Since you're a guy - I think you'll bounce back much faster as in general Leptin Levels in men are lower than women. My DH saw real results within 60days of eating Epi-paleo, using the Leptin Reset, monitoring circadian cycles and applying CT.

> Thanks for your reply Gretchen I really appreciate it! When I was given Armour, it made me feel brutal. But I was under the assumption that if one has a T3:RT3 ratio under 20, that any extra T4 in pill form (like in Armour) would just fuel the fire due to the misbalanced ratio and make one feel worse. Is that off base?

it can but its not always the case - which is why its important to figure out why you have a conversion problem. knowing you have a thyroid problem already - have you stopped by and read Grizz's iodine thread. worth a read if you have thyroid issues. you need Iodine, plus other essentials like selenium and zinc to support the proper conversion... initially I started on selenium and zinc supplements, then 6mths later started adding the lugols protocol.

I actually felt better on Armour than I did on T3. n=1. I just gave you how I cleared my RT3... it doesn't work like this for everyone...

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-02T23:41:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-85343>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=85206)
>
> Sorry for the late response Bennyha, been overwhelmed at work! My thoughts on addressing RT3 is that 1st you need to identify why you're having the conversion problem in the first place.
>
> initially I started w/T3 bt it didn't make me feel any better. Instead my BHRT doc and PCP decided to switch me to Armour. slowly and steadily we fixed the RT3 problem which for me was the result of a thyroid set on idle for years. after 6mths of Armour we cleared the RT3 issue, but once that cleared we discovered that I wasn't producing enough T4... and my pitutary kept pumping out TSH - eventually pushing my TSH level almost to 8. Once I got on the right dosage of armour split across the day -things began to turn around quick for me.
>
> add in the grounding, magnetico sleep pad and CT and suddenly sleep became awesome, breakfast became almost non-existent - there are some days I just can't eat breakfast - I'm too full... so I have my bulletproof coffee and off I go. I've lost weight and now fit in to my pre-pregnancy clothes that I wore 6years ago and they're starting to get loose! I've also moved into a period in which I have more energy, more brain function. My biggest win though is I've been migraine free for 2 whole cycles.
>
> Given your history - you need to start focusing on eat Epi-Paleo using the Leptin reset, focus on circadian cycles and limit your EMFs and CT. If you can run some labs and start working to find a doctor to help you figure out whats going on with your Thryoid. With out labs - we can't pinpoint where your major issues are but at least you can focus on the basics.
>
> Since you're a guy - I think you'll bounce back much faster as in general Leptin Levels in men are lower than women. My DH saw real results within 60days of eating Epi-paleo, using the Leptin Reset, monitoring circadian cycles and applying CT.

First off - congratulations on your amazing progress! I have been interacting with you since the MDA thread and to see things clicking is exciting! God knows you have worked hard for it and deserve it!

Also, thanks for saying that about the T4. I think sometimes people focus on T3 and lose sight of the fact that perhaps balance is key. Pure T3 doesnt work for everyone. My TSH is .004 when the range is .4 - 4.5.... I have been confused lately bc my levels are off but my T3 is slightly elevated yet I still am symptomatic. My T4 is on the low side though and your experience makes me want to addresss that even more!

Problem is, I'm on a combo of armour (T4 AND T3) and Cytomel (T3 only). I can't really increase my T4 with my armour without also increasing my T3. I am on 5 mcg total of Cytomel a day. But perhaps if I go off that cytomel (minus 5 mcg of T3) and instead take one more half grain of armour (adding 4.5 T3) that would address it? another thing I might run by my doc is dropping armour and going to Synthroid and Cytomel combined to have a bit more control over the ratio...

Thoughts?

Morning: 1 grain armour, 2.5 cyto
Noon: half grain armour, 2.5 cyto

T4, Low end of the range,
T3, slightly too high - not crazy high but slightly.

# Thread: Improved Joints
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2013-04-18T15:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84378>

More and more often I'm noticing an old feeling that comes when I move around on my feet - a smooth, warm, fluid range of motion. Instead of painful creaking and groaning, I move like the teen of my youth. I can walk on the balls of my feet silently and assuredly, and more than once have broken out into a little trot just because I can! Downright giddy sometimes! Moving like a child again!

Less hip pain as well. I notice I can stand on one foot and pull the other leg up and put on a sock or shoe while balancing and there is no pain when I bring my leg up. I used to limp constantly, and now I walk "like a cat" (my DW's words, not mine!).

Good solid info and a way of life that is taking me back to our ancestor's ways. I always thought that there was an answer in the "old" ways before modern life, and never found it until I came here.

Thanks to all that have researched, posted, shared, spoken up, asked, and revealed.

"Martin"

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-18T15:11:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84382>

Yeah, that's one of the unexpected benefits of the ancient pathway. I got into this to deal with diabetes and in the process everything about my physicality improved.My 52 yr old body is pain free and I have bounce in my stride and sometimes I feel so good it's ridiculous. It seems to keep getting better. I haven't seen a massage therapist or a chiropractor in over two years and don't miss it at all.

### Inger — 2013-04-18T15:17:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84383>

That sounds Amazing, Martin!  Thank you for sharing! DaliDula too.. yay!

Me too feels like I am getting younger and younger instead of older..lol pretty cool feeling! I have decided that I going to go the opposite way with my age, at least for a few decades...
It is such a blessing to have no pains, no issues.. just feeling like a child all over. huh!

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-18T15:41:21+0300

### Optimalbound — 2013-04-18T17:22:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84398>

Awesome improvement, Martin! I never had much joint pain, myself, but I certainly move with greater strength, fluidity and agility now than what I did a year ago. Downhill skiing was incredibly much more enjoyable for me this season than last year.

### caroline — 2013-04-18T22:31:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84440>

That is so awesome Martin - great news! Now all the things you have put on hold can become your new reality.

I, too, have been completely free of joint pain, arthritis etc. seems unbelievable - but there it is.

We have a few more decades to keep getting younger.........

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-19T06:46:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84465>

this is a sign to begin to do more with your collagen semiconductors........that means massage/structured healing modalities......

### nonchalant — 2013-04-19T17:37:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84483>

Martin, I have a similar story.
Last year a chiropractor was working regularly on my back to try to free it from stiffness and immobility in places. (CT helped so much with the pain and inflammation!) He did get some mobility in it after a few months, but when I would do a "hoola-hoop" motion with my hips, I could hear all the grinding in my lower back. It was progress, however. Later I started the 30 day seafood challenge, and I noticed that my lower back was getting quite flexible. And the grinding sound also went away. I still saw the chiropractor, but fewer times per week. Then I started squatting several times per day, and my back got so good and flexible I only went in once per week. But it wasn't until I started laying my spine on my grounding sheet, and drinking good water, and also sun water, that I was able to say goodbye to the chiropractor. I know the secret now.  Anytime it tries to stiffen up, it gets grounded asap.

Thank you, Dr. Kruse, for your various protocols. They have helped me tremendously. A massage sounds marvelous, but I'm still paying the chiropractor bill... ;-)

### kathylu — 2013-04-19T22:07:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84495>

Same story here. 18 months ago I was walking with a cane from inflammatory arthritis and popping pain meds like candy. Now...no pain! No cane! I also had a problem with my left knee that was quite stubborn...painful, clicking and grinding. My doc wanted to send me for an orthopedic consult, glad I didn't go. The knee is completely healed (as far as I can tell).

Out of the blue a few days ago, my newly 6 year old son said "Mama, you used to be handicapped...I remember your cane! I'm glad you're better." I'm still smiling.

### caroline — 2013-04-20T01:10:44+0300

# Thread: Dr Robert O. BECKER  Silver Ionotophoresis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-robert-o-becker-silver-ionotophoresis.6812/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2013-04-18T15:05:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-robert-o-becker-silver-ionotophoresis.6812/post-84380>

In researching what to do with my abscessed toofer, I ran across this about our boy Becker.

[http://www.rexresearch.com/becker/becker1.htm](http://www.rexresearch.com/becker/becker1.htm)

"Silver can help regenerate human cells that have been destroyed by disease or damaged in accidents."

"The silver-based procedure has been so successful in clinical tests, that one patient who had sustained three crushed fingers in an accident grew new tissue immediately. Within 2-1/2 months, skin coverage was complete and there was normal, full sensation, good blood supply and all joints had a normal range of motion. If left untreated, the 3~year-old electrician's fingers would have fallen off after turning black with gangrene, and he would have been left with a totally useless hand. In fact, his orthopedic surgeon recommended amputation of al1 three fingers, but the patient requested silver-ion therapy that was successful."

"Becker discovered that when positively charged silver ions are electrically introduced into wounds with a proprietary silver-coated nylon fabric used as the positive electrode, large amounts of primitive embryonic stem cells are produced. These stem cells are responsible for the reconstruction of destroyed tissue at a pace considerably faster than if the wound had been left to heal by itself. In other cases, the wound might not heal at all without the introduction of these stem cells."

### caroline — 2013-04-18T22:56:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-robert-o-becker-silver-ionotophoresis.6812/post-84443>

so interesting Martin ... are you able to access this treatment? Sounds like you are clicking on all cylinders these days!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-19T06:40:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-robert-o-becker-silver-ionotophoresis.6812/post-84462>

### tweety — 2013-04-19T02:34:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84458>

What kind of salt does everyone prefer? I have been using sea salt almost exclusively. But I recently had a chat with a naturopath who felt sea salt was not the way to go, that it led to problems like excessive swelling and increases in bad cholesterol. He also felt it contributed to thickening of the blood. I'm not sure I agree in the least with him, but it gave me cause to go read up on my salts. I know there are so many different products out there. Himalayan salts, sea salts, kosher salt....

His recommendation was kosher salt, but I didn't get to ask why.
What does everyone use? And why? I know alot of people in the paleoshpere swear by Redmond's Real Salt.

### nonchalant — 2013-04-19T03:57:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84459>

Kosher salt refers to the size of the salt crystals. Kosher salt (koshering salt) doesn't have the anti-caking ingredients many fine-grained salts have. I personally like to have a variety of salts.

### Inger — 2013-04-19T07:29:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84468>

I use
Celtic sea salt, Himalayan crystal salt, 100% natural "salt flowers" from France. I love them all

### caroline — 2013-04-19T08:26:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84470>

I love Celtic sea salt the best but also use Himalayan....

### johnnyb — 2013-04-19T16:02:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84478>

I mostly use the Real Salt brand. It applies like table salt but has other trace minerals. The Himalayan pink salt has particular taste that I am trying with more foods. It seems to enhance some foods more then others. Since it comes as rocks the grinder it is in changes it's utility. I still use kosher salt some. Mostly for brines or when I want better control sprinkling a pinch. Since I started drinking mineral water I add less salt as I cook.

If this can relieve me of the incredible misery I've been having for months I'm definitely following your recommendation and coming back to say that you got it all right!

### Martin — 2013-06-04T00:41:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-87411>

I loves my pink Himalayan salt. Chunks make great lamps when you hollow them out, or, put them in a basket with an amber nite lite bulb buried in there.

# Thread: Minimum effective dose for CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 38
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2013-04-20T02:31:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84511>

So this is a bit of a strange inquiry, and my guess is there is too little data to really give an effective answer... but it never hurts to ask

I am using CT solely for the vast array of subliminal health benefits (immunity, well-being, longevity, etc.); I am already ~8% BF so don't need it to help me lose fat really. So far, I have experimented with daily cold showers (almost 2 months now). I really like doing them, and at this point I can basically jump right in without much more than a couple seconds of adapting... When I first started I would have to count to 90 or so before my body was tolerant without being uncomfortable. I also can feel myself radiating heat at night now as it gets cooler outside, and find I get overly warm if I wear something more than an A shirt or T shirt. (Aside: I live in San Jose; night temps only dip to ~50's this time of year)

My question is, is there a minimum effective dose to CT? Or, what is the minimum amount that must be done in order to obtain the majority of the benefits? Is it merely a linear sliding scale of "more is better"? Are my showers sufficient or are the benefits 10 fold by switching to extended baths?

If relevant, my diet is primarily keto: 6 days/week I eat 1 large evening meal composed of meat (grass fed beef or various seafood) and veggies cooked in coconut oil. Occasionally I will have a handful of walnuts afterwards, but usually my meal is more than sufficient. One day a week, typically on the weekend, I will allow myself to eat outside of keto, but still keep to 1 meal and use choices like sweet potatoes or trail mix (nuts, seeds, dried berries) and would estimate I don't surpass 150g carbs in the day.

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-20T03:13:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84514>

Any exposure below body temp is theoretically having some effect and it is cumulative. More frequent sessions are more effective than less. More is better. Dr K's protocol does not advocate for full immersion temps below 50 deg although many here practice at temps as low as 32. I am of the opinion that full immersion up to your ears is the way to go. If you have a way to swim at cold temps there is nothing better. Your skin should turn pink. I think Dr k would say you can't do this too much. As for diet try switching your big meal to breakfast.

### caroline — 2013-04-20T03:27:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84519>

Dr. K. told me that cold showers aren't good enough ... nor is the ocean if it isn't cold. Tub and ice is the way to go!
Don't get me wrong - the ocean is great - but if it isn't cold - and it hasn't been this summer ...then you still need a tub and ice......

I was hoping I get similar benefits without having to buy a bag of ice a day to do bath CT... what do you do in the shower? Do you measure the temp of the water....?
Are you only eating one meal a day as per the "Warrior Diet" or doing a Lean Gains type IF 16/8....

### sjoshua — 2013-04-23T22:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84698>

Thanks for the feedback all!

I wish I could relocate my office to a meat locker and just CT all day while at work ;-P Alas, not realistic for a desk jockey... I guess to clarify my inquiry, my intent for asking is to find the most efficient way if time is a factor (sure, the philaspohical answer is likely whether my time is worth more now vs. the benefits/longevity gained, but let's not get into that). From the sounds of the replies, my cold showers aren't enough and I need to up my game to baths. When adopting the baths, what would be the ideal temp/time allotment for achieving the MED? (For more info or some examples of MED, please check this article out: [http://gizmodo.com/5709902/4+hour-body-the-principle-of-the-minimum-effective-dose](http://gizmodo.com/5709902/4+hour-body-the-principle-of-the-minimum-effective-dose) ).

@Dali Dula: Although I realize Jack supports a heavy morning meal, my diet is designed around convenience. I'd much rather spend the time to cook and eat in the evening then try to throw something together before going to work. My compliance is 100%, so I don't consider this to be an adjustable factor.

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=84534)

> @sjoshua.... I'm in the same situation... i don't need to lose weight but am interested in the minimum dose of CT... I have been doing the 20" of cold 10" of warm for 5 minutes (Ray Cronise Protocol) in the shower.... @MeghanK: I keep a consistent, cold temp on the water for my whole shower time (~5-7 minutes); I have not tested the temp prior but I will attempt to check it tonight. Sadly my kitchen thermo recently broke, so I will do the best I can with the laser temp guage (the type for tracking skin/surface temps)... Do you know the temps yours run?

My one meal/day was initially inspired by lean gains/IF, but it is strongly driven by and maintained due to personal convenience. Not having to cook, clean, or otherwise stop what I am doing to spend time pertaining to eating more than once/day is ideal for my lifestyle.
When only cooking once/day, I tend to put more time and effort into crafting a gourmet-esque meal vs. just cookin' up some grub. Also, I have always been one to eat big when I eat, so this allots me the ability to do so without much issue.

### sootedninjas — 2013-04-24T23:45:57+0300

it's the keto diet that makes IF natural.

### sjoshua — 2013-04-25T02:03:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84764>

> [Danco3636 said:](/goto/post?id=84743)
>
> sjoshua do you have any coffee or Bulletproof coffee during the day? Any other snacks? Also do you incorporate workouts during the day? I generally fast 16-20 hours a day and have two meals. For me one meal and I would be stuffed eating the required calories I would need. Unless it was mostly fat.... Now that may work with bacon as a meal.  I generally skip breakfast as well having a lunch or late mid day meal.
> Sounds like it is working for you.

Danco3636: Aside from a coffee in the morning with a splash of cream on the weekends only, I really just drink water. If I drank coffee before work I would be wayyy to wired to sit at a desk! I have maintained a much higher natural energy level since switching my diet ~2 years ago now. No snacking either, never have a need. I waste time with walks (2-2.5 mi) or bike rides (~10 mi) on my daily lunch breaks. 2-3 days/week before dinner and after work around 5-7PM I also do some sort of strength lifting using the reverse pyramid training style (a variation of a high intensity/low set/low rep lifting protocol).

I aim to eat an avg. around ~140g protein/day, which is approx. 1g/lb lean mass (I think this may be more than I really need; I've been doing some rough experimenting with reducing protein and upping fat recently but need to do so in a more organized manner to really dial it in). My fat comes from whatever the protein sources I am eating contain, but I always aim to eat at least 1/2 my protein from a high fat source. Pair that with a pile (1-2 lbs) of broccoli/cauli/carrots/spinach cooked in coconut oil.

Like I mentioned above, I have no problem putting away a lot of food in one sitting. But don't get me wrong, I'm not just force feeding myself. It typically takes me around an hour to eat my whole meal, and sometimes I will be snacking on it for ~2-3 hours.

> [sootedninjas said:](/goto/post?id=84759)
>
> it's the keto diet that makes IF natural.
>
> I'll eat 1 meal around 4pm, following day around 8am or 9am I'll have a variation of bulletproof coffee then around 2pm do some kind of workout martial arts or strength training at the gym. and literally WILL NOT be hungry even after 2 hours.

Sootedninjas: (cool name! ha) Sounds like you are living similarly to me. Curious - do you get hungry at all anymore? On rare occasion I will be too busy and have very little or no meal one day, and then continue my fast to the next evening out of habit (~45ish hours total). When this happens, I may feel a momentary pang earlier in the day, but find that I actually start to feel a little hungry again around my typical meal time that second night.

not really. I don't get hungry at all. sometimes I eat just because it's been 18 hours since my last meal. the beauty of keto diet is that you are not starving at all, not just hungry because being in ketosis is basically being a fat burner. the longest I've been w/o a meal is 25 hours last Saturday. When I moved around, like teaching Martial Arts or lifting heavy things, the less hungrier I get.

looks like you got your diet dialed in. just tweaking it a little here and there. my current tweak right now of Epi-Paleo because it just make sense. I will putting seafood as the main food source in my pyramid then the standard keto paleo of grass fed offal and beef yadi yadi yada....

What is your carb intake ?

Mine is like 50g daily ala Volek & Phinney, Art and Science of Low Carb Performance.

Since, I'm tweaking my diet right now to include more seafood and sea vegetables NOT sure how much more carbs that will add to my diet.

I might just need to start weighing and counting calories for the next 2 to 3 weeks. I hate counting because I find it very stressful BUT it is a necessary evil sometimes when I tweak my diet BUT I soon as I get dialed in to the change I stop counting.

### sjoshua — 2013-04-26T00:30:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84829>

> [sootedninjas said:](/goto/post?id=84765)
>
> What is your carb intake ?
>
> Mine is like 50g daily ala Volek & Phinney, Art and Science of Low Carb Performance.
>
> Since, I'm tweaking my diet right now to include more seafood and sea vegetables NOT sure how much more carbs that will add to my diet.
>
> I might just need to start weighing and counting calories for the next 2 to 3 weeks. I hate counting because I find it very stressful BUT it is a necessary evil sometimes when I tweak my diet BUT I soon as I get dialed in to the change I stop counting.

Ok, it sounds like your experience is similar to mine. I've noticed that even with food in front of me and immediately following an intense workout I'll occasionally have no motivation to eat it. I've been buying grass fed beef by the 1/4 - 1/2 cow for a couple years now, so always have a healthy supply of red meat, and also like to switch things up with mixing various types of fish/mussels, and the occasional fowl meat.

I don't actively track my carb intake, but it is definitely <50g/day 6 days/week. During these days the only carbs I eat are a couple pounds of cruciferous veggies, with the occasional use of peppers/onions/mushrooms for variety. My estimate would be ~30-40g average. One day a week, typically saturdays and also typically following a strength training session, I will allow myself additional carbs through sweet potatoes and/or trail mix incl. dried fruit, nuts, chocolate; but I tend to keep this ~150g.

Art and Science of Low Carb Performance is definitely a book that has interested me, but I haven't had the opportunity to read yet. Do you recommend it? I realize this is purely individual, but was it educational, or moreso a review of principles you were already aware of?

Weighing and counting calories in veggies is fairly moot in my opinion, especially if you are sticking primarily to cruciferous veggies (of which I believe seaweed and probably other sea veggies are generally included along with or would at least be equivalent to) due to their lower carb content. But I understand your concerns about assuring you have things tweaked correctly.

Just curious, do you have macros you aim for vs. your weight/lean mass? Have you experimented with adjusting protein/fat ratios to perfection?

Also, have you implemented any forms of CT into your regimen yet?

1) Def we have similar diet. that is exactly what happens even if there is food in front of me. I bulk buy my grass fed beef too and pastured eggs BUT rarely eat fowl. too much omega 6. I always loved seafood BUT the cost is just sometimes prohibitive specially wild caught. But I've decided to change up and eat more seafood instead. However, I grow up eating a lot of offal so that will always be in the table and it is the cheapest part of the grass fed cow. Sometime I have a group of 4 people split a whole cow and nobody wants the organ, head, tongue and tail so I always end getting it all. suckers

2) yeah about 50g daily is what Phinney and Volek suggested and def working for me. I highly recommend the book BUT as you said you are basically doing what they are recommending. They do discuss protein intake tho.

3) Oh I am very high fat. When I was tracking my macros it was like between 75/15/10 to 80/15/5 (fat/protein/carb) and my weight is very stable. Carb goes up by 5% because from time to time I will have a 1 week worth of berries (strawberry, Blueberry, Blackbeery and raspberry) and mango too if it is relatively inexpensive, That's about my fruit intake. Protein is about .75g to 1g of protein for every pound of body weight. Heavy lifting days, 1x per week for 1 hour, post workout I might go as high as 1.25g of protein. I do high intensity lift a variation of super slow and static. 7 reps of 1 set. By the 7th rep I should be really struggling. If I can do about 8 or 9 rep w/o struggling then it is time to increase the resistance.

4) Based on Robb Wolf and Mark Sisson discussions, try safe starches if you can tolerate it. I tried BUT heck after 2 days I noticed that my right was kinda swollen and my knuckles was hurting plus I suddenly got 2 big pimples in my face. That was the only thing that change in my diet. After I stop eating the safe starch for 1 week the swollen hand subsided and it was back to normal. So I guess, just like Dr. Kruse mentioned on Paleo Summit podcast he does NOT believed on safe starches.

6) CT, I will start this weekend. Do some face dunking for a week or two.

Have you done any CT face dunking ? Once you get your skin temp to around 55 degrees, how long should you be dunking your face for that session ?

After, face dunking I might get one of those cold vest. Have you used any of those ? If you have which ones you recommend ?

Then after cold vest, cold showers and see how it goes.

### sjoshua — 2013-05-03T19:42:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-85387>

>
> 1) Def we have similar diet. that is exactly what happens even if there is food in front of me. I bulk buy my grass fed beef too and pastured eggs BUT rarely eat fowl. too much omega 6. I always loved seafood BUT the cost is just sometimes prohibitive specially wild caught. But I've decided to change up and eat more seafood instead. However, I grow up eating a lot of offal so that will always be in the table and it is the cheapest part of the grass fed cow. Sometime I have a group of 4 people split a whole cow and nobody wants the organ, head, tongue and tail so I always end getting it all. suckers
>

>
> After, face dunking I might get one of those cold vest. Have you used any of those ? If you have which ones you recommend ?
>
> Then after cold vest, cold showers and see how it goes.

Very cool, I ended up with the heart and liver from my last cow buy, I requested all organ parts they offered that were available. The liver was soo delicious; I was thinking about making it into a liver jerky and having a piece or two every day for a steady supply of organ meat to supplement my other meat choices.

Aside/observation: I had a couple friends in town visiting for the last week and due to this was eating grocery store meats(burgers/sausages) ... currently been burning through all the leftovers since I don't want to waste meat. But I can definitely feel the difference and cant wait to get back into grass fed. I aim to eat a week straight worth next week to remedy this. Maybe a night or 2 of mussels of to get some seafood in the mix.

CT: I have been doing cold showers for 2 months now. I do them 6 days/week, allowing myself a warm shower over the weekend (but I notice I don't keep the water nearly close to as warm as I used to... so its more like luke warmish). I didn't start with face dunks or ice vests, I just went straight to the showers. When I first started, I found it easiest to just push through it and count to myself in my head for a distraction. At first I would count to 90+ before feeling accustomed to the water, but after a week or two of consistently decreasing counting times, it become much easier. As it stands, it only takes me a couple of seconds to adapt and then I am fine.

Also, in an effort to feel the effects a little longer, after shutting off the shower I dont dry myself off right away but allow my body to bask in the remaining cold dripping water for a 60-second count. If I'm off from work and have extra time, I will throw some gym shorts on right away and get outside in the sunlight to let the cold water naturally evaporate; that feels great given the chance!

If you are already LS, which I imagine you are considering what you've written, I don't think you'll need to baby step so much into the CT. Commit to a week's worth of cold showers and go from there. I may start doing the baths once in a while, but time constraints make it more difficult to fit into life.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-16T14:55:54+0200

I think it still goes back to heteroplasmy. Assuming that either had no Neolithic disease diagnosis_,_ the façade is as expected for their chronological age _...._Someone of 25 with the heteroplasmy rate of a typical 50 year old versus someone of 50 with the heteroplasmy rates typically expected in someone much younger/healthier .... ### Dennis Durost — 2017-05-18T03:22:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-214233>

Don't know if I'm spitting in the wind or not but am trying to spot treat midsection with a waist trimming belt stuffed with ice packs. Daily for one hour. A couple of people on youtube tried it and were pleased with the results. One guy lost an inch and a half on his waist after 30 days with an additional two inches coming off over a two month period after the icing was stopped. Worth a shot.

### brent-next — 2017-05-18T09:38:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-214242>

> [Dennis Durost said:](/goto/post?id=214233)
>
> Don't know if I'm spitting in the wind or not but am trying to spot treat midsection with a waist trimming belt stuffed with ice packs. Daily for one hour. A couple of people on youtube tried it and were pleased with the results.

What you are trying to do is similar to the FDA approved fat reduction method called cool sculpting: [http://www.coolsculpting.com/](http://www.coolsculpting.com/)

The problem with trying to do this at home is that it's easy to give yourself frostbite and that won't make you look better at all. If you can afford CoolSculpting and there is one near you, at least go for the free consult. You will at least learn a lot that might make it safer at home.

The danger of doing this at home varies from wildly high to modest. Be careful. Read the research papers on Cool Sculpting.

You may also want to do overall CT for the overall benefits too.

### JanSz — 2017-05-18T14:54:19+0300

One guy lost an inch and a half on his waist after 30 days with an additional two inches coming off over a two month period after the icing was stopped. Worth a shot.

There are two reasons why midsecion is large.

1----The fat that lies just below your skin in most of your body -- the kind you can grab with your hands -- is called subcutaneous fat.
2----In your belly, it’s called visceral fat because it builds up in the spaces between and around your viscera -- internal organs like your stomach and intestines.
\----------------

Increasing metabolism eventually should help with both. That correlates with easy to measure ----> temperature.
If temperature is low (even just by 1/2 degree) it is going to be tough to loose that fat.

There are many ways to talk about it, but that just makes for long discussions.

Under tongue, should be 37C=98.6F
but if you have:
36.67C=98F
it is going to be tough to loose that fat

Accurate digital thermometer helps.

///////////////

### Allin — 2017-05-22T23:27:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-214444>

> [Dennis Durost said:](/goto/post?id=214233)
>
> Don't know if I'm spitting in the wind or not but am trying to spot treat midsection with a waist trimming belt stuffed with ice packs. Daily for one hour. A couple of people on youtube tried it and were pleased with the results. One guy lost an inch and a half on his waist after 30 days with an additional two inches coming off over a two month period after the icing was stopped. Worth a shot.

_I placed a large zip lock bag filled with ice cubes inside another large zip lock bag on my belly for 15 minutes to start. Over time, I increased that time to one hour. I wore a compression shirt at first until I reached 60 minutes and then removed the compression shirt. I would drink 16 oz of clean water before the session. I also ate a can of sardines three times a week for the DHA and worked in 30-60 minutes of sun on my belly most everyday. Now the cost is just about zero...._

16 oz =0.47 liter

If you would limit your daily water intake below certain value
that would force mitochondria to make up the required difference.
Body fat would be used to make that water.

to make one kilo=liter of water 1.1 kg of fat would be used.
Rather efficient way of removing unnecessary body fat.

Dr Boros drinks 0.2 - 0.25 liter of water daily.
For his clients hi suggests 0.75L/day

.

### tweety — 2013-04-22T18:52:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-84628>

I only suspect lime. I definitely had a tick bite at age 8, and last year I did the western blot. Two bands came back positive, one that is usually a good indicator of a positive lyme, but the conventional docs want 5 bands positive to diagnose. My symptoms I attribute to lyme would be like the above poster- inability to improve adrenal fatigue, opposite response to BHRT, and other random things. My blood sugar is always low, and despite being paleo and seasonal for two and a half years I still become fatigued and cranky when hungry. I still experience migraines from over-exertion (even when I do not feel I am over-exerting myself), and little things like this.

Last year a chiro muscle tested me for takesumi supreme, a Japanese bamboo charcoal. I took it sporadically, then forgot about it. A few weeks ago I remembered I had it in the cupboard and looked it up on the internet to see what it is used for. Lo and behold, it is used for people struggling to detoxify their systems of EMF, chronic conditions (like lyme), heavy metals, etc. I have been taking it twice a day now and am on my second week and feel it is putting me back on track. My random swelling is going down, my digestion is improved, and I feel more like myself! I am hopeful it is helping my system clear out some of the things weighing me down so I can really get down to healing.

### cantweight — 2013-04-23T22:05:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-84699>

Lyme treatment is more of an exorcism than a healing process. I was finally diagnosed with Lyme, babesia, and bartonella in 2009 after years of being sick.

My symptoms were fevers over 104 almost daily, horrible night sweats, severe fatigue, pain in most of body so severe I took vicodin daily just to take the edge off...never got relief. The cognitive decline was so bad I was unable to read, spell my name, often stopped speaking in the middle of sentences unaware I was in a conversation, got lost driving in my own neighborhood, crawled to bed at night the pain was so horrific. I was on my way to needing a wheelchair at 33 yrs old and feared not knowing my family as I thought I had alzheimers. Some times I'd be sitting in a room with them and become very upset and frightened because I didn't know who I was with or where I was....it was insane!

I have healed about 80% of my issues. I am in school to become a nurse practitioner so definitely all my cognitive issues have resolved. No more fibromyalgia, no more fevers, sweats, pain, migraines. No problems speaking or walking. I feel quite good aside from being fat and tired.

I do have hormone and thyroid problems. My adrenals are awesome now...after being completely tanked.

Based on what yall have posted as symptoms IMHO you dont have Lyme symptoms that I would treat. I would certainly pay attention to your body if it showed signs, but the treatment is hard on the liver and i wouldn't subject the body to it unless it was necessary.

Activated charcoal is a great binder...make sure you take it away from any other supplement/med as it will take it with it. If you use something like charcoal you must be sure your bowels are moving fantastically.

As for adrenal fatigue i can not recommend the supplement I have used for years highly enough Researched Nutritionals EnergyMultiPlex

[https://www.researchednutritionals.com/store/item.cfm?code=CRN105&cat=2](https://www.researchednutritionals.com/store/item.cfm?code=CRN105&cat=2)

Love that stuff! My adrenals were in horrible shape. Functioning so well now. Cortisol levels good, stress reactions minimal.

If your adrenals are in bad shape it is worth mentioning that adrenal fatigue and Lyme symptoms do overlap a lot.

To really rule out or address the Lyme...if you believe it to be part of your problem I would personally run an Igenex test for lyme and co infections. Labcorp and Quest are very hit or miss. MDL is a lab my family has had good results with also.

Feel free to message me if I can help at all. I have spent 4 years successfully battling this beast. Lyme is endemic in my area and I am constantly helping people I don't even know that get referred to me to make their way through the mess of dealing with Lyme. I cringe every time I hear of someone suffering with lyme because it is beatable with the right ammunition. Just so hard to find a doc that knows how to arm the body.

This is super helpful. Thats kinda what got me thinking. I watched "under my skin" and granted - those are extreme cases -but I didnt have any of those symptons

Before I realized I was gluten intolerant, I did have some neurological/muscular troubles - but that stopped when I cut out gluten (which is why I NEVER cheat in that area)...the adrenal, thyroid probs have been persistent. Despite what my labs say - normal by even Stop the thyroid madness levels - I still do not look or feel normal. I have serveral doc appts lined up over the next few wees, mainly I want a second opinion to see if there is anything else I can tweak.

I am thankful to not be experiecing pain like you mentioned and count my lucky stars every day that of all the ailments I've experienced joint pain has never been an issue.

Becasue I've been resistent to medications - it took me a LONG time to tolerate my thyroid meds and I had to go extremely slow either ramping up and now ramping down from HC - lyme of coinfections or biotoxins have come up as a possible suggestions often.

The NP I am seeing this weekend runs the Igenx lab...I will talk to her more in depth about this and see if she thinks its worth me running the test (as I will be paying for this out of pocket and and dont believe insurance will cover anything). I have an appt with a Lyme Literate Dr but its not until Oct which is why they directed me to her while I am waiting. She only costs $60 as opposed to the $360 I'll be paying for my consult in Oct.

That supp you listed looks AMAZING. It basically has almost everything I take seperately and looks like something Dr. Kruse would put together himself, ha! I am wondering if it would be better for me to just use that and will ask the NP if she can get it for me....thanks for showing that to me.

I just recently ordered Ashwaganda bc its supposed to help support the HPA - we'll see..

i am happy you are doing so much better! That must have been an absolute nightmare. thanks for sharing your story and good luck on your new career. Going into nursing is one of my goals once I leave Chicago. I guess once you have health issues, medical stuff becomes your life so it only makes sense that you'd want to go into it...

>
> To really rule out or address the Lyme...if you believe it to be part of your problem I would personally run an Igenex test for lyme and co infections. Labcorp and Quest are very hit or miss. MDL is a lab my family has had good results with also.
>
> Feel free to message me if I can help at all. I have spent 4 years successfully battling this beast. Lyme is endemic in my area and I am constantly helping people I don't even know that get referred to me to make their way through the mess of dealing with Lyme. I cringe every time I hear of someone suffering with lyme because it is beatable with the right ammunition. Just so hard to find a doc that knows how to arm the body.

Christi, I'd love to hear about your lyme MD.....

### nuttmegs17 — 2014-03-17T01:22:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-115872>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=84699)
>
> Lyme treatment is more of an exorcism than a healing process. I was finally diagnosed with Lyme, babesia, and bartonella in 2009 after years of being sick.
>
> My symptoms were fevers over 104 almost daily, horrible night sweats, severe fatigue, pain in most of body so severe I took vicodin daily just to take the edge off...never got relief. The cognitive decline was so bad I was unable to read, spell my name, often stopped speaking in the middle of sentences unaware I was in a conversation, got lost driving in my own neighborhood, crawled to bed at night the pain was so horrific. I was on my way to needing a wheelchair at 33 yrs old and feared not knowing my family as I thought I had alzheimers. Some times I'd be sitting in a room with them and become very upset and frightened because I didn't know who I was with or where I was....it was insane!
>
> I have healed about 80% of my issues. I am in school to become a nurse practitioner so definitely all my cognitive issues have resolved. No more fibromyalgia, no more fevers, sweats, pain, migraines. No problems speaking or walking. I feel quite good aside from being fat and tired.
>
> I do have hormone and thyroid problems. My adrenals are awesome now...after being completely tanked.
>
> Based on what yall have posted as symptoms IMHO you dont have Lyme symptoms that I would treat. I would certainly pay attention to your body if it showed signs, but the treatment is hard on the liver and i wouldn't subject the body to it unless it was necessary.
>
> Activated charcoal is a great binder...make sure you take it away from any other supplement/med as it will take it with it. If you use something like charcoal you must be sure your bowels are moving fantastically.
>
> As for adrenal fatigue i can not recommend the supplement I have used for years highly enough Researched Nutritionals EnergyMultiPlex
>
> [https://www.researchednutritionals.com/store/item.cfm?code=CRN105&cat=2](https://www.researchednutritionals.com/store/item.cfm?code=CRN105&cat=2)
>
> Love that stuff! My adrenals were in horrible shape. Functioning so well now. Cortisol levels good, stress reactions minimal.
>
> If your adrenals are in bad shape it is worth mentioning that adrenal fatigue and Lyme symptoms do overlap a lot.
>
> To really rule out or address the Lyme...if you believe it to be part of your problem I would personally run an Igenex test for lyme and co infections. Labcorp and Quest are very hit or miss. MDL is a lab my family has had good results with also.
>
> Feel free to message me if I can help at all. I have spent 4 years successfully battling this beast. Lyme is endemic in my area and I am constantly helping people I don't even know that get referred to me to make their way through the mess of dealing with Lyme. I cringe every time I hear of someone suffering with lyme because it is beatable with the right ammunition. Just so hard to find a doc that knows how to arm the body.

Hello! I finally got into see a lyme doc (took a year to get in!). Took igenex and some tests for coinfections and I apparently have lyme, mycoplasma, anaplasma , and she suspects Bartella. I was prescribed rifampin and azuthromycin (to start. We will apparently cycle in other antibiotics and some natural support). she thinks it will take anywhere from 3 months to 24 months or treat. I'm a bit scared but you saying you beat it gives me hope. Can you tell me how you were treated and perhaps things to look out for? I just started the meds today and so far I am just a little tired. I know I will have to support detox and avoid alcohol. Any advice would be greatly appreciated

### cantweight — 2014-03-17T01:46:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-115876>

Im glad you are finally getting some answers. Its a bit of a battle to get all that crap out but so worth it. Be sure to eat strict epi paleo and be good to your gut as the abx are tough. Probiotics are helpful, and liver support like milk thistle. Definitely steer clear of alcohol while your liver is busy dealing with this mess. I also like sun chlorella, welchol, and/or activated charcoal as a binder.

**Lots of water and all the sunlight and grounding you can get.** I cant stress that enough. Dont take it lightly.

Its a bit of a crooked path lyme and co force us to take....on one hand we have to eradicate these bugs by some of the worst means possible and on the other we have to support the well being of our body and brain to the best of our ability. The stronger you can make your body (think redox potential) the swifter your recovery will be and the stronger you will stay in the wake of these meds.

CT if you can but at the very least get your self outdoors. Fresh air, feet in the grass, sun on as much skin as you can bare. Eat clean, avoid NNEMF and blue light, sleep well.

Feel free to PM me and I wish you the very best

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-17T20:22:32+0200
