### cantweight — 2014-03-17T21:45:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-116015>

> [nuttmegs17 said:](/goto/post?id=115974)
>
> Thank you!! I need to work on grounding (Chicago winters drive me indoors) but everything else is on point. What was your testament and how long did it last? Is it normal for me to bee this tired after rifampin? Or is that just herx? Had to call into work this morning due to the drowsiness ..

It took me about 18 months before I felt like I had kicked some serious ass. But there was marked improvement after about 6 months. Remember I was coming from a really bad place...you will probably see a much more swift recovery
I did not know then what I know now and could have really shortened that time for myself by addressing field, and adding in a bunch of DrK's protocols.

I have a practically bulletproof immune system now.....takes a perfect storm of crap for me to get sick now where as before I was at the point I had to avoid public places from Sept-April or I would catch something constantly. Same with my kiddies from fall -spring a non stop rollercoaster of ear infections, sinus infections, strep throat, and colds. This year we made in until March and one finally went down with a sinus infection but he is having other health issues that have put him in a weakened state. So there is a great level of health to be regained and you will be there before you know it

I found the whole treatment process exhausting to my body and tried to get as much rest and sleep as possible. Just be good to your body and give it all the support you can. Try to sweat and detoxify...lymphatic massage would be awesome as well as sauna. Hydrate like mad. It may get worse before it gets better, but it is so worth it!

### cantweight — 2014-03-18T00:49:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-116056>

> [nuttmegs17 said:](/goto/post?id=116044)
>
> Thank you! I hope you are right about the swift recovery part. Any excuse for a massage sounds good to me . Did you experience herx.? Where you on antibiotics? So far, does my treatment sound about right?

Yeah I herxed...bad. I had been infected for about 17 years. I did not have a good doc when I first started treatment. Was on doxy, zith, and mepron. High doses. The support for the rest of my body was lacking and I paid dearly. My second doc went in baby steps and ran every possible lab under the sun to be sure we were addressing everything and not hyper focused on lyme....what a difference that made! She did use abx but more carefully. And did not let me herx too hard. Impossible to say what treatment is right or wrong when so many respond so differently. If I had to go back and do it again I would still use prescription drugs including abx , I would have grounded, tanned/sunlight, hydrated with quality water, and addressed my field. That would have made a huge difference I believe

If at any point you feel too bad call your doc and try backing down meds or upping support. The body does not need more stress...be good to it and it will be good to you.

how can you tell the difference between herx and maybe being on a bad antibiotic?

i started rifapim and azithromycin on Sunday and since then have felt hot off and on and with a sore throat. I know for a fact that the rifapim was making me incredibly tired, so I scaled back from 2x a day to 1 a day (with the thought of increasing next week). Of course, I could have just caught a bug as well so who knows. i left a voicemail with the Dr. but can't tell if this is a bad reaction and maybe I should give it another few days or if this is a sign that I need a different med (s).

thoughts? thanks!

I honestly dont know how to tell the difference...any prescription you take should have a list of side effects to watch for such as rash or whatever. Be mindful of those things.
When I was killing off those bacteria it felt like there was a war going on in my body and often left me in fetal position with a heating pad.
Just keep well hydrated with ice cold water, rest, and support your body as best you can. The body will naturally raise its temperature to fight off infection. Maybe you can hit a sauna and get a good sweat on? Then follow with a cool epsom salt bath to detox. Be sure to rinse after....that always seems like silly advice but I didnt know to do that for the longest time

### KristinP — 2017-04-04T17:51:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-211731>

We discovered the cause of my son's chronic health issues for the past 11 years is borrelia. He is 17 years old now and has been too sick the last two years to do much high school. He is in a state of auto-immunity. In an almost constant healing crisis. Dr. Kruse, I read your article on Lyme and part of the suggested treatment being 3 weeks of antibiotics, however we've discovered that my son has a systemic, putrefying yeast sitting on his heart valves that is causing damage. The yeast I was told feeds off of proteins, not sugar. He likely got it in utero. Antibiotics I'm afraid would make the yeast worst. He is on a paleo diet however we were told that he should "cheat" two days a week to not overwhelm his system with protein. The ND we are working with used a thermograph scan to diagnose these things. The igenex lab testing came back negative however on dr. Horowitz questionnaire he scores a 90. We are confident with his history of tick bites that he has the bacteria for Lyme disease. However the yeast situation complicates things. He is extremely thin and the main symptom since he was six-years-old has been nausea, when he eats anything at all. We've tried introducing light therapy but almost everything even in small amounts seems to exacerbate his symptoms. Any advice on getting him to a stable place where we can effectively treat the bacteria and yeast?

# Thread: Anxiety Gone Because of Leptin Rx
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-gone-because-of-leptin-rx.6836/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Christopher — 2013-04-21T19:29:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-gone-because-of-leptin-rx.6836/post-84579>

I've struggled with anxiety and panic attacks for most of my life. In my late twenties, I was diagnosed with Asperger's Syndrome (Autism) I've been overweight most of my life as well. I've tried numerous times to get healthier by making changes to my diet. Nothing ever worked very well or had lasting results. In fact, the last thing I tried was the ABs diet. That diet had me eating every 2-3 hours with lots of fruit, whole-grain, nuts, and lean meat. I was addicted to the food. When I would eat, I would feel really good for about an hour or two. But, then I would crash. I mean like flipping a light switch off. I would go from a great mood to feeling like the world was ending, and the only cure seemed to be to eat more carbs. I would also self-medicate often with alcohol, marijuana, and occasionally narcotic pain killers or muscle relaxers. Only problem was the marijuana buzz was often ruined by panic attacks, and alcohol was quickly loosing any perceived benefit as well ( in retrospect, I think it was the water in the micro-brew beers that had any positive effect on my mood until the alcohol dehydrated me anyway). Thankfully I knew better than to lean on the rx drugs. I was miserable.

My two children are also on the autism spectrum, and my wife was doing a lot of research on various treatments for autism. I remember reading a couple articles about autism and gluten. Around the same time, I saw one of my FB friends post about being on a paleo diet. I started reading about paleo, and was convinced it was the solution. So about 9 months ago, I jumped right in and cut grains cold turkey. I had a horrible time. My stomach hurt, my chest hurt, I felt like I was dying. I didn't really know what or how to eat, but I was conviced that I should stay paleo. I was desperate enough before trying this, that the thought that the paleo diet might kill me was ok. I knew I could not go back to my previous lifestyle.

After two weeks of thinking I was going to die from paleo, I came across some forum posts about Jack Kruse. Most of the posters, indicated that Jack was crazy, but I was desperate enough to give crazy a try. I came to this website, and decided to give the Leptin Rx a chance. And I'll tell you, that very first day the BAB saved my life. The day before I was having a hard time eating, because of the stomach and chest pain and light-headedness. But that BAB wend down so easy and I immediately felt great. It was amazing. I felt so full of energy, and there was no longer any pain in my stomach or chest. I was also surprised that going from six meals to three was easy, a relief actually. Also, the carb cravings stopped instantly. I used to have major carb cravings in the evening. Sometimes I would lay down for bed, and feel a panic attack coming, and have to get up and eat some cereal to calm down. But now at night, I felt perfectly calm. No carbs, no problem. I would also say before I was a little afraid of the dark, and used to keep a lot of night lights on. But after the Leptin Rx, I preferred the darkness. In fact, I found the dark to be comforting, almost like the feeling of a soft blanket around me.

I should also add that from the beginning of my experiment with paleo, I lost a total of 50 lbs in a fairly short time. That was not my main goal, but no complaints. I'm skinny now. I have no physical symptoms of anxiety at all. I have no food cravings (except sometimes I crave the coconut mana, lol). I don't feel hungry, ever. And most importantly, my mood is stable, no more feeling like the world is ending out of nowhere. I am not miserable any longer! I don't even think twice about using any kind of drugs, and I'd much rather drink spring water than beer.

I do have better energy. I have more mental clarity. I feel now like how I imagined "normal" people feel. I am excited every morning when I wake up. I always look forward to breakfast. I don't feel hungry or like I have to eat it, I just plain enjoy eating it. Sometimes I impress myself with how much meat and fat I can eat for breakfast. If I eat too much, I do wake up drenched in sweat, though. lol.

The straight truths laid out here on this website have been such a blessing. I feel like I've been given a second chance at life. I also look forward to the future, as I know that the more I follow Jack's protocols, the better things will get.

I wanted to share this hear so that others who are skeptical may be encouraged to go ahead and give the Leptin Rx a try. In my case, the Leptin Rx with a keto-genic epi-paleo diet was the perfect cure for my anxiety, depression, and symptoms of reactive hypoglycemia.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-21T21:56:24+0300

# Thread: My Smartphone Gave Me Hemorrhoids!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Christopher — 2013-04-22T03:18:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/post-84592>

To be fair, I must admit that it is not uncommon for me to have a hemorrhoid problem in the Winter time. I face a lot of stress at work around Christmas (retail), and I now realize was is the time of year that I was farthest of from circadian timing. Now the difference is that normally when my hemorrhoids would get inflamed, it would be for only about 7-10 days, then it would go away. Well this year my hemorrhoids were inflamed for probably around 6 weeks straight, with varying intensity. This was like nothing I had ever experienced before. I was especially shocked because I was eating Epi-paleo and doing the Leptin Rx and avoiding artificial light after sunset as much as possible (I often had to work til 9pm but would wear yellow glasses). I figured what I was doing should be lowering inflammation and should prevent a hemorrhoid problem.

I was very frustrated with this problem, and was beginning to think my diet was to blame. Ever since I started eating paleo (July last year) I had been often constipated with occasional bouts of diarrhea. I started squatting while having a bm thinking that would help, but to no avail. I was thinking of giving up, but then miraculously the hemorrhoids went away. I didn't know why at the time, but man, it was a relief.

Now here's what I think happened. I know correlation is not the same as causation, but the coincidence was too obvious for me to ignore after reading the EMF blogs.

This past November, black Friday to be exact, I became a first time owner of an Android smartphone. Previous to this I had a Nextel flip phone that was kind of embarrassing to use, so I didn't use it much and didn't keep it in my pocket much either. But, I was very proud of this new Android phone and kept it in my pocket all the time. I even re-arranged my pockets so I could comfortably keep it in my pocket while working on cars. You know, just in case I needed the flashlight app or something.

At first, I would keep the wi-fi on so I could get FB, email, and weather updates often. But I started to become frustrated with the battery life. I mean this was a new phone, and it needed charging more than my old Nextel, lol. So I started turning off things to save battery. I kept turning off the wi-fi, but every time I looked at the phone, the stupid wi-fi was back on. I messed around with battery saver apps for a while, but the wi-fi was always turning itself on. After several weeks, I figured out how to keep the wifi off, I also figured out how to turn off the 3G (mobile data). So as an experiment I turned both off to see how long the battery would go. And since I wasn't using any internet apps on the phone, I din't really see a need to keep it in my pocket anymore. The result of that experiment was that the battery only needed to be charged once a week instead of the at least once a day it previously required. I've always been a sucker for efficiency so I never turned the wi-fi or mobile data back on.

This experiment correlated exactly with the time that my hemorrhoids cleared up. It was also around that time that I was reading the EMF blogs. I remember it was in the shower one morning that I put it all together. You should have seen me after that shower, running around turning off wi-fi, putting phones on airplane mode, etc. lol. But since then, I thankfully have not had any hemorrhoid issues. I have had some constipation and straining since then that I thought would for sure cause the old roids to flair up, but to this day it hasn't happened.

I got rid of that phone shortly after all this and now went to a pay-as-you-go flip phone that I will only turn on in an emergency. I disabled all wi-fi devices in my home, and got rid of my cordless phones and went to wired. Also somewhere in the middle of this whole ordeal, I got rid of my flat screen television and sound bar that had a wireless subwoofer and had switched my computer to a wired connection for unrelated reasons.

Was it all a coincidence? I will never put a wireless device in my pocket again, so the world may never know. I would rather error on the side of caution in this case.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-22T16:20:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/post-84619>

> [IvanDrago said:](/goto/post?id=84609)
>
> I normally would never comment to share an embarrassing anecdote, but reading this thread has had me connecting some dots that relate to my own experience. I moved back to the city in early January and have been working sporadically and taking courses to upgrade my GPA. This was around the time the EMF series began and I have been doing everything within my power to manage my exposure.
>
> Exactly three weeks ago, I had a research paper due on the last day of the winter semester. Unfortunately, there were no computers available on the main floor so I was forced to head upstairs and finish the paper on the third floor. The computers at my urban University are all Wi-Fi enabled and when I checked, there were approximately fifty(!) wireless networks available. In addition to this, the crowded environment is undoubtedly flooded with wireless signals from various cell phones and other electronic devices. This was the first time I had ventured upstairs for any extended period for any reason in the past four months. I spent the entire day in front of the computer finishing my paper and the following day I woke up with a hemmorhoid! I have had this problem before at a much younger age, but this was both unexpected and bizarre. I only made this connection having read the opening post. So, thank you Christopher for elucidating the root causal factor at play here!
>
> Fortunately, the issue resolved itself within one week after treating it topically with liquid iodine. That being said, I also recall being thirstier than usual on that day and developing a mild headache. In addition, I recall having to urinate excessively that night. If anybody in the lay public were to read this reply, I have no doubt in my mind that I would be painted as a Larry David-esque hypochondriac of the highest order, but the sudden and unexpected recurrence of a painful condition actually did occur (it was a nuisance more than anything). One particular facet of my behavior preceding the emergence of this condition was radically different than it had been during the previous four months. Care to guess what that may have been? My city has been systematically flooded with radiation during the past calendar year, and I have the news reports to back this up. The advent of FibreOp has not been criticized, but celebrated unabashedly.
>
> Furthermore, I have had to go upstairs in buildings transiently during the past four months to deliver items for my job and write an exam. Having spent the past five years at the same institution, I am familiar with virtually all of the individuals who work in these office environments on a personal level. What I have noticed within the past year is nothing short of amazing. In one office, all six of the employees have appeared to gain significant amounts of weight and have developed apparent skin issues (i.e., dry skin). I have also noticed a change in organization. **What was previously a tidy workplace environment has become more disorganized, cluttered, and messy.** On the desks of the employees are more sugary snack foods (e.g., Haloween candy) than I have noticed in the past four years. In addition to the snacks, there are multivitamins, significantly more bottled water (this is _very_ interesting), and anti-inflammatory drugs such as Tylenol and Advil. From my limited perspective, there is now pronounced tension between employees and nobody seems as friendly as they have in the past.

Great testimony, and excellent observation about working environments..

So I have a question - I'm not a \*Neat\* person - I actually am quite good at ignoring clutter/messes. But I have noticed more of a desire to keep things tidy - also my urge to purge (which usually only hits one or 2x/year) has been constant since I started focusing on limiting EMF... This weekend I cleaned and painted one wall in the kitchen and the hallway upstairs... I also continued to work on my garden in the front of the house... I'm not sure if what's going on is the result of mitigating EMF, entering the PPP and staying there... or a combination of the both... I also read 4 books this weekend...

Suddenly I'm able to do things that I never would have had the time for... is this because I'm now aware of what I'm capable of... or is it I'm now aware of what's been limiting my possibilities??? regardless... I was thrilled to realize that I only got online once yesterday morning.... there's freedom from not getting on the computer....

### Christopher — 2013-04-22T18:32:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/post-84627>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=84620)
>
> Thank you, both Christopher and Ivan, for this information. Sure wish DH could get rid of his work Android phone. Christopher, how did you finally get the wireless and 3G part turned off?

I had to go somewhere in the settings menu and un-check the box for wireless network optimizer. That stopped the wifi from turning itself on. Then I unchecked the box for enable mobile data to disable the 3G.

I also noticed when I was using the phone as a GPS in the car (3G had to be on for GPS to work), I would become fatigued and irritable after just a short time of driving. I remember one day getting home feeling just wiped out after hours of driving with the GPS and 3G on. I was experimenting with earthing at the time and tried to earth with a wire plugged into the electrical outlet ground. That did nothing to improve my energy. I then took a 20 minute nap, also no improvement. Then I drank a couple glasses of water. Within 10 minutes of drinking the water, I felt much better. It wasn't even good water, just plain old tap water (flouridated as far as I know). Needless to say, I drink 1 1/2 to 2 gallons of spring water daily now. Since then, I noticed that I don't feel fatigued after driving, with the phone off or in airplane mode.

### IceBarbie — 2013-04-22T20:58:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/post-84633>

Or you have an adrenal issue. You mentioned your hemmorhoids show up when you're under stress. Here is a snippet from a wonderful article about the Adrenal Glands that mentions the connection between adrenals and hemmorhoids:

"Hemmorhoids are another problem which is associated with blood pooling in the abdomen. A hemorrhoid is a vein which has pushed its way (or been pushed) to bulge outside the anal sphincter. The anal sphincter then becomes very tight, and the hemorrhoid, with the pressure of the abdominal and pelvic blood above it, and gravity pulling from below it, cannot get back inside the rectum. Treatment of hemorrhoids must be directed at two areas. First, the hemorrhoids must be treated locally, and second, the source of intra-abdominal and intra-pelvic pooling of blood must be corrected.

Most commonly, the cause of the blood pooling in the abdomen and pelvis is from hypoadrenia. But severe liver congestion can also cause portal hypertension and result in hemorrhoids. You must differentiate between liver and adrenal problems as the underlying cause of the hemorrhoids. For this reason, whenever a person complains of hemorrhoids, one of the first things to be done is listen to the heart. The person does not understand this, but you are listening for the relative loudness of the second sounds at the pulmonic valve area (for the adrenals) and the tricuspid valve area (for the liver).

In treating hemorrhoids locally, it is necessary to recognize that the hemorrhoidal veins can not get themselves back inside the rectum, due to the tightness of the anal sphincter. In order to treat the hemorrhoids successfully, the anal sphincter must be dilated. This is done by putting on a glove (not a finger cot) and, using a lubricant, inserting your index finger into the rectum. Stretch the anal sphincter with the index finger. Then insert the first two fingers and stretch the sphincter. Then insert three fingers and insert them slowly about as fat as they will reach. Finally (if the person is still on the table) insert all four fingers, stretching the sphincter up to the point which the size of the patient will tolerate.

This is a difficult procedure for the patient, but there will be an immediate and dramatic reduction in the extent of the hemorrhoids protruding through the anus. If done correctly, this procedure may only have to be performed once. Then again, you may only get one chance! Occasionally it is necessary to repeat the sphincter dilation once or twice in the future."

[http://www.tuberose.com/Adrenal\_Glands.html](http://www.tuberose.com/Adrenal_Glands.html)

I'm just sayin' that people have had hemmorhoids a lot longer than we've had Smart Phones.

How do you handle stress? Do you use stress or does stress use you? It is a natural condition of existence for us. The dose and our reaction to it, however, is where people either adapt well to it or fall apart. You are not your circumstances or your perceptions. Your attitude determines how you manage this randomness in life. Stress is when demands exceed resources. When this balance is off long term in your life you pay a biologic toll. Today begin to realize, if there's too much of it and it's not properly managed, it becomes DISTRESS. Stress is an adaptive outcome. Stress is a compilation of choices. Begin to learn to make decision that choose peace in your life today.

### BobBrooks — 2013-06-22T12:16:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/post-88651>

> [Christopher said:](/goto/post?id=84592)
>
> To be fair, I must admit that it is not uncommon for me to have a hemorrhoid problem in the Winter time. I face a lot of stress at work around Christmas (retail), and I now realize was is the time of year that I was farthest of from circadian timing. Now the difference is that normally when my hemorrhoids would get inflamed, it would be for only about 7-10 days, then it would go away. Well this year my hemorrhoids were inflamed for probably around 6 weeks straight, with varying intensity. This was like nothing I had ever experienced before. I was especially shocked because I was eating Epi-paleo and doing the Leptin Rx and avoiding artificial light after sunset as much as possible (I often had to work til 9pm but would wear yellow glasses). I figured what I was doing should be lowering inflammation and should prevent a hemorrhoid problem.
>
> I was very frustrated with this problem, and was beginning to think my diet was to blame. Ever since I started eating paleo (July last year) I had been often constipated with occasional bouts of diarrhea. I started squatting while having a bm thinking that would help, but to no avail. I was thinking of giving up, but then miraculously the hemorrhoids went away. I didn't know why at the time, but man, it was a relief.
>
> Now here's what I think happened. I know correlation is not the same as causation, but the coincidence was too obvious for me to ignore after reading the EMF blogs.
>
> This past November, black Friday to be exact, I became a first time owner of an Android smartphone. Previous to this I had a Nextel flip phone that was kind of embarrassing to use, so I didn't use it much and didn't keep it in my pocket much either. But, I was very proud of this new Android phone and kept it in my pocket all the time. I even re-arranged my pockets so I could comfortably keep it in my pocket while working on cars. You know, just in case I needed the [flashlights](http://www.robustbuy.com/led-lighting-gadgets-led-flashlights-c-505_1027_730.html) app or something.
>
> At first, I would keep the wi-fi on so I could get FB, email, and weather updates often. But I started to become frustrated with the battery life. I mean this was a new phone, and it needed charging more than my old Nextel, lol. So I started turning off things to save battery. I kept turning off the wi-fi, but every time I looked at the phone, the stupid wi-fi was back on. I messed around with battery saver apps for a while, but the wi-fi was always turning itself on. After several weeks, I figured out how to keep the wifi off, I also figured out how to turn off the 3G (mobile data). So as an experiment I turned both off to see how long the battery would go. And since I wasn't using any internet apps on the phone, I din't really see a need to keep it in my pocket anymore. The result of that experiment was that the battery only needed to be charged once a week instead of the at least once a day it previously required. I've always been a sucker for efficiency so I never turned the wi-fi or mobile data back on.
>
> This experiment correlated exactly with the time that my hemorrhoids cleared up. It was also around that time that I was reading the EMF blogs. I remember it was in the shower one morning that I put it all together. You should have seen me after that shower, running around turning off wi-fi, putting phones on airplane mode, etc. lol. But since then, I thankfully have not had any hemorrhoid issues. I have had some constipation and straining since then that I thought would for sure cause the old roids to flair up, but to this day it hasn't happened.
>
> I got rid of that phone shortly after all this and now went to a pay-as-you-go flip phone that I will only turn on in an emergency. I disabled all wi-fi devices in my home, and got rid of my cordless phones and went to wired. Also somewhere in the middle of this whole ordeal, I got rid of my flat screen television and sound bar that had a wireless subwoofer and had switched my computer to a wired connection for unrelated reasons.
>
> Was it all a coincidence? I will never put a wireless device in my pocket again, so the world may never know. I would rather error on the side of caution in this case.

Sorry to hear this.. Yes it is very rare problem in winters and I do have similar minute problem in summers... Hope you get well soon

# Thread: The Effects of ELF-EMFs on Collagen
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effects-of-elf-emfs-on-collagen.6841/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HoneyChild — 2013-04-22T13:11:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effects-of-elf-emfs-on-collagen.6841/post-84608>

I came across this study about EMFs and collagen which I thought was pretty interesting

**Suppression of type I collagen in human scleral fibroblasts treated with extremely low-frequency electromagnetic fields**

**Purpose**: To investigate the expression differences of type I collagen (COL1A1) and its underlying mechanisms in human fetal scleral fibroblasts (HFSFs) that were treated with conditioned medium from retinal pigment epithelial (RPE) cells under extremely low-frequency electromagnetic fields (ELF-EMFs).

**Methods**: The ELF-EMFs used in this study were established by slidac and artificial coils. Growth of the treated HFSFs was evaluated by a cell-counting kit-8 assay. The expression of COL1A1 and matrix metalloproteinases-2 (MMP-2) in the treated HFSFs was detected by reverse transcription PCR (RT-PCR) and western blot, and the expression of transforming growth factor-β2 (TGF-β2) and basic fibroblast growth factor-2 (FGF-2) in RPE cells exposed to EMFs was detected by RT-PCR. The expression of COL1A1 and MMP-2 in HFSFs was further confirmed by immunofluorescence staining. Activation of extracellular signal-regulated kinase 1/2 (ERK1/2 also called p44/p42 mitogen-activated protein kinases \[MAPK\]) and p38 in HFSFs was measured by western blot.

**Results**: We found that exposure to ELF-EMFs resulted in a decreased proliferation rate of HFSFs and that addition of RPE supernatant medium could enhance this effect. Compared with that of the control cells, a significant decrease in collagen synthesis was detected in HFSFs under ELF-EMFs. However, the expression of MMP-2 was upregulated, which could be further enhanced via an RPE supernatant additive. The activities of ERK1/2 and p38 were significantly increased in HFSFs exposed to ELF-EMFs, and this effect could be enhanced by RPE supernatant medium additive.

**Conclusions**: Our results suggested that ELF-EMFs can inhibit the expression of type I collagen in HFSFs and contribute to the remodeling of the sclera.

Full paper can be found here: [http://www.molvis.org/molvis/v19/885/mv-v19-885.pdf](http://www.molvis.org/molvis/v19/885/mv-v19-885.pdf)

I had a huge (14 cm) peri ovarian cyst that grew throughout his pregnancy and hyper amniotic fluid. I measured 42 weeks at 38 weeks I thought my hips would break from the strain, but now I believe the extra fluid and the cyst were both mechanisms of protection to the fetus...anything to "pad" him from the invisible onslaught.

He was speech delayed, had tubes in his ears, suffered with lyme and babesia he most likely picked up inutero or via breastmilk. He has motor and vocal tics and a stutter. He is also the only one that is not thin. He's not even remotely obese but my older 2 are quite thin and Luke has a tendency to put on weight very easily. Crazy. Sad. I hate that I put them in that place for so long before realizing what was happening. I truly hope it is not too late to undo.

Hello,

I recently added maca to my morning supps, bc I read "the hormone cure" and it's suggested as a way to naturally balance hormones....however, I have been reading stories online and it appears that sometimes it actually results in...weight gain?!

I am curious about your experiences with it. Why would it make some gain weight and others lose it? Funnily enough, there are entire forums dedicated to "increasing boobs and butts" and many people on those forums swear that Maca works for that....I can tell you one thing, a bigger butt is the last thing I need!

My boobs have felt a little bigger this week. I am on day 18 of my cycle, but its highly irregular so I am unsure if its the maca (If i end up having a 36 day cycle this month, then its too early to be PMS), or if its just PMS (if I end up having say a 25 day cycle). So I figured Id throw it out there.

> nuttmegs- I love maca. I did not experience the response with maca that I did with progesterone. It really helps my libido... but I didn't feel like it was helping my progesterone based on other symptoms.

Thanks, Tweety! Yes, part of me wonders if I feel bigger bc perhaps its already helping my cycle? THerefore, maybe this is a shorter cycle and therefore not weightgain per se but just PMS? I will keep an eye on it. It seems like it has such promise so I hate to throw in the towel with it.

How much do you take?

### tweety — 2013-04-24T01:30:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-84710>

I have been journaling and there were several days I was fine and felt good. The last two I felt so huge, and can see a physical difference. Well, I was hungry while prepping lunch and popped a few hazelnuts and brazil nuts into my mouth. 24hrs later, bloated all over. So, note to self, you know nuts make you retain water- quit eating them!

I take the powdered form of maca, and after breakfast I take a spoonful of coconut cream, a tablespoon of maca, and add a little water until it's pudding consistency and eat. Remember to take a break from maca while you have your period. I noticed almost immediately a change in libido. No other changes thata I can attribute to maca specifically, but that was a big concern for me so I am happy!

### tweety — 2013-04-24T01:31:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-84711>

By the way, as I understand it, maca is an adaptogen. It doesn't just increase progesterone, but all the hormones. But it does so in a way your body needs it. I always assumed it was helping my estrogen and testosterone levels more than progesterone.

### Samson — 2013-04-29T05:48:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-85082>

Is Navitas gelatanized Maca a good brand for Maca or is there another preferred brand?
Gelatinized Maca is known to be highly digestible and more soluble than regular maca. Due to gelatinization, the long chains of starches are broken down, enhancing the concentration of the maca’s powerful main ingredients.

This is the product description of the Navitas gelatanized Maca:

Navitas Naturals Gelatinized Maca Powder is a concentrated form of the Maca root’s beneficial elements; the fiber of the root is extracted making this superfood more digestible and its beneficial elements intensified. Navitas Naturals Organic Maca Powder is cultivated in its indigenous region of the Junin Plateau amongst the Peruvian Andes. After harvest, the maca roots are gelatinized and milled: a process that concentrates the active ingredients through a heated extrusion to remove the starch. This product has only one ingredient, maca root, and is kosher, gluten-free, vegan, and 100% organic.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-02T23:58:48+0300

Navitas Naturals Organic Maca Powder is cultivated in its indigenous region of the Junin Plateau amongst the Peruvian Andes. After harvest, the maca roots are gelatinized and milled: a process that concentrates the active ingredients through a heated extrusion to remove the starch. This product has only one ingredient, maca root, and is kosher, gluten-free, vegan, and 100% organic.

I think its a good brand, its the one I bought and it was a decent price on amazon it tasted fine for what its worth. def stick to gelantanized

# Thread: Will tanning cause freckles/brown spots?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-tanning-cause-freckles-brown-spots.6864/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eve — 2013-04-24T19:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-tanning-cause-freckles-brown-spots.6864/post-84746>

I want to start tanning. I've read the Sunshine blog numerous times, so I know my O6:O3 and vitD levels (at least 60) needs to be in check before I start and to do it cautiously, but I'm scared of getting freckles etc. I am a redhead with fair skin (no freckles) and I don't want any freckles or dark brown spots. My undertones are pinkish and tanning is difficult for me. I don't think my legs have ever been able to tan, LOL. When I was younger, my arms got most of the sun and now I have dark brown spots scattered on them and don't want anymore (Of course, this was pre-epi-paleo, etc).

Plus, my mom has a fair skin and she aged her skin by tanning outside. Of course, she wasn't epi-paleo etc at the time, but it's a big enough scare factor for me. I am 26 and when I am 40, I don't want to be kicking myself!

How are other fair-skinned people coming along?

### Danco3636 — 2013-04-24T20:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-tanning-cause-freckles-brown-spots.6864/post-84748>

Interesting.... Would like to hear answer as well...

### nonchalant — 2013-04-25T01:24:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-tanning-cause-freckles-brown-spots.6864/post-84762>

I used to get freckles on my nose and cheeks when I was younger.
Not sure why, but I don't have any now. And I've got a pretty good tan already this year. I have had some freckles on my arms for years, however, and they are still there.

Has it been several months since you have eaten grain or soy-based fats? Proceed with caution. If it has been a year, you should be just fine.

Also, I had a couple "age spots" on my face, but those are almost completely gone. I am (or was) blonde, not redheaded. I used to burn within an hour of sun exposure, and not necessarily around noon. Now it is difficult for me to get a burn, and when I do (about once per year) I do not peel.

### Hope — 2013-04-25T01:42:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-tanning-cause-freckles-brown-spots.6864/post-84763>

This is exactly what is happening with me. I am red=haired, everything in me and on me is super sensitive. Only tanned three times so far for eight minutes each. Was in FL sun last week, a bit....have freckles on the tops of my shoulders. Too funny. Not worried about it at all. I don't mind them at all...but it's a personal thing. I love how the tanning makes me feel like someone gives me a shot of energy (free electrons)....I want to keep it up, in moderation. I know this doesn't help.....just my n-1

### Eve — 2013-07-22T20:38:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-tanning-cause-freckles-brown-spots.6864/post-90577>

Thanks for the replies.
I wanted to post an update...
In the Spring, I went for a couple of walks outside 20 min in the sun. My face turned pink/red for a few days (which I expected) but then it faded and for the first time in my life, I was left with light brown freckles under my eyes and under my eyebrows...funny.

Then I gathered the courage and started tanning in the tanning salon. First I did the stand-up booth....but all that left me was heat rash (not fun, lol) and burning in some parts of my body, no matter how I gauged the time. Then I switched to the lay down bed...better. After a few sessions, I can develop a tan. I can't do it everyday or else I burn...but every other day or so. The more I do it, the slowly I get more freckles. I have some on my upper cheeks now and a mole darkened on my back. But I developed a large freckle on my chin which I don't like. And I see some light freckling starting on my hands and upper chest, which I'm not a fan of either.

I'm at a crossroad now. I want to tan cause I like how it looks. But I don't like the freckling part...boo.

### nonchalant — 2013-07-22T22:47:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-tanning-cause-freckles-brown-spots.6864/post-90589>

Eve, have you banned "vegetable" oils totally from your life? I think any sort of rancid, oxidized fats can cause skin issues like freckles. Stay away from lotions too. It might take a year or two for your body to completely remove these low-quality fats from your skin and other tissues.

# Thread: Very frequent urination--Please Help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-frequent-urination-please-help.6867/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread

### bennyha — 2013-04-25T15:36:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-frequent-urination-please-help.6867/post-84776>

Hello all. I have a question I was hoping you could help me answer. Very brief background-I have tanked hormones, can't sweat, have chronic fatigue and horrible adrenal exhaustion at the age of 30.

I have always urinated a lot per day since I was a kid, including waking up during the night to go. But I tried upping my water intake to 64 oz for a month or so, and I was urinating up to 30 times a day, including waking up 4 times during the night to urinate (basically every hour and a half). Even since I have brought it down to 40 oz or so a day of water, I am still urinating around 20 times a day and still wake up 4 times each night to pee. To go with this, I am never, ever thirsty. I bet it has been over 5 years since I have truly felt thirsty.

Can anyone explain what might be causing this? I am sure it's all related, but I would like to know what might be causing this so that when I find a Dr. (am currently looking for a good doctor to help me get started in my path to recovery) I can add this to my list of symptoms and know what direction to head in. I also have PE issues, so I was wondering if this tied in with that as well possibly.

Thank you so much for any help or direction anyone can give

T4, T3, RT3, TSH Thyroid testing numbers are totally distorted by Bromide, Fluoride, & chlorine. Fluoride also harms the thyroid.

# Thread: Health Screening at Work Today
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/health-screening-at-work-today.6875/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-26T07:32:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/health-screening-at-work-today.6875/post-84858>

I was going to post my health screening results today that I got from the free yearly mobile bus screening, but I forgot and left the papers at work. The screener checked triglycerides, HDL, LDL, total cholesterol, cholesterol ratio, glucose, blood pressure, body fat ratio, and weight. Results were good. My numbers were all below the recommended numbers they want it below except body fat ratio and blood pressure. My BP registers high if I am nervous. If I recall correctly, my triglycerides were 99 though I had eaten a fatty breakfast with plenty of MCT/coconut oil/butter about 4 hours prior to the screening. HDL and LDL numbers were great though LDL was higher than HDL. I'm not worried about that since I suspect my body is packaging my dietary fat as LDL to use as fuel. She said my body mass ratio should be no higher than 21 for my age and the resistance probe registered about 23. I told her I had lost 60 pounds in the last year (its really about 64 now). She asked me if I lost it on purpose. Yep! I didn't tell her it wasn't from exercising or calorie restriction. <grin> I checked BP later at Kmart and it registered 118/64.

I grabbed one of their brochures on dietary fat. It describes polyunsaturated fat as the preferred fat, mentioned monounsaturated fat, and then describes saturated fat as "bad fat" full of cholesterol even though it mentions coconut oil. I didn't know coconuts made cholesterol. While waiting to be screened I mentioned to some of the other coworkers that the heart's preferred fuel was saturated fat and that polyunsaturated fat was easily oxidized and uses up lots of the body's vitamin E.

I will post the actual numbers in a couple of days.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-27T15:31:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/health-screening-at-work-today.6875/post-84939>

Congrats!!!!!! Health, like time, is a precious gift. We become acutely aware of both when we have little of either left.
It has been said that many people spend their health trying to acquire wealth, then spend their wealth trying to buy back their health. May I suggest today you begin to harmonize your mind, body and spirit. Maintain your inner peace. Exercise physically to maintain your strength, but exercise compassion, empathy and understanding to maintain your heart. Make your attitude excellent. Maintain a sunny disposition; allow yours to shine in your world. Live intentional but love deliberately.......and every moment you have left. Time is of the essence. Use it to the best of your ability to manage your purpose.
Habits are developed daily. Develop the habits of guarding your integrity, character, thoughts and speech. You have begun to regain your health today with those labs.

Here are the numbers I promised to post. The screening was around 1 PM, and I had eaten my first meal around 9:30 AM.

# Thread: Hi from sunny South Africa - part 1 of 3
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sunny-south-africa-part-1-of-3.6880/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Fran — 2013-04-26T15:12:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sunny-south-africa-part-1-of-3.6880/post-84872>

Hi there, I posted this under Hormones 101 but Dr Kruse said I must take this to the forum, so here I am

I'm a 38-year old woman and I've been experiencing hair loss slowly since I was 18. The hair loss is in the frontal region of my scalp. It doesn't seem to fall out at a faster rate, but rather, doesn't grow back or miniaturises. I've tried numerous doctors over the years, with no success. I went to a very helpful GP last year as I had a number of symptoms (super-frizzy hair, slight loss of power in my right hand, memory loss, tenderness in my liver area) that were worrying me. He suspected low thyroid and I've been put on Synthroid (can't get Armour or the like in the country I live in). I've never been entirely sure if the hair loss was hormonal or caused by obscene stress that I was experiencing (and which didn't let up well into my twenties), however, I'm now very overweight (heaviest was 126 kg and I'm currently at 96kg / 212 pounds) so if it was stress, I believe the hair loss is now caused by my blood sugar mess. I was thin at the age of 18 (68kg / 150 pounds and my height is 1.77m).

I had some blood tests done last year before my GP put me on thyroid meds:

\- Ultrasensative-CRP: 1.31,
\- DHEAS: 3.47 (reference is 1.65 - 9.15)
\- Vitamin D: 17.6
\- Insulin Fasting: 7.5 (reference is 2.1 - 10.4)
\- Glucose fasting: 5.1 (reference is 3.5 - 5.5)
\- 17OH Progesterone: 12.2
\- Free T4: 10.1 (reference is 7.2 - 16.4)
\- Free T3: 3.8 (reference is 3.8 - 6.0)
\- S-TSH: 2.01 (reference is 0.37 - 3.50)
\- Testosterone - Total: 0.58 (reference is 0.22 - 2.9)
\- Sex Hormone Bind. Globulin: 43.5 (reference is 26.1 - 110)
\- Free Androgen Index: 1.3 (0.4 - 5.9)

And then others were done at the same time and I've added the ones which have been highlighted because they are high:

\- Cholesterol-S - 5.4 (reference is 5.0)
\- LDL Cholesterol - 3.3 (reference is 3.0)
\- Bilirubin Total-S - 28 (rererence is 2 - 20)
\- Bili Unconj.-S - 25 (reference is 2 - 14)
\- ALT (SGPT) - 35 (reference is 10 - 32)

I was diagnosed with the start of non-alcoholic Fatty Liver disease and Hypothyroidism. My GP's response was to put me on Euthyrox (Levothyroxine) at 25 ug, follow the diet recommended by Dr Rosedale and he also suggested some EFT sessions - which I did with him, he's very holistic. I also had to take loads of supplements and a shake drink called Estrium that is manufactured by Metagenics (although at half-dosage).

### JanSz — 2013-05-09T04:25:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-water-deficit.6884/post-85697>

According to Mae-Wan Ho, there is a lots of coherent water.

> ISIS Report 25/07/11
> Quantum Coherent Water and Life
>
> Water is quantum coherent under ordinary conditions, according to a quantum electrodynamics field theory that may explain many of its most paradoxical properties including life itself.
>
>
>
> [http://www.i-sis.org.uk/Quantum\_Coherent\_Water\_Life.php](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/Quantum_Coherent_Water_Life.php)
>
>
> > **Liquid water is therefore a two-fluid system \[5\] (in analogy with superfluid helium) consisting of a coherent phase (about 40 percent of total volume at room temperature) and an incoherent phase.** In the coherent phase, the water molecules oscillate between two electronic configurations in phase with a resonating EMF. The common frequency of the EMF and the electronic oscillation of the coherent phase being 0.26 eV; whereas the energy difference of the two electronic configuration of the coherent phase is 12.06 eV, which gives the wavelength of 1 000 A (100 nm) of the coherence domain. The remaining 60 percent incoherent phase is extracted by thermal fluctuations from the coherent phase. The two phases have widely different dielectric constants: that of the coherent phase is 160, due to the high polarizability of the coherently aligned water molecules that are oscillating in concert; while the dielectric constant of the incoherent state is about 15. The externally applied electric fields are therefore only felt in the non-coherent phase.
> >
> > This picture of liquid water, according to Del Giudice and colleagues, is reflected in the many observations supporting a two-state model of water (see \[1, 10\] Two-States Water Explains All? SiS 32), in which a substantial fraction of the molecules exist in hydrogen bonded state resembling that of ordinary ice. In fact, the hydrogen-bonds - short range interactions â€“ are the consequence of the induced coherence in the coherence domains. But there is a rapid interchange of molecules between the CDs and the incoherent phase, hence it is impossible to detect CDs when the detection time is longer than the period of the oscillations, which is less than 10-13 s.
> >
> > **Quantum coherent water and life**
> >
> > Oxidation and reduction or redox reactions are the stuff of energy transduction in living organisms. It involves transfer of electrons from one substance (donor) to another (acceptor) to power all living activities. But where does the electron come from? It comes ultimately from splitting water in photosynthesis by green plants and cyanobacteria. However, it takes 12.60 eV to split water, an energy corresponding to soft X-rays, which is not what the green plants and cyanobacteria use.
> >
> > More than 50 years ago, Szent-Gyorgyi \[9\] suggested that water at interfaces was the key. He proposed that water in living organisms existed in two states: the ground state and the excited state, and that water at interfaces such as membranes existed in the excited state, which requires considerably lower energy to split. A sign of the excited water is that a voltage should appear at the boundary between interfacial water and bulk water, which was indeed observed. This property of water enables energy transfer to take place in living organisms ensuring long-lasting electronic excitations. Szent-Gyorgyiâ€™s ideas were largely ignored by the scientific mainstream that became obsessed instead with molecular genetics.
> >
> > The anomalous water at interfaces has been the subject of numerous research papers and reviews \[11\], and was already known in the late 1940s, as Del Giudice and colleagues point out \[4\]. Most if not all water in living organisms is interfacial water, as it is almost never further away from surfaces such as membranes or macromolecules than a fraction of a micron.
>
>
>
>
> [http://www.amazon.com/Living-Rainbo...1368040106&sr=8-1&keywords=Living+Rainbow+H2O](http://www.amazon.com/Living-Rainbow-H2O-Mae-Wan-Ho/dp/9814390895/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1368040106&sr=8-1&keywords=Living+Rainbow+H2O)

**CO2 is another signalling compound that gets electrons to the place where they need to go.
**
Quoting directly from the post:

" Or put differently, the best case scenario is to have lots of O2 plus lots of CO2 to help dump that O2 in the places that need it. Achieving this at sea level is probably ideal, for the reasons stated in the blog post (more O2, less nn-EMF, less cosmic rays, less requirement for SCN to speed up)

Note: As possibly a separate effect of CO2, this post alluded to the fact that CO2 + cold works to condense cells (good thing, brings things closer for more quantum mechanical effects) -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/a-few-questions-on-ppp-and-fat.8955/#post-102586](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/a-few-questions-on-ppp-and-fat.8955/#post-102586)

I need to study this effect more to comment on how it is affected"

@[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/) Thanks !!!

# Thread: Scientist Proves DNA Can Be Reprogrammed by Words and Frequencies
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scientist-proves-dna-can-be-reprogrammed-by-words-and-frequencies.6888/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-04-27T05:42:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scientist-proves-dna-can-be-reprogrammed-by-words-and-frequencies.6888/post-84929>

[http://www.collective-evolution.com...can-be-reprogrammed-by-words-and-frequencies/](http://www.collective-evolution.com/2011/09/02/scientist-prove-dna-can-be-reprogrammed-by-words-and-frequencies/)

"Living chromosomes function just like solitonic/holographic computers using the endogenous DNA laser radiation.” This means that they managed for example to modulate certain frequency patterns onto a laser ray and with it influenced the DNA frequency and thus the genetic information itself. Since the basic structure of DNA-alkaline pairs and of language (as explained earlier) are of the same structure, no DNA decoding is necessary."

I think this article encompasses everything that Dr Kruse is trying to teach us.

Our DNA is malable by our thoughts and by outside influences of radio frequencies...either to the good of ourselves or other people. The mystic healers are able to generate frequencies within their bodies to influence the DNA of others to repair their DNA in the healing process. Researchers are able to tune radio waves to repair DNA thus validating what the mystics, seers, shamans do within themselves.

If this can be done to repair DNA, think how destructive the EMF radio waves are to our own DNA that is random and uncontrolled.

The article talks about group communication among the same species and how that has served each species through the millenium...When a queen ant is alive and well, all the workers know exactly what to do....no matter how far the queen is apart from the colony. But kill the queen without another to replace her, the workers have no idea of what to do. She contained the building blocks of the colony.

Get a bunch of like minded people together and the synergy of the thought processes feed off of each other.

I recall about 20 years ago, a late night talk show host was lamenting about a huge forest fire raging in the southeast..I think it was Georgia. A caller said why not get all the listeners to focus their individual energy on creating rain over Georgia sufficient to snuff out the blaze. He preceded to encourage all his listeners...about 1.5 million at any one time to bring rain to Georgia when there was no rain in sight for at least 10 days. Within 48 hours there were rain storms cropping up everywhere over Georgia to the extent that there was huge flooding and loss of life. He never did it again. But I think it demonstrated the power of the collective mind...the collective DNA is far more powerful than we can imagine.

"DNA: It apparently is also an organic superconductor that can work at normal body temperature. Artificial superconductors require extremely low temperatures of between 200 and 140Â°C to function. As one recently learned, all superconductors are able to store light and thus information. This is a further explanation of how the DNA can store information. There is another phenomenon linked to DNA and wormholes. Normally, these supersmall wormholes are highly unstable and are maintained only for the tiniest fractions of a second. Under certain conditions stable wormholes can organize themselves which then form distinctive vacuum domains in which for example gravity can transform into electricity".

How many of you are in tune with another person that you know in advance what they are going to say?

Be careful what you wish for because your DNA is listening and that is not a joke.

Much more in the article to contemplate.

### prAna303 — 2013-04-27T09:28:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scientist-proves-dna-can-be-reprogrammed-by-words-and-frequencies.6888/post-84936>

This is kind of funny. Today we are making computers and we think we invent a lot of new things but the computer is just a copy of ourself. The same problems we get when we are running a server we also suffer as humans. ItÂ´s weird that many donÂ´t see that connection today. A powerful server has at least redundant power supplies, redundant networkconnections and so on. On top of that we put a program to run it. If any of these parts donÂ´t work in harmony we get problems, just like we do as humans.

I think that we can understand ourself better today if we compare ourself with these things because the human you has become alien to us.

Many of the things you write above is in line with my own thinkings on life in the big.

Nice thoughts!

# Thread: Welcome to my world...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 225
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MrPinkies — 2013-04-28T01:32:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-84965>

As most of you have read.. I'm on a personal mission to lose 160lbs or so. I'm not really sure how far I am going to go with this.. but.. since the weight seems to come off, why not go all the way??!? Seems like a natural thing to do, right? I am FINALLY, the first time in 13 years, under 200 lbs!!!! I've tried EVERYTHING under the sun to drop the weight.. multipliable personal trainers.. countless gym memberships.. an embarrassing amount of gimmick diets, equipment, etc.. Beyond frustrated, tried the lepton reset, and HOLY COW, I started to drop 1-2lbs per DAY... I started to really understand my issues. I am a sugar addict like an crack addicted- but with sugar. I still struggle every day... however, the REALLY obvious bad stuff, like pizza, etc.. that stuff I don't crave anymore... in fact, a lot of that stuff makes me sick even thinking about it... I still make mistakes and don't lose like I want, but, I guess this is a process... right? I am still the one not to eat fish.. I just can't do it.. fish eyes freak me out!! and their scales.. my husband picking out the bones in his mouth.. ughhhh.. can't or won't.. I guess I won't.. but if I'm still losing weight, should I care? probably.. I just am having a hard time getting over their googlie eyes and the bones things.. and the scales.. There is hope for me.. I will eat shrimp and some other sea-stuff... lol

Enough about me, I guess.. The mix to my situation is.. well.. Autism... My whole family- husband- Christopher (who is actively on the forum too), both kids, especially my son Ryan.. I understood most of the podcast that was recommended (it's in the ask Jack section).. Holy carb-ness... We cleaned out the fridge and the kitchen.. Ryan is definitely starting week three of the straight, no cheating, ketogenic diet, as much spring non-fluoridated H2O as possible, extra fat on the side... We actually had to say to our kids grandparents that they will not be able to spend time with them for a while- a few months or so. I was laughing so hard though the thread that is labeled "\*\*\*t our families says", I almost pi$$ed my pants.. but.. man am I in that boat.. we can NOT trust our families anymore, period. The unbelievable CRAP that spews out. I almost feel like they want us to fail. They either don't accept Autism diagnoses at all or then when something comes along that does work- they do everything in their power to do the exact opposite... Everything we have done to try to chase this- what seems unrealistic- dream of catching up all of the gaps in his skills and all of his sensory issues with his nervous system. Changing his diet has had the MOST impact. It is CLEARLY a night and day difference. Changing the light bulbs.. selling our tv and iPad.. getting rid of our cell phones that gave my husband hemorrhoids that he decided to make so public- silly boy.. hard-wiring our computers.. Just seems like a TON of changes that.. quite honestly.. has made our lives easier.. My kids sleep through the night, FINALLY at five years old, my son SLEEPS!!!! My son rarely screams over noise, lights, or other sensory issues.. Turning off the wifi was CRAZY awesome reaction.. My husband and I kinda looked at each other asking each other if that really was the cause of our son's screaming?? It wasn't entirely, but, we could tell that it did have an impact on him. Even the school called this week and asked if we were doing anything different at home because of Ryan's positive behavior changes, in fact, he's going to be mainstreamed next year.. I never in a million years thought that that was going to be possible if you were to have asked me 6 months ago. Is mainstreaming him the right thing? Who knows.. I am the girl that will jump in with both feet and see what happens I guess... Wish me luck!

I have learned so much.. life is complicated.. but it seems to be getting easier by the day.. Although, it's been interesting, I'm trying to find the positive in this journey.. positive people.. Where are they? OH YES.. definitely in this form, of course! hahahaa...

Keep the positive rollin' cuz I'm likin' it!!!! Up and onward to greatness!! Until next time..

### caroline — 2013-04-28T03:09:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-84973>

I love your post ....... I wish you so much luck ....but it is so much more than that! You have a tenacity that is awe inspiring.
You will influence so many more people than just your immediate family - the world is your oyster! The ripple effects from the changes you are making are endless. I am a person that never gives up too - for a variety of reasons - but then again - I think a lot of people give up way too easy. You need to explore every possible avenue and then do it again if need be!

Keep jumping in with both feet ..... is there any other way?

please keep posting on your progress - good, bad or indifferent - we all learn so much from each other......

### nonchalant — 2013-04-28T05:12:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-84976>

Awesome progress, Mr. Pink!
I think I understand totally about the grandparents.

### cantweight — 2013-04-28T05:25:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-84978>

I am so happy for all of your successes!!! I have about 130 to lose myself and my kids have "issues" as well. My youngest went non-verbal for a brief period as a toddler and later developed 2 motor tics and a vocal tic and a stutter. I had him to some amazing doctors, childrens hospital neurologists, and by chance happened to take him to crossfit with me (yes my fat ass did crossfit..lol) and one of the trainers there told me to take him off grains. LIFE CHANGING!!!

Within 3 weeks this kid had 0 tics and no stutter!!! I could not believe it! He could not believe it!

We have not been able to keep them 100% at bay since the initial diet change but it is hardly noticeable and there is no fear of him needing to leave his classroom anymore, so whew!

I am hopeful that with CT, focus on his supplements and clearing up any nastiness lurking in his labs he will know life without the "issues"

I love your spirit! And it helps to know I am not alone in having so much to lose.....and even more to gain

Good luck and keep us posted!

Mr Pinkies, awesome stuff....!!!

Hey!!!  Awesome goal of 130!! You CAN do it!!! I'm down 120lbs roughly out of the 160 total.. It takes time, but, it WILL happen! (if you go on my profile, I have before and after pictures!)

As far as your kids goes.. all I can say is... AWESOME!!!!!!!!! and.. AMAZING!!!!!! Last, I will definitely keep you posted..  forum stuff is fun and more addicted than FB.. OMG, did I just type that?!?!? I DID! hmm.. something to think about.. lol

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-02T02:08:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85265>

was outside today.. I had one of those moments when I realized that the leaves are starting to come out on the trees.. and everything is starting to look so GREEN... I love these moments.. makes me feel grateful for life..

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-09T03:57:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85690>

Sorry friends that I have been gone for a little while.. I got sick.. my throat got very irritated.. and then came the cough.. and then I couldn't breath.. and you know.. breathing is kinda important.. Of course, it was only a matter of time that I got sick.. My kids were sick first.. Since we have been attempting to mainstream Ryan.. he went from a class of 5 to a class of 25+... so.. he got a little bit of everything.. starting with strep.. then flu.. then pink eye.. then croup... (shaking my fist at the sky).. Croup is one of the scariest things I have ever experienced with my son.. When we got to the ER, he was a shade of grey with purple lips.. Er doctors looked at each other, wondering why the hell we didn't take an ambulance.. I'll tell you why.. the last time Ryan had to take an ambulance, we went less than five miles, and it costed over 1600 dollars... So anyway.. getting sick set me back a few pounds... I blame the cough drops and inhaler, etc.. but.. now that I'm back into the swing of things and I can breath (again, the importance of breathing.. lol).. the pounds are coming off again.. slow but surely.. the smaller I get, the slower it comes off.. oh well.. my main goal was to get under 200 lbs- check! so.. I'm pretty happy with that..

My question to you is.. what do you do for coughs and colds and stuffed up nose??? Sudafed is kinda a favorite to get the nose stuffiness all cleared out.. but.. I'm thinking that's probably not the right thing to do anymore??? or is it?

### caroline — 2013-05-09T04:09:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85692>

My grandson gets croup ..... Very scarey. My daughter always turns the hot water on in the shower and sticks him in there - but I am sure you know that. She does call an ambulance but they have coverage.

### cantweight — 2013-05-09T05:15:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85701>

So sorry you were sick! I hated when the kids would pick up every germ at school..ugh.

We do a lot of neti-pot, or the neil-med squirt bottle for stuffy noses. Sometimes with a drop of eucalyptus or tea tree oil. When it gets really bad I go for the mucinex...guifenisin.
Salt water gargles for the sore throats are good too!

Glad yall are feeling better...your pics are awesome...I can not wait to be under 200!!!!

### Inger — 2013-05-09T08:36:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85710>

> [MrPinkies said:](/goto/post?id=85690)
>
> My question to you is.. what do you do for coughs and colds and stuffed up nose??? Sudafed is kinda a favorite to get the nose stuffiness all cleared out.. but.. I'm thinking that's probably not the right thing to do anymore??? or is it?

Glad you feel better! For stuffed nose you might try salt water flush? One nostril at time, and "breath" up saltwater in your nose. You could put a half raw onion by your bedside too, not so good smell but could help! At least it has helped me.. and then quite any dairy if you eat it... We also put a piece of raw garlic in some cotton into our nose as kids when we had this.. and also some peppermint oil around the nose might help - be careful to not irritate the skin too much tho! I also do herb teas and steam baths, for 10 minutes or so. You need to use a pot and maybe 2-3 liters of water+herbs and then put a towel over your head and breath the steam. You can use rosemary, salvia, thyme, chamomille, mint... I drink the teas too.

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-13T19:06:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85953>

Thanks to everyone that replied about the stuffy nose and sore throat!  I will definitely keep those ideas in mind for next time!

Next, Happy Mothers Day to all of those awesome Moms that we have out there!

Well, a few days ago, Chris (who is also on the forum.. randomly I might add.. lol) and I sent out an email to our parents and siblings... OK, ok.. the emails were all me.. I'm the bad guy.. lol We gave the ultimate awesome news that Ryan and Lizzie both will be going into a mainstreamed classrooms next year.. and that he will be in FIRST grade! Holy cow.. my kid is going to FIRST grade!!!!! This wasn't an option in January.. We were looking at another year in Kindergarten in the ASD classroom.. It took us 7 months to get his/our diet squared away- without cheating.. FINALLY 6 weeks ago, it FINALLY happened.. no screw ups.. We unfortunately had to tell grandparents that we were taking a break from get-togethers to get the diet up and going. They made a huge fuss.. but you know what?? that is their problem, not ours.. I finally feel like we drew the line in the sand.. follow our rules or don't see our kids.. it's that simple now.. sucks that we had to do it, but glad we did.. by the end of the email, it was the "rules", clear cut rules...

So.. Mother's Day came around.. and we started a conversation with my dear husband's parents... They told us that everyone that they talked to told them how dangerous it was and that we were going to ruin our children's kidneys... and if we prayed hard enough, they would be healed from the all mighty upstairs.. Me? I'm not a big fan of the big guy upstairs.. just not.. never was.. never will be... My black and white thinking does the trick.. I like what I can see and touch and feel.. I like hard proof and science.. However, before everyone gets their panties in a bunch, I think evolution fits within what's in the bible.. I don't exactly throw it out the window completely.. or do I? Oh well..

Well, back to my "fun" on Mother's day.. Ryan got over stimulated by having his grandparents over (especially when they tickle him) and he started to spin and run in circles in the living room.. If it had been January, this would have caused him ultimately to get to a point where he would have ended the spinning with throwing himself on the ground screaming.. for hours.. (and I mean.. the whole rest of the day...) on end... Well, I asked him to stop spinning. He didn't like that, but, he sat down on the couch for a minute.. and then he went back to spinning.. I asked him to sit and count to 50. He again didn't want to stop, but, then, he said, "I want to talk to you". I said ok, let's talk.. He told me that he didn't want to stop and that he likes spinning. I forgot what I suggested to him, but, the long of the short was I distracted him with something else that he likes to do.. There was NO screaming.. no tantrum or meltdown..

This is called... MAJOR PROGRESS..

There is NO denying this kind of progress.. there is nothing that could be said for excuses of why this happened.. This happened primarily because we have stuck to this diet and it is working... Can I get an AMEN??!?!?!?!!!

### caroline — 2013-05-14T00:25:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85965>

You most certainly have an AMEN from me.......way to hang in there!

Why is it so tough for people we care about to see great changes in us ..... and then dismiss them out of hand.........????

### cantweight — 2013-05-14T01:06:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85967>

A-freaking-men! I am so proud of you for defeating the grandparent food beast! That is still soooo hard for us! Our parents just dont get it. They tell the kids a little gluten wont hurt you and my 8 year old believes it and regurgitates the words to me. God! Makes me so mad! They just dont get it, and its not even lack of trying, they seriously just dont get it. They think its great when they see the better health, the diminished neuro issues...they just cant put the pieces together.

I commend you on a job well done mom and dad!!! Mainstreamed!!!! That is huge!!! Yay!!

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-14T01:11:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85968>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=85965)
>

>
> Why is it so tough for people we care about to see great changes in us ..... and then dismiss them out of hand.........????

WOOT WOOT!

yep- welcome to my world.. lol

we desperately want our children to have a healthy relationship with the grandparents.. but.. it's more important that their brain function properly.. right? that sounds sane?

I was joking with a friend that "gets it".. and I told her its been like.. oh.. you're allergic to peanuts? Here's a peanut butter and jelly sandwich... as a treat.. because Mom and Dad never let you have it at home! LOL It's sad, but funny and f'ed up at the same time!!! seriously!!

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-14T01:19:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85969>
