>
> Pinkies.. when I read about Ryans meltdown the first thing I thought about was.. maybe you should have let him go to school that day. You could go to zoo alone? I do not like zoo anymore too much.. I like to watch animals in the wild..lol or the lambs and chicks they get good care and makes me feel good!
> I think kids are very sensitive about what they think is right.. and I think maybe Ryan think he had to go to school. Or he likes school. Anyhow.. I think maybe next time you could try to listen to what he said then about wanting to go to school and all the stress would be not there.. Just my two cents Pinkies...
>
> Lifting sounds great! Do you have a yard? If, try to lift bare feet in the sunshine... I bet it will be even more effective! I feel how I am slimming down just from being out doing work like building fences for the lambs.. and garden work.. in the sunshine in bikini and bare feet. To me that is the most slimming thing ever. The body just tightens up and gets better without even thinking about it.. and zero exercise! I need to find a work as a fence builder..lol
> And then.. try to eat lost of raw fish.. and wild greens.. very slimming in my experience..

Wished he had told me, I would have sent him to school! lol he didn't say anything until after we got to the zoo.. probably too much to see, too much noise (forgot his sound blocking headphones), and too many people..

Oh well.. the thing is.. if we didn't do anything out of his comfort zone, he would just do everything the same day in and day out.. he needs new experiences (maybe not the zoo) to grow from the experiences.. I don't want him to be so afraid of the world..

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-28T01:07:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-86976>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=86872)
>
> nice peaceful time when the kids are asleep!
>
> I kind of think the reason some people aren't getting the weight loss they want is because their diet is so varied - my opinion - and I know it sounds weird.... but to me - people that have the best success eat seemingly boring repetitive meals.
>
> I think even weight watchers is saying that these days \[I think I have that right\] just eat simple meals - ones you don't have to think too much about. I love to eat like that ...just some grassfed beef, some shrimp, some grilled fish and some salad - really easy and I love it.......
>
> So - Does Ryan like to eat the same thing over and over? Does he get frustrated when you try to feed him something different? My youngest daughter would only eat mostly noodles from a can for the longest time - she would start gagging if I was cooking something different! Now she has the most incredible palate and she is an excellent cook.
>
> It is a huge help if they can choose what they want to eat and then help prepare it. Gives them a sense of being in control that they need. Also learning to bake bread and letting them do the kneading - gives them a sense of accomplishment and they can also take out their frustrations on the dough instead of you!!!

Yes, Ryan mainly eats organic free range, etc chicken on the bone (has to have the bone in) and eggs.. and seaweed.. and cod... Lizzie only eats organic grass fed beef and salads.. Both kids LOVE different kinds of nuts and coconut manna and grass-fed butter, and coconut oil... anything made from coconut.. lol..

To keep Ryan feeling like his peers, we make paleo cookies and stuff like that.. He gets picked on enough as is..

Meals around our house are very very very simple.. the more simple, the better the kids like it...

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-28T01:13:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-86977>

Day 10 of CT... woke up the the awesome scale saying 2 lbs down! YAY!!!!!!!

I am LOVING CT... OMG.. My favorite "me" thing to do now!

Yesterday, we went to a park as a family and had a blast... we are back on track from Friday.. I realized that getting back on track was MUCH faster and easier than ever before.. WHEW!

Chris has been turning off all breakers (with the exception of the breaker for like the furnace and fridge).. Sleep has been AWESOME.. even though, I really have unrealistic fears about it.. but.. I think that has more to do with my sexual abuse history than anything..

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-31T18:40:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-87199>

Day 14 of CT.... Day 11, 12, 13... check, check, check, and check...

I decided to try CT at night time.. BAD PLAN.. I can't sleep when I'm cold.. I've had the worse sleep this week... but.. the weight is still coming off, so, happiness is..

Life is good.. no real complaints.. Chris got a fever and stayed home a few days.. but is starting to feel better today.. I'm starting to feel the way he was this morning, but, I'm not letting it get the best of me! I'm still kicking butt and takin' names..

Trying to find fun things to do with the kids this summer since we are not slamming and jamming with therapies for once...

### MrPinkies — 2013-06-07T22:08:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-87751>

Finally got pictures done.. Haven't done this in a while... I love looking back to see how far I have come!

Life is good.. great actually. Sometimes, when I somehow manage to get off track, the best way to get back on track is going back to basics.. I don't think that I necessarily got off track this time.. but.. I had a period of time where my weight did nothing.. and I was frustrated.. I pushed through this period of time finally! I'm on week 3 of CT and I'm starting to see results.. Again, it's still my favorite part of the day... Bitter melon did jack squat for me, so, I stopped taking it.. When I stopped taking it, my weight started to drop again.. hmm.. something to think about.. I wonder why that is... ???

Another thing that is on my mind is loose skin.. I wonder, especially my arms.. if CT is going to help tone that shit up or if I should just start lifting weights? Unlike most women I know.. I want muscles.. Super Woman! wait wait.. I'm already that.. jk lol I need to find my own new dream.. My dream needs to be a BIG dream big.. I never thought I would accomplish my current dream.. I thought it was going to take much longer than it did... Finally in our new home, I am able to have my own art space.. I had to catch up paintings from all the years I missed!! and 3 paintings later... LOL I'm already counting down the days until fall when ballet classes start back up..

### caroline — 2013-06-08T04:58:48+0300

You two are awesome!!.....

Wow. Just wow! I am angry I cannot make the pictures bigger no idea why not, but I still can see there is a huge difference!
I love how energized you sounds.. just so full of life  I do think the loose skin will slowly disappear, it just need some time. CT will help and also I think lots of K2? Seaweeds too maybe? You are going to build muscles in sleep with all this CT I promise you. But maybe you could start make a pull up / day or so? Just very heavy and very short sessions. I cant imagine that could hurt any. Better not every day me thinks, maybe a short and heavy work out 3 times / week? Oh man.. ballet sounds awesome.. I want too!

### MrPinkies — 2013-06-13T17:36:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-88035>

I feel like I have been sucked into my own little bubble lately.. pissed at the world for no reason.. numb... thing is.. I have no literal reason for this.. in fact.. I should be happier than a clam right now.. Ryan's last day of kindergarten is today.. I have a ton of play dates and fun things planned for this summer.. I don't have be anything or anywhere or anyone.. I have freedom here.. and for whatever reason.. I feel miserable.. Maybe I need sex... lots of sex.. extra sex.. no.. never mind.. not in the mood for that.. Some days.. I really don't want to live in Michigan anymore.. just for the pure reason that I miss the sun... It's June for crying out loud.. it's suppose to be sunny and warm.. I guess we are making up for all the lack of rain we had last year.. Rain usually makes me a very happy camper.. that's not cutting it either..

I guess I will go and do what makes me happiest right now... a nice long ice bath.. CT, please be my "happiness" hero today!!!

### MrPinkies — 2013-06-25T02:17:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-88860>

Hi friends.. it's been a while!

I'm seriously considering getting my extra skin removed.. I made an appointment with a surgeon that came highly recommended!!!!

Thoughts?

CT is still going well.. and my weight is slowly going down again since I started logging my food again..

### caroline — 2013-06-25T06:11:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-88876>

I would absolutely wait until you are done losing .... and then wait awhile longer......You are young and I wouldn't be surprised if your skin went back to normal - somewhat anyway. I am pretty sure that JK's skin went back to normal... If I am remembering right - he said he used coconut oil.

My body has changed a significant amount thru all of this - and I am alot older than you! My Body comp is way, way better.
I used to wear a size 30 in a jean and yesterday I bought a size 26 jean ...I now have a waist again!

So - I guess what I want to tell you is ...... my body shape has changed alot the last 6 months. I bought a bunch of new clothes and now they are all way too big. It isn't the weight - it is the body comp.
I do have a little problem with my mid section - but it is because a surgeon \[many, many years ago\] kinda split me open from stem to stern and did a horrible sew up job!

Do you use ice packs on your tummy etc?

### caroline — 2013-06-25T06:17:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-88877>

I just want to add - C/T will be the game changer I think. Also - I am so glad to hear you are logging your food. In my opinion - that is why so many on here went astray and couldn't lose. I think a woman needs to give their food alot more thought than men do - just my opinion!

You are doing so great ........ you and Christopher examine everything .... and then see what changes need to be made .....and then you do it! awesome.....

### MrPinkies — 2013-06-25T16:01:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-88890>

Yeah, this surgery isn't going to be until next year.. but.. I need to get the conversation going.. It's hard for most people to understand that I went from a size 26 down to a size 12 in a matter of months... I'm almost in a size 10... I'm about 30 lbs from a healthy weight.. and most of it is extra skin..

As far as CT goes, I CT everyday.. but.. it doesn't do much for me.. I see better results from sprints and lifting or maybe a combo of that and CT..

### nonchalant — 2013-06-25T16:35:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-88893>

When I lost 45 lbs, my loose skin looked awful. Especially on my arms, even my forearms. But within a year it gradually went away, to my huge relief. If you continue to CT I think you will be happy with the changes it brings.

### MrPinkies — 2013-06-25T19:20:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-88902>

well, I'm talking about 130 pound loss.. my stomach literally looks like a deflated balloon.. I don't think diet, exercise, or CT is going to help this amount of skin... it will help some, but, I am at a point where I am somewhat discussed with myself.. I almost rather be fat than have all this extra skin.. lol crazy, right?

### MsYo — 2013-06-25T20:39:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-88908>

Your skin takes a bit longer to catch up after the weightloss. I've lost over 150 lbs and kept 90% off for more than 10 years. Every individual is different but I would recommend a period of stabilization in your weight before considering excess skin removal. Also, exercise (strength training) really helps. Wearing support undergarments for your areas of concern really help.

### MrPinkies — 2013-06-29T21:50:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-89204>

So.. my sister plays for the USA woman's pro football team.. Currently, the USA team is in Finland playing against other countries.. it's kinda like the Olympics I guess... Anyway.. she posted a very cool picture on FB that I thought was very FASCINATING and AWESOME! I was going to share it on JK's FB page, but, it wouldn't allow me to.. I'll have to tinker with that some more.. but anyway.. The team coaches had set up 4 HUGE plastic trash cans, filled it with water and ice.. and each player had to go sit in one... They had ice fights.. oh the pictures are awesome!

My sister had no idea that that is exactly what I do, on a daily bases in my bathtub... but.. she totally gets why I do now.. and I think I notched one more level of craziness with her.. hahahaha.. didn't think that was possible!

Pictured is one of my sister's teammates!

### MrPinkies — 2013-06-29T22:07:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-89207>

So.. I've learned my lesson.. seriously.. I don't care about calories.. seriously do not care.. but.. for some reason.. when I log everything I eat, my weight always goes down.. Doesn't matter if I do anything different or not, it just starts going down..

If anyone is wondering, I use [www.myfitnesspal.com](http://www.myfitnesspal.com)...

So.. I started logging everything again and my weight is FLYING off again! Can I get an AMEN! (7lbs in 5 days this week)

### caroline — 2013-06-30T04:42:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-89220>

You are doing so awesome! What is it about the logging in do you think? Personally - I think it keeps us aware and on target and committed.

From a comment above .... I have been wearing a compression tank top for ages now ..... instead of a bra and love it - and I think it helps alot with body comp. I had forgotten that is why I started wearing it 6 or 8 months ago and now just find it so much more comfortable. If you check out yoga wear etc. you will find this stuff.

I have started using a kettle bell and love it! It helps with core strength and I hope it will tone up my arms some more. It doesn't take very long and it is fun!!!

### MrPinkies — 2013-08-28T14:53:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-94024>

Hi friends!

Summer is coming to a close here and I am really starting to understand how the seasons are going to play out with me... Bring on Autumn- big red, orange and yellow leaves on the trees, cooler temps, and kiddos back to school (its been a looooooong summer lol)!

First, however, a toast... To Jack Kruse.. I MADE IT! I made a full year commitment to the leptin reset. It wasn't easy, but, my drive to achieve this- unstoppable especially once I understood the thought behind the madness. You have changed my life in SO many ways. You have changed my family's life in SO many ways. I'm close to 140 pounds loss, husband lost around 50-60 pounds, and last, both kids and husband's Autism symptoms are.. 98% of the time- mild and manageable and every day it seems to get that much better!! I have never, until now, pictured my son in a mainstream classroom!! Pure miracle how far we have come in one year! We look forward to the blessed years we have ahead of us! Thank you!

In other news.. I have been a stay at home Mom for the past 6 years (almost 7).. Since I have lost the majority of my weight, I've been thinking... what now? what's next? What's the next big goal in life going to be? What can I do? What will make my heart soar??? From when I was 2 - 18, I was a ballet dancer. I took a 10 year break due to injury, getting married, having babies, gaining 160 lbs, etc etc etc... I've gone back to several classes now.. kinda like riding a bike- the body never forgets! I was asked if I wanted to work on taking my teachers exam!!! Apparently my dream of just going back and taking a class or two wasn't a big enough dream!! lol

### AnnieW — 2013-08-30T16:34:51+0300

### MrPinkies — 2013-08-30T17:29:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-94347>

> [AnnieW said:](/goto/post?id=94345)
>
> Did it really only take you a year to lose all that weight? I need to lose about 130 and it would be great if I could do it in a year.

Hi Annie! No, it took me a year and a half. I still have another 20-30 lbs to go. Here's the thing, you didn't gain it in one year, you probably won't lose it all in one year. I tried my BEST to stick to the leptin reset as much as possible, but, the further I got into it the more I realized that I had to do more than just the reset. CT is important.. Seasons and timing of meals are important.. I barely lost anything this summer because of carbs (fruit) around the house.. The coolest thing, though, PERFECT time to start! Fall time/ winter time was when I lose the most and FAST.

Good luck and let me know if you need help!

### AnnieW — 2013-08-30T19:03:34+0300

### MrPinkies — 2013-08-30T21:01:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-94370>

> [AnnieW said:](/goto/post?id=94360)
>
> Well congratulations to you on losing all that weight and getting even closer to optimal! I can't wait until I can get rid of this "extra person" I'm carrying around! I saw your pictures and you look fantastic. I carry my weight differently (all in the gut) so I can only hope to look as good as you when I'm all done! The doctor says I need to get down to 200 lbs to have a "safe" pregnancy.
>
> Thanks for the tips and thanks for putting your journey out there to inspire all of us.

TRUST your doctor when they say that they want you under 200 lbs for a safe pregnancy!!! I got over 300 lbs with both pregnancies and there were many many many complications that could have been avoided.. The pregnancies were difficult.. I was BEYOND tired. and my poor hips, back, and knees- just the strain from the extra weight that I gained and had already!! Not to mention that I ended up with c-sections.

You can do it lady! Jump in with two feet and give it 100% and before you know it- you'll be dropping 1-2 lbs a day! It's addicting, but, at the same time, it was (for me anyway) a serious REHAB.. and then.. you'll notice a HUGE difference in mood and the way you feel.. Your clothing will fit better.. Shopping will become fun again- I can definitely promise you that! You'll be breathing this lifestyle without even thinking about it any more.. Then everyone in your world will ask you how you dropped the weight quickly and not a single person will like your answer.. but.. that's ok.. not your problem.. Do this for YOU.. it's YOUR HEALTH.. YOUR BODY... I do not do this to become skinny anymore.. Although, in honesty, that was the goal in the beginning.. but.. if I can avoid cancer and etc.. and live a very long life! AMEN SISTER! that is what I am doing! lol

Question... HOW BAD DO YOU WANT THIS???? This isn't something you can half ass.. well.. you could.. and I have at times.. but.. that's why it has taken me so long.. Let me make this mistake for you!

### AnnieW — 2013-08-30T23:15:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-94395>

Mr. Pinkies,

Could you give me a rundown of what you would eat in a typical day when you were losing so much? I have been following the protocol and reading more about EMF and CT. I can't CT yet because I get those hives when ice is on my skin. Were you doing the EMF or CT protocols when you lost your weight or was that something you incorporated in later? I'm doing my best to limit EMF but my situation at home is tough. My husband is in IT and we have a ton of technology in the house. We have wireless everything and hard wired computers in the bedroom across from ours. I do have an iPad for work but I'm trying to limit how much I use that. I mainly use it in the kitchen when I look up recipes or something, but not too much in bed. Replacing light bulbs is a no-go according to my husband because he doesn't want amber bulbs. So I just try to minimize the lamps we have turned on in the evening and buy lower watt bulbs. It's all I can do for now. Luckily I don't watch too much TV and I'm actually thinking about canceling our DirecTV when the contract is up next month. That will save us some money at least.

Thanks for your help and sorry for so many questions.

### caroline — 2013-08-31T03:02:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-94415>

### MrPinkies — 2013-08-31T16:48:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-94470>

> [AnnieW said:](/goto/post?id=94395)
>
> Mr. Pinkies,
>
> Could you give me a rundown of what you would eat in a typical day when you were losing so much?
>
> Thanks for your help and sorry for so many questions.

No prob!

What I HIGHLY suggest doing is to go to Myfitnesspal.com. Create your profile. You can adjust the carb intake to 5 or 10% (I HIGHLY suggest no more than 30 carbs a day). My user name is Mrpinkies on there too.. Come find me and add me as a friend, and you can read though all of my daily food intake. My food hasn't really changed.. I kinda stick with the same foods.. If you have any questions, let me know! I have lots o recipes too!

Basically, you want to get as much fat and protein (50-75g) as you can stuff yourself with in the morning ASAP.. 4-5 hours in between 3 meals, drinking 1-2 cups of spring water an hour.. I tried 2 meals a day and I couldn't do it.. I got too cranky and tired.. and last, make sure you eat 4-5 hours before bed.. I usually get around 2 gals of water per day.. Do NOT cheat.. just don't do it.. remind yourself if you cheat, its two weeks to get back on track and who wants that? Well, I've gone off track before.. it's a bitch.. lol When you record what you eat, especially in the beginning, you are more mindful.. I think JK called it the Hawthorne Effect.. Lunch is more of a snack.. and Dinner is dinner.. The more you get rid of the JUNK (premade by human hands), the better your success is going to be.. If it comes from an animal or plant from the ground, you are probably A-OK.. \*wink\* except for corn.. Corn is the devils spawn.. LOL along with the rest of the grains..

### cantweight — 2013-08-31T20:49:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-94501>

Hey Kate! I just signed up for the my fitness pal...can you friend me, cuz I cant figure it out..lol. My user name is cantweight726

I cracked up when it told me I need to eat 1500 calories a day and 210 carbs! Ive been down that road before! Reminds me of my weight watchers days when they told me to add a medium vanilla milkshake once a week to up my weightloss!! ha ha!!

But it does look like the perfect spot to track my carbs...thanks for the tip!

Having some crazy issues with my youngest so I need to become much more rigid in his diet. I always give in at birthday parties and let him have cake...this last fall out made even him realize it's not worth it. He wouldnt stop screaming the other night and his OCD stuff flared. He was so crazed I was frightened by him and for him. It was a week ago that he had cake and he is still not himself yet. His gut is just a freaking land mine i can not screw around with. He is so delicate its hard to tell what throws him off balance. I am re-reading all the gut blogs, the tourettes blog and trying harder to do right by him.

you continue to be my inspiration! thank you

> if you blow up with anything you eat = bad mitonuclear coaptation.........not a food issue.......a light/mito issue is more accurate

I was just thinking about redox potential, our environment, and such, and how it can impact our abilities to use different foods. Dave Asprey is all about being "bulletproof", and many follow suite; yet, there is no tolerance to dairy, fermented foods, wheat, etc. It causes a "decrease in performance", acne, bloating, etc. I don't think it is so bulletproof to not even be capable of eating certain foods without a decrease in performance, no matter what form of performance we're speaking about.

I don't think there is anything inherently wrong with the food, just the reaction to said foods. What is causing the reaction(s) should be looked at. It is likely circadian mismatches, nnEMF, blue light, etc.

# Thread: Newbie with Adrenal Fatigue pleading for help!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-adrenal-fatigue-pleading-for-help.6901/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx

### Charles — 2013-04-28T14:24:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-adrenal-fatigue-pleading-for-help.6901/post-84987>

I just found this site today after searching for "adrenal fatigue and fasting". Ive suffered with what Ive been told is adrenal fatigue / fibromyalgia for a good few years. Im male and 40. It has been severe at times and occasionally quite mild. I have dedicated much of the last few years to getting well and seen many doctors, alternative therapists, read tons of stuff and tried many things and spent a small fortune in search of a cure.

I just did a 3.5 day fast/detox under guidance at a detox clinic. It wasnt a water fast, we had coconut water, potato water, carrot juice and some special shakes and colonics every day. I felt ok during the fast, not great but ok. I had my bad moments. The day I finished I felt great, full of energy and buzzing but yesterday (day 2 after finishing) I felt a little tired and then this morning I did a very hard yoga class, sweated loads and afterwards had a total crash. I haven't had a fatigue crash like this for months.

I also suffer with lower back issues which seem to be down to tight hamstrings and hip flexors that dont seem to want to let go. Ive noticed that when the fatigue is bad so is the tightness in these muscles and thus the back too. I read somewhere that adrenal fatigue affects the kidneys which in turn affect these muscles. Its interesting to know that if I can sort out the adrenals my back pain might go too

Im pleased to have found this site and the website but I can't work out half of whats being said by Dr Jack. Ive got qualifications in anatomy and physiology, I'm fairly intelligent and I've been reading about all this stuff for years but I'm really struggling with the amount of words I dont understand and the technical jargon.

I dont need convincing that Dr Jack has the solution to my issues and I get what he's saying about it being an issue in the brain. I was told this a couple of years ago by someone who said it was a dysfunction/signal issue of the hypothalamus. I would just appreciate some simple instructions step by step in layman's terms of what action to take please.

I live in the UK.

Any help would be MASSIVELY appreciated

With thanks

Charles

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-29T00:11:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-adrenal-fatigue-pleading-for-help.6901/post-85055>

Just a suggestion to have your thyroid checked. I've seen many with fibro type pain find out they are hypothyroid and once they start on meds it can almost, if not completely, eliminate the pain.

Get a TSH, FT3, FT4, and rT3 (if you can in UK). Also check TPO and TGAB antibodies for the auto-immune version of hypothyroidism. After you get the lab results post them here for further help.

### caroline — 2013-04-29T08:55:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-adrenal-fatigue-pleading-for-help.6901/post-85097>

### caroline — 2013-04-29T11:34:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-adrenal-fatigue-pleading-for-help.6901/post-85102>

I am pretty sure Dr. K. would say not to fast and no exercise except for maybe light walking.
Please read the leptin R/X and implement that - that is pretty easy to do - but please follow it to the letter. Dr. K. explains the no exercise etc.
Next - read the C/T blog and slowly start implementing that - just baby steps at first.
Are you still eating wheat? sugar?
You need to be eating epi-paleo ....lots and lots of seafood - with raw oysters at the top of the list.

As far as testing .... see what your Docs can do for you as far as what Pattycakes suggested above. Here in Australia - I can get a lot of them but have to pay for a few such as rT3 and IGF-1. I order a saliva panel from ZRT labs in the U.S. to get hormones levels and 4xdiurnal cortisol and DHEA. I have no idea what you can get in the UK.
Once you have that done you can see where you are at and then go from there.

I would say that the fast you did was really bad as evidenced by your crash. A 4x cortisol saliva test will tell you so much about your adrenals and you want to protect them at all cost.

I think if you read the leptin R/X it will explain alot to you. Definitely don't underestimate the fake lite situation - that was the biggest piece of the puzzle for me and of course EMF's.
After sunset - I don't turn any lights on at all. About EMF's - alot of us have found that turning the breaker off to the bedroom has helped and get rid of your electric alarm clock ....mine was next to my head while I slept!

Sorry - I think this is sounding pretty disjointed! Alot of us have been following Dr. K. for about 2 years and I can see how it is so difficult for new people. Once you get some labs done - it gives you a starting point and a direction to go in as far as reading goes. I find now when I re read something from a year ago - I take much more of it on board - and as I have said - you at least have some background knowledge ....I don't have a scientific bone in my body..LOL

Just ask away - we have all been there. JK encourages questions and loves people that want to learn. This is a life long journey to wellness and it isn't always straightforward ...lots of twists and turns but you need to be fully engaged and 100% all in if you want results. There are some amazing posts here - I am thinking in particular some by Ivan Drago recently. If you have been lurking for awhile maybe you have seen them?
I will look up the thread heading if you want ... don't hesitate to ask......

### SeaHorse — 2013-04-29T14:43:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-adrenal-fatigue-pleading-for-help.6901/post-85111>

I just want to say Caroline that you have a great gift of enthusiasm and support for new members! I really appreciate it too.

Charles...something that made a lot of sense to me as I deal with adrenal fatigue is thinking about the time frame that you are working with. If you've had issues for years...six weeks in Thailand isn't going to turn the ship around despite being a good thing. Fasting can release a lot of toxins that then can stress you out further. Have you heard of the Herxheimer reaction that occurs with toxin release? Are you sure you are hydrated properly? That is a huge factor when detoxing and my understanding of hydration was pretty pathetic until a few months ago. The Iodine thread would be useful to look at too.

The epi-paleo diet is nourishing...it will feed your brain what it craves. Protect your brain from EMF, start regulating with natural light, rest your adrenals, really dive into CT and things will come back in line. I've spent the last year being very careful with exerting myself physically and I'm stronger have more stamina and have better body comp. than I've had for thirty years. I'll be testing my adrenals soon to compare with last years test...I'm confident things are moving in the right direction.

Sending all the best wishes for your recovery.

### Peter — 2013-05-02T18:08:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-adrenal-fatigue-pleading-for-help.6901/post-85311>

Dealing with severe â€˜adrenal fatigue syndromeâ€™ symptoms for about 8 months and recently having a relapse (heavy brain fog atm) I find the information here interesting. E.g. I feel an out of balance circadian rhytm (working at pc till 1-2 a.m. then 6 hours sleep) has attributed to the onset of my crash. Things like cutting back exposure to artificial (screen) light in the evening and getting more direct sunlight has eased symptoms for me. By reducing stress as much as possible, lots of rest, good food and supplements for months I recovered slowly, then had a (for me rather surprising)full relapse a couple weeks ago following relatively little stress .

I have no idea what this condition exactly is, If it would be mainly brain/signaling related instead of being primarily physical damage to the adrenal glands then I have some question.

Why does â€˜overtraing syndromeâ€™ (which afaict has large parallels with adrenal fatigue and also shows lowered cortisol levels) occur in athletes that train too hard/rest or eat too little?
Why do I crash hard after stimulating my adrenals with caffeine for a few days?
Wilson describes in his book that during autopsies in a flu epidemic in 1918 they observed physically damaged glands.

However I find it hard to visualize that these 5 gram glands hold any â€˜reserveâ€™ , and that after proper rest and nutrition to optimally support them (atleast what is consideredoptimal atm) for 6 months they get â€˜depletedâ€™ in days. Or would this be considered normal behavior for overtaxed/damaged organs?

Iâ€™m very interested in experiences of others who have experienced relapses of adrenal insufficiency/fatigue symptoms, how much stress was required, how long they rested and if recovery was faster than after initial onset.

Also I wonder if people have had any experiences of trying to balance neurotransmitter levels (according to their individual need) by â€˜naturalâ€™ supplementation or by taking e.g. SSRIâ€™s and what kind of effects this had on their stress response and symptoms.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-02T18:30:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-adrenal-fatigue-pleading-for-help.6901/post-85313>

Welcome Peter! Glad to have you with us.

> [Peter said:](/goto/post?id=85311)
>
> By reducing stress as much as possible, lots of rest, good food and supplements for months I recovered slowly, then had a (for me rather surprising)full relapse a couple weeks ago following relatively little stress .
>
> Stress isn't just emotional/mental. Anything your body doesn't like causes stress. Like inflammation, food, heat, EMF, noise, whatever.
>
> I have no idea what this condition exactly is, If it would be mainly brain/signaling related instead of being primarily physical damage to the adrenal glands then I have some question.
> ...
> However I find it hard to visualize that these 5 gram glands hold any ‘reserve’ , and that after proper rest and nutrition to optimally support them (atleast what is consideredoptimal atm) for 6 months they get ‘depleted’ in days. Or would this be considered normal behavior for overtaxed/damaged organs?
>
> I don't think it is a problem with the adrenal glands themselves.

I was suffering from some adrenal fatigue last year, to the point where I would be walking and with just a slight misstep, I would not be able to react fast enough catch myself. I would almost hit my nose on the ground, strangely enough. I remember thinking during the fall that it was too late to recover using my feet, so I just concentrated on not wiping out totally. This happened twice. I can't tell you which of Dr. K's protocols made the most difference, but now I can recover from a misstep without the knees, elbows, and hands getting involved. I can tell you that I never miss a sunrise, and if it is cloudy I just stare at the clouds in the morning.

It took me a year to get this improvement, but Dr. K was periodically introducing new protocols. I ran a lot of tests also. Perhaps progress would have been faster if I had access to all the protocols at one time. But that would have been a lot of changes to make in one step.

Peter...it might be helpful to read some of Dr. Kruse's blogs about how adrenal fatigue is not so much about the damage to the glands as it is a brain signalling issue. We can get really healthy and still mess up our signalling pretty quick with stressors like EMF, etc. I've had lots of improvement over the past year or so, but I've also had crashes that were tied to seasonal changes, exposure to toxins, gradually depleting stores of vit D, etc. Overall I know my adrenals are recovering and the crashes have been of much shorter duration. My stamina is vastly improved.

I've also spent a lot of time learning about neurotransmitters, medications, natural supplements etc. due to years of dealing with depression. All I can say is that by following the protocols outlined here, finding and pursuing my passions in life and learning to be curious instead of resentful about who I am has changed my neurotransmitters so profoundly that I don't really feel like the same person. I can remember what deep depression was like and I have had dips back down into mild depression, but my brain is so different now, it's like becoming somebody new. I can feel when my thinking starts to revert to old patterns and I know what I need to do to support myself towards a better path.

Adrenals are part of the whole picture that is shifting and changing all the time. Patience

### Peter — 2013-05-03T10:35:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-adrenal-fatigue-pleading-for-help.6901/post-85376>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=85313)
>
> I was suffering from some adrenal fatigue last year, to the point where I would be walking and with just a slight misstep, I would not be able to react fast enough catch myself.

This sounds very recognizable to me. The degree to which I am unsteady on my feet and experiencing something like dizziness when standing is usually a good indicator of the severity of my overall condition. I believe that it is also a function of the adrenal hormones to support coordination.

@SeaHorse, I donâ€™t look at anything black or white and think that this is a very complex condition involving a lot of complex systems and that you should look at the whole picture. In particular I think that the brain has a very big part in the onset. Things like depression and or bad signaling would increase stress perception, deregulate and tax systems, stress begets stress etc etc. However, when the condition has become full-fledged I think it would be the glands that form the main bottleneck because of the very slow recovery process. If the glands would remain fully capable of producing all the hormones I would expect different reactions from my body. Maybe it would be more recovery related, i.e. when the brain is out of balance it just drains energy from these systems without recovering. Anyway, probably looking way too simple at it, ill read more and thanks for your replies.

# Thread: Have i understood this correctly?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-understood-this-correctly.6904/>

### agatha — 2013-04-28T19:28:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-understood-this-correctly.6904/post-85018>

Hi guys
I've been re-reading the CT series and read in CT6 that in cold the hormone receptors become supersensitive - have I understood that correctly?

If so, does that mean that we don't need huge Vitamin D levels in the winter because the receptors are more sensitive? Is this why SAD is becoming more of a problem - we live warm adapted in winter but don't get our vitamin D because there's no sun. In the past people were exposed to cold and so their low Vit D levels didn't matter?

Also, does it mean that when cold adapted, symptoms of adrenal fatigue should diminish because the body is more sensitive to lower levels of DHEA and other hormones? Has anyone found this to be the case? I'm interested because I see people with adrenal fatigue as part of my job and it takes them months or years to recover. Is cold a more efficient way of recovering?

Am I understanding all this correctly?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-28T19:30:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-understood-this-correctly.6904/post-85019>

# Thread: Alarm clock
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alarm-clock.6909/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-04-28T22:46:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alarm-clock.6909/post-85038>

This may be a silly question, but what does everyone use for an alarm clock? I feel so much better with the alarm clock unplugged, but have been using my phone for the past couple of years. I'm not at the point where my internal alarm clock is working properly, so definitely need something for the morning. If an alarm clock that's plugged in isn't great, phones are out, I'm guess battery too. So what would one do?

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-04-28T22:58:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alarm-clock.6909/post-85040>

You can still purchase old-fashioned wind-up alarm clocks... look on Amazon. If you find you can't stand the ticking noise, then do what I do and get a battery powered lcd digital travel alarm and use that... just place it far from your head.

### cmegan — 2013-04-28T23:04:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alarm-clock.6909/post-85041>

I bought the "Big Ben" from LL Bean...battery run, no ticking noise, and it lights up for viewing only when you touch a button on top....

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-29T00:32:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alarm-clock.6909/post-85060>

none.........have not needed one in 7 yrs

### freesia — 2013-04-29T02:21:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alarm-clock.6909/post-85069>

> [JeffMacDougall said:](/goto/post?id=85040)
>
> You can still purchase old-fashioned wind-up alarm clocks... look on Amazon. If you find you can't stand the ticking noise, then do what I do and get a battery powered lcd digital travel alarm and use that... just place it far from your head.

We bought a vintage German wind up alarm clock. I wake around 6am without a clock but my OH needs an alarm esp for the times he has an early flight or whatever. Oh my.....it's a BEAST!! Some fine tuning and tinkering has made the ticking bearable but the alarm is enough to wake a whole village of Germans in the middle of Oktoberfest ..

### caroline — 2013-04-29T08:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alarm-clock.6909/post-85098>

I find I just generally wake up at the right time - but it does worry me if I have to be up really early and then I don't sleep as well. I will look into a windup one too ... thanks

### SeaHorse — 2013-04-29T14:26:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alarm-clock.6909/post-85106>

There is a really nice chime battery clock made by Now and Zen (ha ha) I'm getting up so early that it is just a backup, but because of school buses etc. I'm happy to have it.

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-04-29T15:05:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alarm-clock.6909/post-85118>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=85106)
>
> There is a really nice chime battery clock made by Now and Zen (ha ha) I'm getting up so early that it is just a backup, but because of school buses etc. I'm happy to have it.

Those look quite excellent...but I don't think I've ever paid that much for an alarm clock (or all the alarm clocks for that matter) in my life. Wow!

### SeaHorse — 2013-04-29T15:14:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alarm-clock.6909/post-85120>

Jeff....you're right they are very pricey. I bought mine about seven years ago and I hadn't realize how much they went up. I used to use it all the time and the chimes are really a pleasant way to wake up. Most of the battery or wind up alarms are too "alarming" for my nervous system first thing in the morning. Waking up before you need to is the best solution and the cheapest!

# Thread: Hypothyroid but always hot, why? plus other questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypothyroid-but-always-hot-why-plus-other-questions.6918/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bugsy1950@gmail.com — 2013-04-29T16:56:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypothyroid-but-always-hot-why-plus-other-questions.6918/post-85132>

When every one around me is freezing I am very comfortable in my T shirt! In summer I suffer from the heat, but don't perspire too much - this has been going on for many years.
For the last month I have been doing the LR but nothing much is happening, I still have lots of problems; falling sleep and staying asleep is one,
I cannot lose any weight no matter how hard I try, (and I have tried for years) very low energy, hypertension etc..
Unfortunately I live in South Africa and do not have access to many of the tests that are mentioned here, no saliva tests; just a few basic blood tests which do not tell very much, on the hormone side everything seems to be within range, except for my testosterone. (I am taking BHRT estradiol and progesterone.)
The biochemistry side is not good either, my LDL is way up high, my HDL and tryglicerides are low (low carbing for years). my HS CRP is 2.30 mg/L and homocystein 11.9 umol/L - not good!.
I am 62 YO female.
Can any one please help me please

### nonchalant — 2013-04-29T17:21:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypothyroid-but-always-hot-why-plus-other-questions.6918/post-85133>

Hi Bugsy. Normally your body takes the LDL cholesterol and makes hormones with it, with the help of thyroid. Your triglycerides sound great, but why is your HDL low? Do you eat animal meats and fats?

I think heat generation from high inflammation might make winter comfortable for you. I also am not cold, usually, but my thyroid is low. I take iodine to help my body make the thyroid hormone. I used to suffer in the summertime, but when I got my inflammation down, I was able to tolerate it better. When I started doing cold thermogenesis, I found that I was able to tolerate much colder temps in winter, and much warmer temps in summer.

As far as your sweating and sleeping, high blood pressure and low energy, I think you should look at what Dr. Kruse has been posting about this year. Drinking lots of non-fluoridated spring water, avoiding EMF, getting sunshine, grounding, and adding CT to the mix. Your body needs more electrons!

When you get healthier, you will lose the weight.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-29T17:54:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypothyroid-but-always-hot-why-plus-other-questions.6918/post-85139>

This is a case for a lab work up........until you understand the gist of the issue you shooting fish in a barrel

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-was-my-hypothyroid-thread-closed.6920/post-85184>

Hi Caroline,
I looked at canary club but the site is not working.

I can give my hormone status:
I found a few conversions.
Free T4 10.5 flag ref 7.2- 16.4 pmol/L
Free T3 5.3 " " 3.8 - 6.0 pmol/L
S-TSH 2.42 " " 0.37 - 3.50 mIU/L
FSH 17.5 IU/L
LH 14.8 IU/L
Estradiol E2 241 pmol/L = 65.650 mg/dl
T-testosterone 0.44 (12.68 mg/dl flag ref 0.22 - 2.9 nmol/L
Sex hormone bind globulin 38.3 " " 14.1 - 68.9 nmol/L
Free androgen index 1.2 " " 0.4 - 5.9
DHEAS 3.15 umol/L = 1.161 mg/dl " " 0.51 - 5.56 umol/L

Cortisol random 499 nmol/L at 9am = 18.09 mg/dl
( THEY ONLY TAKE ONE TEST a day and that is before 9am)

VIT D3 76.0 ng/mL
HS CRP 2.30 mg/L
P-Homocystein 11.9 flag ref 5.0 - 20.0 umol/L = 1.609 mg/dl

Hoping you can shed some light onto this
Thanks

### NittDan — 2013-04-30T16:27:50+0300

### caroline — 2013-05-01T00:55:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-was-my-hypothyroid-thread-closed.6920/post-85215>

sorry - I can't be of much help with labs - others that are much more knowledgeable hopefully will chime in.
your hsCRP is high - that indicates inflammation somewhere - should be below .1
maybe - tell us more about how you are feeling. I am going to go back and look at your original posts and get back to you.

okay .... you certainly have had some frustrations here - sorry!

Your leg cramp question to Jack was way too involved for "Ask Jack" Those would be things that you would discuss in a consultation .... remember that Dr. K. is not allowed to practise medicine on line. You can get help from forum members who may have similar problems.
If Jack does start doing paid for consults again .... you need to sign a waiver and you still need a partner Doc where you are to facilitate - write scripts etc.

So - you are doing the leptin reset? are you following it exactly? I would suggest no red wine for quite awhile until you get this under control. Are you doing C/T? that is important to lower inflammation.
Are you listening to the webinar? JK is very easy to listen to and understand. There is a special available where you can get all of them for 97.00.

Some Women don't lose weight on the leptin reset - it is more about fixing stuff that is broken - ie - hormones, thyroid. cortisol etc. At least you seem to be sleeping pretty good.

Water/ magnesium is huge for leg cramps and are you grounding? what about EMF's? are you limiting those?

this is alot of stuff I am throwing at you - I think if you read the leptin reset and C/T a couple of times and fully understand and then come back with questions.......

In a nutshell
Make sure you ground outside everyday - do you live near a lake, ocean? and limit EMF's - be very diligent about this
do the leptin reset exactly as written
Do C/T

no question - there is a ton of information here and the blogs can be difficult to get thru sometimes - but keep rereading them and it does get easier. Dr. K. is teaching us to be responsible for our own health. It is a very personal journey and what works for one isn't necessarily right for some one else. We are all tying to get to our own n=1
there are specific threads for thyroid etc.issues and the iodine thread has a huge amount of very valuable information.

pm me if you want......

### Lava — 2013-04-30T21:57:29+0300

I add unrefined cocoa butter for the scent! It smells like chocolate, yum. Also, cocoa (and shea) butter add extra emollient properties. It seems to absorb faster and leaves less of a "greasy" feel on the skin.

### curves64 — 2013-05-03T20:20:15+0300

And Gretchen, interesting suggestions in the earlier thread although I prefer the simplicity of these suggestions. I was interested though in the discussion about limited hair or body washing. Something to play with for sure.

Kathylu-I'm going to get some unrefined coco butter or shea butter to add to the mix because you are totally right -- the coconut oil takes a while to absorb and it's always that practical. Is the unrefined version something you can get easily at a local health food store or do you shop online for yours?

Thanks again everyone. This has been hugely helpful.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-07T22:03:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-suggestions-for-skin-care-especially-for-the-face-or-eyes.6928/post-85587>

I use the primal life organics line and LOVE it but its spendy. I waited for discounts to buy them. I'm sure I could make my own but I started the Oil Cleansing method at the height of my travel so convienance is key. I'm sure I'll spin off and create my own. I highly recommend checking out the line and seeing what oils and such she uses and perhaps trying to make your own.

One word of caution on Coconut Oil - it is a known pore clogger. Its also highly antimicrobic which can be very drying so isnt suitable for everyone. It can cause rashes, be incredibly drying, etc. The wash I use has coconut oil, but its dilluted with a bunch of other essential oils.

I love Teem Oil, Jojoba Oil etc. Great stuff!!!

There's a great blog called crunchybetty and she has some good recipes on there including a wonderful skincare 101 guide. Check it out! I kinda want to try her cellulite banisher. The secret ingredient is basically coffee grounds - leftover bulletproof coffee soap anyone?  I'm totally trying it once I get the molds (she uses oversize icecube trays). Honestly, I want to make my own everything, but I'm running out of storage space! #firstworldpaleoproblems

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-10T00:06:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-suggestions-for-skin-care-especially-for-the-face-or-eyes.6928/post-85763>

> how much CO do you put in your hair? do you put it on your scalp? I'm doing a psudeo "no-poo" routine - washing it on the weekends after being at the pool with miessence products... and I use baking soda in tea during the middle of the week. I'm also growing my hair out so I'm trying to get it to the point I can wash it once a week and have it look good, or pull it into a bun or topnot...
>
> any tips on the CO would be appreciated...

Unfortunately, I do not know much about using pure CO. Everything I use seems to have it in it, but its mixed with other things.

It just occured to me that this group WOULD LOVE my face wash. Its the ocean fash wash from primal life organics. It contains saponified coco oil, Dusle seaweed, pink himalyan salt among a few other things (rose hip oil, which is supposed to be great for anitaging)....its not really gritty at all and it cleans really well. Its epi-paleo facewash  I only use it once a day to take off make-up after my ride home (otherwise, I generally just use water)

I wish I had more space at my house so I could buy all the supplies oils etc I wanted to make my own, but if you go to her sight she kinda explains the benefits of each ingrediant. It might make a great jumping off point for anyone interested in making their own stuff.

Also, I kinda have to rave over my $12 beauty balm. Its going to last a long time and its incredible. I slather it on every night before bed and my skin is glowing. It contains Coconut Oil, fermented cod liver oil, high vitamin butter oil and then a few essential oils (lavendar and citrus maybe?). It smells more like butter (yum!) than anything else...its really not fishy. Its only suitable for bed though bc it takes a long while to sink in. Plus, you know, the smell...

I think it would make an incredible eye cream and have been slathering it on. Another good eye cream ingrediant to look into is Pomegranite Oil -its considered one of the more moisturizing ones.

Plus, I just wanted to clarify that Coconut Oil def works wonders for people, but I threw out the cuationary tale in case some have extremely sensitive skin. I think sometimes people try something that seems to work for everyone else, and then feel bad or like they did something wrong if they have problems with it. Sometimes its just the product!

### nonchalant — 2013-05-10T03:25:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-suggestions-for-skin-care-especially-for-the-face-or-eyes.6928/post-85768>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=85631)
>
> how much CO do you put in your hair? do you put it on your scalp? I'm doing a psudeo "no-poo" routine - washing it on the weekends after being at the pool with miessence products... and I use baking soda in tea during the middle of the week. I'm also growing my hair out so I'm trying to get it to the point I can wash it once a week and have it look good, or pull it into a bun or topnot...
>
> any tips on the CO would be appreciated...

Gretchen, I put in a very small amount of CO after washing my hair. I just get my fingers slightly oily on one side and touch them random spots on the last few inches of my damp hair. Once most of the CO is off my fingers I do run them through the rest of my hair a bit, getting some on my scalp. Then I let my hair dry, and brush. If I my hair isn't very wavy, I put a bit more in (at the hair ends) and brush again. That usually does it.

The trick is not to get too much CO in one spot, because then you will have an oily spot in your clean hair. Though I have found that a small oily area will eventually even out with the rest of my hair.

I have not had success using CO as a conditioner. It got quite static-y the one time I tried last year, in November.

### PrimalPam — 2013-05-12T04:53:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-suggestions-for-skin-care-especially-for-the-face-or-eyes.6928/post-85877>

I make these coconut lotion bars:

1/2 cup coconut oil
1/2 cup cocoa butter
2/3 cup beeswax pellets

Melt in double boiler, pour into molds (cupcake tins or silicone baking cups work great), let harden (about 20 minutes).

I use them for everything, body, face, hands, and as lip balm.

Still not sure if the beeswax is good or bad for you; the only information I can find is if it's not pure beeswax, it can have petrolatum in it, so just make sure it's pure.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-31T21:44:50+0300

# Thread: Confused!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused.6929/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Freddieboy — 2013-04-30T17:39:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused.6929/post-85194>

Hi

Not sure where to post this, so thought as it’s lab related, here would be best.

Recently I’ve been doing a lot of reading, books and internet articles on diets etc. with a view to lowering my ever increasing cholesterol, and trying to stop or reverse my slightly furred up carotid artery, see here:

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5689-Labs-in-worried](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5689-Labs-in-worried)!

I have read the Starch Solution, Eat to live, and various other books that believe a lower-fat, higher carb, plant based diet is the way to go for longevity and lowering cholesterol.

What interests me while reading these books, is they are the exact same format as the Primal Blueprint, the Paleo solution, Low carb living etc. whereby they frequently cite studies to back up their proposals to a healthy diet.
They are also littered with success stories throughout, as do the Paleo books, like ‘how I lost x pounds’ or ‘How I lowered my Cholesterol’ etc.

It’s the same with articles on the internet, they all contradict each other, some say high fat/low carb, some high carb/low fat, some in between. It makes your head spin trying to fathom out what’s the best way to eat!

So how does one decide which diet to choose?! I’ve been low carb/high fat for 3 years now, and something isn’t right with my cholesterol as high as it is, and with the furring of my artery, I’m worried!

I recently have gone lower fat, higher carbs, 150-200g p/day, in the form of sweet potatoes, some legumes and oats(both have been soaked overnight), and 3-4 pieces of fruit a day.
I’ve cut down on butter, eggs, cream and meat in general.
I have also up my veg intake considerably.

I know Jack recommends an Epi-paleo diet to help with my labs, but I’ve been following that sort of protocol for 3 years, so something’s not right. Maybe I’m one of the small percentage that doesn’t do well on high fat diets.

I’m now reading Nourishing Traditions, what are the views on this book?
Seems to be more of a balanced approach to diet, not favouring higher fat or carbs in particular.

Thanks

### beach bunny — 2013-05-01T00:21:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused.6929/post-85213>

Hi Freddie!

I know it's so confusing isn't it!! And then reading all the contradictory info can make you feel even more overwhelmed.
I am also trying to piece all this stuff together slowly...Reading and listening to some of JKs blogs and webinars helps a lot!
Maybe it's not necessarily the fuels you're using that are causing the rise in the Cholesterol?
But there are likely some underlying issues.
Here is an important point about cholesterol that is often missed:

\*WHEN THE BODY IS STRESSED/INFLAMED IT UP-REGULATES CHOLESTEROL PRODUCTION TO MAKE MORE LIFE SAVING **HORMONES**\- LDL NEEDS TO RISE TO **PROTECT** THE CELL FROM INFECTION/STRESS/INFLAMMATION\*\*

So our bodies actually protect our cells from damage by raising cholesterol!

Not sure if this helps, but reading the Hormones 101 blog can shed some light!

Hope you get answers soon!

### Dextery — 2013-05-01T01:19:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused.6929/post-85217>

Freddie,

Most of us on this forum that follow Dr. Kruse are not really worried about our higher than what the mainstream medical community thinks is acceptable.

People with higher total cholesterol live longer than those with less than 200.

What we do concern ourselves with is the particle size of our LDL cholesterol. There are two types of LDL, small dense LDL and Large bouyant LDL. The small dense particles are the problem. They flow through our vascular system and irritate the lining and the response to the irritation is for the body to come in and lay down plaque to heal the irritation. Over time, this happens over and over til the artery becomes blocked...resulting in a heart attack. We want our large bouyant particles to be at least 90% of our total LDL particles.

You can find out your ratio by getting an NMR or VAP blood test through your doctor or there are various online companies that can do it for you.

I suspect your ratio is going to be close to ok since you have been paleo...epi paleo for quite a while. People with bad ratios eat Standard American Diet. (SAD)

Watch this video which explains a lot. [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0\_7u7aPkUQ0](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0_7u7aPkUQ0)

Stay with the EPI Paleo RX eating program and you will do just fine.

Keep reading this forum, delve into Dr Kruse's blog postings. You have come to the right place.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-02T00:54:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85262>

I'm glad I did. At Chaos's 5yr apt I asked to have her thyroid levels checked. She's got something going on w/her thyroid - now we're going to have to figure out what we're going to do for treatment... not sure I want to wait another 2 mths to run labs...

here are her lab results

TSH 6.86 uiU/ML (.45-4.5 uiU/ML)
Free T4 1.34 ng/dL (.82-1.77ng/dL)
Free T3 3.6pg/mL (2.0-4.4pg/mL)
Reverse T3 23.4ng/dl (8.3-22.9 ng/dl)
Free T3:RT3 ratio 15.1

DHEA-S 4.9ug/dL (2.8-85ug/dL)

follow your instincts... you know your kids better than your doctor!

### bigknitwit — 2013-05-02T01:38:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85264>

As you may remember, 2 of my children were diagnosed with auto-immune thyroid issues, both at age 3. You need to ask/demand thyroid antibody testing, as this will really help direct your course of action. Another one of mine has presented with TSH levels as high as your DD's, but no auto-immune. Her FT3 levels look pretty reasonable, and her FT4 levels as well. Is she symptomatic? My hypo-thyroid young children present as the perpetual tired/cranky/hungry child, despite adequate sleep/food/etc.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-02T02:45:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85268>

AB test does sound like a good idea. How about a spot iodine test?

### caroline — 2013-05-02T02:56:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85269>

I had a young Doc 30 years ago - when our kids were little and he always trusted my instincts completely ..... he always told me I knew what was going on with them way more than he did. He moved his family to the U.S. but we saw him often socially when he came back to visit friends. I would always tell him that I had thought all Docs were like him and he always told me that he had thought all patients would be like me .... how wrong we both were!
At the time - he even thought Atkins was a great idea! ...way ahead of his time...I really missed him for years and years.....
still do actually....

### cantweight — 2013-05-02T05:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85281>

At 6 and 8yrs old my son and daughter were hypothyroid. They both took Cytomel, TG 100 - natural glandular tissue supplement, and slew of other supps.

It was only about 3-6 mos and they were off the cytomel just stayed on the TG 100 and supps for an additional few months. Thyroid function has been fine since

My daughter was always cold, and had similar mood swings to what you mentioned Gretchen. I was also diagnosed bi-polar so I thought...she was gonna be crazy like mama...lol
Little did I know I wasn't crazy at all  My son had low temp. Both had extreme fatigue...extreme. People always commented how well behaved my children were....they just had mad fatigue!

Way to be a kick ass mama!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-02T05:49:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85283>

a poignant thought.........maybe none of you want to think about. Why would a child become hypothyroid in this world so fast? Have you thought about it........forget the emotions. Do you believe evolution works in this fashion? Of is something else altering the rules of the game of life. No answers needed.......I'd rather you think about it......

Do you mind your "mindset"? You need to control the manner you in which you think? At night I look back at my day and contemplate what I did and how I thought about what I did. There are different kinds of mindsets... a millionaire mindset... a creative genius mindset... a growth mindset. These are very specific and each have their own area of focus. A growth mindset combines a love of learning with the cultivation of our talents and gifts. It also combines hard work and dedication with the desire to keep exploring our true potential. What kind of mindset you keep will determine what you can become. Tonight before you sleep, use your thoughts to determine your future. Plan a new course with your dreams tonight; we don't have to think better. We don't have to think smarter, but we must "think" differently to get better. Ask yourself: What am I thinking about now, and change it?

### Inger — 2013-05-02T07:56:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85285>

Wow. Nice stuff. I love mamas with great instincts. That have the courage to follow them. And I love what Jack write above.. about our thoughts. Very powerful and edifying!

### bigknitwit — 2013-05-02T15:39:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85297>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=85270)
>
> she complains of being cold - in heat, and hot in cold. her hands and feet are constantly cold. she's has these mood swings that come out of normal - reminds me of when I was dx w/bi-polar. after recently learning from my PCP that undiagnosed hyperthyroid can be misdiagnosed as bipolar - I'm thinking those mood swings were the driving factor for the testing... b/c they weren't normal 5yo behavior... kwim?
>
>
>
> AB is next up... as is geting in w/a pediatric endo good call on the spot iodine test - we have been supplmenting both kids with 2%lugols...

It's so frustrating when the doctor says to "do nothing" and repeat the test in 2 months. I would definitely push for the anti-body testing, preferably done by labcorp as they give an absolute number, even when it's below the "positive" cut off line. Quest only gives you an absolute number if you're above the positive.

The mood swings that seem to come from nowhere is something I've seen from my 2, but of course there's so many reasons it's hard to discern more. They would come out of nowhere and so very difficult to deal with. I always said that I could always, with enough force/determination control the behavior of the child, but I can not control mood, and it's the mood that's so upsetting, because of course we all want to see our children generally content in their own skin.

My oldest's TSH was as high as 7ish at age 6, negative antibodies. I took him to 3 endocrinologists, including the head of pediatric endocrinology at Children's Hospital in Boston. Nobody would touch him. It was very frustrating, to say the least. The gluten free diet helped bring it down to the 3ish range (which is where he and my other non-AI DD seem to sit). I think I remember someone telling me it was somewhat "normal" for children's TSH to run a little higher than adults. I'm not sure I believe that, but I'm just passing it along as it's something you may hear and need to be prepared for...

Also, I can say that my experience dealing with ped. endos for my auto-immune son was not good. She kept his TSH as high in the range as possible - like 4.5 was fine ("to stimulate his own gland as much as possible"), which left him horrendously symptomatic, but I didn't know as much back then. The final straw was 2 years into treatment when I asked for a celiac screen (having read that one auto-immune = more likely others), and there it was, he railed to celiac marker test. I was so angry... I have had much better luck with a doctor of environmental medicine, as well as a regular family practionner. I recommend looking at the thyroid "top doc" list done by STTM, listing doctors patients have had good experiences with, including docs willing to prescribe NDT. It's a good starting point at least.

I'm not sure that any of this helps, or gives you more insight than you already have. All my kids get a full thyroid panel done annually, you may want to do a screen on your younger child as well. If nothing else, it gives you a baseline.

> Also, I can say that my experience dealing with ped. endos for my auto-immune son was not good. She kept his TSH as high in the range as possible - like 4.5 was fine ("to stimulate his own gland as much as possible"), which left him horrendously symptomatic, but I didn't know as much back then. The final straw was 2 years into treatment when I asked for a celiac screen (having read that one auto-immune = more likely others), and there it was, he railed to celiac marker test. I was so angry... I have had much better luck with a doctor of environmental medicine, as well as a regular family practionner. I recommend looking at the thyroid "top doc" list done by STTM, listing doctors patients have had good experiences with, including docs willing to prescribe NDT. It's a good starting point at least.
>
> I'm not sure that any of this helps, or gives you more insight than you already have. All my kids get a full thyroid panel done annually, you may want to do a screen on your younger child as well. If nothing else, it gives you a baseline.

thanks... we held off on screening Havoc b/c he's not showing symptoms or the crazy mood swings. though once we find a good endo we will consider it.

I've got 3 endos we're looking at, and I'm also getting references from friends who have kids that are seeing endos. so far I've received a warning to stay a way from one at Childern's National Medical Center. I'm also considering taking DD to see my PCP - as she treats kids. I may just send her an email - and see if she's treated hypo kids before. even if she doesn't treat kids for thyroid - she might have a good recommendation. NDT will be our only option medication wise.

What worries me about DD is the fact that we're both gluten and grain free - she knows she shouldn't be eating things like cookies/crackers etc. so I worry what has happened is that my un-diagnosed hypothyroidism has been epigenetically transferred to dd. Her labs pretty much resemble mine when I'm not on meds... with the exception that she has symptoms, and I don't.

Thanks for the heads up on the Higher TSH - definitely going to do some research on this. Since we have UHC we can only get our lab work done via Labcorp. so that's a positive.

one thing I'm thinking is that looking at her labs, and her DHEA-S levels - she's got some gut issues going on. going to have to work on fixing that for her as well.

# Thread: I recieved 2 CT scans, Am I cooked?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Arclite — 2013-05-02T20:09:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85326>

Monday morning I was stabbed twice by a mentally ill meth addict, once in the lower back on the spine, and once on the ribs, about four inches below my armpit. I was able to fend off the attack and subdue him until the police arrived. I was taken to the hospital by ambulance where I was promptly told I would undergo a CT scan to check for internal injuries. At this point the nurses, doctors and paramedics who were treating me had not discovered the wound on my ribs, the deeper and more dangerous of the two. Adrenaline from the fight was still in my veins an I had no sensation of pain. I was carted into the CT room, but at the last moment protested and refused to undergo the procedure for fear of recieving a massive amounts of radiation to my abdomen and testes. the techs and nurses looked at me in stunned disbelief. The head nurse came to assuage my fears and told me I would not recieve any more radiation than I would get in a day out in the sun. Hesitatatingly, I agreed after his reassurance. Mistake. He hwas several orders of magnitude short of the true danger. after the scan was complete I went to take a piss, and while looking at my wound in the mirror discovered the other wound on my side. It had been giving me a bit of pain but I chalked it up to soreness from the altercation which ensued after the stabbing. I grabbed the MD and showed him the wound and he ordered another CT, which he downplayed as well as being far less iradiating than an x-ray.
The wounds were only 1.5CM deep and not near any vitals. The only competent care I recieved was the suturting. I am certain The hospital damaged me far more than my assailant, those wounds will heal in weeks, but the radiation is permanant. Every member of the staff from the technicians,to the nurses and up to the MD were totally ignorant about the dangers of CT scans. I can't say they lied to me, I really believe they are just morons, well-meaning, but _extremely_ dangerous morons. The doctor told me lighheartedly of an infant who has recieved over 25 CT scans. I shudder to think of the implications for that child. The CT has become a substitute for competent medical care. Why did I trust those dumbfucks who couldn't even find the second stab wound when they had worked on me for hours, who treated me as a piece of meat, like an inanimate object? Two hiroshimas worth of radiation for two lacerations, what a fucking mess healthcare is in this country.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-02T20:52:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85329>

you did not consent to these and they still did them?

Oh, Arclite, so sorry to hear of this incident. I can imagine you were quite shocked at the time.
Dr. Kruse talks about what he does for long airplane trips, perhaps that will help? For mitochondrial support/repair? Ubiquinol and vitamin D and such?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-03T03:01:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85358>

The sun adds photons while ionizing radiation depletes us of them.........this is why life evolved in the sun and no next to a quasar........This is why people call BS on us. When you do not know just admit it.

### Arclite — 2013-05-03T08:05:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85366>

I feel totally despondent. I interrogated the radiology staff at the hospital this afternoon but they spouted such absurd nonsense. The first woman they sent me told me that the dose of radiation I received was beneficial; that we humans require some radiation, and that our government would not allow these machines in hospitals if they weren't completely safe. Yikes. The second gentleman was more knowledgeable and pleasant, but still far from able to satisfactorily answer my questions. My uncle is a radiologist so I am going to talk to him tomorrow and have him explain my dose chart in plainer terms than mGy-Cm, I fear though he is too heavily invested in the field to give me an objective response. I should have gone to a goddamn veterinarian.

### caroline — 2013-05-03T09:50:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85372>

We need to - somehow- evaluate our situation for ourself and put the brakes on anything they are trying to talk us into - at least until we have a clearer picture ....or are unconscious!

### Arclite — 2013-05-04T02:44:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85407>

78 mGy or 7.8 Rad. Jesus, thats likely as much radiation than I have absorbed in my entire 24 year life, all to my balls and abdomen. I told them I didn't want to do it! Radiologist uncle has a grim sense of humor that has made me feel even worse. The answer I continue to receive is "we must weigh benefits with risks" They don't understand the risks. Not even a 30 year radiologist does. Only you guys do. they look at me like Im not right in the head, like what kind of person asks so many questions? God I hate them.

I said at last we agree on something...this is my last visit.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-19T19:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-86474>
