### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-19T23:07:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-86488>

Xrays are high energy EMF's. The photoelectric effect dictates what happens here. Nature's laws say if you shine light or ultraviolet light on a piece of metal, the light can knock electrons out of the metal. Back in the early 20th century, the photoelectric effect made science uncomfortable because their experiments could not show this to be true. The wave theory of light suggested as you turned up the brightness of the light, you should eject more electrons, because brighter light has more energy in it. But scientists had trouble with these ideas was because in their experiments this did not happen in their labs. If you shine light onto a piece of an element or group of elements like an amalgam, no electrons were ejected, no matter how bright the light was made. But when you changed the light to different color like violet, you could turn it down real low and still electrons will be ejected. The key is not the color.....it is the frequency of the wavelength. Since X-rays are very powerful because of its wavelength it is considered and ionizing form of EMF........and that means it has the power to eject a ton of electrons from the amalgam that can cause more damage. So this is why if you have a lot of metal in your body for any reason at all......X rays are more dangerous for you than people without metal in their body. Soon you will all be learning a lot more about this......as the QB series develops.

# Thread: Article: Cell's Response to Electricity Explained
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-cells-response-to-electricity-explained.6942/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jane — 2013-05-02T21:07:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-cells-response-to-electricity-explained.6942/post-85331>

My DH has been giving me a hard time and pooh-poohing the effects of EMF on the body until he read this article in our local newspaper yesterday.

MAY 1, 2013 MERCED SUN-STAR

CELLâ€™S RESPONSE TO ELECTRICITY EXPLAINED

An exacting nanosecond jolt of electricity can briefly open a cellâ€™s wall, allowing for the delivery of drugs or DNA. This procedureâ€”called electroporationâ€”is widely used in biology, biotechnology and medicine, including cancer treatments.

Why the cellâ€™s wall opens, however, has remained a mystery until now.

UC Merced applied math Professor Mayya Tokman and her collaboraters have figured out what is going on at the nanoscale and recently published their findings in PLOS ONE. The open-access peer-reviewed journal is part of the Public Library of Science, a nonprofit public publisherâ€¦.

Using complex computer models, the research team discovered that the electric charge causes water molecules to rearrange themselves to create a water bridge across the cellâ€™s wall, connecting the inside and the outside of a cell.

â€œItâ€™s such a basic thingâ€”waterâ€”that controls this process,â€ Tokman said. â€œItâ€™s interesting that this is happening with the cells that make up our bodies or any other biological tissue.â€

In the past, researchers believed electricity might cause lipid molecules that make up the cell wall to move and create holes, but had little evidence to show it. Additionally, a cell wall has many components, including nanomoters and ion channels.

â€œItâ€™s very difficult to figure out whatâ€™s going on,â€ Tokman said. â€œThe cell membrane is a zoo, plus the creation of holes in the cell wall cannot be observed directly through a microscope because the openings are too small.â€

In fact, the only way researchers know a cell membrane opens is by exposing the cell to dyes that fluoresce only when they come in contact with chemicals inside the cell.

Experimental researchers do not have a way to determine the strength and the duration of electric pulses, which is needed to control the quantity and the size of the openings in a cell wall.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-02T21:46:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-cells-response-to-electricity-explained.6942/post-85334>

Jane, glad your hubby is beginning to understand.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-03T02:57:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-cells-response-to-electricity-explained.6942/post-85357>

Water is the huge key to how biology really works.........it allows the amazing quantum effects and many new strange concepts that you are beginning to take into account now as your read the blog, including Superposition, Entanglement, Complementarity, Duality, Uncertainty , Exclusion, Decoherence, Ehrenfest theorem, Tunnelling, and Non-locality.

Most of the work in the Quantum biology field seems to have been originated by physicists and mathematicians rather than by biologists. This is not surprising given the complex mathematical training required to be fluent in matters related to quantum mechanics. Quantum biology suffers from one severe problem: mainly that biology is a massively complex system whereas quantum mechanics is massively deep. Quantum mechanics is the body of scientific principles which attempts to explain the behavior of matter and its interactions with energy on the scale of atoms and atomic particles. — Just before 1900, it became clear that classical physics was unable to model certain phenomena. Coming to terms with these limitations led to the development of quantum mechanics, a major revolution in physics — Some aspects of quantum mechanics can seem counter-intuitive, because they describe behavior quite different than that seen at larger length scales, where classical physics is an excellent approximation. In the words of Richard Feynman, quantum mechanics deals with “nature as she is — absurd."

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2nqHOnVTxJE&feature=relmfu](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2nqHOnVTxJE&feature=relmfu)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Introduction\_to\_quantum\_mechanics](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Introduction_to_quantum_mechanics)

Hi Dr. K:

While watching the PPP webinar, I sat bolt upright as you touched on the emerging spectrum of diseases facing our children. My son (6) has EE. Now, I will not bog you down with all the details of his particular case, but since I follow this blog and believe in the direction of where it takes us, could you please share a couple of high level actions I might be able to take with regards his diet. Recently, his night terrors have become more pronounced. It used to be an outlier symptom now it is more frequent.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-03T02:48:28+0300

My buddy got diagnosed at 25 and he just paid the price when his wife's pregnancy with twins had one die and the other is still in the hospital 7 months later........I warned them........but of course I was just an alarmist then. ### PrimalPam — 2013-05-12T04:41:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eosinophilic-esophogitis.6943/post-85873>

A few years ago, I was diagnosed with EE via an Upper Endoscopy. My main symptom was food that would not go down or come back up and terrible GERD. I was prescribed "Prilosec for life", and not happy with that diagnosis (which was cascading upon many other diagnoses other drs were giving me), I took to the internet, and found my way here.

This thread just made me realize: I haven't had a food swallowing issue, and barely a GERD issue, for over a year. EpiPaleo it is. (and the CT has to be helping too).

I know it must be a difficult thought, removing grains/sugar/dairy from your 6 year-old's diet and replacing with seafood, broccoli, kale, coconut oil. It was for me too, but whenever I veer off the wagon, the EE/GERD symptoms return immediately.

Although there are so many other things here at play: EMF, water, etc.

### Dextery — 2013-05-04T04:06:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-flow-in-fingers-predicts-future-propensity-for-a-heart-attack.6961/post-85420>

[http://losangeles.cbslocal.com/2013/05/03/medical-test-uses-fingers-to-predict-heart-disease/](http://losangeles.cbslocal.com/2013/05/03/medical-test-uses-fingers-to-predict-heart-disease/)

The researchers attach blood flow measuring devices on the fingers of both hands and then they restrict flow in one arm for 5 minutes and release the flow and measure how well the blood flows. In healthy folks, the flow is very rapid and in folks with endothelial problems, the flow only goes back to normal...not a high rush. Endothelial dysfunction is seen in 30 year olds by this predictor of heart disease 7 years into the future.

I seem to recall a therapy for heart disease patients involved putting blood pressure cuff devices all over the body to force the heart to work harder to pump blood through the vascular system...thus strengthening the heart muscle.

It may have been a therapy to increase blood flow to the brain. Too many years and to much floridated water.

Perhaps Dr K can relate the flow rate test with treating already diseased patients.

# Thread: Cold receptors and systemic inflammation in the colon
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-receptors-and-systemic-inflammation-in-the-colon.6974/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### agatha — 2013-05-04T23:15:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-receptors-and-systemic-inflammation-in-the-colon.6974/post-85459>

This is an interesting study - yes, I know it's in mice butr we have to start somewhere..

**TRPM8 activation attenuates inflammatory responses in mouse models of colitis**
Rithwik Ramachandrana et al 2013

Abstract

Transient Receptor Potential Melastatinâ€“8 (TRPM8), a recently identified member of the transient receptor potential (TRP) family of ion channels, is activated by mild cooling and by chemical compounds such as the supercooling agent, icilin. Since cooling, possibly involving TRPM8 stimulation, diminishes injury-induced peripheral inflammation, we hypothesized that TRPM8 activation may also attenuate systemic inflammation. We thus studied the involvement of TRPM8 in regulating colonic inflammation using two mouse models of chemically induced colitis. TRPM8 expression, localized immunohistochemically in transgenic TRPM8GFP mouse colon, was up-regulated in both human- and murine-inflamed colon samples, as measured by real-time PCR. Wild-type mice (but not TRPM8-nulls) treated systemically with the TRPM8 agonist, icilin showed an attenuation of chemically induced colitis, as reflected by a decrease in macroscopic and microscopic damage scores, bowel thickness, and myeloperoxidase activity compared with untreated animals. Furthermore, icilin treatment reduced the 2,4,6-trinitrobenzenesulfonic acidâ€“induced increase in levels of inflammatory cytokines and chemokines in the colon. In comparison with wild-type mice, Dextran Sodium Sulfate (DSS)-treated TRPM8 knockout mice showed elevated colonic levels of the inflammatory neuropeptide calcitonin-geneâ€“related peptide, although inflammatory indices were equivalent for both groups. Further, TRPM8 activation by icilin blocked capsaicin-triggered calcitonin-geneâ€“related peptide release from colon tissue ex vivo and blocked capsaicin-triggered calcium signaling in Transient Receptor Potential Vaniloid-1 (TRPV1) and TRPM8 transfected HEK cells. Our data document an anti-inflammatory role for TRPM8 activation, in part due to an inhibiton of neuropeptide release, pointing to a novel therapeutic target for colitis and other inflammatory diseases.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-05T04:35:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-receptors-and-systemic-inflammation-in-the-colon.6974/post-85468>

good news for me and Amgen......

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-06T05:14:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electron-deficiency-might-be-an-underlying-factor-in-most-chronic-disease.6977/post-85499>

everyone must make a living........but being authentic doing it is the key point. Mercola has to go with the hot hand but he has 55 employees that scour the net looking for content to "leverage". To me you need to have a clarity of purpose to gain people's thoughts and not their wallets. Without a true vision the people will perish, and without personal reflection, there is no clarity of purpose. There is a loss of passion........Some of the most important moments of clarity in life might have come from our internal reflection. Some call them epiphanies, moments of truth, or deeper levels of awareness. These defining moments can put all the other moments of our life into context.

If Mercola can spread the CORRECT clarity of the message than I am happy about it. We must provide our species with this clarity to make our way. We must aspire to be a great humanitarians, that should be our single focus. I look at Mercola as a cog in that great wheel.
i choose to focus on the good he does and not the bad. We must strive to
be love to others regardless of our differences, we must become as compassionate and understanding as possible in this journey.

Many people in the paleosphere lack this focus and clarity in my view. People who lack the clarity, generally lack a purpose. Therefore, they lack courage and the determination because they don't have a dream, a purpose they they could follow. People with no clarity often find ways to discourage others who have dreams, who have a purpose.

Often frustration is caused in our minds due to lack of clarity. It is the clarity of purpose that makes our lives exciting, it's clarity that fills our minds and hearts with passion. This is the missing piece of the puzzle of life!

The best way to succeed in anything in life is to have a specific purpose, a clear vision, a plan of action, and the determination to make it happen.

Some such moments of clarity have awakened me to what I can become, but my greatest moment of clarity, so far, **is the realization of what I need to BE**.

**_Not what others think I should be, not what I think others want me to be, but what I can truly be._** Do not worry about Joe.........worry about you and your vision for transformation. Then pay it forward in your own way. Life has a way of paying you back when you pay it the homage it deserves.

# Thread: Having difficulty finding info on being underweight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TribalSpice — 2013-05-07T09:54:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85558>

I am trying to find the link between LR and being underweight. How does that work (I have been scouring the blog, but have come up with nothing to explain it)?

I understand low WAT would = less Leptin produced when eating, but how does this not convert to storing fat and making more WAT? If someone who is better at the search function than me help, I would really appreciate it.

### Jonathin — 2013-05-07T19:57:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85576>

I have been tall and thin my whole life (58yrs). I think you might want to frame your inquiry differently. Being underweight is more than not having much fat. Becoming leptin sensitive for someone like us might mean that our appetite might need increase along with anabolism. Try high intensity weights if you have energy. How can we build tissue if digestion and hormones are poor? LRx is the start. Adjusting psychological behavior to a more claim or balanced mode rather than indulging in optional stressful states might be beneficial. I am just throwing out some ideas that may or may not apply to you.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-07T20:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85585>

I believe Jack summarized it when he said the brain does not count calories; it counts electrons, and if someone's electron counter is broken it means leptin signaling problems.
The "satiety" switch in underweight people may turn off too soon among other things.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-07T21:56:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85586>

correct.......underweight implies slow movement across the mitochondrial membrane or a lot of uncoupling occurring for some reason.

### TribalSpice — 2013-05-08T02:05:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85602>

So, how does this relate to rT3 levels?

For example, my BIL is 6'6 and built like a broom handle. He has high rT3 and is on an epi-paleo diet and doing CT. He is noticing big changes in his health and well being, but not yet putting on much weight. I am trying to workout how someone has high rT3, but low body fat. Would someone normally who was skinny with LR have high T3, but mitochondrial issues?

Thanks

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-08T04:58:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85618>

Stress! I'd bet his high rT3 is related to pregnenolone steal, and an altered cortisol pattern from stress ... ie mismatches in his circadian cycle

### caroline — 2013-05-09T04:22:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85696>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=85688)
>
> I'd take some, Caroline !

Thanks destiny ...... But I want my cortisol or progesterone to want it....but apparently they are turning up their nose at it!

### Destiny — 2013-05-10T14:09:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85784>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=85696)
>
> Thanks destiny ...... But I want my cortisol or progesterone to want it....but apparently they are turning up their nose at it!

Of course, you do, Caroline! I hope you can figure out soon why it is not converting.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-10T16:06:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85787>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=85676)
>
> Okay - teacher's Pet - explain to me why I have high rT3 and high pregnenolone? It seems like no one wants my pregnenolone.......

T3 is used in two ways if I remember correctly - pathway #1 LDL + Vitamin A + T3 = pregnenolnone -> all steroid hormones (cortisol & progesterone, and everything else on down to Vit D) pathway#2 is the T3 for regulation cell metabolism (mitochondria any one???); so your high rT3 is blocking the receptor sites at the cellular level, causing increased in-efficiency at the mitochondria, and more leakiness...

If you're making Pregnenolone, that's a good thing (especially if your not supplementing) b/c that means pathway #1 is working properly. YET you still have HIGH rT3... so what's driving that? you may still have a cortisol problem - due to circadian mismatches - this stuff doesn't fix itself in 6mths - when you've had years of artifical light, emf, SAD etc. it will take time...

so - you've got to figure out whats driving the High rT3, fix that and then your hormone cascade should start fall into place.... if it only was that easy right???

### TribalSpice — 2013-05-11T05:28:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85826>

Thanks, that is awesome

### MeghanK — 2013-08-06T18:17:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-91635>

# Thread: BHRT Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-question.6989/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-07T23:48:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-question.6989/post-85593>

For those on BHRT...

Do you find that there is usually an adjustment period as you change your dose or add soemthing to your routine?

I just started back on a little of P and E (for the 10 days after menses completion)...just .2 or each...and have started getting a little bloated in the stomach area. Since I'm literally on day 2 or 3, I dont know if I should jump the gun and assume that there is something wrong, or if I should expect that there is some time needed for my body to adjust and accept it. i will be updating Dzugan soon but just wanted some feedback.

Or if you do BHRT and never really have any sort of negative re-action, perhaps I'm in the minority...

Thank you!

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-08T00:13:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-question.6989/post-85596>

> [nuttmegs17 said:](/goto/post?id=85593)
>
> For those on BHRT...
>
> Do you find that there is usually an adjustment period as you change your dose or add soemthing to your routine?
>
> I just started back on a little of P and E (for the 10 days after menses completion)...just .2 or each...and have started getting a little bloated in the stomach area. Since I'm literally on day 2 or 3, I dont know if I should jump the gun and assume that there is something wrong, or if I should expect that there is some time needed for my body to adjust and accept it. i will be updating Dzugan soon but just wanted some feedback.
>
>
> Or if you do BHRT and never really have any sort of negative re-action, perhaps I'm in the minority...
>
> Thank you!

I've seen positive results when I make changes such as increasing dosages - ie migraine goes away... but I do think for some people it can take a couple of days. Still reading Reissi's book and he mentions that in women who are peri-menopausal and younger women who aren't it can take a couple of days for their systems to balance out... I'd keep taking notes/journaling and update dzugan ever 3-5days...

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-08T00:22:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-question.6989/post-85597>

I'd keep taking notes/journaling and update dzugan ever 3-5days...

Thanks! Good to know..I'll wait it out a bit and see...I have given them an update and will continue to update them prob every 3 days (as it seems to take at least as many days to get a response/update from them).

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-08T21:47:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-question.6989/post-85663>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=85620)

> It takes about 3-4 days to get an update... follow your instinct and tell him what you're doing...

right on - I just did that and am folloiwng my instincts....slow and low for me I think. Hopefully it will eventually get me where I need to go. I'll be intersted to see if my stomach is still as bloated/upset as it was at the end of the day yesterday. In the morning I'm fine, its only around 4pm when i start to get pressure and a pop-out...I have also been a bit tired, but its different than the Progesterone tired I used to get. Before, I'd get super DRY eyes and feel like a zombie...now I just feel like I didn't sleep enough. Its been harder to wake up...I think I'll adjust but its intersting how much hormones can affect you.

New plan is to add the trie-est from days 5 (after my period ends) -14...thinking .1 or so...then reduce to maybe half that and add in a SMALL amount of P...see how I do with that and go from there.

Granted - I realize that is such a small amount as to not even be beneficial, yet clearly it does SOMETHING bc any more than that and I re-act.

I'm kinda annoyed bc generally THIS is my good time of the month...days 1-14 have always been my best (yes, when I am low in everything including P go figure). I realize P doesnt generally kick in until ovultaion so I'm just trying to mimic that. we shall see

Edited to add: i think I may be slightly more hungry on it as well...I tend to not be very snacky during this time of the month (I can take chocolate or leave it for instance), but the last few nights, I've definitely found myself thinking of food more often...hoping this is temporary?

### diane — 2013-05-09T21:58:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-question.6989/post-85750>

One thing I took away from Dr. Hertoghe's book is that if you are on a dose that's too small, the systems are exacerbated. He was talking about thyroid specifically, but wonder if it's the same as the other hormones, and if there is an opportunity to increase. I think our instinct is to back off when we feel something different, when maybe that's the signal to go higher.

### Dali Dula — 2013-05-08T14:15:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educating-myself.6991/post-85624>

You could start with June 2011 and read all of them in order. I am in the process of doing that now as I was not on board until sept. 2011 and did not read much of the earlier blogs. The Quilt should be read as it is the template. As your knowledge increases you should re-read things because the first time through, unless you're a super genius, all this info will not be absorbed. I would read the leptin blogs to give you a basic understanding of the ancient pathway. Prepare to be a profoundly changed human.

# Thread: You heard it here first. Brain gut connection.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-heard-it-here-first-brain-gut-connection.6992/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2013-05-08T14:40:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-heard-it-here-first-brain-gut-connection.6992/post-85625>

Check this out.

[http://www.scientificamerican.com/article.cfm?id=gut-second-brain](http://www.scientificamerican.com/article.cfm?id=gut-second-brain)

### nonchalant — 2013-05-08T16:37:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-heard-it-here-first-brain-gut-connection.6992/post-85632>

Thanks, Dali, for pointing out this great 2010 article. ;-)

"Technically known as the enteric nervous system, the second brain consists of sheaths of neurons embedded in the walls of the long tube of our gut, or alimentary canal, which measures about nine meters end to end from the esophagus to the anus. The second brain contains some 100 million neurons, more than in either the spinal cord or the peripheral nervous system, Gershon says."

It really is "expensive tissue". Guess that is why Dr. Kruse says 'no snacking, ever'. It's kinda like firing up an incinerator to cook a shrimp. Starting a washing machine to wash a sock. Driving a tank to the local grocery store. (I got a million of these...)

"For example, scientists were shocked to learn that about 90 percent of the fibers in the primary visceral nerve, the vagus, carry information from the gut to the brain and not the other way around."

Well, folks on this website wouldn't be shocked. The brain needs to know what foods are available now, and how it should respond.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-08T17:14:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-heard-it-here-first-brain-gut-connection.6992/post-85636>

when he no better you do better

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-08T17:18:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-heard-it-here-first-brain-gut-connection.6992/post-85637>

"It was totally unexpected that the gut would regulate bone mass to the extent that one could use this regulation to cure—at least in rodents—osteoporosis," says Gerard Karsenty, lead author of the study and chair of the Department of Genetics and Development at Columbia University Medical Center.

Just so you guys no this study was quoted by me in the osteoporosis series as well

# Thread: Whaddya know, the sun isn't so bad for you!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whaddya-know-the-sun-isnt-so-bad-for-you.6993/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cinnamon — 2013-05-08T17:36:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whaddya-know-the-sun-isnt-so-bad-for-you.6993/post-85642>

I caught a few minutes of BBC World News this morning while eating breakfast. Across the bottom of the screen, a headline scrolled that said: "The health benefits of exposing skin to sunlight may far outweigh the risk of developing skin cancer, according to scientists at Edinburgh University".

Here's a link to the related article on the BBC website:

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-scotland-edinburgh-east-fife-22433359](http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-scotland-edinburgh-east-fife-22433359)

An excerpt:

"We now plan to look at the relative risks of heart disease and skin cancer in people who have received different amounts of sun exposure.

"If this confirms that sunlight reduces the death rate from all causes, we will need to reconsider our advice on sun exposure."​

The full report on the study is to be presented this Friday.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-08T18:29:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whaddya-know-the-sun-isnt-so-bad-for-you.6993/post-85650>

clinic this is what my vitamin D blog says as well.

### Huck — 2013-05-09T01:05:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whaddya-know-the-sun-isnt-so-bad-for-you.6993/post-85670>

> Edinburgh University research suggests sunlight helps reduce blood pressure, cutting heart attack and stroke risks and even prolonging life.

I was able to reduce my BP medication last summer. At the time I thought it was from diet and weight loss. In October, I had to increase medication. In early April, we had sunny weather and I was able to reduce medication a little. Then we had a month of overcast days with little sunlight which resulted in me increasing medication again.

I hadn't made the sun/bp connection until you posted the link to the article. I knew sun increases my DHEA, Testosterone, and Vitamin D, but didn't realize it was also reducing BP. Rain, rain, go away.

# Thread: Quick questions on changes from Bulletproof to Epi-Paleo and saving money!?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-on-changes-from-bulletproof-to-epi-paleo-and-saving-money.6996/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-08T22:03:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-on-changes-from-bulletproof-to-epi-paleo-and-saving-money.6996/post-85664>

Hey all! I had some quick questions on some dietary changes I've been thinking about making. I have been thoroughly studying the bulletproof diet for the past two months, literally listening to and annotating and then summarizing every podcast as well as every appearance Dave makes on another podcast. I started doing the same to his Better Baby Book as well. I've then come across this blog/forum and have been swept away. I agree with Dave on some things, and then I agree with Jacks/the posters here opinions. To cut to the chase, I'm on an **extremely** **limited budget**. I pay monthly to do **Brazilian Jiu Jitsu** and I really love doing that and want to compete at a high level, so that takes some of my money away. My **younger brother is extremely ill** and that has taken a toll on finances drastically and my family lives paycheck to paycheck (as we have for the past 2-3yrs). I have been using what's left of my paycheck money to eat a bulletproof/paleo/epi-paleo template. My questions are:

1) I can get wild caught pink salmon from Whole Foods for $2.99 per 14.75oz, but it is in a can. This is much cheaper than purchasing grass-fed beef from Trader Joe's for $6-10lb, and much more nutritious (I would imagine) than eating tons of non-pasture raised/organic/grass-fed beef, chicken, pork,etc. I can also purchase wild caught clams (in a can) at Trader Joe's for about the same price. Should I make this change so I can consume more shellfish/fatty fish as it is MUCH cheaper, or should I just eat a normal Paleo template until I have money after I get a raise/new job (I'm talking not all grass-fed, incorporating chicken, etc.)? I'm worried about BPA and other xenoestrogens, but I feel as though the nutritional power of the fish/shellfish can maybe makeup for it. Would buying farmed fish be okay for saving money, or since it's basically the equivalent (or worse) of corn-fed/soy-fed antiobiotic filled beef, should I completely stay away?

2) I've stopped buying organic eggs and veggies, does this matter? Should I just stop buying eggs altogether and focus on fish/shellfish, or should I continue to eat eggs as I can normally get 18-24 for about $2 (not organic nor pasture raised). Would it matter if I consume these eggs raw? I know you can clean the eggs with a solution made up of iodine and one other compound to kill/limit salmonella, maybe I should do that and start eating a lot of raw eggs as well?

3) I've been thinking about just consuming a regular paleo template until I have more money, but then just saving my money for what have been the three biggest performance enhancers/symptom eradicators (for me) I have come across - Upgraded Coffee, Upgraded MCT Oil + any grass-fed butter, and magnesium. I was thinking about saving money on food and consuming a normal paleo template, but then just making sure EVERYDAY I do a few things: consume 1-2c of Upgraded Coffee, consume 1-2tbsp of Upgraded MCT Oil for ketogenesis and mental focus, consume 1-2c of anti-angiogenesis tea, and consume literally an entire stick of grass-fed butter a day (about 8tbsp). I would also be taking ice cold showers 10mins a day, with occasional cold baths and I also ground for about 20-45mins a day doing Foundation Training in a field

4) Should I focus on making these "detox" purchases - a showerhead filter, buying this athletic organic shampoo/bodywash/facewash/foot soak recommended on Marks Daily Apple, making my own toothpaste without fluoride, and purchasing about 10-15gallons of RO water from Whole Foods to drink a week?

Throughout the changes I've experienced in mental/psychological symptoms, along with the increased physical performance, I can't shy away from Bulletproof diet/Epi-Paleo, but I simply have such little funds these days. I'm trying to stay as "bulletproof/optimal" as possible. I'm hoping that I can just start doing some DEEP Cold Thermogenesis soon on a daily basis along with daily grounding to at least eradicate some of the negatives of not eating a 100% bulletproof diet; also I will focus on cooking toxins presented in the Better Baby Book (Maillard Reaction and denatured proteins). If anyone could give me their input, I'd be more than gracious, I won't even be able to explain.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-09T01:56:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-on-changes-from-bulletproof-to-epi-paleo-and-saving-money.6996/post-85675>

Here's my 2cents. eat as much seafood and shellfish you can afford, while canned or farmed may not be the best seafood, its still better than grassfed beef/pork nutrition wise on any day. If you can find an international market and see about getting mackerel - which is an inexpensive fatty fish like salmon but its lower on the food chain. at our local international market I can get 1Lb of frozen mixed seafood for $3.99/lb - that same 1lb bag of frozen mixed seafood at trader joes is $11.99/lb. I realize not every one has access to international markets its is worth trying to find one b/c they're so much cheaper that traditional stores

as for everything else, coffee is my one vise. If I'm going to drink it I want to drink BP - b/c if I don't want to drink it the next day then at least I won't end up with a migraine.

Costco sells Kerry Gold for 2.28/brick. you can also get good seafood from there as well. Do you have access to a yard to plant a garden? what about container gardening if that's not an option. We garden - it does save us on our veggie bill year round, especially with the collards, kale and other greens.

apply the things you can, and don't worry about the things you can't. not everyone can throw the "Whole Kitchen Sink" at their health, those that can't do need to understand that it will take longer this way. but as long as you're good with it and the progress you make on your time frame - then I see your plan as your road to success!

### caroline — 2013-05-09T02:05:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-on-changes-from-bulletproof-to-epi-paleo-and-saving-money.6996/post-85677>

Great advice Gretchen. I wonder if he has thought of MHS ...... I can get a bag of frozen small fish from a chinese store for $6.00 ..... also lamb heart and lamb liver are grass fed here - actually - lamb in general. You can easily make a liver pate that is so cheap....

BigPapa - you didn't tell us - is your health okay? you are trying to get to optimum? is this performance driven?

### nonchalant — 2013-05-09T04:14:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-on-changes-from-bulletproof-to-epi-paleo-and-saving-money.6996/post-85693>

Welcome, Papa!

1\. I agree with Shijin13. Any seafood is good seafood. Sardines and clams and small, frozen shrimp and such are pretty cheap. I have stopped buying grass-fed, to save money for seafood. I do buy grass-fed pork fat, however, to render for lard.
2\. I found a local supplier of eggs on Craigslist. I pay $2.50/doz, but I would eat them raw without hesitation.
3\. Refined Coconut oil is fairly inexpensive, as an MCT oil.
4\. I use a very small amount of soap, mostly for cleaning my feet (grounding is dirty work). You can make your own shampoo with baking power & water, and for a conditioner, cucumber and lemon; you may like that. You will find that when you cut the carbs, you cut the body odor. I definitely recommend making your own toothpaste (or powder.) I have been using a baking soda/salt combination with added ground clove for flavor, and I love it. Making your own body cleaning products will save you lots of money. If you can get the water for about $0.40 per gallon, that would be a good idea.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-09T06:21:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-on-changes-from-bulletproof-to-epi-paleo-and-saving-money.6996/post-85702>

control what you can.........the rest will come

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-09T09:18:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-on-changes-from-bulletproof-to-epi-paleo-and-saving-money.6996/post-85713>

Thanks for everyone's input thus far!! So I suppose eating canned seafood is not the worst offender to my health?

For those who asked, I'am both trying to reclaim my health, and performance driven. As far as I know (from checkups) I don't suffer from adrenal fatigue/diabetes/etc. and from the mirror test + the fact that I can fast for a whole day with nothing easily, I'm not LR. Dr. Kruse has actually commented on one of my PaleoHacks posts: [http://paleohacks.com/questions/175...he-topic-of-my-bizarre-symptoms#axzz2ROLhaPzh](http://paleohacks.com/questions/175553/last-question-on-the-topic-of-my-bizarre-symptoms#axzz2ROLhaPzh). I essentially suffer from some psychological (possibly stemming from biological roots) symptoms and sensations from ingesting PCP or embalming fluid (this was not a purposeful choice, believe me). I know these substances are anticholingeric, and pairing that with the fact that I suffered during the trip, well I didn't have a bad one but it was worrisome and I lost sleep that whole weekend. For months I was having extreme anxiety, migraines + aura's, and visual disturbances such as objects I look at shimmering or slightly moving/vibrating. It's been 15 months almost and going paleo has been the greatest aid, followed by incorporating Jack/Dave Asprey's protocols. I'am also performance driven as I'm an ex-wrestler and football player, and started up Jiu-Jitsu again and I dream to compete.... and I mean compete at a high level (national/international). Some may call me insane for thinking that I can, but then again people think I'm insane for wearing orange glasses at night and I feel that is more than normal, lol.

I will go to the local Indian and Mexican Markets near my house, I don't have an Asian one, but I'm suspecting I can at least purchase bulk herbs/spices from there and maybe a lot of coconut oil/milk/cream. So is the canned clams/salmon a better choice than the Trader Joe's New Zealand Grass-fed Ground Beef? What about the eggs - should I discontinue them as I know they are o6 skewed? But they do have many valuable nutrients. How about the water, out of all the things after skimming through the latest blogs (I'm actually reading every blog in the Leptin Prescription and annotating them and then going to read/annotate all the citations that were given, then read/annotate/review Mastering Leptin so I can fully understand everything there is to know about Leptin) I think water may be one of the more important variables - both drinking and bathing water.

So here is what I'm thinking thus far until I have the money to do more:
(1) Set aside money to purchase weekly RO water in separate 5 gallon jugs from Whole Foods - buy about 3 of them which can be refilled for roughly $2 each; try and drink roughly a gallon a day
(2) To save money (for now) eat all the canned seafood I can an any un-canned seafood I can find for reasonable prices; drop the grass-fed beef; for variety/other nutrients eat non-grass-fed meat but make sure they're primarily lean cuts eaten rare and I do everything possible to limit cooking toxins; Drink 1-2C of Upgraded Coffee per day but decaf after 2PM; rather than spending tons of money on coconut oil which has less MCT's (although other beneficial FA) consume 1-2tbsp of Upgraded MCT Oil per day; Consume much more salt everyday (trader joe's sea salt & Onnit brand Himalayan Salt); Consume a stick of butter a day for vit.A and butyric acid
(3) As I don't believe in some supplements (Vit.D included), continue supplementing Magnesium, but switch to a more sublingual route or dump magnesium into baths rather than taking my chelated magnesium glycinate
(4) DEEP CT... even though it is summer. I'm talking about 10min ice cold showers everyday, with weekly ice cold baths, drinking ice cold water before and after, and keeping all my rooms as cool as possible with minimal clothing... eventually getting a Cool Fat Burner to wear while on my laptop
(5) Grounding+sunlight as much as possible

Any comments on what I'm thinking up so far? For added info, I will soon be getting a check up through WellnessFX as I earned a free check up, and I'm soon going to purchase a blood glucose/blood ketone meter to do some bio-hacks/experiments on myself, I will post results when I can. Btw, I will be doing Bulletproof Intermittent Fasting most days but I normally go longer than 12hrs without the BPC unlike some BP-IF practitioners. Should I focus on emf/artificial light? I already have blue-light blocking glasses + f.lux and a dimmed screen, but I was thinking of purchasing those greenwave emf filters for my bedroom and getting all the electronics besides my laptop out of the room.

Thank you so much everyone, believe me I appreciate it more than you would expect. I hope to some day be doing what Jack is doing for people as he has been an aid to recovery to so many. Thanks again.

That may be gutsy but I honestly love the sport, want to perform in it at the highest degree, and was a talented wrestler in highschool, I'm confident I can.

So I'm guessing that canned fish/shellfish may be even better than grass-fed beef? I was purchasing New Zealand Grass-fed Ground Beef from Trader Joe's but 6/lb is simply too expensive considering I consume about 140g protein a day and then >180 on days I do my BBS style workout - I was consuming 1lb a day and then the remainder of protein in eggs/veggies. So here is what I'm thinking so far:
(1) Set aside money to consume adequate clean water every day; purchase RO water from Whole Foods and enough to last a week - I can get 5gal refilled for about $2; ensure I drink roughly a gallon a day and consume much more sea/pink Himalayan salt (currently at about 1tsp or more, aiming for multiple tsp)
(2) To save money, consume the canned fish/shellfish sources I can find, seek out more reasonably priced sources; consume meat occasionally, avoid fowl but consume red meat, inorganic/non-grass-fed if I must, but in that case it has to be lean, rare as possible, and avoid all cooking toxins - grass-fed when I can afford it; stop coconut oil and only consume Upgraded MCT Oil @ 1-2tbsp a day; Consume 1-2C of Upgraded Coffee per day but decaff after 2PM... order a metal aeropress filter to keep in kawehol/cafestol (sorry don't know how to spell those two compounds); Maintain cyclical ketogenic dieting through the summer rather than eating tons of carbs everyday - when I do I'll eat non-organic squash, zucchini, sweet potatoes, possibly potatoes with the skins removed, and carrots
(3) Deep CT even in the summer
(4) BP-IF every day as usual but with longer times without BPC (rather than 12hrs then BPC, maybe 14 or so)
(5) supplement with magnesium but a more bioavailable form and in higher dosages

Should I mainly focus on clean water on a consistent basis mixed with lowering EMF exposure? I wear blue-light blocking glasses after sundown and have f.lux plus a dimmed screen, should I purchase the Greenwave EMF filters and possibly a universal grounding mat? I will be removing all electronics in my room minus my laptop. What about other 'detox solutions' such as a showerhead filter?

### caroline — 2013-05-09T10:56:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-on-changes-from-bulletproof-to-epi-paleo-and-saving-money.6996/post-85717>

You are doing great and are really giving all this a lot of thought.
Have you thought of eating lamb heart and liver? Some of us eat lamb heart raw - carpaccio style..... and as I said above you can make a pate from lamb liver very quickly and cheaply. The MHS \[mackerel head smoothy\] is very cheap and great brain food. Some us also eat raw beef - I just stick a piece of rump on a wooden board and leave in the fridge. After about a week it has started to dry and you eat far less than cooked - at least that is my experience. That is what I have for breakfast along with 2 BPC.

I am curious about the BPC - you seem to feel very strongly about keeping that in your diet - may I ask why? and you seem to feel you get much more benefit from MCT oil?

Make sure that you go over Jack'c recommended list of labs ......Vitamin D3 is very important - maybe read more on that and absolutely for sure get Vit D tested. I think the blog is called - the sunshine of your life.

### Dali Dula — 2013-05-09T12:36:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-on-changes-from-bulletproof-to-epi-paleo-and-saving-money.6996/post-85719>

Bone broth from grass fed beef and fish broth from fish heads/ bodies/crustacean shells. These can be made inexpensive and are nutritious. Get an electric crock pot and throw bones and stuff in with some kombu, cook for many hours. Eat this straight up or use it as a base for other things. Powerful ,cheap, easy.

I'm performance minded in the aspect that I do brazilian Jiu-Jitsu and I'm an ex-wrestler/football player; I want to compete in BJJ, and I mean as far as it can take me (national/international tournaments). To sum up my problems I ingested **PCP/embalming fluid** at a concert (not on purpose, believe me), and shortly after I started suffering from bizarre psychological and physical symptoms (probably stemming from biological roots). I know PCP/embalming fluid act as an anticholingeric so that probably explains it - it also has an extremely long half-life inside of the brain. I suffered from panic attacks/anxiety, pressure in the head, migraines with aura, and visual distortions such as objects shimmering/moving/vibrating when I looked at them - this was all made worse if I didn't sleep well, had lot's of stress (which I do due to my younger brother being extremely ill, having no money, having a father who doesn't work, blah blah blah), or ate something bad (gluten/msg/aspartame). Oh by the way, in the past I **cracked my head open** and during football/wrestling I suffered from **2 concussions** within about six months of each other (maybe eight).

I would do MMA but I don't want to suffer down the road from brain problems so I'm fine just doing grappling sports such as BJJ/Judo, and going as far as they will take me. Mainly I care about fixing my "symptoms", and this is what lead me down the road to "optimal/bulletproof".

So I'm guessing from everyone's input that even canned seafood is more nutritious than grass-fed meat for the most part...? Should I just continue to keep buying canned fish and possibly even farmed fish (probably only farmed crustaceans), and throw in the occasional meat during the week? What about clean water/veggies/detoxing? This is what I'm thinking for now and I would appreciate any input:
(1) Set aside money and purchase RO water from Whole Foods every week, enough to drink roughly a gallon a day and use to make coffee; drink it ice cold/make ice with it/etc; Consume more sea salt - I consume about 1tsp a day but I'm looking to up that to two or so
(2) Consume as much fish/shellfish as I can regardless of canning/farmed/etc; look for deals at various local markets (maybe go up to Supreme Lobster in Chicago); don't drink tea/coffee unless I have Upgraded beans - in that case drink 1-2C a day but Decaff after 2PM; stop purchasing coconut oil but buy monthly Upgraded MCT Oil and consume 1-2tbsp a day; oh yeah... purchase a metal aeropress filter to keep in the anti-inflammatory terpenes of BP coffee
(3) Continue BP-IF and go further with my CT
(4) Since it's summer get AS MUCH sunlight as possible and ground EVERYDAY; maybe I can mow lawns/do lawn care as a form of grounding + sunlight + fresh air + 'walking meditation' and do it fasted to push on thoseee fat burning enzymes... then use the cash for fish

How does this ^^ look so far? Any suggestions? What about 'detox decisions' such as getting a showerhead filter, no more toothpaste, organic/chemical free shampoo, etc? For those who asked, the reason I want to keep bulletproof coffee is because of a multitude of things (1) I love coffee but won't sacrifice quality (2) I want to encourage my family to at least take that in to make healthier decisions (3) I can actually feel a difference; after drinking BPC for about a week and then drinking normal Dunkin Donuts coffee my mom purchases, I felt like crap, crashed, and head head rushes and less energy + brain fog; BPC in the morning gives me enhanced focus and energy, mental clarity, and a nice little buzz (4) I feel like it enhances my fasts - sometimes due to work/my schedule I stop eating at about 4-5pm and when I wake up I've already been in a fasted state for about 15-16hrs and I'm not even starving so I don't force feed myself, or I don't have the time to make a BAB, so I have some Salt Water/tsp of salt, and BPC and I'm sure it keeps my body from leaching electrolytes/amino acids.

Thanks so much to everyone who answered thus far! I cannot even begin to express my appreciation, I honestly love the people here as there is just so much information and so much inspiration. I really hope to end up doing what Jack is doing in the future - Jack, Ben Greenfield, Tim Ferriss, and Dave Asprey (oh... can't forget Barry, Inger, etc. lol) have inspired me so much. I wanted to become a psychologist but now I'm into helping people learn about the power that lies within. I've been inspired to attempt to do things such as Barry (ketosis+athletics) and am now engaged in genetics and epigenetics courses through this website:[https://www.coursera.org/](https://www.coursera.org/) - I suggest anyone who wants more education but does not have the schedule/money to do so, look into it. I may sound outlandish or something, but I want to do it, and I'll do what it takes - I was a talented wrestler and most of the people I compete/train with are not into bio-hacking, speed learning, epi-paleo, self-experimentation, etc.

I'm getting the feeling that even farmed fish and canned fish would be better than grass-fed beef, no? If that is so, I'm thinking I will continue buying canned, wild-caught pink salmon from whole foods, and get the wild caught clams and mussels whenever I can at Jewel (where I work), and if I have to, get various forms of farmed fish. I will also do everything possible to avoid cooking toxins (Maillard Reaction, mutagens, etc.) and eat the meat as rare as I can bare while keeping in flavor. I will consume a whole stick of grass-fed butter (Organic Valley Cultured or Organic Valley Pastured, occasional kerrygold) each day, and I will further research raw eggs... The brief skimming I did in the Better Baby Book stated that the statistics on Salmonella and raw eggs show that the chance of catching it are slim to none, and that is mainly factory farmed eggs, and you can make the iodine + some other compound solution to soak the shells in beforehand anyhow. If so, I will consume raw eggs every week and use the whites for recipes or post workout meals (once a week) smothered in butter. I will be keeping the Upgraded Coffee, I just purchased a 5lb bag of Decaff lol. I seriously feel as though it is an extreme performance booster, and it no doubt enhances health. I also have many tests and essays to complete all summer long, so some Upgraded Coffee will help, not to mention how I can use it pre/post workout.

Any input on any of this so far? Should I balance fatty fish out with things like crabs, clams, and mussels? I'm currently engaged in this Useful Genetics course, but I'm reading through every LeptinRx post and annotating them + the comments, summarizing my annotations, and then studying all the citations. I also purchased Mastering Leptin. I'm starting to connect the dots, if only it wasn't for this shitty budget (excuse my language).... What about purchasing RO water from Whole Foods?

Thanks everyone for all your input! I hope to be doing what Dr.Kruse is doing one day, exposing the potential within every human being from the start that is unfortunately neglected often times. I can't even express how much I appreciate everyone's input thus far! It's greatly appreciated. ### BJK77 — 2013-05-10T15:42:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-estrogen.7013/post-85785>

My estrogen levels are low - labs show it and symptoms are consistent with it: hot flashes, dry, no libido, etc. I finally found a doc who will prescribe me a large enough dose of Progesterone to get those levels optimal, but he will not prescribe estrogen because of my age. He told me yesterday my labs look like those of a 50-year old woman and if I were 50 he would prescribe the estrogen, but he can't "in good conscience" prescribe it for me at my age. That makes no sense to me at all, but the many other docs I went to would never give me enough Pg, so I feel like I need to stick with him and take the estrogen matters into my own hands until I can find someone better.

I found I could order Estrogen from an online pharmacy, but they are all european variations and all my google searches aren't showing me ingredients or anything about these forms. Is anyone familiar with any of them: [http://goldpharma.com/search/estradiol/lang/ENGLISH/](http://goldpharma.com/search/estradiol/lang/ENGLISH/) I don't like that it says "Natural and semisynthetic" at the top. I want to know I'm getting completely natural stuff!

I also found this option: [http://www.1-menopause.com/order.htm](http://www.1-menopause.com/order.htm) Is anyone familiar with this product?

> Premarin is the commercial name for a medication consisting primarily of conjugated estrogens. Isolated from mares' urine (pregnant mares' urine), it is manufactured by Wyeth Pharmaceuticals (part of Pfizer since January 2009) and has been marketed since 1942.

I'm going to call this morning to see if I can get an appt. The only thing that worries me is the website talks about this doctor using a 'membership/concierge' approach to medicine and says she only enrolls 150-200 patients. \*fingers crossed\* she still has openings and it's not ridiculously expensive!

Hello! Yes, I work with Dzugan. Overall I like it. I have had a few problems but they are not Dzugan specific....for whatever reason, my body really fights hormones supps. This was even before BHRT. For instance, I have hashi's, and it took me MONTHS to get to a theraputic dose of thyroid meds bc I just had bad side affects. Therefore my motto is LOW and SLOW. Like whatever they normally prescribe, I usually take at least a 1/4 of what they suggest. I think Dzugan knows his stuff and I like that Gretchen and Colleen use him and talk to them bc I think they are getting some education from Jack along with what they know works for instance. My biggest complaint - but this would be true of all of these type of BHRT programs - is that it takes several days to get an update. For instance, I put in an update last week and am still waiting for them to get back to me. It can take 3-5 days. If you happen to be having a bad side effect that is a long time to wait. However, during those times, I usually come here and ask questions and people like Colleen, Gretchen, etc are really helpful until I hear back from them.

They also seem to listen to suggestion about what forms you want to use. FOr instance I am not hearing great things about cream supplementation so am going to inquire about getting sublingual. I have no doubt they will accommadate that.

I hope this helps!

### Lanie — 2013-05-14T01:55:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-estrogen.7013/post-85975>

> [DarleenMB said:](/goto/post?id=85795)
>
> wileyprotocol.com
>
> find a local provider.

I think Darleen and I have agreed to disagree about Wiley. Just be aware that it's not right for everyone. I was on the Wiley Protocol for 7 or 8 months. It's relatively inexpensive (i.e., there's no $1900 sign-up fee, like with Dzugan). The hormones cost about $100-$150 a month, in addition to blood draws and doc appointments.

Some things to be aware of: Wiley practitioners, if doing the protocol correctly, do blood draws to measure hormone levels rather than saliva. Measuring via saliva is often considered a more accurate measurement, as it quantifies the amount of hormone in the system that is free and available for the body to use. Blood tests may present a different picture but not clearly show how the body is using the hormones. See here for more info:

[http://www.drmarcantel.com/hormone-testing-saliva-or-serum-blood-test/](http://www.drmarcantel.com/hormone-testing-saliva-or-serum-blood-test/)

An additional issue I had was with the huge doses of hormones prescribed via the Wiley Protocol. After 6 or 7 months I became very bloated, I gained weight, my breasts increased 1-2 cup sizes (they're already too big), I was tired all the time, and had heart palpitations. My belly started protruding - it looked like I was 6 months pregnant. I continued to have vicious pre-menstrual mood swings. My periods got much weirder. My cycles would last 30-40 days total, and periods would last 10 days, including 1-2 days of obnoxiously heavy flow. My doc (who was also on Wiley) told me that sometimes she didn't have a period for months (she's around 40).

All of these things (except the weight gain, unfortunately) resolved within a month of discontinuing the hormones. Now I'm back to a 27-28 day cycle, my belly isn't sticking out so far anymore (even though I haven't lost the weight), and my bras fit again. Please google "Progesterone toxicity symptoms." I had a lot of the symptoms. While on the protocol my labs indicated my progesterone levels were mid-range on Day 12 (0.6 with range 0.2-1.5) and the low end of the desired range for Day 21 (3.0 with range 1.7-27.0).

TS Wiley is not a doctor - she has a BA in Anthropology, I think. Apparently some people feel great on this protocol, but just be aware that others have had problems.

I hope that helps.

Interesting thread.

I would like to get some discussion/opinion on:

Saliva vs blood testing

is method of supplementation a factor of what should be tested

Progesterone toxicity

\-------------------

> This is same link as in Lanie's post.
>
> [http://www.drmarcantel.com/hormone-testing-saliva-or-serum-blood-test/](http://www.drmarcantel.com/hormone-testing-saliva-or-serum-blood-test/)
>
> Steroid hormones such as **estrogens, progesterone, testosterone, cortisol, and others are mostly bound to carrier proteins** in the bloodstream. It’s only the unbound 1-5% of these hormones that are actually available to target tissues and that actively exert specific effects on the body. Saliva tests, unlike serum (blood) tests, measure the level of these bioavailable hormones. Because of this, salivary hormone levels often relate to specific symptoms of hormone imbalances. A blood test, on the other hand, may show “normal” hormone levels but may not reveal how the hormones are actually affecting the body.

The way I am reading above,
it actually,
contradict conclusion,
that saliva testing provides better information.

I have example that is very strong for testosterone (but it applies to other steroid hormones)
Testosterone exist in the body in three places that it are measured via serum

As strongly bound to SHBG
As weakly bound to Albumin
As Free Testosterone

In the above we can hope that saliva testosterone levels are corresponding to FreeT in serum.
There is calculator
[http://www.issam.ch/freetesto.htm](http://www.issam.ch/freetesto.htm)
input SHBG, Albumin and Totaltestosterone
output ---->FreeTestosterone and BioAvailable Testosterone

You can see that FreeT is indeed very small % of total
It is so small
that it would not hurt much to just forget about it (figuratively speaking)
why
the testosterone that affect body is BioAvailableTestosterone BAT

BAT is combination of FreeT and testosterone that is weakly bound to Albumin

Grossly speaking

half of testosterone is bound to albumin and it is available at the moments notice

concentrating on just FreeT is like missing the boat by 50%
worrying if it is 2% or 2.5% while disregarding 50% elephant

\---------

Note in quotation above,
all hormones are bound to (Binding Proteins)
Some are bound tight some weakly.

Free form of hormones indeed is the part that does the job,
but
when that particular batch of Free hormone is used up, there is a truck load available in matter of seconds.

So, concentrating on just free form is not a very good reason as a defense of saliva testing (thinking out loud, I am not a doctor).

\----------------------
\----------------------

is method of supplementation a factor of what should be tested ?

Just to side step argumentation (saliva vs serum) (and get better overall service)
for my self I supplement with heaps of pregnenolone-MLM
I let the body sort out how much it wants to allocate for progesterone.

Note
using that method I am able to use blood test and actually track my progress and progesterone levels using serum.

Note#2
that was not the case when I would supplement with trans-dermal progesterone directly

Note#3
I am actually talking about two tablets of pregnenolone-MLM a day and I am able to get my progesterone up to 4.5 which is more that you got on day 21.

I am man, I should be happy with prog~1.4 (which is more than yours 0.6 on day 12)
reason I am going that high is because **dr Kruse wants (women) to go for P/E~500**

and when he argues the benefits of high progesterone levels, it does not looks like progesterone toxicity crosses his mind

\---------------------
\---------------------

In the last section you are saying things like
huge doses of hormones prescribed
Progesterone toxicity
and
(luckily) follow this statements with blood tests of your actual progesterone levels on that protocol

> While on the protocol my labs indicated my progesterone levels were mid-range
> on Day 12 (0.6 with range 0.2-1.5)
> and the low end of the desired range for
> Day 21 (3.0 with range 1.7-27.0).

My conclusion
those huge amounts of stuff
were not hormones
it was something else with possibly some traces of actual hormones.

So;
discussing
Progesterone toxicity
does not apply
while you are highly deficient of progesterone.

\--------------------------------------------------------------------------

I am glad that you have better protocol now.
There is always a possibility that you still can do even better.

...

You may want to look at my doodles here:
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508&viewfull=1#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508&viewfull=1#post81508)

JanSz, I'm willing to entertain this thought, because none of this makes much sense otherwise. Why would my levels have been low but I was showing signs of having too much progesterone? I can say this: I moved in the middle of the period of time during which I was on the Wiley Protocol, and switched compounding pharmacies. From both pharmacies I got the exact same Wiley-prescribed Progesterone cream. It smelled the same, looked the same, had the same thickness and texture, and had the same effect on my body, from both pharmacies. So whether or not I was getting progesterone, I'll never know. But I do know that what Wiley prescribes and what is ordered from the compounding pharmacy did not make me feel good.

They heart palpitations and weight gain were the most troubling symptoms. The heart palpitations, which had been bothering me for months, diminished about 75% within 1 week of quitting the Wiley Protocol, and were completely gone within 1 month of discontinuing the hormone supplementation.
