### Christopher — 2013-06-09T23:13:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autism-with-a-side-of-bacon.7107/post-87865>

I have been thinking about really trying CT. I'm convinced that its the next step. I'm just being slow about it. I've been feeling cold alot lately and just not in the mood to make myself colder. I'm hoping the warm weather will make it easier to embrace the cold, lol. I have been noticing that I tend to feel chilly a little while after being in the sun, so I may just cut back on my sun time, or spread it out as in a couple of short times in the sun, instead of spending a whole half hour in the sun. I don't know, but I'll keep the CT in my mind, and I will eventually find a way to make it work.

I wanted to make a list here of the things I have been doing that seem to be working. The main thing is the Leptin Rx. I just can't get enough of the BAB. I find I can eat a half pound of beef, several eggs cooked in coconut oil, a side of saurkrout, a couple ounces of chocolate (half 100% cocao, and half coconut oil), and sometimes as much as half a jar of coconut mana (or half a pound of bacon in Winter) for breakfast. I don't know where it all goes, but it sure is a good time. I tend to go to fish for lunch and dinner. Sometimes with lunch I'll have more coconut mana, or some macadamia nuts if I desire, but I find that I don't feel quite right if I eat too much fat later in the day. Usually dinner is just fish by it self now. At first I was having chocolate, mana, or bacon with every meal, but then I found that if I ate them at dinner, I would wake up in the night drenched in sweat. These are the staple foods that seem to not cause any digestive issues.

The other thing that has been helpful is lowering emf exposure. More on that later.

### Christopher — 2013-06-10T03:40:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autism-with-a-side-of-bacon.7107/post-87884>

I've always kind of noticed that using the computer makes me feel bad. I get tired and irritable, my eyes get bloodshot, and I would often get pain in my back, and wrist. For a while I thought it was because of my posture while sitting, so I made a standing workstation. That did not make any difference. The only thing that would help would be limiting my computer time. That was hard because I get obsessive about computers, and sometimes things get so slow at work that the only thing I would have to do is play on the computer (or so I thought). Driving my car would also cause similar results. Although, changing my posture while driving did help the back pain.

As I was getting the information from Jack's blogs, I began to limit my artificial light exposure. Previously I had changed all our lights to cool blue cfls. I have a bit of an obsession with lighting, and would agonize over getting all the lighting "just right". It never did seem right, so I was always moving lamps around and swapping bulbs of varying wattage. I thought the bluer light was relaxing for some reason, I guess I've just never cared for regular "warm" colored lights. Anyway, Jack's blogs put that to an end. I got rid of all the cool white lighting and switched to warmer lighting. It happened in phases, but now we only use amber or orange incandescent lighting after sunset. We still have regular incandescent lights (40 - 60 watt) for pre-sunset time. I should also add that everyone thinks the orange lighting is much cozier at night. I finally figured out the right lighting for relaxing, lol. I also have a pair of yellow glasses that fit over my perscription glasses. I got them years ago to cut down on glare durring nighttime lighting. So I put those on if the computer is on after sunset, or if I'm at work after sunset. My cowerkers all laugh at me. That's ok, I think they're funny too, but I can tell a difference in how I feel as soon as I put them on. I'd like to get some amber glasses, but those wont allow me to tell the difference in wire coloring at work. The yellow lenses are ok for that.

My sleep has improved since limiting my light exposure, but still some nights seem to be better than others. I started to notice that my better mornings were related to evenings that I had not watched tv the night before. Kate and I had a habbit of watching some tv shows together to unwind before bedtime. But I was finding that I was better off skipping the tv and doing chores, even though I had the yellow glasses on either way. So the tv had to go. And the sound bar went along with it (with a wireless subwoofer). Kate thought I was going crazy, and was just punishing everyone. But the free time we have now with no tv is great. We spend alot more time actually talking to eachother, its nice. Its also nice to have the time for all the food prep and cleanup that we do now that we don't consume processed fast foods.

Anyway, that all lead up to the EMF blogs. I made the smartphone-hemorrhoid connection here: [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6839-My-Smartphone-Gave-Me-Hemorrhoids!](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6839-My-Smartphone-Gave-Me-Hemorrhoids!) So then the wifi was turned off, the cordless phones and smartphones went on ebay, and we decided to move. We were in a small second floor apartment. The size of the place was great in helping us get rid of some tings we didn't need, but it had served its purpose and it was time to move on. We found a townhome for the same price that has a full basement . In the new place, I have set up the computers with extentions for the keyboard, mouse, and monitor wiring. The computer towers are now five to six feet away from where we sit or stand. And of course, nothing is wireless now. I just wish I could convince all the neighbors to turn off their wifi. Oh well. We do have cell phones, but will only turn them on to make a call if absolutely necessary. I also have started turning off many of the circuit breakers at night. the only circuits left on are for the refrigerator, and furnace/AC. That means only candle light at bedtime. That's something I've always wanted since I was a child. I used to love it when the power went out, lol.

Bennefits? No more back or wrist pain. No more bloodshot eyes if I use the computer. I do still get a little fatigued, but nothing like I used to. I still try to limit my computer usage. For reading, I'd much prefer an actual book, or at least my kindle (wireless disabled, w/ passive e-ink display).

Things have reached a point of balance for now. I'd like to do more EMF mitigation, but that will reqire money we just don't have right now. For now, keeping up the diet is about all we can afford. I'm hoping to sqeeze out the money to at least get a tri-field meter sometime soon. But in the mean time, it looks like CT will be the next step. It sounds like CT will give the best result for a low monetary cost. I'm sure my sleep could be better. And I'm tired of having "goose bumps" on my arms all the time. CT should fix that, right?

### Dextery — 2013-06-10T04:01:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autism-with-a-side-of-bacon.7107/post-87887>

> I'd like to do more EMF mitigation, but that will reqire money we just don't have right now.

One thing you can do right now for less than $10 is to shield your smart meter...which you probably have.

For $10, everyone can shield their smart meter. Get a one gallon paint can, clean it up or buy an unused one at home depot. Also buy a small role of aluminum screening and also buy two 7 inch hose clamps. Cut the bottom out of the paint can and place it over the meter. Use one of the hose clamps to secure it to the smart meter. The can is a little larger than the meter. The can will bulge a bit when secured tightly. Then cut a piece of the aluminum screening that will fit over the end of the can facing away from the wall it is mounted on. Use the other hose clamp to secure the screen material. Trim as necessary. Take the remaining screening material and mount it behind the meter on the back side of the wall the meter is attached to. This prevents the emf from the meter radiating back through the wall as the meter is omnidirectional. The meter can still transmit your electrical usage to the power company. For people with the meter mounted on a pole or elsewhare, get the aluminum screening and drape the screen over the meter and all around the back side of the pole.

### Christopher — 2013-06-20T19:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autism-with-a-side-of-bacon.7107/post-88504>

The last couple weeks have been kinda rough. I got sick again with a stomach flue for a few days, and the kids seem to have this upper respiratory thing that won't seem to go away. We are waiting for a phone call from the doctor's office today to find out if the kids tested positive for pertussis (whooping cough). And to top things off, the smart meters were installed today.

I almost started CT, lol. Kate bought me a compression shirt. It was a mens size small, and it fit me looser than my under shirts, lol. I'll get to it eventually....when I'm done procrastinating.

I read two books that have really challenged my self-perception and concept of reallity, Exploring Conciousness by Rita Carter, and Design In Nature by Adrian Bejan and J. Peder Zane. Since reading these books, I have been feeling a bit of anxiety and sorrow. There were certain ideas that I had about what I am, and what reallity is that just don't fit anymore. Those ideas have gone, and I am experiencing an emptiness in there abscence. It reminds me of an article: [http://takehisheart.com/powerofthewilldeathofapet.htm](http://takehisheart.com/powerofthewilldeathofapet.htm). My conciousness is an evolving process. Who I am now, is not who I was, and not who I necessarilly will be. If there even is an "I". Deep thoughts have been running through my mind. I am humbled by the concept that everything that moves is alive. This includes technology. Just as the existence of human beings has caused many other forms of life to go extinct over the years, our technology as the newest life form is causing humans go go extinct. Its all just the flow of energy causing motion. The Constructal Law, has thus kicked my concept of reallity square in the berries. The Laws of Thermodynamics are serious business.

I also had a wonderfull experience on one of my last days off. I spent the day doing oil changes and car washess on both of our cars. The oil change part is never really fun, just gives that sense of accomplisment. But the car wash.... I was barefoot with no shirt on, it was warm and sunny, there was water all over the place.... I felt better that evening than I have felt in a long time. I also had a computer free day at home for Father's day, as in both computers were unplugged and never turned on all day. It was great. And the grilled butter burger egg bacon lettuce wraps, mmmm....

### SeaHorse — 2013-06-20T20:04:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autism-with-a-side-of-bacon.7107/post-88506>

Christopher....have you listened to the June webinar? So interesting to see how our concepts of self and reality are influenced and shaped. I too have felt that "loss" when things are shifting fast...there can be a real void in the wake of this kind of experience or thinking...However, it is in the void that all the new good stuff happens too. I have to remind myself that accepting each state and just letting it be will bring along the next stage of being.... and there lies the wonderful quality of flux. It's good to mark the changes without grabbing on to the emotions too long...hope this makes sense. You certainly make sense to me describing these how things don't "fit" anymore.

Have a listen to the webinar...really interesting stuff.

### Dextery — 2013-06-20T20:08:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autism-with-a-side-of-bacon.7107/post-88507>

Christopher,

It is time now to shield your new smart meter.

I had originally posted the following here: [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthre...ideo-about-smart-meters&highlight=smart+meter](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7345-Have-you-seen-this-video-about-smart-meters&highlight=smart+meter)

You can shield your meter by the following cheap fix:

For $10, everyone can shield their smart meter. Get a one gallon paint can, clean it up or buy an unused one at home depot. Also buy a small role of aluminum screening and also buy two 7 inch hose clamps. Cut the bottom out of the paint can and place it over the meter. Use one of the hose clamps to secure it to the smart meter. The can is a little larger than the meter. The can will bulge a bit when secured tightly. Then cut a piece of the aluminum screening that will fit over the end of the can facing away from the wall it is mounted on. Use the other hose clamp to secure the screen material. Trim as necessary. Take the remaining screening material and mount it behind the meter on the back side of the wall the meter is attached to. This prevents the emf from the meter radiating back through the wall as the meter is omnidirectional. The meter can still transmit your electrical usage to the power company. For people with the meter mounted on a pole or elsewhare, get the aluminum screening and drape the screen over the meter and all around the back side of the pole.
You are now shielded 95%. I don't have an emf meter, but the youtube videos I have seen do things similar to this and they block the radiation quite well.
I have now grounded my paint can by using an old piece of extension cord. I just inserted a bare end under one of the hose clamps and then down to a grounding strap that was placed there by the telephone company to ground their equipment. You can always use a short piece of Â½ in copper pipe and pound it into the ground and attach your grounding wire to it.

These are some of the videos:

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cmS5pVEZHzg](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cmS5pVEZHzg)
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CL2JQ0FbgvU](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CL2JQ0FbgvU)
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EoM43CXB4FY](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EoM43CXB4FY)
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LN10megpzLM](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LN10megpzLM)
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uoRtC0e9Rm0](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uoRtC0e9Rm0)

The last video shows a man doing muscle testing to demonstrate how debilitating the RF from a smart meter is. For those of you who have been treated by a kinesologist, muscle testing is a way the practitioner can idenitify weak parts of the body and directs them to where a spine adjustment should be made. And he demonstrates how very important it is to shield the back side of the meter. He also uses a salt lamp and/or salt crystals to block the emf. Not so sure about that method but it seems to work.

Shield your meter so that the RF does not tear your cells apart.

### Christopher — 2013-06-23T19:39:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autism-with-a-side-of-bacon.7107/post-88753>

I know the void brings good things, freedom in particular. As parts of the old "me" die, I feel sorrow because of the loss. But, it is necessary for the new "me" to begin. Its a process I'm familar with. The period of mourning is a little tough. I know its been taking its toll on Kate. And the new "me" will also bring more changes. It seems the more I try to learn, the faster the changes come. These changes can be frightening. But, it seems the more risk, the greater the potential reward. When life starts to feel overwelming, the new plan is to get the whole family in the car and go to one of the local parks. I do love being outside in the summertime. We all went to the track at a local school the otherday. It was a good way to relieve some stress. I was amazed at Ryan's ability to run, and run, and then keep running some more. I think he ran like two miles, lol. I did some sprinting and walking barefoot in the grass of the infield.

Yes, I've watched the June webinar. It kinda explains why I've always been drawn to marijuana. I've had hit or miss experiences while "high", but I've always liked the feeling of the day after effect. I often wished I could just get the day after effect without the intense experience of the "high" itself. Kat's afraid, I'm going to get into "drugs" again, but I've been thinking more of getting into meditaiton. My mind is in to fragile of a state right now for anything too intense. I'm not sure yet quite how I want to recreate my view of reality. I may just have to take a break and let my mind settle a little before taking another dive into my search for ultimate truth. Also I'm not sure how much of my depression is based on my thoughts vs. my environment. Time will tell. I'm quite familiar with cognitive behavioral therapy, and mixing that with meditation/midfullness, should help the thought side, and my local environment can be improved in some ways, but I know the EMF, RF in particular, is going to continue to increase around the world, despite my efforts.

Dexter, don't worry, I will probably get some kind of shielding up this week for the smart meter. I eventually plan on making a faraday cage for our sleeping area. Still not sure, if that will be upstairs or in the basement. I need to do some testing.

As far as CT, I have a compression shirt that fits now. So that's one step down. Its been warm outside, and I've been feeling healthy again. I think I'm almost ready to give it a proper try.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-23T22:35:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autism-with-a-side-of-bacon.7107/post-88765>

Well the last two blog's should be burned into your mind........when you have a neocortical disease you need to realize that pregnenolone is a neurosteroid that affect the CNS. Pregnenolone was originally only thought to be made in the adrenal glands but now we know it is made most predominately in the brain. This is why the current blog, QB 10 is a huge factor. Most neurodegeneration and spectrum disorders are associated with low pregnenolone levels peripherally and in the brain........but here is a pearl for you to know.........pregnenolone also increases brain acetylcholine levels.......this is why the brain in AD has low Acetylecholine, cholesterol and low pregnenolone levels.......the reason should be intuitive now......it is because of an alter Vitamin A signal and a low free T3 level because the body energy's status is poor. This is why we refer to AD as type three DM now. This answer here is a big insight to why AD and other brain disorders are exploding........in the modern world.

### Christopher — 2013-12-01T17:09:42+0200

Time for an update. I didn't realize it has been so long. I kept waiting to post again because I was going through a lot of changes. Physically, I have been feeling good since the beginning of summer. Mentally, I have completely re-aranged my entire belief system. I mean like completely rebuilt from the ground up. I'll try not to get to caught up on the details, as I have learned that some things are just for me, and don't apply to others in the same way. I've made some discoveries about my self that have been truly liberating. I find the impact of a change in thinking can be just as drastic as a change of diet and lifestyle.

Today on facebook, I saw Kruse posted one of my first posts on the forum. Anxiety gone because of the leptin rx. It was a nice reminder of where I came from, and how thankful I am to be where I am today.

### Christopher — 2013-12-02T02:36:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autism-with-a-side-of-bacon.7107/post-102008>

Along the line of the thoughts I was having this morning... I was thinking about a certain, lets say, ideology I have now. It causes me to feel joy and happiness. If I had tried to accept this ideology 2 years ago, it would have caused me to have a severe panic attack. But now I embrace it, and have it as a tool I can use to get the most out of life. Now instead of searching for the meaning of life, I am searching myself to figure out what I really want to make of my life. The possibilities are endless...

Kate and the kids are doing great. Kate has lost like, a million pounds now and is still going. Ryan is doing very well in his mainstream first grade classroom. Lizzie is in preschool now and loves it. After reading some stuff about mythology from Joseph Campbell, I decided to introduce the kids to the Star Wars movies. They both love it, especially Ryan. It has given him some variety to his special interests, which is a good thing. Numbers and Letters were getting old. lol.

Something else I did this summer was grow an ass. lol. But seriously, my but used to be saggy and wrinkly since my early 20s. Kate used to say it was like the ass of a 90 year old man. But, not anymore. My weight went up from 130 to 135 and my buns look like how they should have looked when I was 20.

# Thread: Natto and some other PQQ rich foods
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/natto-and-some-other-pqq-rich-foods.7109/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9

### Albert83BCN — 2013-05-19T21:02:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/natto-and-some-other-pqq-rich-foods.7109/post-86478>

Sorry if it has been discussed before (new to the forums!)... I've just found this study on the richest dietary sources of PQQ.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1136652/pdf/biochemj00065-0028.pdf](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1136652/pdf/biochemj00065-0028.pdf)

The thing is that Natto seems to be a huge source, but its soy in the end, which is pretty condemned in the paleosphere. I was wondering what your thoughts were on this, would be Natto really that bad? May the high PQQ content and fermentation process overcome the downside of its possible phytates, goitrogens and phytoestogens?

I know Jack is pretty sold on to the PQQ stuff and I myself as an endurance runner I'm very interested in trying to protect my mitochondria from the possible damage I may be putting them on...

Thoughts?

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-19T22:03:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/natto-and-some-other-pqq-rich-foods.7109/post-86480>

There are plenty of other legumes besides soy and Bacillus subtilis bacteria would munch on them happily. Natto can be also be made using lentils, beans, and peas. It is possible to buy a small bottle of the bacterial innoculant from Gem Cultures, put a tiny squirt in a cup of water and drink it while eating a high protein meal which I have done. That tiny bottle can innoculate over 80 pounds of beans. The question for me is whether ingesting the innoculant will produce any PQQ.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-19T22:26:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/natto-and-some-other-pqq-rich-foods.7109/post-86484>

There is a lot more than just natto:[http://pyrroloquinoline-quinone.com/pqq-info/pqq-rich-food/](http://pyrroloquinoline-quinone.com/pqq-info/pqq-rich-food/)

### Albert83BCN — 2013-05-20T10:40:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/natto-and-some-other-pqq-rich-foods.7109/post-86525>

Thanks **PMGlenPDQ** for the input, I wasn't aware that it can be made with other legumes, BTW on food stores I haven't seen it and even if I had it's still legumes in the end... for now I may try to improve a little bit my diet scheme by adding some more parsley and celery as a spices for my plates and use some kiwis from time to time where I'd use oranges, but It's a pity that I suspect that the benefit will be pretty poor compared to supplemental form.

Thanks **Jack** for the link although all these foods where listed on the other study as well! hehe

### JohnGalt — 2013-08-09T08:44:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/natto-and-some-other-pqq-rich-foods.7109/post-91916>

Albert, Yes, Natto is a soy product BUT it is a FERMENTED soy product. And this makes a difference. The process of bacterial fermentation breaks down and destroys many of the phytates, goitrogens and phytoestrogens that make UNfermented soy such a problem. Generally speaking, FERMENTED soy products are MUCH safer to consume. I hope this helps.

### Scompy — 2016-04-06T20:04:32+0300

2 huge connections just made:

### Penny — 2016-04-06T23:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/natto-and-some-other-pqq-rich-foods.7109/post-192189>

Maybe it's the potassium in the greens... BTW, when calcium effluxes, it is replaced by the single bond of potassium (calcium has a double bond) - but as far as natto goes, just do what the japanese do: buy a small package of slimy natto in an asian store ($1.79). Get a package of soy beans from Trader Joes ($2.00) or at said asian story. Boil the soy beans/lentils/peas. Cool. Run some boiled water over the shallow pyrex dish to sterilize it. Put the cooled beans in the bowl, mix in the natto and stick it in your oven with the light bulb on for 1-2 days - trust me, slime will form...  To get it down, use scallions and coconut aminos...

### Penny — 2016-04-06T23:10:10+0300

Put the cooled beans in the bowl, mix in the natto and stick it in your oven with the light bulb on for 1-2 days - trust me, slime will form...  To get it down, use scallions and coconut aminos...

# Thread: Italian court rules Autism caused by MMR vaccine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-20T18:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/post-86542>

[http://www.collective-evolution.com...cine-caused-autism-us-media-blacks-out-story/](http://www.collective-evolution.com/2013/05/08/italian-court-rules-mmr-vaccine-caused-autism-us-media-blacks-out-story/)

"The debate over vaccines continues as an Italian court ruled in favor of the Bocca family whose nine-year-old son became autistic after receiving the MMR (Measles/Mumps & Rubella) vaccine. I came across this case and felt it was a good idea to report on this as the vaccine debate has been a hot topic here lately. Although the case concluded in 2012, the information is just as relevant today.

UPDATE: We realize the controversy surrounding Wakefield and his study, however the point is others have replicated his findings numerous times with accuracy and it backs up his claims. Instead of focusing on Wakefield, observe the other studies involved and the massive growing movement to re-think vaccines based on science and evidence. We also recognize that Autism is a spectrum disorder, meaning that there is a wide range in how it affects people. A phenomenon due to vaccines could account for some type of Autism on that spectrum. On the other hand we recognize that there could be be some genetic differences within ones DNA that could result in showing behavior and other types of characteristics seen in what we have labelled as Autism or ASD."

Dr Wakefield is interviewed regarding the use of MMR in England and how the British government has not discontinued using MMR.

Dr Wakefield is also interviewed by Dr. Mercola.

The mainstream press in the US has completely suppressed these stories of overseas of ASD aspects of vaccines.

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-20T20:14:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/post-86546>

This really doesn't have anything to do with Autism, but about money...

Can shots make symptoms worse in kids with Autism, yes, absolutely.. I saw it happen with both kids.. Both kids regressed, but, with therapy regained almost everything they lost.. and it took a long time to do so... was it the cause of their Autism, uhmm no... I knew both kids were different from the beginning.. For example, Lizzie, from birth to 18 months, very very very rarely squeaked or coo'ed or babbled.. she had a straight face.. She HATED to be held, but loved her swing... Could barely breast feed her.. She didn't track items and she didn't turn to see what sounds were... At 6 months when her hearing and vision tests came back normal, I bet the doctor 50 dollars that she had Autism. He called me crazy, but paid up when her tests came back a year later... I want everyone to know that going to a primary care doc for Autism is like going to a foot doctor for a shoulder problem.. and somehow.. around 19 months, she randomly just started using words labeling things (one year into speech therapy)...

Here's my thing.. whatever the view on shots are... Me personally, I would rather give my kids shots and have Autism symptoms worse... than some horrible disease that is preventable... thoughts?

Also, I read an article not too long ago that there was a study done where they did brain scans on babies that were not born yet.. they showed that kids with Autism brains actually formed differently in the womb. I'll have to find it again and post it.

This is a brilliant post in many ways. It's so easy to look for someone to blame. You manage to remain objective in what be an emotional situation.

I work full-time with kids with autism and have for 7 years. The debate continues but some facts remain. Wakefield's study which links autism to vaccines, is not a credible one. The Lancet, which originally published the study, has since retracted it:

[http://www.nytimes.com/2010/02/03/health/research/03lancet.html?\_r=0](http://www.nytimes.com/2010/02/03/health/research/03lancet.html?_r=0)

Wakefield's study has NOT been replicated.

And if anecdotes are compelling, here's mine: I had my 3 year old daughter vaccinated. She does not have autism. I have no regrets about my choice to vaccinate her.

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-20T21:20:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/post-86552>

Autism Speaks website states:

" What causes Autism?
Not long ago, the answer to this question would have been “we have no idea.” Research is now delivering the answers. First and foremost, we now know that there is no one cause of autism just as there is no one type of autism. Over the last five years, scientists have identified a number of rare gene changes, or mutations, associated with autism. Research has identified more than a hundred autism risk genes. In around 15 percent of cases, a specific genetic cause of a person’s autism can be identified. However, most cases involve a complex and variable combination of genetic risk and environmental factors that influence early brain development.

In other words, in the presence of a genetic predisposition to autism, a number of nongenetic, or environmental, influence further increase a child’s risk. The clearest evidence of these environmental risk factors involves events before and during birth. They include advanced parental age at time of conception (both mom and dad), maternal illness during pregnancy, extreme prematurity and very low birth weight and certain difficulties during birth, particularly those involving periods of oxygen deprivation to the baby’s brain. Mothers exposed to high levels of pesticides and air pollution may also be at higher risk of having a child with ASD. It is important to keep in mind that these factors, by themselves, do not cause autism. Rather, in combination with genetic risk factors, they appear to modestly increase risk.

A small but growing body of research suggests that autism risk is less among children whose mothers took prenatal vitamins (containing folic acid) in the months before and after conception.

Increasingly, researchers are looking at the role of the immune system in autism. Autism Speaks is working to increase awareness and investigation of these and other issues, where further research has the potential to improve the lives of those who struggle with autism."

We always need to employ the precautionary principle when we really do not know the efficacy vs safety curve for anything we advocate.

# Thread: Living on the Equator - Thoughts?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### badger — 2013-05-22T19:41:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86677>

Hello, first post here. Thanks for clicking.

So, in about a month I will be relocating somewhere South of America, to +/- latitude 0Â°. I've read a good bit of blog posts here, I've read nearly the whole CT series and I'm starting to get into Gut/Brain etc... Trying to figure out where I'm at on my health journey.

From what I can understand, one major emphasis here is on the evolutionary effects of changing light cycles on carb availability & human circadian biology, as well as exposure to cold environments and adaptation to the cold, & the effects thereof.

Where I'll be living there is no change in light cycle, it's 12h light / 12h dark, forever and always. It's also in the mountains, so temps fluctuate from highs ~80F to lows ~50F. There is a wet season, and a less wet season.

Both myself and others have observed what seem like metabolic effects on northerners (americans and europeans mostly) who move into this region. People lose weight, sometimes quickly and dramatically, despite a diet full of white rice and white potatoes, very little in the way of vegetables, and (beef, pork, chicken) + seafood. People walk around more and tend to be less sedentary. People claim to feel better, generally more alive and vital, whether that's because of better soil health, less stress, better air quality, healthier lifestyle, or positive cultural effects, I'm not sure. I have an intuition that it has to do with changes in circadian biology when living close to the equator.

I'd be interested to hear what people here think about the possible short term & long term effects of living close to the equator. How do recommendations for optimal health via diet + lifestyle change in such an environment vs. in northern, 4-seasons climes?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-23T02:22:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86691>

you must the read Quantum biology 7......a must.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-23T02:24:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86692>

### badger — 2013-05-26T00:18:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86855>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=86692)
>
> When you live equatorial things in your diet become flexible provided you understand water conduction and the photoelectric effect.

So, taking care of healing the various semiconductors you speak of in QB7 and discuss in epi-paleoRX would create the natural ability to absorb and convert sunlight into chemical energy... then living at a latitude where one would be able to absorb MORE sunlight over the course of the year, consistently spread throughout the year, translates into the ability to take on more flexibility in the diet without sacrificing optimal health? Now by flexibility do you mean... more leeway in carb intake??

I remember reading the blog post where you were talking about the health differences in the people of northern and southern India, how the people in the tropical south were vastly sicker than in the mountainous north. Is there an example of biologically-appropriate and 'ancestral' life-ways and diets from the equatorial zone? Polynesian, lowland Amazonian tribes, perhaps? What of the possibility of someone with non-native genetics adapting to such a different bioregion?

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-26T00:31:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86857>

I'm awfully interested in this as well. My fiance and I don't plan on staying in America for our whole lives (but I was thinking somewhere in Colorado would be nice - mountainous, can perform Intermittent Hypoxia Training, etc.). Realistically we will be here for a minimum of the next 2-3 years after I further my education and seek out what graduate schools to attend, possibly even the next 5-7 years. After, though, we've thought about leaving the country. She has family out in Poland (small towns, farms, grass-lands with plenty of streams, rivers, ponds for CT and grounding), and although this sounds outlandish, some innovative architects have created housing in Vietnam that can float in case of floods/natural disasters, are made from recycled, biodegradable products, extremely sturdy and protective against winds, and has "hanging gardens" on the side of the home for herbs and veggies. I know from what I've been learning here that a decision such as this would impact my health, performance, longevity, and subjective well-being dramatically.

What's confusing me is, I know as a species we evolved in Africa near the equator, then migrated to different regions. In fact, one group of early human ancestors ended up mating with Denisovian's, so some have Denisovian blood to this day (I wonder how that would affect their dietary choices to fully express epigenetics/genetics). But I believe that Dr. Kruse stated (correct me if I'm wrong), we have "aquatic ape ancestors" from the ice age, so then wouldn't cold, dark, and inland be the best location to live rather than dry, light, warm and arid? If you became cold adapted to the point of Dr. Kruse or Wim Hoff, would you even need as much sunlight if eating a proper diet (high protein/fat, zero carb) for uncoupling of proteins and what not? Wow, I have a lot more to learn, haha.

### Optimalbound — 2013-05-26T01:42:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86861>

A couple of years ago, I spent a week on St. Helena, a tiny island in the south Atlantic, 11 degrees south of the equator. I don't exactly know the dietary habits of the locals, but they have an endless supply of fresh seafood and sunshine, grassfed meat, absolutely no cell phones at all, and hardly any wifi. Yet I've never seen so many obese and unhealty looking people anywhere. Does anyone have any idea what's going on there?

### caroline — 2013-05-26T03:44:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86868>

A few years ago when we were in the Cook Islands - people didn't seem to be very healthy. I had to see a Doc and she told us that the incidence of Diabetes is very high ......

### badger — 2013-06-04T05:16:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-87424>

Also remember, the equator is more or less ringed by so-called "third world" countries, places that have been largely colonized and systematically dismantled and stripped of resources by the empires throughout history. I'd think much of the sickness and malnutrition that exists in the tropics has to do with bad dietary choices, which have supplanted traditional diets in many tropical places. Apparently the Maori, native Hawaiians, and other comparable tropical, tribal people, in terms of their retention of traditional life-ways and diets, are very vibrant, strong and healthy people still. One can still go to many places in the Andes where 80 yr-old women carrying giant bundles on their backs for miles uphill can be seen commonly. I guess another question that comes up is, if you live in the tropics it never gets below freezing (unless you're 10,000ft up a volcano), so what is the importance of cold adaption and Cold Thermogenesis there?

Having a time wrapping my brain around all this... maybe I should start at the Gut/BrainRX and go from there

### kathylu — 2013-06-04T07:15:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-87425>

I grew up in Hawaii. Many obese Polynesians there. Culturally obesity is quite acceptable there...the royalty of Hawaii (the Alii) were all fat, purposefully so. It represented wealth, health and fertility. That being said, the modern diet there is basically all carbs. Rice, beans, taro root (poi), cassava, sweet potatoes, saimin (noodles), lots of sugar and processed foods. Probably the healthiest thing is the seafood and prodigious quantities of Spam eaten...

I think the population has subverted the healthy environment...artificial light, too many processed carbs and grains. Their bodies can only take so many insults...the environment can't help them.

### badger — 2013-06-17T02:06:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-88199>

Hey Dr. Jack, I've definitely been diving deep, reading as much as I can muster (although sitting in a chair and looking at an artifical-light box is kind of tragi-comic, so I keep it to a reasonable minimum) and going hard with the CT face-dunking. Listened to all the podcasts I could find as well. Thank you so very much for your big heart and compassion for the world and the misled modern warm-adapted hominids, ha! For your drive and commitment to your message and the delivery of said message.... The flower does not bloom until its time is right, else the bloom will not set seed for the flowering of future generations. That's a message from a mentor of mine...

OK, so I hear what you're saying in CT 6. Doesn't it stand to reason that it would also be a circadian mis-match for a person to perform the CT protocol long-term while living in a climate that doesn't see a classic "winter", sub-freezing temps, or is the mis-match inherent in living in that "sub-saharan" rift valley in the first place (which I think I remember you saying), particularly if said person's genetic heritage is from a further northern latitude? I get that despite where you might live, if you're a human being you will benefit from cold adaptation, particularly if you're sick.

In practical terms, I've been doing CT on and off for a few weeks and have buckled down over the past 5 days now that I've landed here (on the equator). There's also a creek right next door that I've been getting in which is around 60deg, my guess. The climate is actually fairly cool since I'm at about 4,000ft, right in the 55-75 range. I've noticed with the face dunks, I can stay under and breathe through a straw, up to 8-10 minutes now. Strangely enough this makes my sinuses really itchy and I sneeze and get mucous-y as hell. Also noticed how, after dunking in the creek for 20 min or enough till I started shivering, afterward my hands were numb for over an hour and just wouldn't get warm.. obviously I'm not cold-adapted. My hands also fall asleep really easily when I'm sleeping, lying down in bed. Just saying. Bad circulation?

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-25T05:48:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/post-86834>

Hey everyone! I have a quick question on what I should do in regards to the LeptinRx. I've been reading and annotating all the blogs starting from Dr. Kruse' very first blog, including looking through all the citations and comments and suggested resources. I've just reached Hormones 101... I can't even explain how much I have learned from only these first few blogs. What I came to ask, though, is I skimmed a blog of Jack's (Adrenal Rx/Fatigue blog) and it made me think I should do the Leptin Rx. I have the dark circles under my eyes, I don't _always_ wake up energized (more often than not, though), I wake up a lot during the night, and I have inconsistent sleep patterns. The thing is, I've already been IFing for months now, and I can easily go the day without getting hungry. The past month I just started Bulletproof Intermittent fasting, which is essentially a fast minus fat and Upgraded Coffee. I have even begun fasted state body by science training with an elevation training mask. I'm also cycling in and out of ketosis. What I'm getting at is I'm obviously fat adapted, not fully adapted (24-36months... I've been adapted for a few months), and I don't think I'm LR as I'm lean and trying to build some muscle.

I just know that I have had very faulty sleep schedules as a child all the way up until the last few months (after discovering Dave Asprey/Jack), sleeping many nights 4-5hrs then waking up to a full day of school followed by intense football or wrestling practice. Couple this with a poor diet and poor lifestyle choices, throw in a few months of vegetarianism in there, I feel as though I have at least damaged my adrenals/thyroid a bit. I can't afford testing though. I can just tell subjectively and from the dark circles under my eyes/skin quality.

I was thinking about marrying some of the LeptinRx ideas with the post-LeptinRx ideas. What I mean is, consume a BAB, skip lunch, eat dinner 4-5hrs before sleeping. I will set alarms to wake up every day at 9 AM meaning I'll get 6-8hrs of sleep a day, ASAP after rising I will consume a tbsp of sea salt and get out in the sun and look into the sky for a few minutes while my BPC is brewing. Workout once a week BBS style and refeed with some carbs since it is summer and getting close to June 21 (the time Jack stated he consumes the most carbs). My only concerns were HIT exercise and the carb refeed. **Would the refeed and/or exercise some how completely disrupt the LeptinRx**, or no since I'll still be refraining from snacking and creating a morning ritual of breakfast/sea salt/sun/self oxytocin release/Cold shower? **Should I even attempt this for the next 8-12weeks or refrain since I'm most definitely not LR/IR.... maybe just jump to the post-leptinRx style of eating**? To put it into perspective, I do the BBS workout as I feel I need to gain weight (maybe another reason for the Leptin Reset) and once I have the funds again I will be engaging in BJJ while striving to compete in tournaments, the well structured HIT will give me an edge.

Sorry for lengthening this more than I thought I would, I just don't know which route I should go. Thanks to everyone who has commented on my recent threads and helped me, and thanks in advance for all of you who may chime in here!

### Dextery — 2013-05-25T06:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/post-86835>

Think about marrying the Leptin RX with the epi-paleo diet. Eat lots of seafood at BAB along with lots of eggs and bacon and yams or sweet potatos and lots of coconut oil this summer. Switch to animal fats in the winter. This is enough to carry you through to dinner. Then eat a very large green salad with oilive oil and vinegar, lots of veggies slathered with butter and more seafood. Sardines work well for me. They are very cheap at Walmart in a can with tomato sauce. I rinse off the tomato sauce and heat them in a frying pan with medium chain triglyceride oil or coconut oil smothered with any kind of cheese I happen to have. I also get canned oysters at Walmart. 8 oz for $1.86. Eggs and seafood...the staples of life.

Charles Washington...google him...is a runner that is 100% no carb and does quite well in his races. He has long ago converted himself to burn fat rather than carbs. Even in the summer.

Drink only non fluoridated water. No tap water (bulk water) Many buy spring water for .89 a gallon and most drink at least a gallon a day. You will get hydrated by getting the water into your cells. If the water has fluoride in it, it does not penetrate the cells at all. You will know when you are fully hydrated properly because your skin will feel very smooth all over and you will start to not need as much food because you will be using the energy from the water to burn. And you will put on weight and strenth with less food.

Think about using Lugol's iodine in your water which will kick out of your system, any fluoride, bromine, chlorine, and aluminum out of your body so that your liver, thyroid begin to wake up and operate properly. Grizz on this forum is the Iodine expert. Look at the entire Iodine thread to see what Iodine will do for you.

BBS is great once a week as he says for 20 min or less. Plus think about Tabata type sprinting program 3x per week. run full out 20 sec, rest 10 sec...repeat 8 or 10 rounds and then stop. That is all you need.

Get your food and water correct and your dark circles will go away and you will start to feel awesome.

### Dextery — 2013-05-25T07:54:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/post-86838>

I really think you are over thinking your road to optimal health. Do the Leptin Reset with Oysters and Fish and Eggs and Bacon. Look for cuts of meat that look like roasts for under $2.99 a pound and slice them up into mini steaks and have one with the fish. BASalads with olive oil and vinegar each evening and veggies with butter. Notice almost nobody on this forum ever talks about refeed at any time. Just eat normally. Carbs are not really in the lexicon to stay in ketosis. For me it is the best for the brain and the body.

Do BBS once a week to failure for 20 minutes...then you are done. Will not interfere with the Leptin RX. Just eat real food twice a day

Omega three fatty acids are just fine. Once we eat fish with O3 we eventually get to a ratio of O6 to O3 of 4 to 1. Don't worry about it. We need Omega 6 fatty acids to coagulate blood in the event of an injury or a surgery.

Do as much cold thermogenesis as you can to become cold adapted. Living in Illinois, you have winter once a year. Make the most of it.
JERF. Just eat real food and you will be just fine. Eat your eggs, bacon, sardines or oysters and/or beef steaks or beef liver...all cooked in animal fat or butter or coconut oil and be happy. You will see changes in body comp that will surprise you.

You did not mention water in your response. You haven't gotten to the Quantum biology series to learn why drinking non fluoridated water is extremely important for you. Never drink tap water again. Your body will thank you.

A lot of us - when doing the BAB went down to 2 meals a day pretty quickly .... I never eat lunch - not hungry but I do eat dinner around 4:30pm

You seem to really worry about your food alot ...but you are underweight - sorry - I am obviously missing something.....

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-27T02:48:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/post-86913>

I **was** fasting for a variety of reasons - autophagy (which being in ketosis already promotes chaperone mediated autophagy, so that's +2), increased concentration while fasting with the inclusion of Bulletproof Coffee, and then lifting when fasted drastically increases protein synthesis and growth hormone/testosterone release; also I could do it.... by that I mean I could wake up and if I want just not eat, if I felt hungry have some BPC and continue up until dinner. I've been doing the LeptinRx for two days now, tomorrow I plan on working out and eating some carbs but probably still a very small amount (50g post workout from sweet potato) covered in butter and upping the protein a bit, still having my BAB and no snacks... but for instance, today I woke up late and finished my BAB probably 45mins after rising, it's now almost 7PM and I haven't eaten, I'm about to eat dinner but I don't even feel as though I have to. It's also hard for me to get in calories to gain weight/muscle spacing out my meals this far but I feel as though it might be beneficial to my liver/pancreas/galbladder/etc.

I do worry about my food and it is something I'm working on daily. Don't want that unnecessary rise in cortisol now do we ? But I mainly worry about it because if you read my paleohacks question from awhile back (which Dr. Kruse commented on), well, then, you would see. I notice INSTANTLY how food impacts my well being and if it's in a negative way, it's normally in the form of visual disturbances which further facilitate anxiety.... not really something I want to deal with especially being recently engaged, in college, and trying to get back into martial arts to compete.

Again, though, I'm not IFing right now.... Leptin Reset time; my questions were really just if I could consume carbs after I workout and if I can continue my weight lifting; I was worried about that interfering with the processes of resetting leptin and my ultradian/circadian rhythms, but I was justifying it due to (1) it being summer (2) carbs post workout (3) still following the rules of no snacking and a BAB (4) post leptin eating states that big protein meal + carbs is eaten in the afternoon after working out.

### Inger — 2013-05-27T08:25:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/post-86930>

BPC777,
I am so glad if I could be to any help! Now.. I personally am not so sure this BP coffee fasting is a great idea.. if you have dark circles under your eyes and stuff.. I think more than one cup of coffee / day is not really good long term... at least that is what all my research and my body tells me. If I were you I would try more raw, helps for anxiety too, at least for me. Raw meat and fish and veggies (and fruits if you like) does wonder for my mind! I need it.. I really do. I no way feel well with much cooked, it makes me feel a bit numb and anxious inside.. very minor but I do not like that at all. I can go with a tiny bit lightly cooked fish or mussles though, but raw is always better, makes me feel better! You should try it BPC77. I am also quite sure raw feeds the muscles better. Bodybuilders used to eat raw meat, it is almost like a steroid! So if you want to gain muscles and fitness I would suggest do not cook your food.. you can make nice smoothies of raw eggs, and nice carpaccios or tartar or jerky or pemmican of raw meat and heart and from raw fish you can make lovely chevice, tartar, sashimi etc. I also have very little money so I often buy frozen wild caught pollock, and I thaw it and I make it into a nice raw pate', add whatever you like and in the end it tastes super good! Because I do not really like it plain and raw, like I do with salmon and mackerels etc.

Oh.. try to get raw grassfed heart.. lamb or beef or moose or deer.. Oh man that is SO good for mood, for anything! It is quite cheap too! If you have stress in your life this is the best thing I can recommend in addition to raw seafood. But you must eat it raw to have that great effect. It tastes great, like meat with a touch of liver!

# Thread: Beginne; what is my problem
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beginne-what-is-my-problem.7165/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LRichard12 — 2013-05-27T17:16:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beginne-what-is-my-problem.7165/post-86945>

Hi my name is Lucas. I am 22 years old, 6foot tall, 150 lbs, around 12-14% body fat. I have recently (over the past year) lost 100 lbs (from 250 to 150). I was obese my entire life, suffered from high BP, chronic fatigue, laziness, low motivation, brain fog, poor memory, depression, etc. I then developed heart palpitations and the chroic fatigue and neuro problems worsened. I tried everything in the book, had every blood test, have taken every anti depressant and stimulant the neurologist and GP could throw at me. I've taken every supplement under the sun (and spent a small fortune doing so), and had even turned to drugs to help (found hydrocodon and tramadol worked the best). I've learned to manage and press on, by the grace of GOD, day by day drinking coffee, yerba mate, green tea, taking endless combinations of supplements, and eating what I thought was a balanced diet. Then after coming back from Africa (I am a missionary from the USA, based in Dublin, serving in West Africa) last year weighing in over 250lbs (probably closer to 300) and decided that it was time to lose weight. So I switched to a pure veggie, fruit, and low fat cottage cheese diet, cut out all added sugars and deserts, ate mueslie cereal, skim mile, flax seed, goji berries, bannnanas, and apple juice for breakfast, etc. I started jogging and hiking at least 3 mornings a week and eventually joined a gym. I lost a few lbs and felt a bit better though still fighting with chronic fatigue, low motivation, and heavily relient on coffee. Also was still experimenting with antidepressants.

Then I decided to go super low calorie, eating mostly protein shakes, low fat cottage cheese, and fat free unsweetened organic yogurt (used styevia to sweeten). I dropped a few more lbs, but was hungry all of the time! Then I was introduced to Atkins (thank GOD). I switched to Atkins and lost the rest of my wieght fairly quickly, going down below 150, while building muscle. I still stayed very low calorie (1200/day) for a while and ate only 3 meals a day, no snacks. However, every one kept telling me that I needed to up my calorie intake. So I uped them by adding in 2 snacks of nuts (either almonds or walnuts) and more olive, walnut, or coconut oil. Up until that point I had full appetite control, and had no trouble eating less than 1000 calories per day if I wanted to. Then once I added the snacks and uped my calories I began to get hungry. However I maintained control.

Then I went to Africa in February. While in Africa my appetite continued to increase, eventually becoming ravenous. I lost control for a couple of weeks and gorged on peanuts, gaining a couple of lbs. Then regained control forcing myself to stay around 1800 calories and <25 net carbs perday. Now I am back in Dublin and I'm over come by my hunger, my obsession with food, it is all I can think about, and I cant seem to stay full. I am eating 3 solid meals a day, under 20 net carbs, 100 grams protein, 150 grams of fat (mostly from olive oil and avacoados), and all of the low carb veggies that I can ingest. I still seem to be hungry.

Recently I discovered Mark Sission's Primal diet and the paleo diet, read MAstering Leptin, and the perfect 10 diet. I then came across Jack Kruses Leptin RX. After reading into it I think that my problem is low leptin. So I tried a primal refeed (sweet potatoes, berries, low fat organic unsweetened yogurt, honey, etc) last week, with around 500 grams carb, 50g fat, 130 or so protein, and nearly 3000 cals, which kind of helped. Then I started the Leptin RX , eating 50 grams of protein at breakfast (eggs, hard cheese, almonds, and coconut oil) after which I am still hungry. So today I am trying another refeed (roast beef (for morning protein) sweet potatoes, white potatoes, parsnips, low fat organic unsweetened yogurt (with stevia and FOS to sweeten)). I'm really hoping that this works! Tomorrow its back to low carb/Leptin Rx. I am trying to maintain nutritional ketosis (for the thereputic and weight loss benifits), however I find that first thing in the morning my fasting blood ketones are consistently .3 mmol (they where around 2.0 to 3.0 fasting before leaving for Africa in Feb), then creep up to nearly 2.0 by the afternoon. So I'm trying not to over do the protein (I keep to low 100's; 120 or so from eggs, hard natural cheese, nuts, and 2 meals of wild salmon a day), eat high fat (150 or so), and 20 net carbs (from veggies, organic unsweetened greek yogurt, and nuts). I have been abvoiding snacks and processed foods for over 1 month now, I drink only filtered water, go for everything organic, and take evey supplement recomemended by the lowcarb/paleo books I've read (I.E. GPC, PS, NAC, Acyl-L-Carnitine, L-Carnosine, vit d3, vit E, Magnesium, Potasium, Chromium, R-ALA, Calcium, Zinc, Omega 3, 6, 7, 9, extra DHA and EPA, vit C, green tea w/hoodia, cinnamon, kelp (also eat Kombu), 5 grams+ natural sea salt, Melatonin (2mg 1 hour before bed w/ magnesium to sleep), broad spectrum probiotics, and 40 grams of F.O.S per day, and an iron free multi vit). Still have little to no energy, irritable some times, weak, hungry!, obsessed with food, and craving sweet potatos and chocolate. It wasn't like this before. What's my problem and what do you recommend I do to fix it.

BTW I just ordered PQQ and reservatrol, will add it to my L-Carnosine, NAC, L Carnitine, RALA, GPC, and PS; hoping that that will help.

Please help. Thank you!

Lucas

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-27T17:45:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beginne-what-is-my-problem.7165/post-86948>

you need more fat with your 50 gms of protein in your diet......when you lose this much weight fast it is critical. I have not talked about this but this is exactly what happened to me when I lost 133 lbs in 11 months.

**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-27T18:02:59+0300

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2013/05/130525143852.htm](http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2013/05/130525143852.htm)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-27T18:04:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-q10-has-a-powered-study.7166/post-86951>

Professor Mortensen said: "CoQ10 is the first medication to improve survival in chronic heart failure since ACE inhibitors and beta blockers more than a decade ago and should be added to standard heart failure therapy."

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-27T18:04:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-q10-has-a-powered-study.7166/post-86952>

References: 1. Heart Failure Congress 2013 [http://www.escardio.org/congresses/hf2013/Pages/welcome.aspx?hit=nav](http://www.escardio.org/congresses/hf2013/Pages/welcome.aspx?hit=nav) 2. SA Mortensen, A Kumar, P Dolliner, et al. The effect of coenzyme Q10 on morbidity and mortality in chronic heart failure. Results from the Q-SYMBIO study. Presented at Heart Failure Congress 2013 Final Programme Number 440. The full title of the Q-SYMBIO study is: "Coenzyme Q10 as adjunctive treatment of chronic heart failure: a randomised double blind multicentre trial with focus on changes in symptoms, biomarker status with BNP and long term outcome"

### Albert83BCN — 2014-05-08T20:16:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-q10-has-a-powered-study.7166/post-125904>

This thread comes quite in handy... today my dad visited his cardiologist. I explained to him many times how statins are not the answer to fight CVD, that cholesterol is life and that needs to be managed through lifestyle... and to tell his doc to reconsider the therapy... he asked her but she made no sense, she argued that statins not only work by lowering LDL but that their have other benefits that he needs. Yeah I'm aware that they are anti-inflammatory and reduce interferons but that can certainly be ruled out with proper diet and lifestyle, then you have good immune function plus the benefits of having natural LDL levels to fight diseases... but of course she's not dialed in this idea. Higher LDL than 140 is bad, period...

The saddest thing is that my dad believes in me and I think he knows that I'm into the correct path, yet he is very troubled to fight and go agains his doc's RX... he would be medically left alone since he doesn't have a private insurance like me to go find a better one... Today I talked to him and explained how that strategy will lead to poor performance mitochondria, like cars, that will downgrade the ferrari in us into a thrased car... this idea has inspired a [quick post on my blog](http://www.nutricioblog.com/2014/05/08/sobre-els-mitocondris-el-coenzim-q10-leficiencia-i-els-cotxes/). I've Ubiquinol at home for myself, I love to give advice but don't like to interfere with other people's decisions even if they're my parents but I think this time I'll push a little bit to get him starting using it.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-05-08T23:29:22+0300

It has been like a homework to tie many things I've been learning this last year. Well, the thing is that he fully understand the message I'm telling him but he's afraid of departing with his doc, which is assigned by the National Health Service (here we call it 'Seguridad Social'), considering that he does not contemplate the possibility of getting a consult with a new one from our choice... that, of course, is his choice, and I'm fully aware that he might consider the change... I told him... he doesn't make a move foreward... I cannot push him further if he is not willing to. ### Albert83BCN — 2014-05-09T01:02:21+0300

But by doing that he got intro trouble with her doc when he had to bring back the labs to her.. as she is the one that orders the labs in the first place because we need someone to order and evaluate them... it's a catch 22... Now he's 140 TC... the doc is fine, and I'm pissed off because I know he's cutting back his natural sources of life with a damn pill.

I know that the optimal move would be to find a more collaborative cardiologist that is skeptic of the diet heart hypothesis and has a more holistic and evolutive approach to health but he doesn't want to pay for it nor I'm sure that someone like this exists here in Barcelona... that is homework for me to do....

### Gracelind — 2014-05-09T06:40:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-q10-has-a-powered-study.7166/post-125982>

Hi Albert,
Is your dad taking CoQ10 with the statin? Research shows that statins interfere with CoQ10 and that supplementing may help ward off the muscle pains and sometimes myopathy that some patients develop. Because of the muscle disease I have (myositis/myopathy) I know quite a few people online who developed pretty devasting polymyositis or dermatomyositis after taking statins. For some years the docs refused to acknowledge a connection until the evidence was overwhelming. The percentage of folks who take statins then develop myositis is relatively small for those people it's a major issue.

If his doc hasn't put him on CoQ10 she's quite behind on that part of the research.

My father was on lovastatin for years. Due to my mother's illness turning both their lives upside down we finally realized that the statin was affecting his brain and his thinking. He complained of being so confused all the time. When, with the doc's okay, we took him off, he stopped complaining about his thinking, though he still showed signs of mild dementia (he was 87). After a mild MI a different doc put him on simvastatin and the confusion flared again till he discontinued it.

It's so hard watch those we love go down the wrong path, thinking they are doing the right thing.

### Lahelada — 2014-05-09T13:54:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-q10-has-a-powered-study.7166/post-126014>

I am sure that you can find a doc that will respond if you reason awith them as you do here.A lot of docs I think work from the principle "If all you got is a hammer ,eveeything looks like a nail" Bring them a new tool!

### Albert83BCN — 2014-05-09T14:25:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-q10-has-a-powered-study.7166/post-126015>

@[Gracelind](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/329/) I'm aware of that link, thank you very much for you insight!! Yesterday I told him to start with the Ubiquinol I have at home and he agreed, right now is a good move for him to do but @[Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/) is right, we should go find the right doctor to suit a better strategy, problem is that after all the things he went through and now he seems like he has beat them and feel 'overrall well', his perception of reality is that probably he can keep like this and make it to elderly hopefully without impact... well, I think that's what he wants to believe. I'm not like that, I try to analyse what the probable outcomes will be on the future based on the knowledge I can play with right now, and I see a different picture for him not based on my wishes.

PD: You're right, knowing what many don't know and being not able to apply it to the ones you love because the force that leads the mass is stronger than any effort you might do, it's so upsetting... I guess there's some times when even if you know better you are not allowed to do better.

# Thread: Very fundamental blog for understanding is now live.  QB 8 Quantum Scaling
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-fundamental-blog-for-understanding-is-now-live-qb-8-quantum-scaling.7170/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-27T22:36:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-fundamental-blog-for-understanding-is-now-live-qb-8-quantum-scaling.7170/post-86967>

New at the blog before the podcast: [http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-8-quantum-scaling/](http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-8-quantum-scaling/)

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-27T23:25:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-fundamental-blog-for-understanding-is-now-live-qb-8-quantum-scaling.7170/post-86971>

Enjoyed this post thoroughly, going to give it some time then re-read it and annotate it to really let it sink in. It reinforces a lot of what I find to be subjectively true, and it reinforces your whole idea about the "quilt". I spoke a lot about 'awareness' in my philosophy class, and this blog really resonates with that idea. I'd love to sit down and have a chat with you about things outside of wellness, nutrition, disease, etc. and more geared toward the dualistic nature of life and reality. That being said, I have some questions and comments about what has been said in this blog.

I see how your tying things up in terms of 'hard work', 'dedication', and 'density'. I'm making metaphorical bridges to how that correlates with my life and well-being, or anyone in general on the road to recover/optimal. I think you're one of few, probably only a couple people who look at health in terms of a unified system and not fragmented units, and I love that. Using BHRT can obviously help people by increasing hormone levels, but BHRT needs to be looked at in the grand scale of things..... we can give you this and it will increase your testosterone levels, BUT if we use this improperly it may destroy other hormones due to your clock being off (ultradian/circadian rhythms) and we may damage your endogenous production because we didn't take into account all the variables outside of one simple number on a piece of paper. I could see this in terms of diet as well. You provide yourself with the density (nutrient/electron density), maybe not epi-paleo but a step in the right direction (you eliminated bad fats and added grass-fed butter to your diet), but the density doesn't make up for the directional flow of things - although you are eating 'healthier' you didn't look at the macro aspect of life... your lifestyle choices, you still are pessimistic which causes unneeded stress which stresses (not in a hormetic way) every organ and gland in your body.... and then you slip up on your new diet. I see this in the ketogenic performance aspect as well. Many people are just simply stuck in the dogma and not willing to be open minded. Let's just say hypothetically everything we learned here is false, how do we know until we try? Yet people will tell you that you need carbs for performance/strength/explosiveness/muscle/thyroid/etc., but get the hell out of some disunified aspect of the literature and look at it connected to everything, then try it for yourself. We know fat is more dense than carbs in terms of ATP.... the only thing lacking is the discipline (to adapt, to put up with the adaptation phase with decreased performance, and to change) and the work (going out there and kicking ass regardless of what people say).

My only question was how you are going to tie this into fueling performance when on the podcast today/night but I get the feeling I see where you're headed.

### caroline — 2013-05-28T02:09:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-fundamental-blog-for-understanding-is-now-live-qb-8-quantum-scaling.7170/post-86980>

Big Papa ..... You are only eighteen? ....... Wow .... You are a thinking machine.....I can't even begin to imagine your future!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-28T03:43:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-fundamental-blog-for-understanding-is-now-live-qb-8-quantum-scaling.7170/post-86990>

> [BigPapaChakra777 said:](/goto/post?id=86971)
>
> Enjoyed this post thoroughly, going to give it some time then re-read it and annotate it to really let it sink in. It reinforces a lot of what I find to be subjectively true, and it reinforces your whole idea about the "quilt". I spoke a lot about 'awareness' in my philosophy class, and this blog really resonates with that idea. I'd love to sit down and have a chat with you about things outside of wellness, nutrition, disease, etc. and more geared toward the dualistic nature of life and reality. That being said, I have some questions and comments about what has been said in this blog.
>
> I see how your tying things up in terms of 'hard work', 'dedication', and 'density'. I'm making metaphorical bridges to how that correlates with my life and well-being, or anyone in general on the road to recover/optimal. I think you're one of few, probably only a couple people who look at health in terms of a unified system and not fragmented units, and I love that. Using BHRT can obviously help people by increasing hormone levels, but BHRT needs to be looked at in the grand scale of things..... we can give you this and it will increase your testosterone levels, BUT if we use this improperly it may destroy other hormones due to your clock being off (ultradian/circadian rhythms) and we may damage your endogenous production because we didn't take into account all the variables outside of one simple number on a piece of paper. I could see this in terms of diet as well. You provide yourself with the density (nutrient/electron density), maybe not epi-paleo but a step in the right direction (you eliminated bad fats and added grass-fed butter to your diet), but the density doesn't make up for the directional flow of things - although you are eating 'healthier' you didn't look at the macro aspect of life... your lifestyle choices, you still are pessimistic which causes unneeded stress which stresses (not in a hormetic way) every organ and gland in your body.... and then you slip up on your new diet. I see this in the ketogenic performance aspect as well. Many people are just simply stuck in the dogma and not willing to be open minded. Let's just say hypothetically everything we learned here is false, how do we know until we try? Yet people will tell you that you need carbs for performance/strength/explosiveness/muscle/thyroid/etc., but get the hell out of some disunified aspect of the literature and look at it connected to everything, then try it for yourself. We know fat is more dense than carbs in terms of ATP.... the only thing lacking is the discipline (to adapt, to put up with the adaptation phase with decreased performance, and to change) and the work (going out there and kicking ass regardless of what people say).
>
> My only question was how you are going to tie this into fueling performance when on the podcast today/night but I get the feeling I see where you're headed.

Here is more for the disbelievers like Peter Attia, Robb Wolf, et al about ketogenic diets and optimal performance. The quantum world works on stochastic calculus......not ordinary linear calculus. Stochastic calculus inherently assumes a square root dependence on the variable of time. This is why markets are random and why people results are random. What this implies is that when time scales are short the result will be quite random. As time lengthens then the real benefits show up. It is why some people have massive improvements in a short period of time when they get all the details correct.......and why other s never do. It also belies why ketogenic performance takes 24-36 months to reveal itself. Too bad the biochemists don't read more physics. They might learn a few things.

Estrogen receptor b and the progression of prostate cancer:
role of
5a-androstane-3b,17b-diol

Endocrine-Related Cancer (2010) 17 731–742

(3b-Adiol) = 5a-androstane-3b,17b-diol

.......................

These data as a whole indicate
that testosterone might control prostate physiology
through several contrasting mechanisms:
a) androgenic signals via AR/testosterone and AR/DHT complexes,
b) estrogenic signals via ER (a and b)/E2
and
c) alternative estrogenic signaling via ER (b)/3b-Adiol.
The goal of this study was to assess the effects of 3b-Adiol on PC cell proliferation, migration, and
invasiveness in vitro as well as in vivo in xenograft models of tumorigenesis in nude mice

**3b-Adiol**, DHT, and E2(R,R0)-cis-diethyltetrahydro-2,8-chrysenediol (R,R0-THC) were obtained from **Sigma–Aldrich**.
