### Albert83BCN — 2013-07-29T16:41:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alberts-story-and-journey-to-optimal.7216/post-91092>

I also have now the results of my latest bloodwork, see previous Cortisol and DHEA-S info on post above

It's not very comprehensive, my doc just wanted to re-check liver markers a month and a half later as I got some weid numbers with AST/ALT. He also checked for hepatitis and a few different things. The results are a little bit weird. I've the same total cholesterol (that was expected because I have not followed his instructions about a low fat diet...) but better glucose levels and rockstarish trigs, transaminases got a little bit better but still high (should I order another bottle of NAC+Milk Thistle? finished it just the day before the labs) but... seems that hemolitic anaemia is back, I got a pretty low hematocrit and red cell count and also a little bit lowered white cell count...

HEMATIES **4.18 10e6/uL** \[ 4.20 - 6.10 \] -> Got worse, latest was 4.32 borderline
HEMOGLOBIN **13.1 gr/dL** \[ 12.00 - 16.00 \]
HEMATOCRITE **39.1 %** \[ 38.00 - 52.00 \] -> Got worse, latest was 39.9
MCV **93 fL** \[ 80.00 - 100.00 \]
MCH **31.3 pg** \[ 27.00 - 33.00 \]
MCHC **33.5 gr/dL** \[ 31.00 - 36.00 \]
RDW **13.2 %** \[ 11.60 - 16.00 \]

LEUCOCYTES **3.9 10e3/uL** \[ 4.00 - 10.00 \] -> Slightly better but still low, 1 1/2 month ago was 3.4
Segmentation **46.9 %** \[ 40.00 - 75.00 \]
Linfocytes **43.1 %** \[ 20.00 - 45.00 \]
Monocytes **6.3 %** \[ 2.00 - 10.00 \]
Eosinophiles **2.7 %** \[ 0.10 - 7.00 \]
Basophiles **1.00 %** \[ 0.00 - 2.00 \]

Platelets **221 10e3/uL** \[ 130.00 - 425.00 \]
MVP **8.4 fL** \[ 6.00 - 17.00 \]
PCT **0.19 %** \[ 0.15 - 0.50 \]
PDW **14 %** \[ 12.00 - 22.00 \]

GLYCEMIA **72 mg/dL** \[ 60.00 - 115.00 \] -> Almost same as last time (77), current 72 is my personal best I think
CHOLESTEROL **305 mg/dL** \[ 100.00 - 250.00 \] -> Almost the same as last time, it was 308
TRIGLYCERIDES **45 mg/dL** \[ 0.00 - 200.00 \] -> Down from 77, not bad!
CREATININE in blood **1.18 mg/dL** \[ 0.10 - 1.30 \] -> Slightly down
TRANSAMINASE GOT (ASAT) **33.41 U/L** \[ 0.00 - 40.00 \] -> Slightly down
TRANSAMINASE GPT (ALT) **43.57 U/L** \[ 0.00 - 40.00 \] -> Down by 10 points (previous 53) but still high...
G. GLUTAMILTRANSPEPTIDASE **14.73 U/L** \[ 4.00 - 49.00 \]
BILIRRUBIN DIRECT **0.56 mg/dL** \[ 0.05 - 0.40 \] -> Previous was in normal ranges
BILIRRUBIN TOTAL **1.63 mg/dL** \[ 0.20 - 1.20 \] -> Previous was in normal ranges
ALKALINE PHOSPHATASES **154.00 U/L** \[ 80.00 - 306.00 \] -> Went up from 135

ANTIGEN HBs (AUSTRALIA) **<1.00** Index \[ 0.00 - 1.00 \]
Ac ANTI-HVC **<1.00** Index \[ 0.00 - 1.00 \]

I'm a little bit confused... I attributed my previous anaemia to a combination of a vegetarian diet plus heavy endurance running. But now it has been 6 months into paleo and 1 month and a half not running and doing moderate strenght training plus a little bit of walking every day... so now I'm a little bit lost in this regard. I expected to see equal or higher values than last check but instead I went back a little bit... one thing I changed is that I stopped taking my oral iron... (but now I eat red meat and liver!) maybe it was helping?

The high bilirubin thing plus low RBC and low WBC and bad liver markers... could it might have something to do with Wilson's disease? Never heard about it before but a quick search for 'high bilirubin low hematies' prompted me to that...

Maybe I should really see a blood speciallist as JanSz suggested?

My intuition (plus all the reading here and other sites) is telling me something is off with my liver and that's why I cannot clear LDL due to poor LDL receptor, also tied with low conversion to free T3, and maybe all the low T to free T conversion might have to do with this... but I do almost everything that is in my hand to address that (no PUFA nor sugars even no High GI carbs for months right now... and eating epi-paleo) so I seem to be loosing my last bullets... which step should I consider next? Also, everyday I'm considering more and more possible auto-immune problems

### Albert83BCN — 2013-08-10T20:32:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alberts-story-and-journey-to-optimal.7216/post-92039>

Seems like my prayers didn't get too far, hehe.

Well, time to move on, since this is a journal I guess I have some things to report!

First of all, last week I had a whim and ordered a Kindle eReader from Amazon. I always want to read Jack's stuff but the fact that I had to stay near the PC or the mobile pulled me back most of the times. I started printing some stuff and bringing it to the park or beach for a nice read but it was pretty messy... almost always opted to read some of the books I have near hear instead (right now reading WDISHTSWMLTAN (lol!) from Dr Kharrazian, PHD from the Jaminets and Gary Taubes why do we get fat) so I thought that If I got myself an eReader It would be a heck of an aid to stay plugged with the quilt. Spot on, I'm now devouring the stuff, but I'm still way ahead in time as I started from scratch (right now I'm in August 2011 lol).

Second thing, and that might be a hardcore change in my life, I'm considering starting a BS in Nutrition! Seems a little bit crazy to enroll again in the university when I'm 30 and have been for 10 years studying Computer Science... as if I didn't have enough things to deal with already! But honestly... one day that idea hit my mind... and once it hit... It started forming very strongly... and I'm the kind of person that follows his insctincts

The thing is that I have been long time right now interested in nutrition, sports performance and health, OK migh be just as a hobby and passion... but the real fact is that I've been long now spending more time in this subjects than rather my own profession, and that tells me a lot. Yet, most of my friends know me as the nutrition and gym freak more any than the computer guy... so in the end maybe that's where I was supposed to be headed to?

To be honest, what I'd really like to enroll in might just be medicine. But that's a killer since it requires a lot of free time and it's no joke.. it would be impossible to work and attend classes and practice, but as for Nutrition, look how life brings on coincidence or fate, right now this year a brand new university inaugured one month ago is the first one that will allow to do it here in Spain totally online! As I've been investing a lot of hours at home doing my research until now, I'm pretty confident I can keep doing the same thing, but at least I'd obtain an acreditation somewhere in the future! Cool. More cool even, with one more year I could even get another BS in Sports Science.

Well, still not 100% sure I'll take that route (money and doubt pull me back)... but at least 80% convinced already ;D

One thing I didn't mention (I guess still too new here in this forum) is that it has been several years right now that I'm working with two mates on what could be our startup, an online social network with tracking tools. We will allow to track exercise activity (think Garmin, Runkeeper, and the likes), nutrition (think myfitnespal, calorie counter, etc) and other custom parameters (quantified self tool!)... ok I know there's already a lot of tools like these, but as an enthusiast, I wanted to bring to the world the tool that I'd have like to use for my self (very like what Jacks does here in order to change the medical profession) so that's how I get into this, our tool will have (hopefully) some cool features that most others don't have, and it's that you will be able to create custom items so that there's no single data that you could be limited to enter, ie: you could create the HS-CPR biomarker if there's no one in the tool already and then start using it!).

Ok, that's one of the things I think that might justify why I'm planning to do. Seems my life has been long tied to nutrition, health and exercise concerns and that getting into the BS might be a great addition to my profile, not just for fun but maybe even to talk cool of me if I even have a CEO profile in my website hehe. If the project fails I can always enjoy giving epi-paleo advice to my beloved ones and have a little more credit than just the guy who reads stuff on the internet. You see a BS gives that kind of trust to people, ironically I don't look at these kind of rigid things that strong...

Last but not least, these last days seems I'm feeling overall better than in the past. I've been mostly epi-paleo for 7 months now I think but I'm right now putting on more calories, I want to check two things on myself. One is about the debate on the paleosphere that low carb brings thyroid issues, some say it's spot on, some say its a calorie thing, I know Jack thinks is not either. But I've to find out what works best in my context. I also wanted to see if I can gain weight on the low carb paleo template going insane on calories, I want to get some insight about all the debate on CICO matters, not much, a little bit, not at all...

Regarding my possible cortisol issues, as I'm a little bit out of options, and I've been one month and a half without running, and had to decide what to do in the near future, I started running again, right now very easy and short runs, and going to up a little bit as I build more base. I'm doing the runs first thing on the morning, fasted, then immediately the BAB after finishing. I know this is not adviced on the reset but honestly, if I have to run I think it will be better than at evening, when I used to, as I suspect that this got my cortisol patterns backwards. And it might aid in upping morning cortisol, which could be nice in my case. As I also do strengh training, I keep this at 4 to 7PM and the, right after, I have the last meal of the day.

Well, I guess I'm done for today, it came out as a pretty long post! hehe.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-01T15:47:55+0300

### Danco3636 — 2013-06-01T17:03:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ben-greenfield-ct-optimal-performance-podcast-is-now-live.7220/post-87252>

Listening now. Thanks!!!!

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-01T18:59:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ben-greenfield-ct-optimal-performance-podcast-is-now-live.7220/post-87259>

Very interesting podcast. Established that what I'm doing since it's summer seems okay (eating carbs - although I eat them only around training). It's making me even more excited to complete all the blogs on the site and read through all associated information to really learn how to apply the material as best as I can.

There are some things I'm confused about.... Ben seems to plan on still consuming quite a bit of carbs on his ketogenic diet, he seems to be making it ketogenic by supplementing MCT's rather than being truly in ketosis and then adding MCT's to further it. Wouldn't he not be able to enter the PPP if he's consuming up to 200g carbs in a day, or is his training diminishing all the carbs he is eating? In addition to that, I have a question for YOU (Dr. Kruse) specifically. Do you eat carbs in summer because it is **optimal**, or simply because the long light cycle/photoelectric effect allows you to get away with it? I'm just wondering if it would even be worth my time to do CT now that it is summer and be in ketosis, because I've been increasing CT duration/intensity and cycling in and out of ketosis this whole spring/start of summer.

You also speak about D-Ribose rapidly replenishing ATP substrates, how do you feel about consuming that solely as a post-workout carb source? Thanks, I really enjoyed the podcast!

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-06-02T00:04:59+0300

[http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com...-you-do-a-hard-workout-triathlon-or-marathon/](http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2013/05/what-happens-to-your-body-after-you-do-a-hard-workout-triathlon-or-marathon/)

### RickD — 2013-06-02T00:27:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ben-greenfield-ct-optimal-performance-podcast-is-now-live.7220/post-87277>

Good Podcast Jack. One small criticism though that I know the Ironman community will misinterpret. At the end of your conversation you said you're presently ramping up your carb consumption going as high as 250gms/d. While I understand why you do it you should have pointed out that at this level you're not in a state of ketosis so it defeats the purpose of going ketogenic to access the PPP. Most triathletes are going to equate that number with their current carb consumption and not make the necessary changes they need to make to get into the PPP.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-02T00:41:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ben-greenfield-ct-optimal-performance-podcast-is-now-live.7220/post-87278>

What is the reason for the carbs in summer? I understand that it can be tolerated due to the longer light cycle/photoelectric effect, but for someone looking to eventually enter and maintain the PPP for performance, wouldn't carbs even in summer (or at least a large amount) not make sense?

### RickD — 2013-06-02T00:53:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ben-greenfield-ct-optimal-performance-podcast-is-now-live.7220/post-87280>

BPC,

Jack one time stated something to the effect that his focus is on optimal health so he follows a more evolutionary template eating more carbs when they're in season, but if he were focused on performance he'd keep carbs low year round.

From personal testing to remain in the optimal ketogenic zone I have keep my carbs below about 50-75gms/D even if I'm doing times of higher training volumes. And as Jack as stated numerous times the longer you stay in this zone the easier things become. Today for example finished a 3 hr bike ride with over 8000 ft of climbing and only drank water and felt good the entire ride. Wish I would have discovered this back when I was competing.

### Dextery — 2013-06-02T02:58:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ben-greenfield-ct-optimal-performance-podcast-is-now-live.7220/post-87287>

Regarding Kobe Bryant...I wonder what mismatech resulted in his injury?

"Unfortunately for the Lakers, it all came to an end against Golden State on April 12, when Bryant tore his Achilles tendon on a routine attempt at a drive to his left. Ever the warrior, Bryant sank two clutch free throws in a game L.A. desperately needed to make the playoffs, then limped off into the locker room knowing his - and ultimately the Lakers' - season was finished."

Novak Djokovic, tennis star, has been low carb for 2.5 years and has been free of injury. He has come on very strong very quickly.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-02T03:17:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ben-greenfield-ct-optimal-performance-podcast-is-now-live.7220/post-87289>

@IvanDrago, Thanks for your thoughts. I'm glad to hear about your recovery and you're seemingly amazing rebound onto the road to both recovery and optimal-ness, lol. As you said, I'am on a similar path. It seems that in the past 12 months or so I have been absolutely BOMBARDED with information I never imagined was out there. I simultaneously found the Perfect Health Diet, Dave Asprey/Bulletproof Diet, and Epi-Paleo. I used to want to be a psychologist but now I just really want to spread the knowledge I have learned and help people, I really would love to get into anti-aging but I'll have to find some way to put myself through medical school.... Maybe I can just take the Paul Chek courses and become some sort of all encompassing alternative trainer including body, mind, and spirit practices. I'm really glad about Epi-Paleo, though, and I'm glad to start applying the knowledge to myself. I'm seeing energy improvements and improvements in psychological symptoms I've been trying to dissipate. My BF% goes up and down around carb refeeds, I think I need to structure them better because for 1-2days after I'm bloated and puffy feeling.... I don't want that lol. My ultimate goal: PPP. Just glad I started adapting to this lifestyle at 18 rather than later, as 24-36mo's now won't take that much time off of me in terms of a martial arts lifestyle. Keep me updated on your performance and what not, I'm really intrigued by your background.

@Dextery: I'm not quite sure, but I would imagine it was just simply all the traveling. I know Dr. Kruse has spoken about flight and how it disconnects us from the Earth so we essentially age at exponential rates... He obviously had to travel a lot being in the NBA and he was also in the Olympics; I'm sure he was often disconnected/un-grounded, not including the toxins you can be exposed to from traveling. He also plays under massive artificial lights and sometimes you play at night, I'm sure having blue light shining in your face until 11-12 or later at night is no help. He also just started eating a paleo-style diet from my knowledge, so he probably didn't even fully adapt to that lifestyle. Nonetheless he is a great example of what can be accomplished when you get nutrition down. Some MMA fighters are now adopting the Bulletproof Diet and have noted how easy it is to maintain weight while increasing their conditioning + increased energy (Keith Jardine is one of them, Frank Mir is paleo as well).

# Thread: Protein and types of fat on the Epi-Paleo diet?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protein-and-types-of-fat-on-the-epi-paleo-diet.7221/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hemming — 2013-06-01T18:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protein-and-types-of-fat-on-the-epi-paleo-diet.7221/post-87257>

I've read through the main website and some posts but I can't seem to find a definitive answer. I basically want to know how much protein is recommended relative to bodyweight. I would also like to know what type of fat is preferred. I did read in the CT guide that MCT>saturated>MUFA>PUFA but is this also valid for the diet in general? Is the general rule to stay below 100g of carbs/day?

I might add that I'm 26y male, 5'9'' and 116lbs. All my blood work is normal except for my amylase level which is elevated.

### Dali Dula — 2013-06-01T19:08:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protein-and-types-of-fat-on-the-epi-paleo-diet.7221/post-87260>

The hierarchy of protein sources is as follows:
1\. shellfish
2\. crustaceans
3.fish
4\. organs of pastured ruminants
5\. meat of pastured ruminants

Good fats come from grassfed animals, coconut oil, olive oil. Avoid most veg and seed oils.
Your body should be happy with less than 100g carbs/day. Even less if you can.
Unless you are doing the leptin reset eating in the above manner should fulfill your protein requirements. Eat more fat than protein. Feel good.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-01T19:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protein-and-types-of-fat-on-the-epi-paleo-diet.7221/post-87261>

The "MCT>saturated>Mufa>Pufa" is the order for fat consumption prior to CT, if I remember correctly, not necessarily the best fats overall for the diet. I'm still slowlyyy getting through ALL the blogs and associated citations, so I'm not as experienced with "epi-paleo" as others are, but I can tell you this: other paleo blogs/books say limit PUFAs to 4% of calories a day due to their unstable nature and what not. This diet is extremely seafood heavy. Shellfish are the base of the pyramid followed by crustaceans (crab and what not) followed by fatty fish (salmon)... then organ meat, then skeletal meat (of land based animals). I'd say that this diet is then heavy in PUFAs in contrast to other paleo diets being low in PUFAs (Perfect Health Diet recommends only 1-1.5lbs of fatty fish a week, rather than a day).

This diet is definitely not low in saturated fat though, as saturated fats are an important building block for all processes in our body. Butter, ghee, and coconut oil tare probably the best. There are some things I too don't understand. Some people have asked about sources of MUFAs and how to get more o6 fatty acids in their diet when their o3/o6 ratio gets skewed, and Dr. Kruse has said responded with things such as tallow, bacon fat, lard, etc. So I'm guessing animal based fats from healthy animals would be better than olive or avocado oils. This also leads me to wonder whether I should be eating so many damn avocado's per week or not, lol. MUFAs are suggested to be eaten with fish, though, because Saturated fat blocks DHA absorption into the brain. After awhile of eating plenty of seafood you can continue to use saturated fats with fish as you may actually want to block that absorption.

Protein, well it depends on how active you are and your goals, but this diet is typically High Fat, low to very low carb, moderate protein. I would say you can eat like 0.6-1.0 grams per pound of body weight and be fine as long as you are becoming fat adapted. Less than 100 carbs is ideal, although I'm now confused. To be in ketosis you generally have to limit carbs under 50, although that is highly individual and also based off of activity and MCT consumption, fasting, etc. But Dr. Kruse in his recent podcast said in summer (from June 21 onward, specifically) he eats up to 250-300g of carbs a day, meaning the diet would be largely carb based. So I suppose you are supposed to be low carb in spring/fall, very low carb/keto in winter, and a carb consumer in summer, although I don't know how you would access the PPP that Dr. Kruse was speaking about on the podcast if for 3 months out of every year you were not eating adequate fat.

Hope this clarifies some things!

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-01T19:17:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protein-and-types-of-fat-on-the-epi-paleo-diet.7221/post-87262>

Dali Dula, can you clarify something for me? In one post on the Leptin Prescription set of blogs, Jack said listed his hierarchy of protein for the LeptinRx (not necessarily best food choices, but best protein), and he had meat from game before ruminant animals; i.e. elk/deer over beef/lamb. What do you think would be best? Also, what about the MUFAs, should I opt for my usual avocado and maybe canned olives, or would some tallow suit my body better? Thanks!

### Hemming — 2013-06-01T20:25:58+0300

Dali Dula> Thank you for answering but I was looking for more concrete advice as that was what I had already read in the blog.

BigPapaChakra777> Thank you. That particular fat recommendation is indeed for CT, which is why I asked if it was valid in general (I would assume it is).
Regarding the protein my goal is to rebuild my body after an eating disorder which I think would put me in the upper range. I agree that 100g carbs/day is probably too much to stay in ketosis I just saw the number in a blog post.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-01T20:30:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protein-and-types-of-fat-on-the-epi-paleo-diet.7221/post-87266>

Looks like were on similar paths then. I was never diagnosed with an E.D. but my actions and thoughts for a long time have lead me to believe I had/have one. # Thread: 35 Yr Old Male Firefighter - My Journey to Feeling Awesome
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 109
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-06-04T04:32:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87423>

I've been doing paleo and crossfit for about 3 years.

I AM NOT DOING CROSSFIT WHILE ON THE RX

Two years ago I did a strict paleo challenge inspired by the good people over at [http://whole9life.com/2012/01/whole-30-v2012/](http://whole9life.com/2012/01/whole-30-v2012/)

I was strict and didn't give in once only to find that I didn't really feel any better than before.

I've been in pretty decent crossfit shape for the last few years but my energy has never been great.

9 months ago I stumbled onto Jack's Leptin RX and determined that I was surely leptin resistant.

So I gave it my best shot.

My sleep hasn't been great for the last several years, and I suspect it's because I'm always getting woke up at the fire station at night the 10 days a month that I'm on duty.

But about a week after starting the Rx it really tanked and I would wake up in the middle of the night multiple times and have a hard time going back to sleep.

After 7 weeks I gave up and went back to regular paleo and tried Leangains for a while.

My sleep hasn't been the same since even thought I've tried about every supplement out there.

I tried 20mg of Ambien a few times and that didn't help either.

After revisiting Jacks Leptin post again I've decided to give it another shot.

I see now that there were a few things on the Rx that I didn't follow.

**1)** I don't think I ate enough fat
**2)** I didn't eat progressively smaller meals throughout the day
**3)** I at heavy cream (today I realized that heavy cream should not be a part of my reset).
**4)** I drank coffee
**5)** I did some cardio as it's hard not to with my job (my crew at work likes to play football in the afternoons for workouts)

**But I have begun to feel so crappy that I decided to give this another try.**

In addition to poor sleep, my energy is low, I never like exercising and my libido is rock bottom **despite being married to the most wonderful woman on the planet**.

Additionally after I do workout I usually have the strange irritating feeling in my back muscles and sometimes have odd muscle pains in my arms.

I'm actually scheduled to get blood work done for my cholesterol (because it has been really high LDL 270) so I am going to ask if they can also check my reverse T3 and DHEA while they are at it.

The bloodwork isn't for another week but I decided to get the Rx going now.

I hope to post here at least every few days with my meals and how I'm feeling.

Today was day 1 and here is what I ate:

**BAB**: 9oz gound pork, half leftover avocado, 1/2 green pepper and a bit of onion and 3 scrambled eggs with probably 3 tablespoons of butter; cup of green tea with a tablespoon of coconut oil.

**Lunch**: 6oz sirloin steak cooked in plenty of butter; salad with oilive oil and basalmic; 1/2 cup of cream (yes I love it and can drink it straight).

**Dinner**: 6oz ground pork; 1 fried egg in butter; mixed veggies in butter with some cream

Even though I love the heavy cream it doesn't set well with me (little bit of bloating) so I'm going to have to axe that. I'll give the cream to my wife so she can make yummy stuff for her and the kids.

I'll post again in a day or two.

### Inger — 2013-06-04T07:17:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87426>

Jgotti,
do you still crossfit? If.. I think it is not a good idea if you want to heal. From all that I know you need to quit all exercise (except of barefoot walking outside, swimming a bit in the ocean or a lake and other similar relaxing things).

I also would feel not good on what you are eating, if I am honest. Now I am very sensible to food choices to feel my very best (and I want that). Is he pork pastured? I would never eat any other... and the eggs too, are they pastured? Chicken normally get a awful feed and that all goes into the eggs too. If I get no good quality eggs I eat none. I do very well on red meat for BAB and fish / seafood for lunch and dinner. Try to get grassfed meat or game, if you get grassfed organs like heart, liver.. etc they will be very energizing and healing too! Try to eat them raw. At least as raw / rare as you can. You are going to feel the difference! I always eat my meat raw, I really feel the difference in energy. You could make you a nice tartar for breakfast, or some self made jerky? Dipped in pastured raw butter or coconut oil?
Also for better energy, try the pastured eggs raw! Do a smoothie! Maybe with some wild berries like blueberries or lingonberries? They have very little carbs and much taste so you need only a tiny bit. Or raw organic cacao / chocolate, if it suits you.

Is there growing any stinging nettles or other edible herbs around where you live, away from roads etc.? If, they are huge. Use them instead of store bought salad. They are way richer in.. anything! You can make smoothies, or eat them plain, or make a nettle soup! Or use it as spinach. Linden leaves are also very healing and have a wonderful mild taste. You need to explore the wilderness and what it gives you for free to eat. All those things are so energizing and healing, what your body needs. Also, they are for free.

I really think your body is so hard trying to tell you, please quit the exercise. You need to listen to it.. or it will go downhill.

### caroline — 2013-06-04T08:40:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87427>

Inger has lots of great ideas for you! Definitely ditch the cross fit. You need to follow the leptin reset closely. Details matter big time. I wouldn't eat pork and maybe let the cream go for now - maybe later.
Are you paying attention to light cycles? hugely important. What about EMFs? The most important!

### caroline — 2013-06-04T08:44:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87428>

Also - you need to get your vit D checked and did you do a saliva ASI? Shift work is a nightmare - you need to work at it even harder.........sorry.....

### Albert83BCN — 2013-06-04T09:08:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87429>

Welcome to the forum **jgotti**!

Like you I'm very new here and I carry almost all the same issues you do! So I think you could benefit to have a look at my thread if you haven't done already, I suspect that if one of us takes a step into the good direction the other could benefit a lot from it.
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7216-Albert-s-Story-and-Journey-to-optimal](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7216-Albert-s-Story-and-Journey-to-optimal)!

As for the energy thing, mine is still pretty consistent and didn't have such an impact, but in respect to all the other stuff I can relate a lot: poor sleep, waking a lot during night to pee and drink water, apathy, low libido... if you're able to, I'd try to get results from my blood work regarding thyroid status (TSH, T4, T3, T3r) and sex hormone panel, the most complete you can have, the better. I suspected that I'd find issues if asked and yes I did found them. From here on we could work on getting rid from these hopefully.

For the better sleep (which will for sure impact all the other) most people resort to magnesium and salt as their first options. Magnesium because its damn important and it can be difficult to get being low carb and seems that natural paleo source would be quality water that we don't get today. And salt because being low carb water retention is very hard and that can leave you drained from sodium. I've added a small table spoon to water at some times that I felt a little bit grogy during the morning and worked fine, now I want to try before bed to see if it makes any improvement, I suspect we get up so much times because we become dehydrated and we need to both take more and get rid of the one we have. I guess Jack would say that our nanotubes are not working well right now and that we have to improve in this aspect, epi-paleo template with cold thermogenesis and drinking coherent water is supposed to help a lot here, I'm trying to marry these concepts right now, maybe you want to have a look at this stuff.

As for the crossfit thing. I know how hard is to stop exercising (I do both bodybuidling like strength training and endurance running) and being a firefighter you both need to mantain fitness and also would be expected to do it from your crew. What worked pretty well for me (I think) is to try to avoid the glycolitic pathway as much as I can. This is the one that will drain you the most and even more if you're on low carb. You could try to get more aerobic activity that relies 100% on fat, it must be very light so walking could do the job. Then if you want to train intensely, it'd be better to stick with the ATP-creatine system, which uses your available energy from muscles but not more. This happens when you do exercise intensely but for not more than 8-10 seconds. Then you start needing more ATP and the fast way to get it is entering the glycolitic pathway. So 30' of training with high weights and short reps like 5x5 series or something like this with plenty of rest between series could serve you to maintain strengh without draining you and you could mantain good cardiovascular health by walking a lot or cycling lightly, etc.

Good luck!

EDITED: Well, seems that running in the ATP-PC pathway isn't optimal either and what we should be seeking to achieve while exercising is to enter the PPP (Pentose phosphate pathway) for ultimate performance with minimal health impact, listen to this podcast if you haven't already:
[http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com...BenGreenfieldFitness+(Ben+Greenfield+Fitness)](http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2013/06/jackkruse/?utm_source=feedburner&utm_medium=feed&utm_campaign=Feed%3A+BenGreenfieldFitness+%28Ben+Greenfield+Fitness%29)

And read the two blog posts that it references from this site:
[http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/](http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/)
[http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-8-quantum-scaling/](http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-8-quantum-scaling/)

### GlenPDQ — 2013-06-04T17:30:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87451>

Seafood is more nutrient dense than most land-based food. Oysters, clams, mackeral, and the small fish such as sardines are quite nutrient dense. The only thing similar to them might be eggs. I prefer soyfree produced eggs which is a whole other story. After 'EMF', Dr. K's favorite word is SEAFOOD.

Remember to avoid all artificial light after sundown as much as possible. Sleep masks are helpful as well as the wearing blue blocker glasses in the evening. Go to bed early (soon after sundown).

Drink only non-flouridated and non-chlorinated water.

Turn off EMF sources like wifi before going to bed, power down cordless phones, and pull the batteries on all 3G, 4G, etc, phones before sleeping. DNA is not happy with the pulsed EMF according to Dr K. This helps at night when the body tries to repair/heal itself, and to produce the necessary hormones.

Try to get your hs-crp inflammatory markers tested the same time you get tested for vitamin D and cholesterol. It should help later down the road when you retest to check your progress.

Fat is your friend, including coconut oil.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-04T17:35:33+0300

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=87426)
>
> Jgotti,

I do very well on red meat for BAB and fish / seafood for lunch and dinner. Try to get grassfed meat or game, if you get grassfed organs like heart, liver.. etc they will be very energizing and healing too! Try to eat them raw. At least as raw / rare as you can. You are going to feel the difference! I always eat my meat raw, I really feel the difference in energy. You could make you a nice tartar for breakfast, or some self made jerky? Dipped in pastured raw butter or coconut oil?
> Also for better energy, try the pastured eggs raw! Do a smoothie! Maybe with some wild berries like blueberries or lingonberries? They have very little carbs and much taste so you need only a tiny bit. Or raw organic cacao / chocolate, if it suits you.
>
> Is there growing any stinging nettles or other edible herbs around where you live, away from roads etc.? If, they are huge. Use them instead of store bought salad. They are way richer in.. anything! You can make smoothies, or eat them plain, or make a nettle soup! Or use it as spinach. Linden leaves are also very healing and have a wonderful mild taste. You need to explore the wilderness and what it gives you for free to eat. All those things are so energizing and healing, what your body needs. Also, they are for free.

I suspected that I'd find issues if asked and yes I did found them. From here on we could work on getting rid from these hopefully.
>
> For the better sleep (which will for sure impact all the other) most people resort to magnesium and salt as their first options. Magnesium because its damn important and it can be difficult to get being low carb and seems that natural paleo source would be quality water that we don't get today. And salt because being low carb water retention is very hard and that can leave you drained from sodium. I've added a small table spoon to water at some times that I felt a little bit grogy during the morning and worked fine, now I want to try before bed to see if it makes any improvement, I suspect we get up so much times because we become dehydrated and we need to both take more and get rid of the one we have. I guess Jack would say that our nanotubes are not working well right now and that we have to improve in this aspect, epi-paleo template with cold thermogenesis and drinking coherent water is supposed to help a lot here, I'm trying to marry these concepts right now, maybe you want to have a look at this stuff.
>
> As for the crossfit thing. I know how hard is to stop exercising (I do both bodybuidling like strength training and endurance running) and being a firefighter you both need to mantain fitness and also would be expected to do it from your crew. What worked pretty well for me (I think) is to try to avoid the glycolitic pathway as much as I can. This is the one that will drain you the most and even more if you're on low carb. You could try to get more aerobic activity that relies 100% on fat, it must be very light so walking could do the job. Then if you want to train intensely, it'd be better to stick with the ATP-creatine system, which uses your available energy from muscles but not more. This happens when you do exercise intensely but for not more than 8-10 seconds. Then you start needing more ATP and the fast way to get it is entering the glycolitic pathway. So 30' of training with high weights and short reps like 5x5 series or something like this with plenty of rest between series could serve you to maintain strengh without draining you and you could mantain good cardiovascular health by walking a lot or cycling lightly, etc.
>
> Good luck!
>
> EDITED: Well, seems that running in the ATP-PC pathway isn't optimal either and what we should be seeking to achieve while exercising is to enter the PPP (Pentose phosphate pathway) for ultimate performance with minimal health impact, listen to this podcast if you haven't already:

Here's that heavenly recipe for the Brown Butter Ice Cream:

Ingredients

1 can coconut oil
1 stick butter
1/8 tsp pure stevia
4 Egg yolks

Brown the butter in a nonstick pan then put in blender with stevia and egg yolks and blend. Then add the coconut milks and blend again, refrigerate for at few hours (it should be pudding like). Then put it in your ice cream maker until it gets to desired thickness.

Next time I will take a small handful of toasted pecans and crush them up and add them toward the end if the ice cream maker process.

I have had luck finding grass fed beef sellers on Craigslist, if you are in the US. I am in San Jose which is one of the most expensive areas in the country, but I was still able to find a local farmer selling their 100% angus grassfed beef for $3.25/lb "hanging weight" (Hanging weight is the starting weight after the initial gutting, which still includes skin and bones.) This price INCLUDED butchering and vacuum sealing! I had to purchase a 1/4 cow minimum (which was ~190lbs hanging weight/130-140 lbs take home meat), but if you work at a fire station just get a group of folks together to split the order.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87585>

Have you read the EMF series yet? turning the breaker \[bedroom\] off at night is to make sure there are no EMF's that you aren't aware of. You need to get rid of your clock radio, TV and anything else in bedroom that produces EMF - even your watch if it has a battery. There could be faulty wiring in the walls etc. that you are't aware of - there is a lot to think about.
Oxytocin .... orgasm twice a day..... not necessarily connected to C/T ....lol

in the evening - walk in damp grass - barefoot and just enjoy being outside - in other words get grounded. try no TV at all - I think you will sleep much better - just relax and enjoy simple things in the evening - like chatting with your family or meditating.......

Thanks for the recipe .... sounds great. Some people find alot of coconut oil can upset their stomach. Coconut manna does it to me if I eat too much.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-06T01:46:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87586>

I have read the series, it's good.

I don't have a clock and turn my phone off at night.

I do have a watch, but for I have to get up for work before the sunrise.

I do have 1 tv in the house but we never watch it - I literally don't have 1 single channel. We watch a movie on it maybe once a month.

That's exactly what we do in the evening - my wife and I usually sit and read to each other before we go upstairs for bed.

Hope you like the "ice cream" as much as I did.

You could also add bacon and pecans - I've done that before but without the butter.

The combination of all three would probably be killer

Butter = fat + proteins

Clarified butter=Ghee

I think butter is in sort of questionable cathegory.

Speaking of 15 tbsp
preferably variety would be better

coconut oil (or MCT)
olive oil
butter
beef tally
lard

I am not hearing much about red palm oil
lubricate lungs, good for asthma
\-----------------------------------
\* Butyric acid - 4 carbon atoms (in butter) - promotes healthy cells
\* Caprylic acid - 8 carbon atoms (in milk, coconut oil)- immune system - used in the treatment of some bacterial infections.
\* Lauric acid - 12 carbon atoms (in coconut oil, palm oil, & breast milk) - immune system
\* Myristic acid - 14 carbon atoms (in cow's milk & dairy products) - immune system
\* Palmitic acid - 16 carbon atoms (in palm oil & meat) - lungs & heart
\* Stearic acid - 18 carbon atoms (in meat & cocoa butter) - heart

...................

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-06T22:29:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87659>

**Day 4**

5 Hrs straight of sleep last night at work then another hr after going to the bathroom - that was nice but feeling really wiped out today.

BAB: 10oz chicken, 3 scrambled eggs, 1 green pepper, 4 tbsp butter, 1 tbsp coconut oil

Lunch: 1.5 cans tunu, 3 tbsp paleo mayo (totally Leptin RX friendly), 2 hardboiled eggs, 4 pieces bacon, big salad with 3 tbsp olive oil, green tea with 1.5 tbsp coconut oil and 1.5 tbsp butter

Dinner: some leftover tuna, about 1.5 eggs, about 8oz green beans cooked in bacon grease and butter, 2 tbsp coconut oil

Tried my first CT with Ice - only got 9 lbs of ice so far but will have 20 lbs by the end of the week - went 20 minutes. Don't have a compression shirt so I soaked a t-shirt and wore it.

20 min wasn't to bad, I'll go for 25 tomorrow

### Inger — 2013-06-07T07:16:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87679>

Oh no FFL.. still eating lots of chicken and eggs? If you really want to see a change I think you have to let those go... I am afraid. Especially the chicken, if the eggs are pastured it might be ok. I see you have very low HS CRP. Amazing! Must be the loving relatioships with your wife..

About the Tilapia. What I have read about the Tilapia farming, I would not buy it if I could get any other, like Pollock. Here you can always buy Pollock from the freezer. It is wild caught and very cheap! Also look for precooked frozen mussels. They might be very cheap where you live too, at least they are here. Always look for the best option you can afford, never do anything because it is convenient only.. and that you like the taste. Because your health is the most important thing from all.

I also think broth always have to b from grassfed or wildcaught / game. Think like this, all what the animal is fed you will get into your body too.. and in todays world the feed the animals get normally is.. awful. Not to speak about the other stuff like hormones.. etc. you just do not want those into your body...

Very clever of you to start CT  you are doing so well! If you want you can make ice yourself just fill some empty cartons, plastic bottles or ziplock bags with water and put in the freezer...

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-07T16:28:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87734>

**Day 5**

An okay night sleep, woke up numerous times but did ultimately sleep till the sunrise.

Went outside right after I got up but the trees and house block the sunrise but I was out there about 5 minutes

BAB: Pollock (aparrently 1.4 lbs but the package must have been mistaken - there was no way there was 1.4 lbs there), 5 eggs cooked in bacon grease and butter, 4 pieces of thick sliced bacon, about a cup of leftover green beans warmed in bacon grease, decaf green tea with butter and coconut oil

Lunch: Chicken salad with paleo mayo and cooked green pepper in olive oil. 1.5 tbsp coconut oil

Dinner: Grilled chicken with veggies cooked in a ton of oil and a bit of butter, 1.5 tbsp coconut oil

CT: After lunch - big strides - I soaked a tshirt in water and put it on that put the ice on top of the shirt while I laid in the tub. About 3 minutes into it I decided that it wasn't really cold enough so I put the ice under my shirt directly on my skin. Wasn't terrible. Went a total of 25 minutes. Will shoot for 30 minutes today

**Day 6**

Asleep by 9:45, woke up at 2am, then again at 4:45am and couldn't go back to sleep. Ate my BAB around 5:30am

BAB: 6 scrambled eggs with butter and oil, 1 green pepper and about 7oz leftover grilled chicken with some veggies. Decaf green tea with coconut oil and butter

Lunch: changed my mind about skipping - Medium salad with paleo ranch (Leptin RX friendly), 5 pieces bacon, 4 eggs, 10 pecans, 1 can coconut milk

Dinner: Leftover chicken and veggies cooked in oil and butter; tbsp of coconut oil

### Inger — 2013-06-08T21:01:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87812>

Oh No FFL.. I really would not touch the chicken.. I will tell you something. Last two times I tried chicken from regular shop I got sick. Big herpes on my lip and sick feeling, feverish and no energy for a few days. I then tried my parents chickens cooked, I wanted to see if it was the cooking that made me sick as I usually eat almost everything raw.. but no. I did not got sick after eating my parents pastured chicken, prepared the same way. After this incident I NEVER touched chicken again. I would not eat it even if for free, believe me. You really need to check what they get feed.. you will not believe it. I bet they get pumped with hormones too because the meat look totally different from our pastured chickens. To me it is no surprise at all that you feel not good eating mainly the cheapest chicken and eggs.

You need to find other food sources ASAP. Organs you can get very cheap normally, but they need to be from grassfed though, or lamb. You need to ask. Ask where you buy your meat, research in internet.. you will find. If you could buy cheap New Sealand mutton where you live? Prefrozen? Would be 1000 times better than the chicken I promise.

Visit different shops.. and search the freezers for wildcaught fish (even farmed is way better than the chicken).. mutton.. mussels.. look everywhere. Maybe your wife can do it? We women are usually experts in finding stuff..lol

Try to think, your health is most worth of anything. Maybe you can save on something else? Or sell something you do not need? There will be a way, there always is.

### Inger — 2013-06-08T21:46:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87814>

IDK what food costs in your country, but here it is expensive, way more expensive (Finland). To me really cheap means; 1 pound of Pollock, wildcaught from northern Atlantic. 2,50-3,00 â‚¬ Mussels, from Denmark, 250 grams, 2,40 â‚¬.
Here one (!) oyster costs 2,40 â‚¬. In comparison it is crazy expensive. I can also get lambs heart for 8,00 â‚¬ / kg in fall. And New Seeland mutton for 6,00 â‚¬ / kg. To me very cheap! I am very happy if I can feed me on 10 â‚¬ / day. I can.. and doing great quality foods, if I use prefrozen fish, mussels and meat. And also Organs. When I start to buy many veggies, fruit and nuts it gets way more expensive. Because I only buy organic. That is why I use many wild edibles, they are for free. Instead of veggies. And then I buy the cheapest wild picked berry, lingonberry. Very healthy and not expensive.

Mackerel, whole costs 14,00 â‚¬ / kg. Expensive to me. I still buy them. I use the whole thing. I do smoothie from the heads too. I sometimes ask for fishheads also, for free. I have many in my freezer now, I make broth out of them (from non fatty fish only - fatty fish I always eat raw).

I used to have a boat and fish for pleasure.
To fish for sharks one have to have a mackerel for bait.
Next to marinas we have a fishing stores, among other things they sell all kind of bait fish.
I is frozen right when caught, right on the boat.
But because it is designated as no food but other, it is much less expensive.
If you live close to marina (salt water marina), it is possible to buy this fish.
They are same fish as in food store but packed in 3"-5" high pallets about 20-25# each pallet, frozen solid.

//

THANK YOU BOTH FOR ALL THE GREAT INFO YOU HAVE GIVEN ME - I am drinking Fluoride Free water today

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-09T21:27:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87853>

**Day 7**

Wow - last night I slept 7 hrs and 20 minutes straight. So excited!! that's the longest straight I've slept in several months. I suspect it was because I took in more salt yesterday than normal so I didn't wake up to go to the bathroom. Or maybe it was the 1500mg of Holy Basil with 3mg Melatonin. I have taken 1000mg of Holy Basil before with Melatonin.

BAB: 6 eggs scrambled with butter and oil; few ounces leftover chicken; 1 whole avocado; spinach and about 1/3 green pepper; bulletproof decaf green tea

Lunch: Breakfast casserole with eggs and sausage and some spinach

Dinner: Smoked chicken, brussel sprouts bacon slaw (super yummie), a handful of berries and a heaping tbsp of coconut oil
**(I am working towards little to no chicken but it's what we have in the house now)**

CT: 40 minutes with 10lbs of ice on the chest and stomach for 40 minutes.

First day with only non fluoride water - drank almost a gallon. Will be interesting to see if it affects my energy in a positive way as some have said that my low energy could be caused by dehydration due to fluoride in the tap water.

### Dextery — 2013-06-10T06:08:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87899>

>
> Those prices are quite a bit better than where I live here in Missouri but I did just see Mackerel at Wal Mart for $1.25/can which is better than Salmon and way better than Tuna.

At Wal Mart, look for sardines packed in tomato sauce in an oval tin 15oz for $1.50 My brand is Sol-Mex. I also get Polar brand oysters in a 8oz can for about $1.85 each. Look for them in canned meat section. With the sardines, I drain off most of the tomato sauce. I just pan fry both products in a little butter and garlic and sometimes lemon juice.

> [fireforlunch said:](/goto/post?id=87853)
>
> Wow - last night I slept 7 hrs and 20 minutes straight. So excited!! that's the longest straight I've slept in several months. I suspect it was because I took in more salt yesterday than normal so I didn't wake up to go to the bathroom. Or maybe it was the 1500mg of Holy Basil with 3mg Melatonin. I have taken 1000mg of Holy Basil before with Melatonin.
>
> BAB: 6 eggs scrambled with butter and oil; few ounces leftover chicken; 1 whole avocado; spinach and about 1/3 green pepper; bulletproof decaf green tea
>
> Lunch: Breakfast casserole with eggs and sausage and some spinach
>
> Dinner: Smoked chicken, brussel sprouts bacon slaw (super yummie), a handful of berries and a heaping tbsp of coconut oil
> **(I am working towards little to no chicken but it's what we have in the house now)**
>
> CT: 40 minutes with 10lbs of ice on the chest and stomach for 40 minutes.
>
> First day with only non fluoride water - drank almost a gallon. Will be interesting to see if it affects my energy in a positive way as some have said that my low energy could be caused by dehydration due to fluoride in the tap water.

Country Life Betaine Hydrochloride with Pepsin -- 600 mg - 250 Tablets
This is stomach acid.
Most people are low (and some are getting heartburn because of it)
To figure out dose you have to do trial and error.
Eat them right before meal.

1/day--> Natural Beta-Carotene by NOW Foods - (25000IU - 180 Softgels)

2/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules lef.org

1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day
Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles
[http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW](http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW)
recalculate dose if using other iodine/iodide source.

5/day Biotic Research Bio-D-Mulsion Forte, 1drop=2000iu Vit D
5/day CarlsonVitamin K2 Menatetrenone 5 mg

\=======================

Iodine is halide (type of chemical) it is good for us
"unfortunately" most peoples know only about its thyroid connection, there are many other.

there are other halides that replace iodine in our bodies
you need to supplement with excess iodine iodine to push bad guys out
you must have plenty of selenium first
before you start using iodine eat selenium for two weeks
brazil nuts also have selenium, I sort of doubt that you will get enough from one brazil nut/day
I got above dosage by titrating and watching my selenium levels on Spectracell test.
Spectracell is very powerful test. Good idea to have it done.

bromide is added to flours
fluoride ---toothpaste, water
chloride--- swimming pools
there is one more

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-10T16:52:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87916>

**Day 8**

Sleep was okay last but a woke up a couple a few times. Up at 5:45 went to stair at the sunrise for a few minutes then the BAB

BAB: 1/2 can of salmon in bacon grease; 1 avocado; 1 brazil nut; smoothie made from 1 can coconut milk, 3 tbsp butter, 3 eggs, cup of decaf green tea with 2 tbsp butter and 2 tbsp coconut oil

Lunch: leftover breakfast casserole with sausage, eggs and spinach; large salad with paleo ranch, still hungry so I made 3 scrambled eggs in butter and oil

Dinner: 1/2 can of salmon in oil and butter, 1 small chicken thigh, leftover brussel sprout slaw

CT: none today, at work

I guess I need to add even more fat to my BAB's as I'm often hungry by the time lunch roles around

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-11T15:20:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87962>

**Day 9**

Sleep was horrible last night a work. Had a few calls and didn't get to sleep until after 2am.

No BAB because of fasted blood works this morning at 10:30  - Guess it will have to be a BAL

BAL: 8oz can of oysters (first time ever - wow that's different) cooked in bacon grease and butter, 6 eggs in bacon grease and oil, a few ounces of leftover smoked chicken, mixed salad with paleo ranch

Dinner: 3 Flounder filets cooked in coconutoil and lightly breaded with coconut flour, several asparagus cooked in oil, about 1/4 onion cooked in butter; mixed one tbsp of coconut oil and 1 tbsp butter and ate it off the spoon - yuuuummm

CT: 45 minutes with 18# ice bags directly on my chest and stomach

Took 800mg Magnesium Malate for the first time an hr ago - praying for a good nights sleep

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-12T16:29:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87998>

**Day 10**

Sleep was okay, woke up at 2:45 then again around 4:30. Laid in bed for a while then fell back asleep till 6:10. Missed morning sun so I could get my BAB in on time and not be late for work.

Took 800mg of Magnesium Malate a couple hrs before bed last night. Hopefully it will start helping with sleep here before long.

BAB: 7 eggs, ground pork, about a 1/4 onion all cooked in butter and oil; a couple ounces of leftover smoked chicken; 1 avocado; Mixed about 2tbsp coconut oil and 1.5 tbsp of butter with a little unsweetened shreaded coconut that made a nice yummy dessert

Lunch: A couple filet's of leftover flounder re-heated in oil and butter, mixed salad with olive oil and basalmic vinegar, little bit of leftover smoked chicken.

Dinner: Leftover flounder filet warmed in butter; 8oz green beans cooked in oil and butter; leftover ground pork

CT: None today - at work

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-14T14:50:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88084>

**Day 11**

Sleep was okay, woke up at 3:45am but was able to go back to sleep off and on. Unfortunately I didn't get to be till 11pm

BAB: 6 eggs scrambled in oil and butter; Some leftover pork sausage; decaf green tea with butter and coconut oil

Lunch: Mixed salad with paleo ranch; brazil nut

Dinner: 2 hamburger patties; mixed veggies cooked in oil and butter; 2 tbsp of coconut oil and butter mixed

First day of lower protein to try and actually get in ketosis - Shooting for 130 grams a day

CT: 50 minutes with 18# of ice on my chest and stomach

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-22T02:45:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88632>

**Starting Over**

I have been dreading this post.

Had to go out of town for several days and I completely dropped the ball on my eating as I was staying with a friend and let's just say they're not into low carb.

I will have to go out of town several times in the next couple of months but it will be with family and I can make whatever I want so I don't forsee any issues.

so here is today's food journal:

BAB: 4 oz grass fed beef liver, 5 eggs and about 4oz sausage; 2tbsp butter, 1 tbsp coconut oil

Lunch: Salad with half avocado, 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp coconut oil and 1 tbsp butter

Dinner: about 8oz serloin steak coocked in butter and olive oil, cauliflower with sausage

CT: none today - at work

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-22T04:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88645>

yea, I just pushed delete and I'm moving on.

Starting over of course

### caroline — 2013-06-22T05:26:38+0300

**Day 2**

Lunch: Salad with paleo ranch dressing

Mowed the yard in my bare feet

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-24T03:42:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88776>

Up to look at the sun for a few minutes

BAB: leftover paleo meatloaf friend in butter, 5 eggs, 1 tbsp coconut oil

Lunch: Salad with olive oil, cucumber sausage mash

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-24T05:42:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88783>

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-24T06:02:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88784>

Thanks for responding.

I did read them.

I have a goal to retire early in 3 yrs from the fite dept.

Its not something i can just walk away from simply because i dont have another way to support my family right now.

If i did have something else i would quit in a heartbeat

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=88783)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-24T06:15:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88785>

OK.........think this thru......you cant do it or you wont?

You worried about your self clearly..........

So what happens in 2 yrs if you die?

What good are you to them dead?

When you say you cant do it now........it implies that you believe time is promised........and it is not. Ask James Gandolfi and his 8 month old.

You better focus on the real problem and speed up your ideas on retirement. Just my two cents.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-24T06:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88786>

If you are not good for you you are no good to anyone else........never forget it.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-24T17:25:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88808>

I really appreciate your input and your pressing these issues.

You're right, I won't quit my job because we are already scraping by at my current salary.

When I say scraping by I mean, we rent out two rooms for extra money, we don't drive fancy cars or have car payments and rarely eat out or do all the stuff that most families do.

I have looked several times over the past several months for a job that does not require a degree and has regular day hours.

I have seen several things in the medical field but even without a four yr degree they still require a two yr degree (ie respiratory therapist type stuff).

I have been toying with the idea of getting an online degree in an IT related field (that stuff seems to come somewhat natural to me) but I'm looking at two yrs at least.

Of course I could quit my job today and go work at walmart or something like that but then we would probably loose our house because we wouldn't be able to make the payments and then you're talking about some major stress that would certainly keep me up at night even more than I deal with now probably.

Is there some kind of secret to supporting my family that you are thinking about that I'm not thinking of?

Again I really appreciate you pressing these issues.

I know I am sometimes limited by my little thinking mind so if there is something else I could be doing I want to figure it out.

I would love to be home every night with my family and get a real nights rest every night.

Another option I am pursuing right now is moving to a slower station on our department.

But I have to wait for an open slot.

A friend recently went to a slower station and he said his sleep improved quite a bit at home and at work.

> OK.........think this thru......you cant do it or you wont?
>
> You worried about your self clearly..........
>

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-24T18:10:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88816>

John

make the best changes you can given your circumstances. Sometimes you have to be willing to take a huge leap and move outside your comfort zone, to make things happen for you.

### caroline — 2013-06-25T06:57:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88880>

Have you thought of selling your house and then living cheaply for awhile? and pursuing your passion? It is only stuff .... you can always get more stuff later if you really want.........

Your health and happiness is what is important ....to you ..... and to your family. Maybe have a family meeting and figure out what is important to all of you and set a plan in motion to achieve that.

Believe me .... your family isn't going to care about "the stuff" if you are gone...... When you lose the person you love most ....everything else is a very, very distant second .... at best.....

You will figure it out.......you are here and you now know what is at stake......

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-25T15:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88888>

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-26T20:41:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88999>

**Day 6**

Slept from 10pm to 4:30am straight then in and out till 6am.

Missed the sunrise but I did get about 5 minutes of watching the sun around 6:15

BAB: Leftover Tilapia; 10 pieces of bacon; about a half onion cooked in butter, oil and coconut oil

Lunch: Salad with olive oil and vinegar; coconut oil, butter and bacon grease all mixed together

Dinner: Smoked chicken with some grilled veggies; mixed 2 tbsp butter with 2 tbsp coconut oil with a little salt - very yummy

CT: 25 minutes with ice on my chest and stomach laying in water about 74 degrees

Gonna try several days without eggs and see if I feel any increase in energy

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-27T18:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-89073>

**Day 7**

BAB: Half a can of mackerel cooked in bacon grease; a few ounces of leftover smoked chicken; decaf green tea with 2 tbsp butter and 2 tbsp coconut oil

Lunch: Salad with olive oil and vinegar

Dinner: smoked chicken with mixed veggies cooked in olive oil and butter, some coconut oil on the side

CT:35min in the tub - water was around 65

Really felt crappy today and very irritable.

Mowed the yard in my bare feet

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-29T05:50:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-89172>

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-02T00:26:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-89322>

**Day 10**

First full day of a week long vacation at my sisters house which will entail sitting by the pool all day pretty much every day

staired at the sun for about a minute than my BAB

BAB: ham and some salad with olive oil

Lunch: Salad with olive oil and a couple pieces of bacon

Dinner: Steak cooked rare; salad with a couple pieces of bacon and olive oil dressing

CT: 45 minutes in a cold bath with about 20 lbs ice

**Still feeling really fatigued and I see that I'm gaining weight even thought I'm following the Leptin Rx.**

I assume it's because of the hyperthyroid

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-02T00:28:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-89324>

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-02T01:09:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-89325>

I'll go back and read through a decent amount of this thread, I started reading in the beginning and started reading this last page, I just wanted to put it out there you should maybe consider changing up the fats and see how that affects you. Olive oil has been notorious lately for adulterated sources, many times you may not even be getting pure olive oil, and the olive oil that is in there may be very low quality and adulterated. You may be having reactions to the compounds in the olive oil. The one thing I love about Dave Asprey's work is he really advocates experimenting with how things make you feel, such as overcooked foods negatively affecting cognitive function. Maybe stick to the butter/coconut oil for a few days, then add back in the olive oil and see if you have less energy/worse cognition/weird feelings.

Keep up with the CT as well. I don't know how many people on these boards feel about cold showers but I love them. On days you can't do ice baths or long sessions of spot icing, just take a quick cold shower, even if only 2-3 minutes. It won't be as pronounce of an effect as an ice bath, but as long as you are getting goosebumps you should be activating BAT as far as I know, I believe shivering indicates more of a glycogen depletion.

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-25T14:33:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-90796>

Been a while since I posted.

I got tired of posting all the food I eat so I stopped not realizing that for my own good it would be best to post concerns, triumphs and difficulties here on my journal.

Still eating eating good, trying to get fish several times per week.

I'm really going to go after getting rid of this thrush on my tongue.

I'm not going to eat any dairy including butter for now and I'm going to start drinking a liter water everyday with 1/8 teaspoon of borax

Let's see how it goes.

### fireforlunch — 2013-08-10T06:30:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-92002>

Thought I would give an update.

Still plugging away on the reset.

Still waiting to get moved to the slower station.

The good news is that the endo that my regular doctor referred me to has diagnosed me with hypopituitarism and that he will probably move forward with hormone replacement therapy (thyroid and testosterone) but he wants an MRI first to find out why my pituitary gland is not functioning correctly.

The MRI is next week - can't help but wonder if it will show trauma from a head injury I sustained 12 years ago that gave me a severe concussion and a temporary bout of short term memory loss.

So it's looking up - Hopefully he will be open to natural thyroid.

Can't wait to feel even a little better than I do now.

# Thread: Looking for A List of Suggested Tests
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-06-04T19:06:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87460>

First of all I want to say THANK YOU SO MUCH to all of the people who have responded to my journal post, that is really motivating.

Wondering if Jack has a list anywhere of suggested tests.

A little about me:

35 year old male in decent shape 190 lbs but pretty sure I'm leptin resistant.

Additionally My sleep is terrible (wake up multiple times in the night) always tired during the day, no motivation to workout (not currently working out while on the Rx) but when I did I recovery was pretty difficult, experience strange muscle soreness off and on.

My sex drive is pretty much rock bottom as well.

I'm also a firefighter so I often get up multiple times a night on duty which is suspect part of my sleep problem.

Now we're are really tight on the budget so I can't afford every test imaginable so if you could suggest the ones in order of most importance that I might be able to help with supplementation.

Thanks so much for your input

John

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-04T20:12:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87462>

Well it is really covered in the book where I wrote a whole chapter on the labs I like but here is a sketch: [http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/)

Do you still recommend those?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87469>

The Advanced Profile on this link has 4 cortisol samples plus DHEA, which is an adrenal stress test.

The book is a very worthwhile purchase .... I didn't read it for awhile even tho I bought it when it first came out. I was so busy reading and re reading all the blogs
but it really is a must read if you are dialed in to all of this.....
