### JanSz — 2013-06-05T05:09:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87495>

> [jgotti said:](/goto/post?id=87460)
>
> First of all I want to say THANK YOU SO MUCH to all of the people who have responded to my journal post, that is really motivating.
>
> Wondering if Jack has a list anywhere of suggested tests.
>
> A little about me:
>
> 35 year old male in decent shape 190 lbs but pretty sure I'm leptin resistant.
>
> Additionally My sleep is terrible (wake up multiple times in the night) always tired during the day, no motivation to workout (not currently working out while on the Rx) but when I did I recovery was pretty difficult, experience strange muscle soreness off and on.
>
> My sex drive is pretty much rock bottom as well.
>
> I'm also a firefighter so I often get up multiple times a night on duty which is suspect part of my sleep problem.
>
> Now we're are really tight on the budget so I can't afford every test imaginable so if you could suggest the ones in order of most importance that I might be able to help with supplementation.
>
> Thanks so much for your input
>
> John

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)

On above post you have some good information.
Keep it and review from time to time.
When you are able to arrange do all tests listed there.
\-----------------------

> sleep is terrible
> My sex drive is pretty much rock bottom as well.
> I'm also a firefighter so I often get up multiple times a night on duty which is suspect part of my sleep problem.
> Now we're are really tight on the budget
>
> 35 year old male in decent shape 190 lbs
> but pretty sure I'm leptin resistant.

\-----------------------
You have said about all that we need to know.
How toll are you?
Do you have all body parts? Gallbladder, appendix, else.
Any past diseases, accidents, hit to the head, scrotum.
Do you snore? (Sleep Apnea??)(Deviated septum?---frequent colds?)
\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
You have to get familiar with all dr Jack Kruses recommendations (and make a best effort to apply them).

No birds except eggs.
(Beef +pork+eggs)=25% proteins
shellfish=50% proteins
fish=25% proteins

Fat=70% of food
proteins 30% of food
carbohydrates only those that do not raise insulin or glucose
water=mineral, spring, RO

Breakfatst within 30min of wakeup= 50% or more of daily food

\------------------------------------------
ATM, your worst offender is your work. It screws up your Circadian Rhythm.
You must make best accommodations possible, if that fails, you must change jobs.

Since it is 10 nights out of 30 when you are on call as firefighter, possibly you can accommodate this, but it already looks highly doubtful.

Thank you to firefighters in my town, they have saved my daughter's newly bought house, two years ago.

At this time doing saliva cortisol/DHEA test does not make sense.
You already know the answer, the tests results are bad.

> I'm actually scheduled to get blood work done for my cholesterol (because it has been really high LDL 270)
>
> vit D was 96

Post here all tests that you have for the last 10 years.
Scan each page of test report, post it here.
Go to all your doctors, get from them your test results.

Make sure that you do not eat

any statins---when your cholesterol is high, including natural statins like red yeast rice

any finasteride, duosteride, Proscar, Propecia, Avodart Saw Palmetto
when your hair is falling of, when your prostate hurts

\---------------
LDL is very important cholesterol
it is a building material for all steroid hormones
it is high because it is not used
that is why I suspect that you have low steroid hormones
add to that your sleep, fatigue, sex problems
you have got a job in front of you

Some of the reasons that LDL is not used may be that you have low
Vit A, (D=96=ok), FreeT3 (and one more, someone pls remind me)
There are other reasons.

One other super important reason for high LDL is bad Fatty Acids profile.

When you look at the link on top to the post with my data, there are

1-- saliva cortisol test ----- you do not need that now
2-- blood test Spectracell-----you need that
3--blood test Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum at Mayo clinic or Genova Diagnostics------you need that test
4--blood test at LabCorp--- I can get that list shorter but you need at least half of that list, then again, go for the whole list

I am assuming that you have insurance, most if not all you can have it done at no cost to you, but you must find doctor who will write script asking for those tests.

My doctor (note, I am not a doctor) would likely write you script for those tests, he is here:

tristate-health.com

Otherwise look for osteopath doctor with Family Practice in your neighborhood.

Post your ZIP code, I will try to find list of them within your area, then you would call them one by one.

If you have HMO for insurance, it may get difficult, specially anything with Aetna.

Good luck.
...

> (wake up multiple times in the night)

Separately, to get special attention.

Buy a 2-3 cases at Costco or supermarket, of S. Pellegrino sparkling natural mineral water. Case=12 bottles.
Drink only that water, do not drink any other liqid.
After you finish 2-3 cases, figure out if it made any difference.
It actually helped me with my nightly trips.

Also Perrier is another suspect.
I have not used it because it is sold in plastic bottles.

....

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-05T16:48:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87548>

Lots of good questions and comments, let me see if I can answer all these

I didn't realize that he recomended a specific split of macros all I remember him saying in the quick start guide is eat a ton of fat and don't count carbs

and of course the BAB

Today's BAB was just over 100g of protein with roughly 100g of fat

Quitting my job is not an option at this point but I have put in request to move to a station that get a lot less night calls.

Since I already know my cortisol and dhea are bad do I do more than the Leptin RX?

I've seen suggestions for an Adrenal Rx and I will start following those if I'm not already but what about the dhea?

The only thing that is out of wack on any blood work over the last 10 years is my cholesterol - total 370 (HDL 76, LDL 280, Tri 90). Testing small particle

LDL next week.

I've never had any type of hormone testing done in my life.

I have never taken any kind of statins in my life.

I take no meds nor have I for the past 15 yrs.

I will have a look at all the recommended tests and see what I can do to get them.

I have United Healthcare and my zip is 64137 - thanks so much for all your input.

All I ever drink is water - I will look into your suggestion.

I think that answers all the questions you asked.

I don't know if this is relevant but I have had two concussions in my life - first when I was 15 at football practice - not to bad but I had some forgetfulness.

The second was 13 yrs ago when I was 22. I had a mountain bike accident (was wearing a helmet) but I don't remember anything.

>

>
> those tests.

### JanSz — 2013-06-05T22:20:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87575>

> I did address the issue of posting my med records when I noted that the only thing that has ever been out of wack is my cholesterol.
>
> Why would I scan and post all that stuff when the cholesterol is the only issue.

You should realize that laboratory ranges do not equal healthy ranges.
Laboratory ranges are just statistical manipulations of given laboratory patients and then you cut out 5% from top and 5% of bottom, best and worst cases.

Since population is rather unhealthy, since laboratories deals with peoples who are already complaining of some deficiencies, what you are getting is of not much value.

This is a long winded way of saying that the fact that your other labs are within range does not men that they are perfect.

> I am following JK's suggestions to the best of my ability - I am looking for an affordable grass fed beef provider right now and I've already started eating more fish.
>
> Did I forget anything?

I am getting impression that you may possibly be mixing
Paleo diet ------ grass fed beef
with
Dr Kruse's Epi-Paleo-Rx-----mostly shellfish, crustaceans then some fish and on the end some beef
and
lots of good water

.....
...

I eat a lot of these, they are delicious, just open can and gulp them down.

Snows Clam Chopped, 6.5-Ounce Cans (Pack of 6)
by Bumblebee

except that I buy them locally at Costco. Do not remember exactly, around $8 for a six pack.

.
Live oysters, dozen for $5.40 (they are rather small) at my local supermarket.

At Chinese supermarket they have pre-shucked oysters.

I did eat them raw, few cans, newer any problem.
But when I found live oysters at American supermarket, I switched.
May go back again. Much more oyster's meat per $$.

Chinese supermarket is good for all kind od fish, plus it it easy to get heads and the rest for broth.
\--------------
I used to have a boat, fished tuna and other fish.
I kept fillets, there were always Asian ladies looking for the heads and rest of the carcass.

Now I know why.

.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87473>

Hi!

I am going on a last minute 7 day trip that will involve a lot of sun exposure in Florida! I know that sunscreen is not recommended, but I am very fair skinned, not yet optimal, and have not been able to do any sun exposure yet. If one HAD to use a sunscreen, what would be the best choice? Any recommendations?

Thank you!

### caroline — 2013-06-05T02:48:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87479>

How is your Vit D? once mine was in the optimal range I don't have a problem at all .... and C/T helps alot ...you seem to tan better and no burning. Do you have time to go to a tanning bed before you go? That would help alot.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-05T02:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87480>

Hat and long sleeves?

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-05T03:36:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87486>

as your Alpha MSH increases from CT and your Vit D levels go up - you won't need sunscreen...

### caroline — 2013-06-05T04:18:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87492>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=87486)
>
> as your Alpha MSH increases from CT and your Vit D levels go up - you won't need sunscreen...

exactly right ..... I haven't used a drop of sunblock for the last couple of years ..... and I was in the sun for hours every day and didn't burn at all .... and I have never had such a deep tan before! BTW - I am very fair skinned...

### endless — 2013-06-05T04:42:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87494>

Seeing as you are asking about NOW and not later when you've had time to optimize your Vit D and CT more.....I'd use a combo of covering up and a zinc based sunblock on areas that get lots of exposure, like your nose or tops of feet. A big hat might be a good idea.

### Grizz — 2013-06-05T13:56:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87521>

> [cmegan said:](/goto/post?id=87473)
>
> Hi!
>
> I am going on a last minute 7 day trip that will involve a lot of sun exposure in Florida! I know that sunscreen is not recommended, but I am very fair skinned, not yet optimal, and have not been able to do any sun exposure yet. If one HAD to use a sunscreen, what would be the best choice? Any recommendations?
> Thank you!

Use a sunscreen with an SPF of 30 or more ( more is not better ) that specifies blocking BOTH UVA and UVB.
[http://www.scienceworldreport.com/a...ain-ingredients-increase-risk-skin-cancer.htm](http://www.scienceworldreport.com/articles/7296/20130604/sunscreen-safety-lotions-contain-ingredients-increase-risk-skin-cancer.htm)
[http://www.ewg.org/2013sunscreen/9-surprising-facts-about-sunscreen/](http://www.ewg.org/2013sunscreen/9-surprising-facts-about-sunscreen/)

DON'T LAY ON THE BEACH FOR HOURS EXPOSED TO THE SUN, wear a large hat with slacks & long sleeve shirt. Be safe, not sorry. Let your friends get skin cancer, you know better.

Grizz

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-05T15:14:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87528>

I use coconut oil.......that is about it. And I do avoid massive sun 11-2PM.

### Dextery — 2013-06-05T07:57:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tough-time-raising-vitamin-d-levels.7243/post-87506>

As I posted elsewhere, some brands of supplemental Vitamin D3 have little or no Vit D3. Dr. William Davis found that Nature Made D3 had virtually no D3 based upon his observation as reported here in a letter to the manufacturer. [http://blog.trackyourplaque.com/2010/04/response-from-nature-made.html](http://blog.trackyourplaque.com/2010/04/response-from-nature-made.html)

My suggestion would be to switch brands. I use this this from Iherb and it keeps me right around 100ng/ml taking 10,000IU/day. [http://www.iherb.com/Country-Life-Gluten-Free-Vitamin-D3-5-000-IU-200-Softgels/18038](http://www.iherb.com/Country-Life-Gluten-Free-Vitamin-D3-5-000-IU-200-Softgels/18038)

Or on the Vitamin D Council website there are two companies that advertise there.

That should help get you a better form of sulphated cholesterol.

> Cholesterol sulfate is produced in large amounts in your skin when it is exposed to sunshine. When you are deficient in cholesterol sulfate from lack of sun exposure, your body employs another mechanism to increase it, as it is essential for optimal heart- and brain function. It does this by taking damaged LDL and turning it into plaque. Within the plaque, your blood platelets separate out the beneficial HDL cholesterol, and through a process involving homocysteine as a source of sulfate, the platelets go on to produce the cholesterol sulfate your heart and brain needs. However, this plaque also causes the unfortunate side effect of increasing your risk of cardiovascular disease.
>
> So how do you get out of this detrimental cycle?
>
> Dr. Seneff believes that high serum cholesterol and low serum cholesterol sulfate go hand-in-hand, and that the ideal way to bring down your LDL (so-called "bad" cholesterol, which is associated with cardiovascular disease) is to _get appropriate amounts of sunlight exposure on your skin_.
>
> She explains:
>
> _"In this way, your skin will produce cholesterol sulfate, which will then flow freely through the blood—not packaged up inside LDL—and therefore your liver doesn't have to make so much LDL. So the LDL goes down. In fact... there is a complete inverse relationship between sunlight and cardiovascular disease – the more sunlight, the less cardiovascular disease."_
> [http://articles.mercola.com/sites/a...10/15/dr-stephanie-seneff-on-cholesterol.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2011/10/15/dr-stephanie-seneff-on-cholesterol.aspx)

> If cholesterol is not sulfated by sunlight naturally, it oscillates and vibrates differently than un-sulfated cholesterol, changing its physiologic ability within a cell membrane. If you do not understand this you can not have any “clarity on cholesterol”. This causes a de-coherence in the cell, making the quantum mechanisms of tunneling and entanglement unavailable for energy transfers in a cell. # Thread: Making sense of all this..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-sense-of-all-this.7244/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### badger — 2013-06-05T06:55:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-sense-of-all-this.7244/post-87500>

Hi everyone, some input on my labs would be greatly appreciated as I am a newbie to Dr. Kruse's site, and am trying to get my house in order before I move to south America where I can get many labs done cheaply, which I'm quite excited for..

I'm male, 23 years old, 5' 7", 150 lbs, my only activity is really walking several miles through the woods every day or every other, not much happening physically right now, but that'll change in about a month.

The first # is from January, 2013. The second is from 2 days ago, June 2013, WellnessFX. If there is only one #, it was from the recent test.

Total Cholesterol - 206, 255 (H)
LDL - 132, 184 (H)
HDL - 52, 57
Triglycerides - 79, 72
vLDL-C - 14
LP(a) - 4
Apo B - 153 (H)
hs-CRP - 5.18 (H)
25-hydroxy vit d - 81

interestingly,

% Neutrophil is down from 40.6 to 32, and % Monocytes is wayyyy up from 6.9 to 14!

Everything else was in the low risk range. Lots of inflammation huh??

Apo B: A measure of all non-HDL particle numbers
Apo B - 153 (H)

optimal<80
borderline risk(80-100)
high risk>100mg/dL

\---------------------------------------
Consider getting
23andme.com
test

$99
\--------------------------

Familial Hypercholesterolemia Type B

Cholesterol has many important functions in the body. But too much cholesterol, specifically low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol, can clog arteries and raise the risk of heart attack, stroke and other forms of cardiovascular disease. Lifestyle factors like diet and exercise can affect cholesterol levels, but genetic factors play a part too. Some forms of inherited high cholesterol are caused by mutations in the genes that encode the proteins that help the body clear LDL cholesterol from the bloodstream. When a mutation occurs in the APOB gene, the condition is called familial hypercholesterolemia type B (also known as familial defective apolipoprotein B-100).

\------------------------------------------

Genes vs. Environment
Inherited forms of high cholesterol can be due to mutations in several different genes. This report is about mutations in APOB, which cause a condition known as familial hypercholesterolemia type B (FH type B, also known as familial defective apolipoprotein B-100). 23andMe reports data for three APOB mutations. You can still have an APOB mutation that could cause FH type B even if your data indicates that you do not have the mutations covered by this report. Mutations in genes other than APOB (not reported here) can also cause inherited forms of high cholesterol. Regardless of your genotype, non-genetic factors such as your diet and exercise habits can affect your cholesterol levels. If you are concerned about cholesterol or cardiovascular disease, consult a medical professional.

A genetic counselor can help you understand more about your 23andMe reports and respond to your genetic health questions. 23andMe is collaborating with InformedDNA to give you direct access to board-certified genetic counselors that have been specifically trained to guide you through your 23andMe results.

> [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apolipoprotein\_B](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apolipoprotein_B)
>
> high levels of ApoB can lead to plaques that cause vascular disease (atherosclerosis), leading to heart disease. There is considerable evidence that levels of ApoB are a better indicator of heart disease risk than total cholesterol or LDL.\[1\] However, primarily for historic reasons, cholesterol, and more specifically, LDL-cholesterol, remains the primary lipid test for the risk factor of atherosclerosis.

/////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////

I'm continually piecing this health puzzle together on my own really since around Nov 2012 when I read Nora G.'s book, cut out the "gluten free" food and went low-carb Paleo which my Naturopath at the time never recommended to me (she definitely had a vegetarian bias..) but conceded that perhaps a low-carb diet would be a good choice after seeing an improvement in my labs after I'd made that switch in November on my own. # Thread: Magnetico 20 gauss sleep pad worth the money? Or is 10 gauss sufficient?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-20-gauss-sleep-pad-worth-the-money-or-is-10-gauss-sufficient.7255/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennyha — 2013-06-06T15:47:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-20-gauss-sleep-pad-worth-the-money-or-is-10-gauss-sufficient.7255/post-87616>

I have a question about the Magnetico pad. Is the 20 gauss really necessary, or is a 10 gauss sufficient enough?

I don't have the money at all for one of these, but my mom came across one of these where she lives for $1400 for the 20 gauss for a queen. She said she would buy it and I would have to make payments to her. Like I said, I don't have the money at all, but if this would really help me feel better (have had horrible adrenal/thyroid/hormone problems for past 4 years and I'm only 31), I would consider to try and find a way to make it work. Or, would that money better be served on getting quality labs and a good dr. to try and sort through things?

Thank you for any help anyone can give me on this. I am totally in the dark about these Magnetico sleep pads and EMF in general

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-06T16:12:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-20-gauss-sleep-pad-worth-the-money-or-is-10-gauss-sufficient.7255/post-87617>

### vkiernan — 2013-06-07T14:34:03+0300

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-06-07T14:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-20-gauss-sleep-pad-worth-the-money-or-is-10-gauss-sufficient.7255/post-87729>

> [vkiernan said:](/goto/post?id=87727)
>
> Jeff, What have you noticed thus far? I can't convince my dh to get one.

I've written about my experiences in this thread (multiple posts scattered throughout, so read them all to see the progression): [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5697-my-magnetico-sleep-pad&highlight=magnetico](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5697-my-magnetico-sleep-pad&highlight=magnetico)

In a nutshell, in conjunction with a Schumann resonator and grounding, I'm getting the best sleep of my life. All help, but I noticed the greatest effect with the 10 Gauss pad. Later in the year I'm thinking of getting another 10 G pad to place on top of my original one for a total of 20 G, with the expectation of additional improvements.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-07T15:42:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-20-gauss-sleep-pad-worth-the-money-or-is-10-gauss-sufficient.7255/post-87731>

I've had the lower pad on our bed for a week, and I really like it. It caused sleep disruptions for the first 4 nights, and my urine was darker for a couple days, but my sleep has been good for the last 3 nights. Occasionally I have used the bed for some quick recovery, like yardwork. If my back is talking to me, I go lay down on the bed for 15-20 minutes and it refreshes me. We are going out to the country this weekend, and for the first time I'm not looking forward to it with eagerness. I know I'll be without that pad for a night.

I'll be putting the upper pad on next week.

### Lava — 2013-06-07T18:56:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-20-gauss-sleep-pad-worth-the-money-or-is-10-gauss-sufficient.7255/post-87740>

Hi all ~ Now that the radio antenna is out of our yard (YAY) I am looking at getting the a 20 gauss pad. My question is: We have a Tempur-Pedic mattress. Just the mattress on top of bed frame. So the pad just goes between the bed and the mattress?

### nonchalant — 2013-06-07T19:12:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-20-gauss-sleep-pad-worth-the-money-or-is-10-gauss-sufficient.7255/post-87742>

Hi Lava. The Magnetico needs to go under 4" or thicker mattress. So yes, on top of the frame.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-20-gauss-sleep-pad-worth-the-money-or-is-10-gauss-sufficient.7255/post-87755>

>
> do what you can afford........the nice thing is they are modular so you can add later on if you need too.

I definitely cannot afford this right now, as money is ridiculously tight. However, I ended up getting it for under $1000 so I got some financial help and decided to buy it and make payments on it. I figured if I can get the 20 gauss for much cheaper than I will be able to get the 10 gauss in the future, there is no better time than the present. Called the cable company and decided to cancel cable to offset the price. I'm desperate to get feeling better, even if that means saying bye bye to my beloved cable

Thanks everybody for chiming in! Also, keep checking on these on sites like Ebay. The one I got the lady purchased from an estate sale and it hasn't ever even been used! It would have otherwise cost me over $3600 including shipping and I got it all for a little over one grand with shipping. Having to make payments on it like its a car for the next year, so I really hope that it's worth it!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-20-gauss-sleep-pad-worth-the-money-or-is-10-gauss-sufficient.7255/post-88914>

# Thread: Affordable options for flouride free water - Ideas?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 41
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-06-07T17:55:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/post-87738>

Someone on one of my threads said to only drink San Pellegrino Sparkling Mineral Water because it is flouride free.

Even if I drink less than 1 gallon per day that's going to be a few hundred bucks a month - just can't make that work.

JK suggests that low energy on low carb/high fat is caused by dehydration because of the fluoride in most drinking water so I'm trying to find and affordable option.

The following site lists several bottled waters that do not include fluoride - [http://fullspike.com/water/bottled-water-without-fluoride](http://fullspike.com/water/bottled-water-without-fluoride)

Are those really all okay?

I also found a water filter at amazon with good reviews that claims to eliminate over 90% of fluoride:
[http://www.amazon.com/Master-tmjrf2...0617530&sr=8-1&keywords=fluoride+water+filter](http://www.amazon.com/Master-tmjrf2-Counter-Water-Filtration/dp/B005A3WRA8/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1370617530&sr=8-1&keywords=fluoride+water+filter)

Thanks for your input

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-06-07T18:54:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/post-87739>

I use the Big Berkey with (4) 9" British Berkefeld Ceramic SuperSterasyl Water Filters, available from Amazon...
[http://www.amazon.com/Berkey-British-Berkefeld-Ceramic-SuperSterasyl/dp/B002ZB4T5O](http://www.amazon.com/Berkey-British-Berkefeld-Ceramic-SuperSterasyl/dp/B002ZB4T5O)

I also use these additional filters to handle the fluoride and arsenic, also available from Amazon...
[http://www.amazon.com/Berkey-PF-4-Fluoride-Arsenic-Replacement/dp/B00BWIWYC6/ref=pd\_sim\_hg\_3](http://www.amazon.com/Berkey-PF-4-Fluoride-Arsenic-Replacement/dp/B00BWIWYC6/ref=pd_sim_hg_3)

These aren't super cheap but the concept is simple and there are no moving parts to break. I've had mine for years and it works great. Just my two cents worth.

[http://www.lowes.com/pd\_136858-8692...se+osmosis+water+filter+under+sink&facetInfo=](http://www.lowes.com/pd_136858-86921-KR10_0__?productId=3548972&Ntt=reverse+osmosis+water+filter+under+sink&pl=1&currentURL=%3FNtt%3Dreverse%2Bosmosis%2Bwater%2Bfilter%2Bunder%2Bsink&facetInfo=)
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I am still on a fence about water.
Dr
Kruse have likely lots more to say about water.
What hi said so far is just a beginning.

Other than structured water, I think the water may also be living (or dead).

Coming from drinking my municipal tap water I changed to
Poland Spring water (cooking)
and
San Pellegrino mineral water (high mineral content)(direct drinking).

I quickly realized that I do not have a much affection to Poland Spring water (and I am originally Polish )
Or rather I should say I fell indifferent to Poland Spring water
when
at the same time I am drinking San Pellegrino.

There is something good/different about San Pellegrino.
Since I started drinking it I feel bad about drinking coffee, then I stopped drinking coffe and green tea.
I just steep the Green tea in cold San Pellegrino and drink it (because I feel I should for its content, not because I like it). And previously I was enjoying my coffee and teas (possibly 73 years of bad addictions??)

Then,
I am talking with my friend (in Poland) who tells me similar sounding story about Perrier.
It costs there $8 per bottle so he can't really use it exclusively, but feel strong about it (while there is abundance of good Polish spring waters there).

I am buying my water at Costco. The reason that I picked San Pellegrino over Perrier was that San Pellegrino comes in glass bottle.
Perrier was in plastic.
Now, I am in my local supermarket, and I see Perrier in glass bottles.
I got 4 bottles so I could try it.
I have just finished 3 bottles of those four. I am totally disappointed with Perrier, it feels very bland and indifferent, just like the other waters.
Perrier is low mineral content.
San Pellegrino is high mineral content.

May be it is about minerals.
But I am definitely partial about Pellegrino.
\--------------

I am still on a fence about RO water.
I do have old RO water system from the days my wife had salt water aquarium.
At the time I was not thinking about water as we are thinking today.
I tried once RO water, and vaguely remember that it was definitely much different in taste than our tap water.
At the time I thought that the reason was that the water had definitely acidic PH while tap water is neutral if not alkaline.
Acidic should feel good (fruit juices are acidic)
Alkaline---->soap
(See the explanations for reasons of PH differences in my previous post).
But now I am not sure if that (the PH) was the only reason.

\----------
Currently my RO water system is not operational, supplying water pipe got damaged when I had to put new water heater in the basement.
But otherwise the system is operational and I use it for humidifier during winter months.
My tap water is hard, but when I use RO water humidifier stays clean all the time.
Not sure how good that RO system is for me to drink from it, it is at least 15 years old and I newer changed filters since I got it from my wife, after she was done with salt water aquariums.

.....................

Structured water
Live water
Dead water

..................

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-09T17:46:04+0300

### Jonathin — 2013-06-09T19:19:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/post-87846>

We collect rainwater from a downspout diverter into a 55 gal barrel. We then transfer that water to 15 gal plastic barrels that are covered. They are all high-density polyethylene. As needed, we run the water through the Berkey filter. There is no fluoride to worry about just dust, dirt, microbes and a small amount of toxics. We are in the country so pollution is minor. Since the rainwater is running out due to our regional climate I have designed a simple and inexpensive RO system that produces 1 gallon every 15 min \[100 gal/day\]. It fills the lower part of the Berkey for our use. The top part is not used. No it isn't automatic. I could see this would not work in some household! The conventional RO pressure holding tanks require maintenance to be sanitary plus the water sits inside a rubber bladder. I also treat some water in a magnet/magnetite vessel to structure/energize it.

Fluoride filters have a very limited life and flow rate whether they use bone charcoal or alumina. How do you know when they are expired? Fluoride does not taste like chlorine. Are you going to spend $250 on a fluoride meter to know when they aren't working

Steam distilled water is not wonderful. Steam vapor is high in protons so the condensate is acidic. Read Gerald Pollack's new book regarding the nature of evaporation. Maybe putting the water in a grounded vessel might give it some electrons.

I would hope that everyone knows that using bottled water on a regular basis is insane for a number of reasons.

Until then if I drink a gallon a day that's only around $10/month for $.37/gallon

### Jonathin — 2013-06-11T19:55:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/post-87970>

"Please post few"

consumer expense - I would rather spend my money on more or better food
container pollution - recycling is great but does not happen everywhere
transportation / energy expenditure - water weighs alot
truth in labeling / quality - do you trust a label? Do you trust that there won't be bottling errors?
security - how many days of water are you going to have on hand before going to the store again

### Jonathin — 2013-06-11T20:31:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/post-87971>

> [GlenPDQ said:](/goto/post?id=87956)
>
> If I recall some of Pollack's work, one MIGHT be able to make NZ water by adding a collagen or a liquid protein to water and then shining light on it. Did not Pollack's work show that protein membranes appear to generate a structured water with a strong negative charge under infrared light.

Hydrophillic surfaces (usually with a negative charge) are a seed of charge that allows structured water to build layers like layers of hexagonal chicken wire. Energy in the form of infrared light allows the expansion of the structured layer (exclusion zone or EZ layer). Any number of interfacial situations can create a structured water to various degrees, i.e. the surface of a lake to the inside of a nano-tube. There seem to be a number of water structures that can be artificially generated with magnets, vortexes, ultrasound. I think it is still be determined the value or biological effects of these types of water.

A little off topic:
For me as a farmer I am fascinated by the structuring of water in a soil that has a high level of microbiological activity and clay-humus complexes. This in coordination with photosynthetic plants that pump carbon nutrients through their roots into the soil creates an expanding soil zone of high energy and ehanced nutrient availability. A healthy soil is a work in progress like our own personal health.

### Grizz — 2013-10-02T14:53:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/post-96693>

> [Ltwo said:](/goto/post?id=96667)
>
> Has anyone seen any water test results for Primo water?

This is a GREAT topic. Here is the water analysis:
[http://www.primowater.com/PrimoNew/files/92/92ac9e80-5937-4ce2-8a8f-666816d8aed3.pdf](http://www.primowater.com/PrimoNew/files/92/92ac9e80-5937-4ce2-8a8f-666816d8aed3.pdf)

According to EPA estimates, 40 million Americans are exposed to levels of lead in water well above the EPA’s proposed maximum contaminant allowances.
(U.S. Tap Water Report released by the Environmental Working Group)
Primo is lead free, chlorine free, fluoride free and pretty much everything else free!
[http://www.primowaterdirect.com/Our-Water/Water-Facts](http://www.primowaterdirect.com/Our-Water/Water-Facts)

Notice: NO fluoride and that is where most of the water filters fail. The only 2 methods that are effective is reverse osmosis & distilled water. $1.37 for 3 gallons is about the cost of electricity to distill 3 gallons at 10 cents per Kwh. In most areas of the USA, Primo is less expensive than the electricity to distill water !

Primowater is made via reverse osmosis filters.
[http://www.primowater.com/Great-Value-FILL-IT-YOURSELF.aspx](http://www.primowater.com/Great-Value-FILL-IT-YOURSELF.aspx)

Grizz

# Thread: How to recover from anorexia?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-recover-from-anorexia.7308/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 53
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hemming — 2013-06-07T20:38:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-recover-from-anorexia.7308/post-87745>

I'm a 26y old male, 5'9''. I've been having anorexia for around one year and lost weight from 143lbs to 109lbs at my lowest, I'm now at 116lbs. I ran >70k/week, biked 40k/week and did some weight exercises in a large part of that period and then burned out. At the moment I bike 45min each day to and from work and do some body weight exercises and free weight exercises 3-5 times/week.

I just had some labs done and almost all of it is in the normal range. My zinc and red blood platelets are too low and my amylase is too high, my TSH is 1.34 (I know that doesn't say much alone) it has fallen from above 2 since my last test. In December I had the following tests (SI units)
LDL 3.4
HDL 1.62
Tri 0.82
CRP <0.2 (it doesn't say HS)

I'm experiencing all the symptoms of low cortisol. Generally I'm very fatigued, my legs feel like I've ran a marathon, I'm unable to concentrate, indecisive and very confused about almost everything.

I've been eating paleo for eight months but didn't learn about this site until a couple of weeks ago. I eat a ketogenic diet but have a fear of protein (besides food in general) as I'm scared of the side effects of eating too much protein (even though none of blood samples show any indications of this). Recently I've also become afraid of fat and try to restrict that too - I'm contemplating that that might have something to do with low energy level too as I'm simply not eating enough. But when I try to eat more fat I get scared of my lipids and when I try to eat more carbs I get scared of all the adverse effects from that.

Basically my question is how would it be best to recover and get my former energy, body and strength back? Any concrete advice on macro ratios would be greatly appreciated. The food is self explanatory but how should I balance carbs, protein and fat? Should I just go all out on eating as much as possible for the three big meals, should I follow the BAB protocol? How many calories should i eat?

At the moment I bike 45min each day to and from work and do some body weight exercises and free weight exercises 3-5 times/week.
>
> I just had some labs done and almost all of it is in the normal range. My zinc and red blood platelets are too low and my amylase is too high, my TSH is 1.34 (I know that doesn't say much alone) it has fallen from above 2 since my last test. In December I had the following tests (SI units)
> LDL 3.4
> HDL 1.62
> Tri 0.82
> CRP <0.2 (it doesn't say HS)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-09T15:37:21+0300

"Will" directs your passion and is the faculty by which you decides upon and initiates action. A person with a big "WANT TO" don't read history to get encouraged, there are so driven that they make history. A person with a big "WANT TO" will
use failures as stepping stones and rejections fall off like water on a duck's back. People with a big "WANT TO" have seen the end result already and will never be satisfied unless the find a way to reach their vision. These men and women are relentless and unstoppable because their vision consumes them. People with a big "WANT TO" are not waiting to be validated , pampered, patted on the back. They are self motivated and fearless to stand alone . They will take life by its horn and aren't afraid to walk the road alone because that's the right thing to do. Today consider the power of your "will" to succeed in any endeavor, because you can climb any mountain and overcome any ceiling if you have a "Big Want to".

It is a question of timing when your broken.......it is nothing with the coffee. The coffee effect is well known.........

The same is true for a normal person and a diabetic. A normal person can eat a snickers bar without too much risk.......and diabetic can not. Why? Their hormone panels reflect that their molecular clocks are set differently because of their individual epigenetics. Time is relative to our lifestyles and everything else according to one famous 1905 paper. Einstein 101.

### Dextery — 2013-06-15T21:41:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-recover-from-anorexia.7308/post-88125>

> I'm seeing my doctor at a specialist clinic Monday, any tests I should ask for that relates specifically to anorexia in addition to the ones you generally recommend and cortisol? I'll be asking for HS-CRP, lipid panel and thyroid panel.

If you do not ask for a reverse T3 test along with the normal thyroid panel, most likely you will not get it. Go to [www.stopthethroidmadness.com](http://www.stopthethroidmadness.com) in advance of your visit to your doctor to learn about T3/RT3 ratios. If they will do it, also get a NMR lipid profile done. Most importantly, it will tell you your total LDL particle number and your small dense LDL number. You want that number to be around 10% or less. Report back.

From other sites I have read about anorexia it says to eat a large amount of carbs and protein but I am wondering if paleo high fat and high protein low carb would be better for my recovery? I used to be very lean about 10% body fat before the anorexia but now since I gained all the weight back it is mostly in the form of fat and I am at, at least 20% bf. I am just wondering which protocol you think I should follow and any other tests or advice you have I would really appreciate as I dont know what to do?

I've done some more and longer ocean swims and I feel so much better overall now. I've noticed that I can stay in the water for longer, I don't feel cold when I get out of the water and have no shivering afterwards. Additionally, the cold showers doesn't feel that cold anymore and my body stays warm during them and my heart rate is more or less unaffected.

Besides these effects, my mood is much better and I feel better in general. I'm not at all cured but I really think that CT has helped me with the first steps of recovery.

### Hemming — 2013-06-30T19:56:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-recover-from-anorexia.7308/post-89247>

Jack, I have a couple of questions for you which I really hope you have time to answer.

1) The last week I've felt more hungry than usual - is that a good or bad sign in my situation?

2) I eat according to the Epi Paleo diet and have no cravings for bread, candy, ice cream whatever. As you have written in your blog I've noticed that carbs from vegetables doesn't really satisfy me whereas I rather want protein and especially fat. I know you don't think protein is harmful if the body is running optimally but should I worry about eating too much protein? I really like coconut products, butter and olive oil - should I just eat more of those as my hunger might be because I'm not eating enough fat?

I eat three meals each day with no snacking and have them at regular times with breakfast within 30min of waking up and dinner some hours before going to sleep. I sleep 8-9 hours each night easily. I take cold showers each day and swim in the ocean (water temp. 61-67F) 2 times each week for 15-20min.
I realise that some of these question might be a bit 'immature' but right now I'm just trying to get some guidance on what to do. Some of my insecurity about this might also be due to a state of confusion due to my low weight but I find it very difficult to convince myself that I need to gain weight.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-30T21:17:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-recover-from-anorexia.7308/post-89248>

Eat the protein and fat........and no protein wont kill you.....plus it helps increase carnosine which is big for those with Eating disorders like Anorexia, bulemia, and T2D. the issue is you need a lot of carnosine to offset the glycation in your inner mitochondrial membrane that is slowing down your current. If your hunger is returning that is an excellent prognostic sign that your hypothalamus can rewire and you did not fry your hypocretin neurons.......

### Hemming — 2013-06-30T21:59:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-recover-from-anorexia.7308/post-89250>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=89248)
>
> Eat the protein and fat........and no protein wont kill you.....plus it helps increase carnosine which is big for those with Eating disorders like Anorexia, bulemia, and T2D. the issue is you need a lot of carnosine to offset the glycation in your inner mitochondrial membrane that is slowing down your current. If your hunger is returning that is an excellent prognostic sign that your hypothalamus can rewire and you did not fry your hypocretin neurons.......

Thanks a lot for the concise answer Jack! I'm happy to hear that protein is actually more beneficial for me as it's something I've been fearing for a while.

I'm also happy that my increased hunger is a sign of recovery and that I should just respond to it by eating more protein and fat. My biggest problem now will be actually responding to that hunger without feeling guilty - which is why is so good to get that information from you to get some comfort in what I'm doing is 'the right thing'. As a follow-up question - could my continued chronic fatigue be because I'm still not eating enough in general?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-01T02:43:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-recover-from-anorexia.7308/post-89264>

could be do to a lot......but recoveries are based on stochastic math........and therefore nothing in recovery is linear, except your belief that recoveries are in fact linear.

### Hemming — 2013-07-01T11:31:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-recover-from-anorexia.7308/post-89287>

Thanks Jack. Ironically, I've actually studied stochastic math so I should be able to understand it except that this mental state makes you confused about even the most simple things.

Reading more and more of your blog I see that almost all the answers are there - it's just about getting to them.

# Thread: High Carbs In The Summertime, What?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-carbs-in-the-summertime-what.7310/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-06-07T22:04:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-carbs-in-the-summertime-what.7310/post-87750>

Can someone explain or point me to where JK explains why he eats high carb in the summertime?

I was listening to his recent podcast with Ben and after saying that carbs are not needed for performance he went on to say that in parts of June, July and August he eats over 250 grams of carbs per day.

I'm a bit confused.

If a performance athlete doesn't need them why would anyone else?

### Hemming — 2013-06-07T22:28:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-carbs-in-the-summertime-what.7310/post-87756>

He eats them because they're available in abundance during the summer time. He eats seasonally.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-07T23:53:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-carbs-in-the-summertime-what.7310/post-87764>

carbs are only designed to grow in long light cycles when the photoelectric effect is massive. Read EMF 7 and the QB 7-9 to get this point......it is massively important.

### Danco3636 — 2013-06-08T00:14:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-carbs-in-the-summertime-what.7310/post-87768>

I've also heard Jack mention that to express the PPP for performance and speed up the adaptions to remain keto or VLC throught the summer or long light cycles. This is what I am doing.

### sjoshua — 2013-06-08T01:36:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-carbs-in-the-summertime-what.7310/post-87770>

I believe your compliance depends on what you are hoping to take away. If I'm correct, Jack's ultimate goal is optimized health, which would dictate following the seasonal food availability trends.

But, like Danco said, if your goal is achieving performance and improving your body's fat adaptation then consuming ample carbs following seasonal trends would be working against you, hence a performance-minded individual would be more likely to remain in/around ketosis.

### Dali Dula — 2013-06-08T02:06:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-carbs-in-the-summertime-what.7310/post-87772>

>
> But, like Danco said, if your goal is achieving performance and improving your body's fat adaptation then consuming ample carbs following seasonal trends would be working against you, hence a performance-minded individual would be more likely to remain in/around ketosis.

This would also apply to anyone with broken glucose metabolism-diabetics. I manage tight control with T2D and will not change my carb consumption much in high light cycles. I plan on picking and eating wild berries and possibly some pears and peaches ripe off the tree. That's it.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-08T02:30:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-carbs-in-the-summertime-what.7310/post-87775>

# Thread: BAB, leptin reset, and chronic hunger...please chime in
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-leptin-reset-and-chronic-hunger-please-chime-in.7317/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennyha — 2013-06-09T02:05:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-leptin-reset-and-chronic-hunger-please-chime-in.7317/post-87816>

Hello all. I have a question regarding the Leptin Reset, the BAB, and hunger. I have been doing the Leptin Reset for the past year or so. I am 6'0 and 155 lbs and probably 12% body fat or so. My hormones are out of whack, and my Reverse T3 is elevated. I also used to be a binge eater, and I think Jack said those with eating disorders in the past take longer to become LS. I'm thinking maybe I haven't become LS yet because I'm not doing it entirely correctly. The problem is, I NEVER feel full. So I will stop my BAB at 3,000 calories because it just seems like I will do more harm than good (digestion and gut wise) if I eat more than that. During my binge days, I could eat up to 10,000 calories in a single day-no joke! Those days are behind me, but I still feel hungry in the morning despite eating a huge breakfast.

My typical breakfast is 6 large eggs, 8 oz grass fed ground beef, 4 TBS coconut oil, 4 TBS lard, 1 TBS olive oil, and some veggies. I don't tolerate butter, as I break out after I eat it, just I now just avoid it.

My questions are:

1) Should I just keep eating until I somehow feel full, even if that means eating an entire jar of coconut oil in one setting? I have gained 7 lbs in the past 2 weeks eating large amounts trying to tame the hunger, but it just won't subside no matter how much I eat. Fat seems to not fill me up one bit. I could eat a huge tub of lard and not feel full.

2) Is it possible to be leptin sensitive if you feel constantly hungry? I have chronic fatigue for the most part, so it's almost as if the food I eat isn't fueling me.

3) If yes to eating to satiety, how high should I go? I have an enormous appetite for my size, and could maybe go up to 6,000 calories or so. How should I add my calories? I'm assuming fat.

Thank you for any help you can give me

### Dextery — 2013-06-09T02:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-leptin-reset-and-chronic-hunger-please-chime-in.7317/post-87817>

It sounds like your ghrelin hormone that tells you that you are hungry is in runaway mode. The Leptin hormone plays the role to tell your brain hypothalamus that you are no longer hungry or satiated. Leptin resistance can account for this. Ghrelin is overwhelming Leptin. Become leptin sensitive and your cravings probably will abate.

[http://www.obesityaction.org/educat...les-2/general-articles/ghrelin-the-go-hormone](http://www.obesityaction.org/educational-resources/resource-articles-2/general-articles/ghrelin-the-go-hormone)

Have you ever done a 24 hour fast? You know you will not die by not eating for 24 hours. Try it and see how you do. Remember, you are totally responsible for what you eat regardless of what the gremlin hormone GHRELIN says to you. Train yourself not to listen to GHRELIN.

### Inger — 2013-06-09T06:30:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-leptin-reset-and-chronic-hunger-please-chime-in.7317/post-87824>

I see you guys as a former bulemic and binge eater... I also need quite big amounts of food and fat alone does not really fill me up either. I need lots of protein for breakfast. I can eat one pound of raw elk meat easily and it has so much protein because it has so little fat in it.
The only thing I can suggest is.. what if you try to eat the amount of food in, say, mussels? I am curious to see what would happen. Or what if you try to eat the ground beef raw.. and the eggs too, or ditch the eggs all together? Hubs like to eat beef tartar once in a while, but he can eat quite a small amount only and he is satiated, way sooner than with cooked meat. IDK why. But you could try. Try to eat the salmon raw too. 1 pound of raw salmon is very satiating to me. You could benefit from eating the food raw. At least I think it would be worth a try...

I would say Dextery could be right, in such extreme situations it could be good to use your brain to limit the food if the raw thing does not help. I cannot eat as much as I want because I have not money for it. But I am not going hungry either. I am also only a small woman.. so you guys should eat the double amount of what I eat = 2 pounds of raw ground elk. With lots of coconut oil on the top. I really wonder if you would still be hungry after...

Another thing, maybe your body craves micronutrients? That is why fat does not cut it either, because it has no selenium, zink, Iodine.. etc. That is what I tell myself when I am kind of shocked how much meat I can eat. It might take a long time to fill up the bodys reserves. Supplements might not cut it at all as they not always get assimilated.

### HoneyChild — 2013-06-09T12:26:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-leptin-reset-and-chronic-hunger-please-chime-in.7317/post-87829>

Are you doing CT? Eventually CT should help reduce your hunger.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-09T15:41:13+0300

> [Dextery said:](/goto/post?id=87817)
>
> It sounds like your ghrelin hormone that tells you that you are hungry is in runaway mode. The Leptin hormone plays the role to tell your brain hypothalamus that you are no longer hungry or satiated. Leptin resistance can account for this. Ghrelin is overwhelming Leptin. Become leptin sensitive and your cravings probably will abate.
>
> [http://www.obesityaction.org/educat...les-2/general-articles/ghrelin-the-go-hormone](http://www.obesityaction.org/educational-resources/resource-articles-2/general-articles/ghrelin-the-go-hormone)
>
> Have you ever done a 24 hour fast? You know you will not die by not eating for 24 hours. Try it and see how you do. Remember, you are totally responsible for what you eat regardless of what the gremlin hormone GHRELIN says to you. Train yourself not to listen to GHRELIN.

Thanks for your reply. Yes I have fasted many, many times. I have probably done over 200 "fasts" in my life of at least 48 hours. I understand ghrelin and its cause on hunger as well, at least partially. I just find it weird I can't ever seem to feel full

I'm thinking maybe I haven't become LS yet because I'm not doing it entirely correctly. The problem is, I NEVER feel full. So I will stop my BAB at 3,000 calories because it just seems like I will do more harm than good (digestion and gut wise) if I eat more than that. During my binge days, I could eat up to 10,000 calories in a single day-no joke! Those days are behind me, but I still feel hungry in the morning despite eating a huge breakfast.
>
> My typical breakfast is 6 large eggs, 8 oz grass fed ground beef, 4 TBS coconut oil, 4 TBS lard, 1 TBS olive oil, and some veggies. I don't tolerate butter, as I break out after I eat it, just I now just avoid it.
>
> My questions are:
>
> 1) Should I just keep eating until I somehow feel full, even if that means eating an entire jar of coconut oil in one setting? I have gained 7 lbs in the past 2 weeks eating large amounts trying to tame the hunger, but it just won't subside no matter how much I eat. Fat seems to not fill me up one bit. I could eat a huge tub of lard and not feel full.
>
> 2) Is it possible to be leptin sensitive if you feel constantly hungry? I have chronic fatigue for the most part, so it's almost as if the food I eat isn't fueling me.
>
> 3) If yes to eating to satiety, how high should I go? I have an enormous appetite for my size, and could maybe go up to 6,000 calories or so. How should I add my calories? I'm assuming fat.
>
> Thank you for any help you can give me

Bennyha,

I am intriged by your post. If I didn't know any better I would have thought that you were copying my symptoms. I havent been suffering with the extreme hunger as long as you have, but have come to the point where I can literally eat a whole jar of Coconut oil with 70 grams of protein and still be hungry, I've even resorted to frinking olive oil from the bottle while eating macadamia nuts. I too have gained weight. I face chronic fatigue, low motivation, low libido, etc.

In CHRIST,
Lucas

### bennyha — 2013-06-11T18:56:52+0300

It's been a long adventure and one that I don't wish upon my worst enemy. I continue to put on weight, but eating 5,000 calories a day will do that. I am just hoping that I can somehow become LS. I have been doing the Leptin Reset for nearly two years now, and still haven't had success killing my hunger.

I ate 3,500+ calories for breakfast this morning alone-1 lbs ground beef, 4 eggs, 4 pieces bacon, 5 TBS lard, 14 TBS! CO. For whatever reason, I just don't get full on fat and protein. Haven't for the past few years. I have mainly been very low carb for the most part the past couple years as well, and that is when all my problems began as well. I know carbs are evil, but I do feel better physically and mentally as well as from a satiety perspective as well when I eat between 50-100 grams of carbs a day. I know they are bad in long run, so I'm trying to just keep on keeping on eating very low carb.

I have had some hormone testing over the past year or two. I have low Free T3, a really high TSH, low Free T4 (but barely in range). My RT3 was high but in range, but my FT3:RT3 ratio was well under 20. My temps are all over the place, and vary based on my settings, but run about 1 degree low. Also, my heart rate is always between 50-60. The fact I can't sweat for 2 years now and have an extreme sensitivity to cold tells me things are wrong. I have more adrenal fatigue symptoms though. I believe my thyroid is dysfunctional because my wiring is way off. I used to binge eat and fast for days at a time. I went from high to low cortisol. I have been trying all this stuff for 2 years and have found no relief. I am at odds about what to do next...nothing seems to work.

But aren't low thyroid labs just a leptin problem?

Can one be leptin sensitive when having low thyroid lab numbers? That's my real question.

Could some of my perceived leptin problems and hunger problems be solved if I went on thyroid medicine to get my thyroid optimized? So confused on this...

Leptin problems can certainly result in hypothyroidism but I still think that nutritional deficiencies such as iodine, selenium and B12 can cause problems too and need to be resolved. Jack has said a number of times that hormone replacement might be needed for a lot of people because our environment has altered so much we need need to do everything we can to fight back.

For me personally, the only health problem I really have is zero libido and when I got my sex hormones tested they came back very low. I've been trying my best with diet, cold baths and nutritional supplements but I have a sinking feeling that I'll need to start buying what I can over the counter - estrogen, progesterone and dhea. I haven't had my thyroid tested but I'm on the iodine protocol just now. Will hopefully get my thyroid tested soon.

Lack of sweating can be leptin resistance but I watched a Youtube video of Dr Jorge Flechas talking about how iodine deficiency can do this too.

Destiny posted this on the thyroid thread:-

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=87724)
>
> I had to drop my T3 medication even more since I started taking seven 12.5 mg Iodoral every day. I am at a 1/3 of the previous dosage. I guess my thyroid is kicking in?

A lot of people here are on thyroid/sex hormones in addition to all the nutritional/lifestyle protocols Jack has laid out. I set out to heal myself through diet/lifestyle but I'm just not sure if this is possible considering what we've all been doing to ourselves over the years. For me personally I was vegetarian most of my life and was brought up thinking that fat, salt and sun are all bad for you. Then combine this with artificial light and EMFs... things aren't going to fix themselves quickly.

I think you should seriously consider going to a doctor in order to get thyroid meds. Perhaps over time your body will heal itself and you'll not need to keep taking the meds forever.

Kent Holtorf, MD: Most patients who come in have been on numerous diets and lifestyle changes and they generally are very knowledgeable in that area. **Low-carbohydrate** diets will suppress thyroid function and increase reverse T3 more than comparable calorie reductions with adequate carbohydrates, so while a low-carbohydrate diet may result in initial weight loss, patients are prone to regaining weight unless the reverse T3 issue is addressed.

# Thread: Grandma's Experiences Leave a Mark on Your Genes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grandmas-experiences-leave-a-mark-on-your-genes.7318/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-06-09T22:50:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grandmas-experiences-leave-a-mark-on-your-genes.7318/post-87861>

[http://discovermagazine.com/2013/ma...ve-epigenetic-mark-on-your-genes#.UbSjy5xkkSn](http://discovermagazine.com/2013/may/13-grandmas-experiences-leave-epigenetic-mark-on-your-genes#.UbSjy5xkkSn)

**Your ancestors' lousy childhoods or excellent adventures might change your personality, bequeathing anxiety or resilience by altering the epigenetic expressions of genes in the brain.**

"Szyf and Meaney considered a hypothesis as improbable as it was profound: If diet and chemicals can cause epigenetic changes, could certain experiences â€” child neglect, drug abuse or other severe stresses â€” also set off epigenetic changes to the DNA inside the neurons of a personâ€™s brain? That question turned out to be the basis of a new field, behavioral epigenetics, now so vibrant it has spawned dozens of studies and suggested profound new treatments to heal the brain."

Dr Kruse has talked about the genes of our grandmothers that are passed on influence the being of their children and grandchildren. But I did not understand that how the grandmother experienced life... say whether a hard cruel life or a life of affluence...affects how the children and grandchildren express their genes. Epigenetics of behavior...a loving, touching, caring mother passes along those epigenetic genes to the offspring...& to the grandchild. The offspring will indeed receive these traits. Likewise the rock hardened life of a cruel mother with little or no loving or caring will pass along those epigenetic genes to the child and grandchildren.

The offspring of rats that were shy and reticent were switched to a mother rat that exhibited outgoing social behavior did not change their epigenetic expression of reticence...nor did their offspring.

Very interesting read. You never knew how important your grandmother was/is to who you are.

There are some on this forum that say they have never met a challenge they did not like and always are upbeat and exuberant - and there are others that say they are somewhat a loner and not outgoing but are doing quite well. Some of our greatest thinkers have been shy and retiring.

Can anyone identify how their grandmother affected their state of being today?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-09T23:03:28+0300

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-12T13:13:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs.7336/post-87992>

Got a copy of my labs yesterday.

My TSH is 1.55 mIU/l, Free T4 17.0 pmol/L, Total T3 0.51 nmol/L (low-range is 1.3-3.1), and cortisol was 694.9 morning fasting.

Doc wasn't concerned about the Total T3 being low. Then again he barely knew what Leptin was, and could offer no advice for my fatigue, hunger, low libido, etc except 'are you eating enough fiber?' This is the doctor who has had me try every SSRI, SSNRI, TAD, and stimulant on the market for my chronic fatigue and lack of motivation.

I had asked for a leptin test but he said that he doesnt know where that can be done in Ireland. I got him to refer me to an endocrinologist in the best private hospital in Dublin, in hopes that his guy will perhaps have a clue about leptin resistance, adrenal fatigue, or whatever is going on with me.

Any insight? I'm fatigued, gaining wieght, and hungry. Would appreciate some advice.

### Rylee — 2013-06-13T17:46:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs.7336/post-88036>

>

This is the doctor who has had me try every SSRI, SSNRI, TAD, and stimulant on the market for my chronic fatigue and lack of motivation.

I got him to refer me to an endocrinologist in the best private hospital in Dublin, in hopes that his guy will perhaps have a clue about leptin resistance, adrenal fatigue, or whatever is going on with me.
>
> Any insight? I'm fatigued, gaining wieght, and hungry. Would appreciate some advice.

Your T3 appears to be very low, and you don't include reference ranges for your other labs so it's difficult to comment on them.

You have symptoms and (possibly) labs consistent with hypothyroidism. If you're on Facebook, find Stop The Thyroid Madness on there and join the group. I'm learning a lot from those folks - you can post your labs (with reference ranges) and others who know more about this than you or me will comment and interpret for you. Not as good as a doc, perhaps, but in some ways better. Good luck.

### gsteppe14@gmail.com — 2013-08-21T08:08:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs.7336/post-93327>

You have T3, not FT3, but when your T3 is that low, I believe you can infer that your FT3 would also be low. Your endo should be concerned. Limited T4 to T3 transfer and hence you have hypothyroid symptoms, despite your TSH. If you are obese or have inflammation, your low T3 may not be reflected in your pituitary's TSH. Your tissue T3 is low; your pituitary T3 isn't. So your TSH seems normal but that doesn't mean you're not hypothyroid. So in this case, you really have to go by FT3. Get your FT3 and RevT3 measured. But it's obvious that FT3 will be low and RT3 will be high.

I'd go get an endo that uses compound pharmacies and have him prescribe custom T3 doses. The reason why you feel lousy is because you're hypothyroid. Also get those Ft4 pmol converted so we can interpret it correctly.

Your endo is not gonna know about leptin resistance. I just realized you're overseas. I really don't know to what extent compound pharmacies are available overseas. I know there're some. Go to LDN sites and see if you can find compound pharmas that prescribe LDN; they would also take doc orders for T3, not just packaged stuff like Cytomel or Armor.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-21T16:03:32+0300

# Thread: Am I in ketosis and don't even realize it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-in-ketosis-and-dont-even-realize-it.7337/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-06-12T16:38:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-in-ketosis-and-dont-even-realize-it.7337/post-87999>

Been doing the Leptin Rx for a little over a week now.

Less than 50g of carbs every day except maybe 2 days where I was around 70g.

I can't really say that anything has felt any different and my clothes don't fit any different.

Could to much protein be keeping me out of ketosis?

Most of my BAB's are around 100g of Protein and I started eating even more fat - easily over 300 grams between foods and oils (coconut oil, olive oil and butter)

I probably consume around 180g of protein each day and could easily do more.

As I've said in many of my posts my sleep definitely isn't optimal (wake up numerous times in the night) so maybe the poor skeep is keeping me from feeling the benefits.

I've read from other people talking about ketosis that it feels awesome - more energy, feel like they are burning more fat.

I should also mention that I am only drinking fluoride free water - even got some fluoride free toothpaste as many have suggested that the fluoride could be dehydrating me and robbing me of energy.

Would be interested in hearing any thoughts.

### LinD — 2013-06-12T17:27:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-in-ketosis-and-dont-even-realize-it.7337/post-88001>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-in-ketosis-and-dont-even-realize-it.7337/post-88002>

Thats what i am wondering.

Hoping Doc chims in with an opinion

> [LinD said:](/goto/post?id=88001)
>
> Too much protein resulting in too much glucose might be an issue.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-12T18:47:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-in-ketosis-and-dont-even-realize-it.7337/post-88003>

what do you keto dip stix say?

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-12T21:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-in-ketosis-and-dont-even-realize-it.7337/post-88007>

For the time being I would recommend lowering your protein substantially. Eat high fat, very low - low carb, moderate protein. I would say limit protein to 150g max, preferably less than that even. If you're fat adapted you don't have to worry about losing muscle, you're body will just eat up its own body fat (even if you are extremely lean) and use dietary fat differently. Protein will be used for muscle synthesis and tissue growth/repair as well as other processes. This is where it gets fuzzy for me, though, as protein has the potential to knock you out of ketosis. Amino Acids are 48-58% percent gluconeogenic I believe - some can be used to make ketones even, some glucose, and some can be used for either. So it gets quite confusing. Different Amino's stimulate different amounts of insulin as well. Take a look at this: [http://www.lucastafur.com/search/label/high protein](http://www.lucastafur.com/search/label/high%20protein)

"So what happens when we eat a high fat/high protein diet? Let's look at Eskimos (1). On average, they consume approximately **280g of protein**, 125g of fat and 54g of carbohydrate. This gives us a total of 2461 kcal of which 45.5% is protein, 45.71% is fat, and around 8.77% is carbohydrate. This is both high protein and high fat, and very low carbohydrate. Kind of my ideal diet. When researchers studied their metabolisms, **they found that they are not in ketosis** during their usual diet. Ketosis is developed during fasting, but to a much lesser degree than other human subjects. The authors concluded:
"**Eskimos show a remarkable power to oxidize fats completely**, as evidenced by the small amount of acetone bodies excreted in the urine in fasting."

This is extremely interesting to me. I mean, it is literally fascinating to me for a variety of reasons. I really have to continue learning genetics and biochemistry to connect all the dots more in depth, but this leads me to some ideas. As you can see, regardless of the high protein count (actually, extremely high), they are still extremely fat adapted, and use fat for their biological needs. They show ketone build up during fasting, but less than typical individuals - this is probably because they utilize their body fat and dietary fat much more efficiently. The other thing is they measured acetone buildup in the urine, rather than blood/breath samples. This means that they could have actually been circulating a LOT more ketones, but they were utilizing them so efficiently, a small amount if any showed up in the urine. There are other factors that can affect the urine analysis as well. What I'm getting at is, despite the extremely high protein intake, they were still utilizing fatty acids profoundly well, and they were quite possibly in a state of deep ketosis - it just wasn't displayed due to inaccurate measurements.

Now, the reason I recommended limiting protein, is because I'm not quite sure how they (the eskimo's) could consume so much protein without (1) harmful effects (2) without GNG destroying the state of ketosis. [http://www.ketotic.org/2012/08/if-you-eat-excess-protein-does-it-turn.html](http://www.ketotic.org/2012/08/if-you-eat-excess-protein-does-it-turn.html) this is also a good read. According to Peter Attia, who is one of the only people publicly doing ketogenic experiments mixed with extreme athletic abilities, he had trouble producing ketones with up to 50g carbs and 150g protein, despite activity levels and source of carbs/protein (nuts which are slowly digesting). Other VLC advocates also advocate slightly low protein levels. But these other links display that it is quite possible to maintain ketosis and beta-oxidation while consuming lots of protein. Maybe it just takes time. So maybe you start VLC/High Fat/Moderate-moderately low protein, and after a long period (24-36mo's) you can dial up the protein and not worry about GNG from amino acids taking place.

Sorry if that was confusing or just a giant rambling, I'm just interested in this a lot. I'm actually about to go make a post comparing some ideas about ketosis/ketogenesis because as I can see from some recent readings, there are far different ideas for how one should eat a ketogenic diet and it gets even more complex in terms of physical performance.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-13T01:49:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-in-ketosis-and-dont-even-realize-it.7337/post-88012>

Big Papa, this is awesome, thank you for the input.

I have been eating so much protein to help with hunger but usually I'm still hungry, but I'll do it.

If backing the protein puts me in ketosis and I really experience all the benefits that people talk about then a little hunger will be worth it.

I already went way over 150g today so I will start tomorrow.

I will probably skip lunch so I can have a bigger dinner.

That Peter Attia guy sure has a ton of great info.

Thank you again - absolutely fantastic

[http://www.ketotic.org/2012/08/if-you-eat-excess-protein-does-it-turn.html](http://www.ketotic.org/2012/08/if-you-eat-excess-protein-does-it-turn.html) this is also a good read. According to Peter Attia, who is one of the only people publicly doing ketogenic experiments mixed with extreme athletic abilities, he had trouble producing ketones with up to 50g carbs and 150g protein, despite activity levels and source of carbs/protein (nuts which are slowly digesting). Other VLC advocates also advocate slightly low protein levels. But these other links display that it is quite possible to maintain ketosis and beta-oxidation while consuming lots of protein. Maybe it just takes time. So maybe you start VLC/High Fat/Moderate-moderately low protein, and after a long period (24-36mo's) you can dial up the protein and not worry about GNG from amino acids taking place.
>
> Sorry if that was confusing or just a giant rambling, I'm just interested in this a lot. I'm actually about to go make a post comparing some ideas about ketosis/ketogenesis because as I can see from some recent readings, there are far different ideas for how one should eat a ketogenic diet and it gets even more complex in terms of physical performance.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-13T02:12:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-in-ketosis-and-dont-even-realize-it.7337/post-88014>

No problem, glad I can help lead you in the direction of some more information. That's what's great about forums like these, we can all partake in each others journey's to recovery/well-being/enhanced performance/etc. Peter Attia's blog is definitely one of my favorites. I had created a word document composed of hundreds of blog posts, video presentations, and scholarly articles on ketosis, I haven't gotten to reading it yet as my fiance has my laptop and I have hers lol. I'm going to create a post soon (maybe tonight or tomorrow night) on the differences in ketogenic diets because I have noticed a lot, especially if you look at the experiments of Peter Attia vs. that of Ben Greenfield - Peter Attia is extremely active and still limits carbs to below 50, whereas Ben Greenfield consumes 50-100g of carbs on an "easy day" in his current ketogenic experiment. There are differences in beliefs of protein, and even the kinds of fats that should be consumed (polyunsaturated vs. saturated vs. mct). So many variables to target. I'm hoping some more knowledgeable people, or even Dr. Kruse will comment when I post it because I feel as though it will clarify things not only for me, but for many others.
